definitions: Baal-Tamar; a place near Gibeah associated with Israel's battle against Benjamin in Judges.
Baal-tamar.note: Location: Near Gibeah in Benjamin on the battlefield of Judges 20.

definitions: Baal-Zebub; the false god of Ekron, later used in Scripture as a name associated with satanic power.
Baal-Zephon; the place by the sea opposite which Israel encamped before the crossing and Egypt's overthrow.
Baal-zephon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Defenneh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Baalah; a name used of Kiriath-jearim and of other localities associated with border descriptions and sacred history.
Baalah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Baalath; a town fortified by Solomon, probably in the western part of the land.
Baalath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Maghar.

definitions: Baalath-Beer; a southern town of Simeon, also called Ramath of the south.
Baalath-beer.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Malhata.

definitions: Baale; another name or form connected with Baalah, commonly identified with Kirjath-jearim.
Baali; my lord or my baal, used in contrast with the covenant language God requires from His people.
Baalim; the various Baals or local manifestations of the Canaanite false god Baal.
Baalis; king of the Ammonites in the days of Gedaliah.
Baana; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including one of Solomon's officers.
Baanah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by one of the captains who slew Ish-bosheth.
Baara; a woman named in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Baaseiah; a Levite named in the line associated with temple music.
Baasha; a man named in Scripture, son of Ahijah, father of Elah.
Babbler; one who prates idly, or in Acts one viewed as a picker-up of scraps of learning.
Babbling; present participial form of 'babble', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'babble'.
Plural form of 'babbling'. See 'babbling'.
Babe; an infant or very young child.
Babel; the city where human speech was confounded and which afterward became identified with Babylon.
Plural form of 'babe'. See 'babe'.
Babylon; the famed city on the Euphrates and, by extension, the kingdom or imperial power centered there.
In prophetic passages it may also stand for proud worldly power set against God.
Babylonians; the people of Babylon or those belonging to the Babylonian empire that carried Judah into exile.
Babylonish; belonging to Babylon or to the Babylonian realm and style.
Babylonish.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Babylon.

definitions: Baca; the valley so named in Psalm 84, perhaps associated with weeping or balsam trees.
Bachrites; descendants of Becher reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
The back; the hinder part of the body or of a thing.
Plural form of 'backbiter'. See 'backbiter'.
Backbiteth; older third-person singular form of 'backbite'. See 'backbite'.
Speaking against another behind his back; slanderous detraction.
Plural form of 'backbiting'. See 'backbiting'.
Backbone; the spine or back-bone.
Plural form of 'back'. See 'back'.
Backside; the hinder part, rear part, or back.
Backslider; one who turns back from former faithfulness or obedience.
Backsliding; present participial form of 'backslide', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'backslide'.
Plural form of 'backsliding'. See 'backsliding'.
Backward; toward the back, behind, turned away, reluctant, or slow.
Bad; evil, harmful, unsound, or of poor quality.
Bade; the past tense of bid, meaning commanded, invited, or told.
Badest; older second-person singular form of 'bad', used with 'thou'. See 'bad'.
Plural form of 'badger'. See 'badger'.
The state, quality, or condition of being badnes.
A bag is a pouch, sack, or purse for carrying or storing something.
Plural form of 'bag'. See 'bag'.
Baharumite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Baharum in Scripture.
Baharumite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Tumeim, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Bahurim; the village near Jerusalem where Shimei cursed David during Absalom's rebellion.
Bahurim.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Tumeim.

definitions: Bajith; a Moabite town or temple-site named in Isaiah's burden of Moab.
Bakbakkar; a Levite or porter named in the chronicler's records.
Bakbuk; one of the Nethinims or temple servants named after the exile.
Bakbukiah; a Levite associated with praise and thanksgiving in the post-exilic community.
Bake; to prepare by dry heat, especially bread or cakes in an oven, pan, or on coals.
Baked; past tense or past participial form of 'bake'. See 'bake'.
Baked foods, pastries, or prepared sweet cakes.
Baken; baked.
A baker; one whose work is to prepare and bake bread or baked food.
Bakers; those whose work is to prepare and bake bread or baked food.
Baketh; older third-person singular form of 'bake'. See 'bake'.
Balaam; son of Beor, the diviner hired by Balak to curse Israel but constrained by God to bless.
Balac; a man named in Scripture, son of Zippor.
Baladan; the father of Merodach-Baladan, or the dynastic name associated with him.
Balah; a southern town in the Simeonite or Judean lists, likely related to Bilhah or Baalah in parallel records.
Balah.note: Location: Often identified with Tulul el Medbah.

definitions: Balak; a man named in Scripture, son of Zippor.
an even proportion, remainder, or weighing instrument; also to make equal or steady.
Plural form of 'balance'. See 'balance'.
Plural form of 'balancing'. See 'balancing'.
Bald; lacking hair, or made bare and stripped.
The state, quality, or condition of being baldnes.
Ball; something rounded and rolled together, or a thing tossed like a ball.
Balm is a fragrant resin or healing ointment, often suggesting remedy or soothing.
Bamah; an altar or high place mentioned in Scripture.
Bamoth; a high-place locality in Moab associated with Israel's wilderness route and Balaam's movements.
Bamoth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Quweiqiyeh.

definitions: Bamoth-Baal; a Moabite high-place locality from which Balaam viewed Israel.
Bamoth-baal.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Quweiqiyeh.

definitions: Band; a bond or binding, a strip or fastening, or a company or troop.
Banded; bound together, fastened, or formed into companies or groups.
Bands; bonds, fastenings, strips, or companies and troops.
Bani; a biblical personal name, especially borne by Levites and post-exilic heads of families.
Banished; past tense or past participial form of 'banish'. See 'banish'.
expulsion from a place, land, or kingdom.
A bank is the raised side, border, mound, or ridge beside a river, ditch, or similar place.
Plural form of 'bank'. See 'bank'.
A banner is an ensign, standard, or displayed sign of a people or host.
Plural form of 'banner'. See 'banner'.
A feast or rich entertainment of food and drink.
Plural form of 'banqueting'. See 'banqueting'.
Baptism; a rite or experience by which a person is placed into, under, or upon something so as to be identified with a person, message, people, covenant, suffering, cleansing, or judgment.
Plural form of 'baptism'. See 'baptism'.
Baptist; a baptizer, especially John the Baptist.
Baptize; to place into, under, or upon for the sake of identification, consecration, cleansing, or appointed purpose.
Baptized; past tense or past participial form of 'baptize'. See 'baptize'.
Baptizest; older second-person singular form of 'baptize', used with 'thou'. See 'baptize'.
Baptizeth; older third-person singular form of 'baptize'. See 'baptize'.
Baptizing; present participial form of 'baptize', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'baptize'.
A bar is a rod, bolt, crosspiece, enclosure, hindrance, or strong fastening.
Bar-jesus; a false prophet and sorcerer mentioned in Acts.
Bar-Jona; son of Jona or Jonah, a patronymic used of Simon Peter.
Barabbas; the prisoner and insurrectionist released instead of Jesus at the demand of the crowd.
Barachel; the Buzite father of Elihu in the book of Job.
Barachias; a man named in Scripture, husband of Jehosheba, father of Zechariah.
Barak; the judge and military leader who, with Deborah, overthrew Sisera and the Canaanite host.
Barbarian; one outside the Greek-speaking civilized world, regarded as foreign in speech and culture.
Plural form of 'barbarian'. See 'barbarian'.
Barbarous; savage, cruel, or foreign and uncivilized according to the speaker's viewpoint.
Foreigners regarded as outside the speaker's own language or culture; barbarians.
Barbed; past tense or past participial form of 'barb'. See 'barb'.
Barber; one who cuts hair or shaves the beard.
Bore; carried or brought forth.
Bare may also mean uncovered or laid open.
Barefoot; without shoes or sandals.
Barest; older second-person singular form of 'bar', used with 'thou'. See 'bar'.
Barhumite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Barhum in Scripture.
Barhumite.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Tumeim, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Bariah; a descendant of Shemaiah in the genealogies.
Bark; the outer covering of a tree.
Barked; past tense or past participial form of 'bark'. See 'bark'.
Barkos; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Barley is a grain used for bread, food, and harvest provision, commonly reckoned among humbler staples than wheat.
A barn is a storehouse for grain and produce.
Barnabas; a Levite of Cyprus and companion of Paul, noted for encouragement and generous service in the early church.
A barnfloor is the floor of a barn or a place where stored grain is handled.
Plural form of 'barn'. See 'barn'.
A barrel is a vessel or jar for holding meal, liquid, or other provision.
Plural form of 'barrel'. See 'barrel'.
Barren; unfruitful, unproductive, or unable to bear children.
The state, quality, or condition of being barrennes.
Plural form of 'bar'. See 'bar'.
Barsabas; a surname borne by New Testament believers, especially Judas Barsabas and Joseph called Barsabas, surnamed Justus.
Bartholomew; one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.
Bartimaeus; the blind beggar near Jericho whom Jesus healed.
Baruch; a biblical personal name, especially borne by Jeremiah's faithful scribe and companion.
Barzillai; the Gileadite who sustained David in his distress and was honoured by him afterward, and also others bearing the same name.
Base; low, mean, ignoble, or of low degree.
Baser; lower, meaner, or more contemptible.
Plural form of 'base'. See 'base'.
Basest; older second-person singular form of 'base', used with 'thou'. See 'base'.
Bashan; the rich region east of Jordan, famed for cattle, oaks, and strong cities.
In poetry and prophecy it often suggests strength, richness, and height.
Bashan.note: Location: East of the Jordan and the sea of Galilee, in the fertile uplands of what is now southern Syria and northern Jordan.

definitions: Bashan-Havoth-Jair; the district of Jair's towns in Bashan east of Jordan.
Bashan-havoth-jair.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Qamm.

definitions: Bashemath; the name of women in the family narratives of Esau and Ishmael's line.
A basket is a vessel of woven work used for carrying or holding things.
Plural form of 'basket'. See 'basket'.
Basmath; the name of women in Genesis, connected with Esau's wives and the Ishmaelite line.
A bason is a basin or broad vessel used for holding liquid.
Plural form of 'bason'. See 'bason'.
A bastard is one born out of lawful marriage.
Plural form of 'bastard'. See 'bastard'.
A bat is the flying creature so called, counted among unclean creatures in the law.
A bath is a Hebrew measure for liquids; in other contexts it may mean washing or a place for bathing according to usage.
Bath-Rabbim; a gate or gateway mentioned in Scripture.
Bath-rabbim.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Hesban.

definitions: Bath-Sheba; the wife of Uriah who afterward became the wife of David and mother of Solomon.
Bath-Shua; a woman named in Scripture, and in one place the wife of Judah.
Bathe; to wash the body or soak in water.
Bathed; past tense or past participial form of 'bathe'. See 'bathe'.
Plural form of 'bath'. See 'bath'.
Plural form of 'bat'. See 'bat'.
Battered; past tense or past participial form of 'batter'. See 'batter'.
Battering; present participial form of 'batter', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'batter'.
Battle; a fight or engagement in war.
a protective wall or parapet built along the top of a structure.
Plural form of 'battlement'. See 'battlement'.
Plural form of 'battle'. See 'battle'.
Bavai; a ruler of half the district of Keilah in the days of Nehemiah.
Bay; of a reddish-brown color, especially of horses.
Bazlith; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants in the return lists, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Bazluth.
Bazluth; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants in the return lists, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Bazlith.
Bdellium; a precious gum or resin, or possibly a pearl-like substance according to interpretation.
Be; to exist, remain, occur, happen, or stand in a given state.
Beacon; a signal-post, ensign, or conspicuous marker set up to be seen from afar.
Bealiah; a Benjamite warrior who came to David at Ziklag.
Bealoth; a southern town of Judah or Simeon named in the Negev settlements.
Bealoth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Beer Yeroham.

definitions: A beam is a great piece of timber used in building.
In Scripture it may also denote anything long and weighty, and in figure something grossly obvious.
Plural form of 'beam'. See 'beam'.
Plural form of 'bean'. See 'bean'.
A beard is the hair growing upon a man's chin and cheeks.
Plural form of 'beard'. See 'beard'.
Plural form of 'bearer'. See 'bearer'.
Bearest; thou bearest; you bear, carry, endure, or bring forth.
The precise sense must be determined from the passage.
Beareth; older third-person singular form of 'bear'. See 'bear'.
Bearing; present participial form of 'bear', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bear'.
Plural form of 'bear'. See 'bear'.
A beast; an animal, especially one considered as a creature of the field or under man's use.
Beasts; animals, especially those viewed as brute creatures.
Beat; to strike repeatedly, smite down, or drive by blows.
Beaten; struck, hammered out, subdued by blows, or worn smooth by use.
Beatest; older second-person singular form of 'beat', used with 'thou'. See 'beat'.
Beateth; older third-person singular form of 'beat'. See 'beat'.
Beating; present participial form of 'beat', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'beat'.
Plural form of 'beauty'. See 'beauty'.
Fair, comely, or lovely to behold.
It may refer to outward beauty, but sometimes to moral fitness or pleasant excellence.
Beautify; to make fair, adorn, or render beautiful.
Beauty; fairness, comeliness, or that which is pleasing in form and appearance.
Bebai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Came to be; was made or turned into a certain condition.
Becamest; older second-person singular form of 'become', used with 'thou'. See 'become'.
For the reason that; on account of this.
Becher; a son of Benjamin from whom a family line was reckoned.
Bechorath; an ancestor in Saul's Benjamite line.
Beckoned; past tense or past participial form of 'beckon'. See 'beckon'.
Beckoning; present participial form of 'beckon', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'beckon'.
Becometh; older third-person singular form of 'become'. See 'become'.
Bed; a place for lying down, and by extension a riverbed, seabed, or layer in which something rests.
Bedad; the father of Hadad king of Edom.
Bedan; a judge-like deliverer named in Samuel, often identified with Barak or another known figure.
A bedchamber is a private chamber for bed and rest, an inner sleeping room.
Bedeiah; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Beds; places for lying down, and by extension channels, layers, or plots.
A bedstead is the frame or structure of a bed.
A bee is the stinging insect that makes honey.
In Scripture bees can also figure swarming attack or sharp trouble.
Beeliada; one of David's sons born at Jerusalem, also written Eliada.
Beelzebub; a Philistine idol-name later used in the New Testament as a title for the prince of devils.
The past participle of be.
Beer; the wilderness stopping-place where the princes dug the well sung of in Numbers.
Beer.note: Location: In the wilderness east of the Jordan on Israel's approach to Moab, probably between the Arnon and the plateau route.

definitions: Beer-Elim; a well or encampment in the Moabite region named in Isaiah's oracle.
Beer-elim.note: Location: In or near Moab east of the Dead Sea, likely connected with the wilderness route toward Arnon.

definitions: Beer-Lahai-Roi; the well associated with Hagar in the wilderness and later with Isaac's dwelling.
Beer-lahai-roi.note: Location: In the southern wilderness between Kadesh and Bered, probably in the northern Negev or Sinai fringe.

definitions: Beer-Sheba; the southern landmark city of the land, frequently used in the phrase from Dan even to Beer-sheba.
Beer-sheba.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Beer Sheva.

definitions: Beera; a prince of the Reubenites carried away by the king of Assyria.
Beerah; the well or place-name in the wilderness narrative, and also a personal name in genealogical use.
Beeri; the father of Judith wife of Esau, and also the father of the prophet Hosea.
Beeroth; a Gibeonite town remembered in Saul's history and among the Benjamite settlements.
Beeroth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Burj.

definitions: Beerothite; a person from Beeroth, the Benjamite town associated with men like Naharai and the murderers of Ishbosheth.
Beerothite.note: Location: From Beeroth in Benjamin, usually placed north of Jerusalem near the Gibeon area.

definitions: Beerothites; the people or inhabitants of Beeroth in Benjamin.
Beerothites.note: Location: From Beeroth in Benjamin, usually placed north of Jerusalem near the Gibeon area.

definitions: Plural form of 'bee'. See 'bee'.
Beesh-Terah; a Bashan town given to the Levites, usually connected with Ashtaroth or its district.
Beesh-terah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ashtara.

definitions: Beetle; an insect named among creeping things, though the exact species is debated.
Cattle or oxen used as beasts for food or sacrifice.
Befall; to happen to, come upon, or overtake.
Befallen; happened, come to pass, or come upon.
Befalleth; older third-person singular form of 'befall'. See 'befall'.
Befell; the past tense of befall.
In front of; in the presence of; earlier than in time.
Beforehand; beforehand in time, previously, or in anticipation.
Beforetime; in former time, formerly, or in days past.
Beg; to ask earnestly or to seek alms.
The past tense of begin.
Begettest; older second-person singular form of 'beget', used with 'thou'. See 'beget'.
Begetteth; older third-person singular form of 'beget'. See 'beget'.
Beggar; one who asks alms or lives by begging.
In a beggar manner or degree.
Begged; past tense or past participial form of 'beg'. See 'beg'.
Begging; present participial form of 'beg', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'beg'.
Begin; to start, commence, or enter upon a thing.
Beginnest; older second-person singular form of 'begin', used with 'thou'. See 'begin'.
The beginning; the first part, origin, or starting point of a thing.
Plural form of 'beginning'. See 'beginning'.
Begotten; past participial form of 'beget'. See 'beget'.
To deceive, cheat, or lead astray by subtlety.
Beguiling; present participial form of 'beguile', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'beguile'.
Begun; the past participle of begin.
Behalf; side, cause, or interest, especially in the phrase on behalf of.
Behave; to conduct oneself or bear oneself in a certain manner.
Behaved; past tense or past participial form of 'behave'. See 'behave'.
Behaveth; older third-person singular form of 'behave'. See 'behave'.
Behaviour; conduct, manner of life, or outward deportment.
Beheaded; past tense or past participial form of 'behead'. See 'behead'.
Beheld; the past tense and past participle of behold.
Behemoth; the great beast described in Job, often taken as a huge and powerful land animal.
Behind; at the back of, after, later than, or left remaining.
Look; see; attend carefully to what is before you.
Beholdest; thou beholdest; you behold, look upon, or regard with attention.
Beholdeth; older third-person singular form of 'behold'. See 'behold'.
Beholding; present participial form of 'behold', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'behold'.
Behoved; past tense or past participial form of 'behove'. See 'behove'.
Existence, creaturehood, or the state of being.
Bekah; a Hebrew weight and money measure equal to half a shekel.
Bel; a Babylonian false god, akin to Baal.
Bela; the name of more than one person in Scripture, especially a son of Benjamin and also a king of Edom.
Belah; a name borne by early Edomite and Benjamite ancestors; it corresponds to the form Bela in related records.
Belaites; descendants of Bela reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Belch; to pour out or utter forth abundantly, especially words.
worthlessness or wickedness; also a personification of lawless evil.
Belied; past tense or past participial form of 'belie'. See 'belie'.
Belief; faith, persuasion, or the thing believed.
Trusted, received as true, or rested upon with confidence.
Often more than bare assent, carrying the sense of inward reliance.
Plural form of 'believer'. See 'believer'.
Believest; thou believest; you believe, trust, or receive as true.
Often in Scripture it means not mere opinion, but personal reliance.
Believeth; older third-person singular form of 'believe'. See 'believe'.
Believing; present participial form of 'believe', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'believe'.
Bell; a small metal instrument that rings when struck or shaken.
Bellow; to roar or cry out loudly, as an ox or in great anguish.
Plural form of 'bellow'. See 'bellow'.
Plural form of 'bell'. See 'bell'.
The inward part of the body, especially the stomach or womb.
Often used figuratively for appetite or bodily craving.
Belong; to be the property of, to pertain to, or to be properly connected with.
Belonged; past tense or past participial form of 'belong'. See 'belong'.
Belongest; older second-person singular form of 'belong', used with 'thou'. See 'belong'.
Pertains properly to; is due to; is the rightful possession or portion of.
Belonging; present participial form of 'belong', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'belong'.
Dearly loved; much loved and prized.
Belshazzar; the Babylonian king in whose feast the handwriting appeared on the wall.
Belteshazzar; a man named in Scripture.
Bemoan; to lament, bewail, or make sorrowful complaint over.
Bemoaned; past tense or past participial form of 'bemoan'. See 'bemoan'.
Bemoaning; present participial form of 'bemoan', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bemoan'.
Ben; a Levite musician or singer named in connection with Davidic worship.
Ben-Ammi; the son born to Lot's younger daughter, regarded as the forefather of the Ammonites.
Ben-Hadad; the royal name of Syrian kings who appear in conflict with Israel.
Ben-Hail; one of the princes sent by Jehoshaphat to teach in the cities of Judah.
Ben-Hanan; an Edomite chief named among the dukes descended from Esau.
Ben-Oni; the name Rachel gave to Benjamin at his birth, meaning son of my sorrow.
Ben-zoheth; a descendant named in the genealogies of Judah.
Benaiah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by David's valiant captain who later served Solomon.
Plural form of 'bench'. See 'bench'.
Bend; to bow, curve, incline, or direct toward a point.
Bendeth; older third-person singular form of 'bend'. See 'bend'.
Bending; present participial form of 'bend', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bend'.
Bene-Berak; a town in Dan's inheritance on the western lowland side of the territory.
Bene-berak.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Khayriyya.

definitions: Bene-Jaakan; a wilderness-stage locality in Israel's journeyings, connected with the sons of Jaakan.
Below; under; in a lower place or condition than another thing.
Plural form of 'benefactor'. See 'benefactor'.
A favor, good turn, or profitable advantage.
kindness, generosity, or goodwill.
Beninu; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Benjamin; the son of Jacob and the tribe descended from him.
Benjamite; a member of the tribe of Benjamin.
Benjamites; the members of the tribe descended from Benjamin.
Beno; a descendant in the line of Merari named in the genealogies.
Bent; curved, inclined, or firmly set toward a purpose.
Beon; a Transjordan town, probably the same place as Baal-meon in Reubenite territory.
Beor; the father of Balaam.
Bera; king of Sodom in the days of Abram.
Berachah; a Benjamite warrior who came to David.
Berachiah; a biblical personal name, especially borne by the father of Zechariah the prophet.
Beraiah; a Simeonite name in the genealogies.
Berea; the Macedonian city whose Jews were described as more noble for searching the Scriptures.
Bereave; to deprive, especially of children, loved ones, or comfort.
Bereaved; past tense or past participial form of 'bereave'. See 'bereave'.
Bereaveth; older third-person singular form of 'bereave'. See 'bereave'.
Berechiah; a biblical personal name, including the father of Zechariah the prophet.
Bered; a place near the well Beer-lahai-roi in the narrative of Hagar.
Beri; a son of Asher from whom the Beriites were reckoned.
Beriah; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including sons of Ephraim and Asher.
Beriites; descendants of Beriah reckoned among the families of Asher.
Berites; a clan or local people associated with Berothah or Beeroth, mentioned in Old Testament narrative.
Berith; a covenant-related title appearing in the name Baal-berith.
Bernice; the sister of Agrippa before whom Paul made his defence.
Berodach-Baladan; king of Babylon who sent to Hezekiah after his recovery, the same ruler elsewhere written Merodach-Baladan.
Berothah; a northern Syrian locality named in Ezekiel's ideal border between Hamath and Damascus.
Berothai; a Syrian city from which David took much bronze after defeating Hadadezer.
Berothai.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Bourdai.

definitions: Berothite; a person from Beeroth or Beroth, used of inhabitants of that Benjamite town.
Berothite.note: Location: Usually linked with Beeroth in Benjamin north of Jerusalem, near the Gibeon district.

definitions: Plural form of 'berry'. See 'berry'.
Beryl; a precious stone named among the jewels of Scripture.
Besai; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
To entreat earnestly; to beg or plead with urgency.
Beseeching; present participial form of 'beseech', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'beseech'.
Beset; to surround, press upon, or closely hem in.
Beside; at the side of, in comparison with, or apart from.
Besides; in addition to, apart from, or except for.
Besiege; to shut up a city or place with armed forces in order to take it.
Besieged; past tense or past participial form of 'besiege'. See 'besiege'.
Besodeiah; the father of Meshullam in the rebuilding records of Nehemiah.
Besom; a broom used for sweeping.
Besor; the brook near which David and his men halted, leaving the faint with the baggage.
Besor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Nahal HaBesor.

definitions: Most excellent, choice, or chief among several.
Bestead; to put into a certain condition, especially one of distress or difficulty.
Bestir; to stir oneself, rouse oneself, or act with effort.
Bestow; to give, confer, or place upon.
Bestowed; past tense or past participial form of 'bestow'. See 'bestow'.
Betah; a Syrian city taken by David in his wars with Hadadezer; related to the place called Tibhath in the parallel account.
Betah.note: Location: Often identified with between Baalbek and Kadesh.

definitions: Beten; a town in the inheritance of Asher on the northern side of the land.
Beten.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Ibtin.

definitions: Beth-Anath; a town of Naphtali whose Canaanite inhabitants were not fully expelled.
Beth-anath.note: Location: Often identified with Safad el Battikh.

definitions: Beth-Anoth; a hill-country town of Judah listed among the settlements near Hebron.
Beth-anoth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Beit Anun.

definitions: Beth-Arabah; a wilderness-border town between Judah and Benjamin in the Jordan valley region.
Beth-arabah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain el Gharabeh.

definitions: Beth-Aram; a town east of Jordan built up by Gad, also related to the later Livias region.
Beth-aram.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Iktanu.

definitions: Beth-Arbel; a place remembered in Hosea for a massacre so devastating that it became a prophetic example of judgment.
Beth-arbel.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Irbid.

definitions: Beth-Aven; a place near Bethel, sometimes used as a contemptuous prophetic name for idolatrous Bethel.
Beth-aven.note: Location: Near Bethel in the central hill country, commonly associated with the Beitin area.

definitions: Beth-Azmaveth; a Benjamite town whose people returned from exile with Zerubbabel.
Beth-azmaveth.note: Location: Often identified with Hizma, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Beth-Baal-Meon; a Moabite and Reubenite town east of Jordan, known also in related forms such as Baal-Meon.
Beth-Barah; a ford or crossing-place mentioned in Scripture.
Beth-Birei; a southern Simeonite town in the Negev, probably the same place elsewhere called Beth-Lebaoth.
Beth-birei.note: Location: Often identified with Jebel el Biri.

definitions: Beth-Car; the place to which the Philistines were driven after Samuel's intercession and Israel's victory at Mizpeh.
Beth-car.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Beit Ur al Tahta.

definitions: Beth-Dagon; a town named after Dagon in the tribal lists of Judah and Asher.
Beth-dagon.note: Location: The name is used for more than one site, including lowland Judah and the territory of Asher.

definitions: Beth-Diblathaim; a Moabite town named in Jeremiah's prophecy against Moab.
Beth-diblathaim.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Libb.

definitions: Beth-el; a notable place in the land of Israel, remembered from the history of Jacob and later worship in the northern kingdom.
The name means house of God and may carry both historical and religious associations in context.
Beth-el.note: Location: North of Jerusalem in the hill country, often identified with Beitin though the site remains debated.

definitions: Beth-Elite; an inhabitant of Bethel.
Beth-Emek; a town in Asher's inheritance among the northern settlements.
Beth-emek.note: Location: In the northern territory of Asher east of Acco, commonly linked with the Emek area.

definitions: Beth-Ezel; a town named by Micah in his lament over the coming judgment on Judah, probably near the Shephelah settlements.
Beth-ezel.note: Location: Often identified with Deir al Asal al Fauqa.

definitions: Beth-Gader; a Judahite place remembered in the genealogies connected with the family of Penuel.
Beth-gader.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Beth-Gamul; a Moabite town named by Jeremiah among the places under coming judgment.
Beth-Haccerem; a place near Jerusalem used as a signal point in Jeremiah's warning of invasion.
Beth-haccerem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ramat Rahel.

definitions: Beth-Haran; a fortified town built by Gad east of the Jordan, later known in a related form as Beth-haram.
Beth-haran.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Iktanu.

definitions: Beth-Hogla; a border town between Judah and Benjamin near the Jordan valley.
Beth-hogla.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Hajla.

definitions: Beth-Hoglah; the border town near the Jordan valley, also written Beth-hogla.
Beth-hoglah.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Hajla.

definitions: Beth-Horon; the pass and adjoining towns controlling an important ascent from the coastal plain into the hill country.
Beth-horon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Beit Ur al Tahta.

definitions: Beth-Jeshimoth; a Moabite border town east of the Jordan near the north end of the Dead Sea.
Beth-jeshimoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Azeimeh.

definitions: Beth-Jesimoth; the eastern border town near the Dead Sea, also written Beth-jeshimoth.
Beth-jesimoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Azeimeh.

definitions: Beth-Lebaoth; a southern Simeonite town, probably identical with Beth-Birei in a parallel list.
Beth-lebaoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Jebel el Biri.

definitions: Beth-Lehem; Bethlehem, especially the city of David and the birthplace of Jesus Christ.
Beth-Lehem-Judah; Bethlehem in Judah, distinguished especially as the city of David and birthplace of Christ.
Beth-Lehemite; an inhabitant of Bethlehem.
Beth-Maachah; the district or town-name preserved in Abel-Beth-Maachah on Israel's northern border.
Beth-maachah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Beth-Marcaboth; a southern Simeonite town whose name suggests association with chariots or riding.
Beth-marcaboth.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tatrit.

definitions: Beth-Meon; a Moabite and Reubenite town east of Jordan, related to Baal-Meon and Beon.
Beth-Nimrah; a fertile Transjordan town of Gad near the Jordan valley and its waters.
Beth-nimrah.note: Location: Often identified with Tall Bleibel.

definitions: Beth-Palet; a southern Judean town in the Negev, later resettled by the returned exiles.
Beth-palet.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Saqati.

definitions: Beth-Pazzez; a town in Issachar named among that tribe's inheritances.
Beth-Peor; a place in the Moabite region east of Jordan associated with Peor.
Beth-peor.note: Location: East of the Jordan in the Moabite highlands opposite Jericho, near Peor and Beth-jeshimoth.

definitions: Beth-Phelet; the southern town in Judah's territory, also written Beth-palet.
Beth-phelet.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Saqati.

definitions: Beth-Rapha; a descendant named in the genealogies of Judah.
Beth-Rehob; a northern district on the approach toward Hamath, associated with Aramean influence.
Beth-rehob.note: Location: In the far north near the entrance to the Beqaa valley between Lebanon and Hermon, on the approach toward Hamath.

definitions: Beth-Shan; the city where the Philistines fastened the bodies of Saul and his sons after Gilboa.
Beth-shan.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shean.

definitions: Beth-Shean; the city remembered especially in the account of Saul's death, also written Beth-Shan.
Beth-shean.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shean.

definitions: Beth-Shemesh; the city to which the ark returned from the Philistines and where judgment fell for irreverence.
Beth-shemesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain Shams.

definitions: Beth-Shemite; an inhabitant of Beth-shemesh.
Beth-shemite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shemesh.

definitions: Beth-Shittah; a place on the line of Midian's flight after Gideon's victory.
Beth-shittah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Salwim.

definitions: Beth-Tappuah; a hill-country town of Judah near Hebron and the central ridge settlements.
Beth-Zur; a fortified Judean town south of Jerusalem, important in later wars and defenses.
Beth-zur.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet et Tubeiqa.

definitions: Bethabara; a ford or crossing-place mentioned in Scripture.
Bethany; the village near Jerusalem associated with Mary, Martha, Lazarus, and the Mount of Olives.
Bethany.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Eizariya.

definitions: Bether; the hill-country or rugged district named poetically in the Song of Solomon.
Bether.note: Location: Probably in the hill country of Judah or Benjamin near Jerusalem, though the exact identification is uncertain.

definitions: Bethesda; a pool or reservoir mentioned in Scripture.
Bethesda.note: Location: Usually identified with Pool of Bethesda.

definitions: Bethink; to call to mind, consider, or reflect.
Bethlehem; the town of Judah known as the city of David and the birthplace of the Lord Jesus in the flesh.
The name is sometimes distinguished from Bethlehem in Zebulun.
Bethlehem.note: Location: In the hill country south of Jerusalem, commonly identified with Bethlehem in Judah.

definitions: Bethphage; the village near the Mount of Olives from which the colt was brought for Christ's entry into Jerusalem.
Bethphage.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tur.

definitions: Bethsaida; a town near the Sea of Galilee, associated with several of the apostles and works of Jesus.
The name is tied to gospel events in the northern ministry.
Bethsaida.note: Location: Near the northern end of the sea of Galilee; the exact site is debated, often Al-Araj or et-Tell.

definitions: Bethuel; the father of Rebekah and Laban in the family of Nahor.
Bethul; a southern town in the Simeonite and Judean Negev lists, perhaps related to Bethuel in a parallel form.
Bethul.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet ar Ras.

definitions: Early; in good time; seasonably.
Betonim; a town east of the Jordan in the territory of Gad.
Betonim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Batneh.

definitions: Betray; to deliver up treacherously, or to disclose by one’s actions or words.
Betrayed; past tense or past participial form of 'betray'. See 'betray'.
Plural form of 'betrayer'. See 'betrayer'.
Betrayest; older second-person singular form of 'betray', used with 'thou'. See 'betray'.
Betrayeth; older third-person singular form of 'betray'. See 'betray'.
Betroth; to pledge in marriage or espouse.
Betrothed; past tense or past participial form of 'betroth'. See 'betroth'.
Better; more good, more fitting, or more excellent.
Bettered; past tense or past participial form of 'better'. See 'better'.
In the space or relation separating two or more.
Betwixt; between, in the space separating two things or persons.
Beulah; a symbolic title meaning married, used in Isaiah for the restored and favoured land.
Bewail; to lament openly or mourn deeply over.
Bewailed; past tense or past participial form of 'bewail'. See 'bewail'.
Bewaileth; older third-person singular form of 'bewail'. See 'bewail'.
To beware is to be on guard, take heed, or be watchful against danger or error.
Bewitched; past tense or past participial form of 'bewitch'. See 'bewitch'.
To disclose, reveal, or betray by outward sign or speech.
Gives thee away; reveals what thou art by speech or manner.
Beyond; on the farther side of, past, or outside the limit of.
Bezai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Bezaleel; the master craftsman filled with the Spirit to make the tabernacle, and also a name among the post-exilic community.
Bezek; a place connected with early conflicts in Judges, especially the defeat of Adoni-bezek.
Bezek.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Ibziq.

definitions: Bezer; a son of Zuph in a genealogy of Samuel's line, and also a tribal and place-name elsewhere in Scripture.
Bichri; the father or ancestor associated with Sheba the son of Bichri.
Bid; to command, invite, ask, or wish.
Bidden; the past participle of bid.
Biddeth; means commands, invites, asks, or wishes.
Bidding; commanding, inviting, asking, or the charge or command given.
Bidkar; Jehu’s captain, mentioned in the judgment on Joram.
Bier; a frame or couch on which a dead body is carried.
Bigtha; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in the book of Esther.
Bigthan; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who conspired against the king in Esther.
Bigthana; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who conspired against the king, also written Bigthan.
Bigvai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Bildad; the Shuhite friend of Job who spoke in the debates of the book of Job.
Bileam; a town in Manasseh's northern territory, known in related form as Ibleam in other passages.
Bileam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Bilgah; the name of a priestly course and of men associated with that priestly house.
Bilgai; a priest named among the covenant signers after the exile, related in name to the priestly course of Bilgah.
Bilhah; Rachel's handmaid and the mother of Dan and Naphtali.
Bilhan; a biblical personal name borne by men in Scripture, including descendants in the lines of Benjamin and Seir.
Bill; a written account, legal document, or formal writing, and in some passages a hooked cutting instrument.
Plural form of 'billow'. See 'billow'.
Bilshan; a man named among those who returned from exile with Zerubbabel.
Bimhal; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Bindeth; older third-person singular form of 'bind'. See 'bind'.
Binding; present participial form of 'bind', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bind'.
Binea; a Benjamite descendant in the line associated with Saul.
Binnui; chiefly a post-exilic Levite and builder in the days of Ezra and Nehemiah; also a recurring name in those records.
A fowl; a winged creature.
Plural form of 'bird'. See 'bird'.
Birsha; king of Gomorrah in the days of Abram.
Birth; the act of being born, and also lineage or natural descent.
Birthday; the anniversary of one’s birth.
Birthright; the privilege, rank, or inheritance belonging to the firstborn.
Birzavith; a descendant in the line of Asher.
Bishlam; one of those who wrote against the rebuilding of Jerusalem in the Persian period.
A bishop; an overseer in the church.
The word emphasizes oversight and care rather than worldly rank.
Bishoprick; an office of oversight, charge, or bishop’s place.
Bishops; overseers in the church.
Bit; the piece placed in a horse’s mouth to guide or restrain it.
Bite; to seize or wound with the teeth, or the wound so made.
Biteth; means bites, seizes, or wounds with the teeth.
Bithiah; the daughter of Pharaoh who became connected with the house of Judah in the genealogies.
Bithron; a rugged district or ravine-country east of Jordan, mentioned in the account of Abner.
Bithynia; a region of northwestern Asia Minor on the Black Sea, named in Acts and among the destinations of 1 Peter.
Plural form of 'bit'. See 'bit'.
Bitten; the past participle of bite.
Bitter; sharp to the taste, grievous to the feelings, or severe in spirit.
In a bitter manner or degree.
Bittern; a marsh bird, often taken as a wading bird haunting desolate places.
The state, quality, or condition of being bitternes.
Bizjothjah; a southern town in the territory of Judah near the Negev borderlands.
Biztha; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Black; dark in color, gloomy, or marked by mourning.
Blacker; more black or more darkened.
Blackish; somewhat black or dark-colored.
The state, quality, or condition of being blacknes.
Blade; the tender shoot of grain, or the cutting part of an instrument.
Plural form of 'blain'. See 'blain'.
Blame; fault, reproach, or just ground of accusation.
Blamed; past tense or past participial form of 'blame'. See 'blame'.
Without just cause of accusation; free from reproach in conduct.
To speak irreverently against God or sacred things; to rail profanely.
Blasphemer; one who speaks impiously or reproachfully against God, sacred things, or worthy persons.
Plural form of 'blasphemer'. See 'blasphemer'.
Blasphemest; older second-person singular form of 'blaspheme', used with 'thou'. See 'blaspheme'.
Blasphemeth; older third-person singular form of 'blaspheme'. See 'blaspheme'.
Plural form of 'blasphemy'. See 'blasphemy'.
Blaspheming; present participial form of 'blaspheme', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'blaspheme'.
Blasphemous; profanely dishonoring God or sacred things.
In a blasphemous manner or degree.
Blast; a blowing wind, a scorching or destructive breath, or a sudden stroke.
Blasted; struck by a destructive blast, scorched, or withered.
Blasting; the act or effect of withering, scorching, or striking with a destructive blast.
Blastus; the chamberlain of Herod whose mediation was sought by the people of Tyre and Sidon.
Blaze; a bright flame or burning light.
Bleating; present participial form of 'bleat', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bleat'.
Plural form of 'bleating'. See 'bleating'.
Blemish; a spot, defect, or disqualifying flaw, especially in sacrificial matters.
Plural form of 'blemish'. See 'blemish'.
To speak good upon, invoke favor upon, or pronounce well of.
When used of God, often to bestow good, favor, fruitfulness, or peace.
The state of being blessed; true happiness under divine favor.
Blessest; thou blessest; you bless, speak good upon, or invoke favor upon.
A good bestowed, spoken, or invoked; favor resulting in benefit, peace, or increase.
It may also mean the act of pronouncing blessing.
Blew; the past tense of blow.
Blind; without sight, and also used of blind persons as a class.
Blinded; made blind, deprived of discernment, or darkened in understanding.
Blindeth; means blinds or darkens understanding.
Blindfolded; past tense or past participial form of 'blindfold'. See 'blindfold'.
The state, quality, or condition of being blindnes.
The blood of the body as the seat and sign of life.
In Scripture often bound up with life given up, guilt, kinship, cleansing, or sacrificial atonement.
The guilt of bloodshed, especially of blood wrongfully shed.
Bloodthirsty; eager for bloodshed or cruelly violent.
Bloody; stained with blood, characterized by bloodshed, or guilty of it.
Bloomed; past tense or past participial form of 'bloom'. See 'bloom'.
Blossom; the flower or bloom of a plant.
Blossomed; past tense or past participial form of 'blossom'. See 'blossom'.
Plural form of 'blossom'. See 'blossom'.
Blot; a stain, reproach, or thing that effaces or mars.
Blotted; past tense or past participial form of 'blot'. See 'blot'.
Blotteth; older third-person singular form of 'blot'. See 'blot'.
Blotting; present participial form of 'blot', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'blot'.
Blow; to breathe, sound, or move by a current of air, and also to strike.
Bloweth; means blows, breathes, sounds, moves by wind, or strikes.
Blowing; breathing, sounding, driving by wind, or striking in ongoing action.
Blown; the past participle of blow.
Blue; of the blue or violet hue used especially in sacred fabrics and adornments.
The state, quality, or condition of being bluenes.
Blunt; dull at the edge or point, or lacking sharpness and keenness.
Blush; to redden with shame, modesty, or confusion.
Boanerges; a surname meaning sons of thunder, given by Jesus to James and John.
Boar; the wild hog.
Board; a plank or flat piece of timber, and also to cover, furnish, or embark upon.
Boards; planks or flat timbers, and in some contexts things boarded or furnished with boards.
To glory, rejoice, or make one's boast.
The word may describe proud self-exaltation or right glorying in God.
Boasted; past tense or past participial form of 'boast'. See 'boast'.
Plural form of 'boaster'. See 'boaster'.
Boastest; older second-person singular form of 'boast', used with 'thou'. See 'boast'.
Boasteth; older third-person singular form of 'boast'. See 'boast'.
Plural form of 'boasting'. See 'boasting'.
Boat; a small vessel for use on water.
Plural form of 'boat'. See 'boat'.
Boaz; a man named in Scripture, son of Salmon, husband of Ruth, father of Obed.
Bocheru; a descendant in the Benjamite line of Saul.
Bochim; the place where Israel wept after the angel's rebuke in Judges.
Bochim.note: Location: Near Bethel in the central hill country, though the exact place of weeping in Judges is uncertain.

definitions: Bodies; physical frames, corpses, or collective groups viewed as one whole.
Bodily; belonging to the body, physical, or corporeal.
Body; the whole bodily frame, a corpse, or by extension a collective company.
Bohan; the son of Reuben, remembered in the landmark called the stone of Bohan.
Boil; to seethe with heat, or to cook in boiling liquid.
Boiled; past tense or past participial form of 'boil'. See 'boil'.
Boiling; present participial form of 'boil', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'boil'.
Plural form of 'boil'. See 'boil'.
Boisterous; violent, stormy, or rough in motion or sound.
Bold; daring, confident, or fearless.
In a bold manner or degree.
Courage, confidence, and freedom of speech or action.
Bolled; past tense or past participial form of 'boll'. See 'boll'.
Bolster; a pillow or cushion for the head.
A bolt is the fastening or bar that secures a gate or door.
Bolted; past tense or past participial form of 'bolt'. See 'bolt'.
Bond; a band or tie, an obligation, or a state of bondage.
Servitude, slavery, or a state of binding subjection.
Bondmaid; a female servant in bondage.
Plural form of 'bondmaid'. See 'bondmaid'.
A bondman; a man in bondage or servile condition.
The word may denote a slave, a man under legal service, or one figuratively under spiritual bondage.
Bondmen; men in bondage or servile condition.
Bonds; ties, obligations, fetters, or states of bondage.
Bondservant; one in bondage or bound service under a master.
Bondservice; service performed in bondage or as a bondman.
A bondwoman; a woman in bondage or servile condition.
The word may be used literally or figuratively of bondage.
Bondwomen; women in bondage or servile condition.
Bone is the hard substance of the body's frame; bones are the solid structure within the flesh.
In Scripture it may also signify strength, nearness of kin, or the innermost frame of a person.
Bones; the hard parts of the body forming the frame.
Plural form of 'bonnet'. See 'bonnet'.
A book; a written roll, record, or bound writing.
Plural form of 'book'. See 'book'.
A booth is a temporary shelter or hut of boughs, branches, or light materials.
It may be used for lodging, shade, or festal observance.
Plural form of 'booth'. See 'booth'.
Booties; plunder.
Booty; spoil taken in war or by plunder.
Booz; a man named in Scripture, son of Salmon, husband of Ruth, father of Obed.
A border; the edge, boundary, or limit of a land, territory, garment, or thing.
In place language it commonly means the bounds of a country or inheritance.
Borders; edges, bounds, or outward limits.
In Scripture it often means the boundaries of a land, tribe, or possession.
Bore; the past tense of bear, or of bore in the sense of piercing or drilling.
Bored; pierced through, drilled, or made hollow.
Born; brought forth by birth, begotten, or produced.
Carried; endured; supported.
Borrow; to receive something from another to use and then return.
Borrowed; past tense or past participial form of 'borrow'. See 'borrow'.
Borrower; one who takes something on loan.
Borroweth; older third-person singular form of 'borrow'. See 'borrow'.
Boscath; a town of Judah remembered as the home of Jedidah, the mother of king Josiah.
Boscath.note: Location: Often identified with Ed Dawaimeh.

definitions: The breast or chest.
Figuratively, the place of closeness, affection, or inward keeping.
Bosor; the Greek form used for Beor, father of Balaam.
Plural form of 'boss'. See 'boss'.
Botch; an ulcerous swelling or sore.
The two together; each of the two named or implied.
Bottle; a skin or vessel for holding liquids.
Plural form of 'bottle'. See 'bottle'.
Bottom; the lowest part, depth, or foundation.
Bottomless; having no seen bottom, immeasurably deep, or figuratively unfathomable.
Plural form of 'bottom'. See 'bottom'.
Bough; a large branch of a tree.
Plural form of 'bough'. See 'bough'.
Bought; the past tense and past participle of buy.
Bound; tied, fastened, obliged, or limited.
Bounds; limits, borders, or restraints.
Bountiful; liberal, plentiful, or generous in giving.
In a bountiful manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being bountifulnes.
Bounty; liberality, goodness, or abundant provision.
Bow; a weapon for shooting arrows, a curved shape, or the act of bending low.
Bent low, stooped, or submitted.
The inward parts, especially as the imagined seat of tender feeling, compassion, or deep affection.
In some places, the literal inner organs.
Boweth; means bows, bends, stoops, or inclines.
Bowing; bending, stooping, or inclining in reverence or submission.
A bowl is a round vessel for holding liquid, food, or offerings.
Plural form of 'bowl'. See 'bowl'.
Bowmen are men armed with bows, archers.
Bows; weapons for shooting arrows, curves, or acts of bending.
Bowshot is the distance an arrow may be shot from a bow.
A box is a small chest, case, or vessel for holding something precious or useful.
Boy; a male child or youth.
Plural form of 'boy'. See 'boy'.
Bozez; a cliff or steep height mentioned in Scripture.
Bozkath; the name Boscath in another biblical spelling, the Judean town connected with Josiah's family.
Bozkath.note: Location: Often identified with Ed Dawaimeh.

definitions: Bozrah; a chief city of Edom, repeatedly named in prophecies of judgment against that nation.
Bracelet; an ornament worn upon the wrist or arm.
Plural form of 'bracelet'. See 'bracelet'.
A brake; a thicket, rough overgrown place, or dense growth.
Brakest; older second-person singular form of 'brake', used with 'thou'. See 'brake'.
Bramble; a thorny shrub or bush.
Plural form of 'bramble'. See 'bramble'.
A branch is a bough or shoot growing from a tree or vine.
In Scripture it may also signify offspring, extension, or a figure of promised growth.
Branches; shoots or boughs growing from a tree or vine.
Brand; a burning piece of wood, or a thing snatched from the fire.
Brandish; to wave or shake a weapon in a threatening way.
Plural form of 'brand'. See 'brand'.
Brasen; made of brass or bronze.
Brass; bronze or a similar metal, especially in older translation usage.
Bravery; splendid show, finery, outward magnificence, and in some uses courage.
Brawler; one given to wrangling, noisy contention, or quarrels.
Plural form of 'brawler'. See 'brawler'.
Brawling; present participial form of 'brawl', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'brawl'.
Bray; to pound or crush in a mortar, or to utter the cry of an ass.
Brayed; pounded, crushed, or uttered the cry of an ass.
Breach; a breaking, gap, rupture, or violation.
Plural form of 'breach'. See 'breach'.
Bread; food, especially the ordinary provision by which life is sustained.
The measure from side to side; width.
Breaker; one who breaks through, or that which bursts forth.
Breakest; older second-person singular form of 'break', used with 'thou'. See 'break'.
Breaketh; older third-person singular form of 'break'. See 'break'.
Breaking; present participial form of 'break', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'break'.
Plural form of 'breaking'. See 'breaking'.
The breast is the front of the body between neck and belly, especially the chest or bosom.
In Scripture it may also suggest affection, inward feeling, or the place nearest the heart.
A piece of armour covering the breast.
Also a sacred ornament worn on the high priest's breast.
Plural form of 'breast'. See 'breast'.
The breathing of life; the air drawn from the mouth or nostrils.
Often used for life itself, or that which is frail and quickly vanishing.
Breathe; to draw breath, utter, or send forth as from the breath.
Breathed; past tense or past participial form of 'breathe'. See 'breathe'.
Breatheth; older third-person singular form of 'breath'. See 'breath'.
Breathing; present participial form of 'breathe', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'breathe'.
Bred; past tense or past participial form of 'breed'. See 'breed'.
Plural form of 'breech'. See 'breech'.
Breed; a kind, stock, or race, and also to produce offspring, rear, or bring up.
Breeding; begetting, raising, or producing offspring, and also upbringing or manner.
Brothers; kinsmen; fellow members of a people or spiritual family.
Bribe; a gift given to pervert judgment or influence conduct unlawfully.
Bribery; the taking or giving of bribes.
Plural form of 'bribe'. See 'bribe'.
Brick; a block of dried or baked clay used in building.
Brickkiln; a place where bricks are baked.
Plural form of 'brick'. See 'brick'.
Bride; a woman newly married or about to be married.
Bridechamber; the bridal room, or by extension the wedding company and festivities.
Bridegroom; a man newly married or about to be married.
To restrain, govern, or hold in check.
Literally, the headgear used to control a horse.
Plural form of 'bridle'. See 'bridle'.
Bridleth; older third-person singular form of 'bridle'. See 'bridle'.
In a brief manner or degree.
Brier; a thorny plant or prickly shrub.
Plural form of 'brier'. See 'brier'.
Brigandine; a coat of armor.
Plural form of 'brigandine'. See 'brigandine'.
Bright; shining, radiant, or clear.
The state, quality, or condition of being brightnes.
Brim; the upper edge or lip of a vessel.
Brimstone; sulfur, associated in Scripture with burning judgment.
Plural form of 'bringer'. See 'bringer'.
Bringest; thou bringest; you bring.
Used in address to one person, often meaning you carry, cause to come, or bring to pass.
Bringeth; older third-person singular form of 'bring'. See 'bring'.
Bringing; present participial form of 'bring', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bring'.
Brink; the edge or border of something steep or dangerous.
Broad; wide, spacious, or extended in breadth.
Broader; more broad or more wide.
Broided; past tense or past participial form of 'broid'. See 'broid'.
Broidered; past tense or past participial form of 'broider'. See 'broider'.
Broiled; past tense or past participial form of 'broil'. See 'broil'.
Broken; shattered, crushed, interrupted, or humbled.
Brokenfooted; past tense or past participial form of 'brokenfoot'. See 'brokenfoot'.
Brokenhanded; past tense or past participial form of 'brokenhand'. See 'brokenhand'.
Brokenhearted; past tense or past participial form of 'brokenheart'. See 'brokenheart'.
Brood; offspring, hatch, or by extension a kind or generation.
A brook; a small stream, often one fed by seasonal rain or flowing through a narrow course.
In Scripture it may denote a lesser stream than a river, sometimes passing through a ravine or valley.
Brooks; small streams or watercourses, often less than rivers.
Broth is liquid in which flesh or other food has been boiled.
Brother; a male sibling, kinsman, fellow Israelite, or close covenant companion.
the state of being brethren, or a fellowship bound together like brothers.
Brotherly; like that which becomes brethren, affectionate, kind, and united.
Brothers; brothers by birth, kinship, nation, or close covenant relation.
The past tense or participle of bring.
Broughtest; thou broughtest; you brought or caused to come.
Brow; the forehead, or the edge or summit of a hill.
Brown; dark-colored or swarthy.
Bruise; to crush, break, or wound by pressure or blows.
Bruised; past tense or past participial form of 'bruise'. See 'bruise'.
Plural form of 'bruise'. See 'bruise'.
Bruising; present participial form of 'bruise', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bruise'.
A report, rumor, or noise spread abroad.
Brute; a beast, or one acting with animal-like ignorance and unreason.
Brutish; beast-like, dull, or lacking spiritual understanding.
Bucket; a vessel for drawing or carrying water.
Plural form of 'bucket'. See 'bucket'.
A buckler is a shield, often a smaller one used for defense in battle.
Plural form of 'buckler'. See 'buckler'.
Bud; the first swelling or shoot from which blossom or leaf appears.
Budded; past tense or past participial form of 'bud'. See 'bud'.
Plural form of 'bud'. See 'bud'.
Buffet; to strike with the fist or with repeated blows.
Buffeted; past tense or past participial form of 'buffet'. See 'buffet'.
Builded; past tense or past participial form of 'build'. See 'build'.
Buildedst; means thou didst build.
Builder; one who builds or sets up a structure.
Plural form of 'builder'. See 'builder'.
Buildest; thou buildest; you build, establish, or raise up.
Buildeth; older third-person singular form of 'build'. See 'build'.
Building; present participial form of 'build', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'build'.
Plural form of 'building'. See 'building'.
Constructed, established, or set up.
Bukki; a biblical personal name, especially borne in the priestly line of Aaron.
Bukkiah; a Levitical musician and head of one of the temple courses.
Bul; the eighth month of the Hebrew civil year, named in the time of Solomon.
Bull; the male of the ox-kind.
A bullock; a young bull, often used for labor or sacrifice.
Bullocks; young bulls, especially those used for labor or sacrifice.
Plural form of 'bull'. See 'bull'.
Bulrush; a reed-like water plant, often associated with marshy places.
Plural form of 'bulrush'. See 'bulrush'.
Plural form of 'bulwark'. See 'bulwark'.
Bunah; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Bunch; a cluster, knot, or gathered group.
Plural form of 'bunch'. See 'bunch'.
Bundle; a quantity bound together.
Plural form of 'bundle'. See 'bundle'.
Bunni; chiefly a Levitical and post-exilic name in the records of return, worship, and restoration.
A load carried or laid upon one.
Also a weighty message or heavy matter pressing on the spirit.
Burdened; past tense or past participial form of 'burden'. See 'burden'.
Burdensome; heavy, grievous, or hard to bear.
the act of burying, or interment.
Placed in burial; interred.
Plural form of 'burier'. See 'burier'.
Burned; consumed or affected by fire.
Burneth; older third-person singular form of 'burn'. See 'burn'.
Ablaze, hot, or glowing with fire or heat.
Also fervent, ardent, or inwardly stirred with strong feeling.
Plural form of 'burning'. See 'burning'.
Burnished; past tense or past participial form of 'burnish'. See 'burnish'.
Burned by fire; consumed or prepared by burning, as context may require.
Burst; to break open, break forth, or break apart with force.
Bursting; present participial form of 'burst', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'burst'.
Bury; to place the dead in the grave, or to cover out of sight.
Burying; present participial form of 'bury', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'bury'.
Buryingplace; a place appointed for burial.
Bush; a low woody shrub.
Bushel; a dry measure, or the vessel used for such a measure.
Plural form of 'bush'. See 'bush'.
Bushy; thick like a bush or full of branches or hair.
Busied; occupied, engaged, or meddling.
A matter in hand; an affair, duty, or piece of work to be done.
Not limited to trade or commerce.
Busy; occupied, active, or meddling.
Plural form of 'busybody'. See 'busybody'.
Busybody; one who meddles in matters not his own.
But; except, only, nevertheless, or however.
Butler; one who serves drink, especially the cupbearer of a king or great house.
Plural form of 'butler'. See 'butler'.
the office of a butler or cupbearer.
Butter; curdled or churned milk-fat, often including what would now be called thick cream.
Plural form of 'buttock'. See 'buttock'.
Buy; to purchase or obtain by paying a price.
Buyer; one who buys or purchases.
Buyest; older second-person singular form of 'buy', used with 'thou'. See 'buy'.
Buyeth; older third-person singular form of 'buy'. See 'buy'.
Buz; a son of Nahor and ancestor of a people or district bearing his name.
Buzi; the father of Ezekiel the prophet.
Buzite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Buz in Scripture.
By; near, through, by means of, or according to.
Immediately, or very soon afterward.
by and by.note: In KJV usage this usually means promptly, not eventually.

definitions: Plural form of 'byway'. See 'byway'.
A proverb-like saying about someone; a name used in reproach or mockery.
Cab; a small Hebrew dry measure.
Cabbon; a town in the lowland of Judah named among the Shephelah settlements.
Cabbon.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tatrit.

definitions: Plural form of 'cabin'. See 'cabin'.
Cabul; a town in Asher and also the district-name used by Hiram for the towns Solomon gave him in Galilee.
Caesar; the imperial title of the Roman emperor.
Caesarea; the major Roman port and administrative city of Judaea, important in Acts and Paul's imprisonment.
It is prominent in Acts in connection with governors, trials, and journeys by sea.
Caesarea.note: Location: On the Mediterranean coast of Judaea, commonly identified with Caesarea Maritima.

definitions: an enclosure for birds or animals; also to confine in such an enclosure.
Caiaphas; the high priest who played a leading part in the proceedings against Jesus.
Cain; a man named in Scripture, son of Adam, father of Enoch.
Cainan; a patriarchal name in the genealogies, represented in Greek usage alongside Kenan.
A cake is a baked portion of bread or meal, often made in a round or flat form.
Plural form of 'cake'. See 'cake'.
Calah; one of the great Assyrian royal cities named in the early kingdom of Nimrod and Asshur.
Plural form of 'calamity'. See 'calamity'.
great distress, disaster, or affliction.
a sweet cane or aromatic reed used in anointing oil.
Calcol; one of the wise men whose wisdom was surpassed by Solomon.
Caldron; a large kettle or pot for boiling.
Plural form of 'caldron'. See 'caldron'.
Caleb; especially the faithful spy who wholly followed the LORD and received Hebron for an inheritance.
Caleb-Ephratah; the locality named in the Judahite genealogy, closely linked with the Bethlehem-Ephrathah region.
A calf is the young of the ox or cow.
In Scripture it may signify ordinary livestock, sacrificial use, or idolatrous misuse as in the golden calf.
Plural form of 'calker'. See 'calker'.
Called; named, summoned, invited, or appointed.
Calledst; means thou didst call.
Callest; older second-person singular form of 'call', used with 'thou'. See 'call'.
Calleth; older third-person singular form of 'call'. See 'call'.
A summons, vocation, or state to which one is called.
Often the divine call or place assigned by God.
Calm; stillness, quiet, or freedom from disturbance.
Calneh; one of the notable ancient cities named with Babel and Assyria in early imperial history and later prophecy.
Calneh.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Tayinat.

definitions: Calno; the name Calneh in another biblical spelling, cited in Assyrian-era prophecy as an example of conquered greatness.
Calno.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Tayinat.

definitions: Calvary; the hill or place of crucifixion where Jesus was put to death.
Calvary.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Church of the Holy Sepulchre, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Calve; to bring forth a calf.
Calved; past tense or past participial form of 'calve'. See 'calve'.
Plural form of 'calf'. See 'calf'.
Calveth; older third-person singular form of 'calve'. See 'calve'.
Came; arrived; approached; happened.
A camel is the large beast of burden fitted for desert travel and carrying loads.
Plural form of 'camel'. See 'camel'.
Camest; thou camest; you came.
Camon; the place east of the Jordan where Jair the judge was buried.
A camp; the place where an army or company lodges.
Camped; past tense or past participial form of 'camp'. See 'camp'.
Camphire; the aromatic plant or henna-cluster spoken of in the Song of Solomon.
Plural form of 'camp'. See 'camp'.
To be able; to have power or ability.
Cana; the Galilean town where Jesus performed his first sign by turning water into wine.
The name marks a locality within the Lord’s early ministry in Galilee.
Cana.note: Location: In Galilee, often identified with Khirbet Qana or Kana el-Jalil.

definitions: Canaan; the land and people so called.
Canaanite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Canaan in Scripture.
Canaanites; the peoples of Canaan, often especially the older inhabitants opposed to Israel's possession of the land.
a woman of Canaan.
Canaanitish; belonging to Canaan or to the Canaanites.
Candace; the dynastic title of the queen of the Ethiopians in Acts.
A candle is a light or lamp, especially as giving illumination in a house or chamber.
In Scripture the word often answers to a lamp rather than specifically to a modern wax candle.
Plural form of 'candle'. See 'candle'.
A candlestick; a stand or support for lamps or candles.
Plural form of 'candlestick'. See 'candlestick'.
Cane; a reed or stalk, especially a sweet or aromatic cane.
Canker; corrosion, decay, or consuming rust.
Cankered; past tense or past participial form of 'canker'. See 'canker'.
Cankerworm; a devouring insect, especially one that consumes vegetation.
Canneh; a trading city or district named by Ezekiel among the merchants associated with Tyre.
Canneh.note: Location: Often identified with Kullan Köy.

definitions: Cannot; is not able; cannot do or be.
Cannot endure; cannot tolerate.
Canst; means thou art able.
Capernaum; the Galilean town that became a center of Jesus' public ministry.
It appears in the Gospels as a principal place of His teaching and mighty works.
Capernaum.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Hum.

definitions: Caphthorim; another spelling of Caphtorim, the people or stock said to have come from Caphtor.
Caphtor; the island or maritime land from which the Philistines were said to have come, commonly linked with Crete.
Caphtor.note: Location: Commonly located in or identified with Crete.

definitions: Caphtorim; a people descended from Mizraim, associated with Caphtor and linked with the Philistine origin traditions.
Caphtorims; a people descended from Mizraim and associated with Caphtor in Philistine origin traditions.
Caphtorims.note: Location: Commonly located in or identified with Crete.

definitions: Cappadocia; an inland region of Asia Minor named among the lands from which Jews were present at Pentecost.
A chief leader, commander, or head over a company or body of men.
In Scripture it may refer to a military officer or another principal leader.
Captive; taken prisoner or carried away.
As a noun, one who is taken captive.
Plural form of 'captive'. See 'captive'.
A state of being taken captive or carried away under bondage.
Carbuncle; a precious stone of bright fiery appearance.
Plural form of 'carbuncle'. See 'carbuncle'.
Carcas; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Carcase; a dead body, especially the corpse of a man or beast.
Plural form of 'carcase'. See 'carcase'.
Carchemish; the great Euphrates city associated with decisive imperial conflict in the prophets.
Careah; the father of Johanan and Jonathan, associated with the remnant in Judah after Jerusalem fell.
Cared; past tense or past participial form of 'care'. See 'care'.
Full of care; anxious; troubled with concern.
Not chiefly cautious in the modern favorable sense.
In a careful manner or degree.
Earnest care, diligence, and sober concern.
Careless; at ease, unworried, or lacking due concern.
In a careless manner or degree.
Plural form of 'care'. See 'care'.
Carest; older second-person singular form of 'care', used with 'thou'. See 'care'.
Careth; older third-person singular form of 'care'. See 'care'.
Caring; present participial form of 'care', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'care'.
Carmel; the well-known mountain or highland region so called, and in some passages a town bearing the same name.
It is especially associated with fertility and with Elijah’s confrontation with the prophets of Baal.
Carmel.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Mount Carmel.

definitions: Carmelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Carmel in Scripture.
Carmelite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Kirmil.

definitions: Carmelitess; a woman from Carmel, especially Abigail before she became David's wife.
Carmelitess.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Kirmil.

definitions: Carmi; a son of Reuben and also the name of an ancestor in Judah's line connected with Achan.
Carmites; descendants of Carmi reckoned as a family in Israel, especially in Judah.
Pertaining to the flesh, especially man's natural and unspiritual state.
Often worldly, fleshly, or governed by corrupt appetite.
In a carnal manner or degree.
A carpenter; a workman in wood, construction, or skilled building trades.
Carpenters; workmen in wood and building.
a Christian at Troas with whom Paul left his cloak and books.
That which is carried along; baggage, supplies, or portable goods.
In some contexts, manner of conduct or bearing.
Plural form of 'carriage'. See 'carriage'.
Bore, transported, or took away.
Carriest; older second-person singular form of 'carry', used with 'thou'. See 'carry'.
Carrieth; older third-person singular form of 'carry'. See 'carry'.
To carry; to bear, transport, or convey.
Carrying; present participial form of 'carry', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'carry'.
Carshena; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
A cart is a wheeled carriage for carrying burdens or goods.
Carved; past tense or past participial form of 'carve'. See 'carve'.
Carving; present participial form of 'carve', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'carve'.
Plural form of 'carving'. See 'carving'.
Case; a condition, matter, or circumstance.
a window frame or a window opening.
Cases; conditions, matters, or circumstances.
Casiphia; the place from which Ezra obtained Levites and temple servants for the return to Jerusalem.
Casiphia.note: Location: Probably in Babylonia, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Casluhim; a people descended from Mizraim, from whom the Philistines are associated in Genesis.
Cassia; a fragrant spice or bark used among precious aromatics.
To throw, put, send forth, set down, or place.
Castaway; one rejected, disapproved, or cast aside.
Castedst; means thou didst cast.
Castest; older second-person singular form of 'cast', used with 'thou'. See 'cast'.
Casts; throws; sends away; causes to fall or be placed.
Casting; present participial form of 'cast', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'cast'.
Castle; a fortress, stronghold, or fortified building.
Plural form of 'castle'. See 'castle'.
Castor; one of the twin figures named with Pollux on the sign of an Alexandrian ship.
Catch; to seize, take, or entrap.
Catcheth; older third-person singular form of 'catch'. See 'catch'.
Caterpiller; a devouring creeping insect, especially one destructive to crops.
Plural form of 'caterpiller'. See 'caterpiller'.
Cattle; domestic beasts or livestock.
Caught; the past tense and past participle of catch.
Caul; a membrane or fold, especially the fat covering of certain inward parts in sacrificial language.
Plural form of 'caul'. See 'caul'.
Brought about; made to happen; compelled or brought to do.
Causeless; without just cause, reason, or provocation.
Plural form of 'cause'. See 'cause'.
Causest; older second-person singular form of 'cause', used with 'thou'. See 'cause'.
Causeth; older third-person singular form of 'cause'. See 'cause'.
Causeway; a raised way or approach.
Causing; present participial form of 'cause', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'cause'.
Cave; a hollow in the earth or rock used for shelter, hiding, or burial.
Plural form of 'cave'. See 'cave'.
Ceased; past tense or past participial form of 'cease'. See 'cease'.
Ceaseth; older third-person singular form of 'cease'. See 'cease'.
Ceasing; present participial form of 'cease', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'cease'.
A cedar is the stately and durable tree famed for greatness, fragrance, and strong timber.
It often signifies strength, loftiness, or noble growth.
Plural form of 'cedar'. See 'cedar'.
Cedron; the Greek spelling of Kidron, the brook east of Jerusalem crossed in the passion narrative.
Cedron.note: Location: Usually identified with Kidron River.

definitions: Celebrate; to keep with solemnity, proclaim with honor, or make known with praise.
heavenly; belonging to the sky or to heaven.
Plural form of 'cellar'. See 'cellar'.
Cenchrea; the eastern harbour of Corinth, mentioned in connection with Paul and Phoebe.
Censer; a vessel for carrying burning coals and incense.
Plural form of 'censer'. See 'censer'.
A centurion; a Roman officer set over a hundred soldiers or a company of that order.
In the New Testament the term often marks a military man of some rank and discipline.
Centurions; Roman officers of that military rank.
Cephas; the Aramaic name of Simon Peter, one of the chief apostles of Christ.
Plural form of 'ceremony'. See 'ceremony'.
Certain; sure, fixed, or some particular though unnamed.
In a certain manner or degree.
Certainty; sure knowledge, assurance, or fixed truth.
Certified; past tense or past participial form of 'certify'. See 'certify'.
Certify; to make known with certainty, attest, or give assurance.
Chafed; past tense or past participial form of 'chafe'. See 'chafe'.
Chaff; the husk or light refuse separated from grain.
A chain is a linked band of metal used for binding, ornament, or rank.
Plural form of 'chain'. See 'chain'.
Chalcedony; a precious stone named among the foundations in Revelation.
Chalcol; another biblical spelling of Calcol, one of the wise men named with Ethan and Heman.
Chaldaeans; the people of Chaldea, later dominant in the Babylonian empire.
Chaldaeans.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with southern Babylonia.

definitions: Chaldea; the southern Babylonian region associated with the Chaldeans and with the land from which Abraham was called.
Chaldea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell el Muqayyar.

definitions: Chaldean; belonging to Chaldea or to the Chaldeans, often with the sense of the Babylonian wise class or people.
Chaldean.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell el Muqayyar.

definitions: Chaldeans; the people of Chaldea, and in some passages especially the Babylonian wise or learned class.
Chaldees; the Chaldean people, or in some contexts the Chaldean language or learned class.
Chaldees.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with southern Babylonia.

definitions: Plural form of 'chalkstone'. See 'chalkstone'.
Challengeth; older third-person singular form of 'challenge'. See 'challenge'.
A chamber is an inner room or apartment, such as a private room, upper room, store room, or reserved place.
Lewd or wanton behavior connected with secret chambers; sensual indulgence.
A chamberlain; an officer of the bedchamber, court, or household entrusted with private service near a ruler or great person.
In some contexts the office is civil or administrative as well as domestic.
Chamberlains; officers of the chamber, court, or household entrusted with private service near rulers or great persons.
Chambers; inner rooms, apartments, or enclosed places.
Chameleon; a creature named among unclean creeping things; the exact species is debated.
an animal named among the clean beasts, likely a type of mountain goat or antelope.
Champaign; open level country or plain.
Champion; a mighty man or combatant who stands forth as representative in battle.
Chanaan; the Greek spelling of Canaan, the land promised to Abraham's seed.
an occurrence regarded as happening unexpectedly; also to happen or befall.
Chancellor; a high officer of state or court.
Chanceth; older third-person singular form of 'chance'. See 'chance'.
Change; alteration, exchange, or a fresh set of garments.
Changeable; subject to change, variable, or inconstant.
Changed; altered, exchanged, or provided with a fresh set of garments.
Plural form of 'changer'. See 'changer'.
Changes; alterations, exchanges, or additional sets of garments.
Changest; means thou alterest or exchangest.
Changeth; means alters or exchanges.
Changing; altering, exchanging, or varying in ongoing action.
