Encouraged; past tense or past participial form of 'encourage'. See 'encourage'.
End; the conclusion, limit, result, or purpose of a thing.
to injure or cause loss to.
Endanger; to put in peril or hazard.
Endangered; past tense or past participial form of 'endanger'. See 'endanger'.
Earnest effort, diligent attempt, or striving toward a thing.
To endeavour; to strive diligently or make earnest effort.
Endeavouring; present participial form of 'endeavour', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'endeavour'.
Plural form of 'endeavour'. See 'endeavour'.
Ended; was concluded, reached its limit, or came to its result.
Endeth; means concludes, reaches its limit, or comes to its result.
Ending; concluding, coming to a close, or the conclusion itself.
Endless; without end, boundless, or continuing without limit.
Endor; the town of the woman consulted by Saul on the eve of his final battle.
Endor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet es Safsafa.

definitions: Endow; to furnish with a gift, portion, or endowment.
Ends; conclusions, limits, results, or purposes.
Endued; past tense or past participial form of 'endue'. See 'endue'.
Endured; past tense or past participial form of 'endure'. See 'endure'.
Endureth; older third-person singular form of 'endure'. See 'endure'.
Enduring; present participial form of 'endure', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'endure'.
Enemies; those who are hostile or set against another.
An enemy; one who is hostile or opposed.
Enflaming; present participial form of 'enflame', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'enflame'.
Engaged; past tense or past participial form of 'engage'. See 'engage'.
Plural form of 'engine'. See 'engine'.
Engrafted; past tense or past participial form of 'engraft'. See 'engraft'.
Engrave; to cut, inscribe, or carve into a surface.
Engraven; carved, graven, or inscribed.
Engraver; one who cuts or carves inscriptions and devices.
Plural form of 'engraving'. See 'engraving'.
Enjoin; to command, charge, or direct with authority.
Enjoined; past tense or past participial form of 'enjoin'. See 'enjoin'.
Enjoy; to possess, partake of, or take delight in.
Enjoyed; past tense or past participial form of 'enjoy'. See 'enjoy'.
To make greater, widen, or set at liberty into a larger room.
increase, expansion, or release from restraint.
Enlargeth; older third-person singular form of 'enlarge'. See 'enlarge'.
Enlarging; present participial form of 'enlarge', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'enlarge'.
Enlighten; to give light to, illuminate, or make to understand.
Enlightened; past tense or past participial form of 'enlighten'. See 'enlighten'.
Enlightening; present participial form of 'enlighten', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'enlighten'.
Hostility, deep opposition, or settled hatred.
Enoch; the patriarch who walked with God and was translated that he should not see death, and also the name of other early figures.
Enos; the son of Seth in the early genealogy of mankind.
Enosh; a name-form of Enos, the son of Seth, in whose days men began to call upon the name of the LORD.
Enough; sufficient in quantity or degree.
Enquire; to ask, seek information, or inquire of God or others.
Enquired; past tense or past participial form of 'enquire'. See 'enquire'.
Enquirest; older second-person singular form of 'enquire', used with 'thou'. See 'enquire'.
Enquiry; inquiry, question, or search for information.
Enrich; to make rich, increase in value, or furnish abundantly.
Enriched; past tense or past participial form of 'enrich'. See 'enrich'.
Enrichest; older second-person singular form of 'enrich', used with 'thou'. See 'enrich'.
An example or pattern set before others for imitation or warning.
Ensign; a standard, banner, or signal lifted up for gathering or display.
Plural form of 'ensign'. See 'ensign'.
Ensnared; past tense or past participial form of 'ensnare'. See 'ensnare'.
Ensue; to follow after or come as a result.
To involve, ensnare, or twist into hindering difficulty.
Entangleth; older third-person singular form of 'entangle'. See 'entangle'.
Entered; went in, came into, took possession, or began.
Entereth; older third-person singular form of 'enter'. See 'enter'.
Entering; the act of going in, or the place by which one enters.
Enterprise; a purposeful undertaking or bold design.
Entertain; to receive, host, or hold in the mind.
Entertained; received, hosted, or held in mind.
To lure, draw on, or lead by persuasion toward a desired end.
Often toward evil.
Enticing; present participial form of 'entice', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'entice'.
Entire; whole, complete, or undivided.
the act or place of entering.
Plural form of 'entrance'. See 'entrance'.
To beseech, plead with, or earnestly ask.
In some contexts, to deal with or treat in a certain manner.
Entreateth; older third-person singular form of 'entreat'. See 'entreat'.
Plural form of 'entry'. See 'entry'.
Entry; the act of entering, or a place of entrance.
Envied; past tense or past participial form of 'envy'. See 'envy'.
Plural form of 'envy'. See 'envy'.
Enviest; older second-person singular form of 'envy', used with 'thou'. See 'envy'.
Envieth; older third-person singular form of 'envy'. See 'envy'.
Envious; full of envy, grudging another's good.
Environ; to surround or compass about.
Painful resentment at another's good; grudging another's advantage.
To envy; to resent another's good or desire it grudgingly.
Plural form of 'envying'. See 'envying'.
Epaenetus; a beloved Christian greeted by Paul as an early convert unto Christ.
Epaphras; a fellow servant of Paul, especially associated with the church at Colosse.
Epaphroditus; the messenger and fellow labourer sent between Philippi and Paul.
Ephah; a Hebrew dry measure for grain and other goods.
Ephai; a Midianite son of Abraham by Keturah, and also a Judahite name.
Epher; chiefly a genealogical name borne by descendants of Midian, Caleb, and Benjamin.
Ephes-Dammim; the battlefield region between Socoh and Azekah where the Philistines encamped in David's day.
Ephes-dammim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Damun.

definitions: Ephesian; an inhabitant of Ephesus or something belonging to that city.
Ephesians; the people of Ephesus, and especially the church addressed in Paul's epistle.
Ephesus; the chief city of Asia in the New Testament and an important center of trade, idolatry, and apostolic ministry.
It is especially associated with Paul, the Ephesian church, and the worship of Diana.
Ephesus.note: Location: On the western coast of Asia Minor, near modern Selcuk in Turkey.

definitions: Ephlal; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ephod; a sacred garment associated with the priesthood, and in some passages an object used in worship.
Ephphatha; an Aramaic word meaning Be opened.
Ephraim; the son of Joseph, the tribe descended from him, or the northern kingdom represented by that tribe.
Ephraimite; a member of the tribe of Ephraim.
Ephraimites; the members of the tribe descended from Ephraim, often prominent in the northern tribes.
Ephrain; the town near the wilderness to which Jesus withdrew before the Passover of his passion.
Ephratah; a personal or clan name associated with Caleb's line, and also a place-name linked with Bethlehem by related form.
Ephrath; a woman in the genealogies of Judah, associated with the line from which Bethlehem Ephratah is named.
Ephrathite; one belonging to Ephrath or Bethlehem, and in some passages one of a family so called.
Ephrathites; people of Ephrath or Bethlehem, and in some contexts members of an Ephrathite line in Judah.
Ephron; the Hittite from whom Abraham bought the field and cave of Machpelah.
Plural form of 'epicurean'. See 'epicurean'.
Epistle; a letter, especially one written with doctrinal or pastoral authority.
Plural form of 'epistle'. See 'epistle'.
equal in measure, value, or rank; also to match, compare, or one who is an equal.
sameness in measure, value, or standing.
In a equal manner or degree.
Plural form of 'equal'. See 'equal'.
fairness, justice, or what is equitable.
Er; the firstborn son of Judah, slain by the LORD for wickedness, and also a name in the genealogy of Jesus.
Eran; a son of Shuthelah from whom the Eranites were reckoned.
Eranites; descendants of Eran reckoned among the families of Ephraim.
Erastus; the name of New Testament men, including Paul's fellow worker and the chamberlain of Corinth.
Ere; before.
Erech; one of the earliest cities in Nimrod's kingdom in the land of Shinar.
Erected; past tense or past participial form of 'erect'. See 'erect'.
Eri; a son of Gad from whom the Erites were reckoned.
Erites; descendants of Eri reckoned among the families of Gad.
To wander from the right way; to go astray in judgment or conduct.
Errand; a message, commission, or business sent on.
Erreth; older third-person singular form of 'err'. See 'err'.
Mistake, wandering from truth, or false belief and conduct.
Esaias; the Greek name for Isaiah the prophet.
Esar-Haddon; king of Assyria, remembered in Scripture for conquest and resettlement.
Esau; the elder son of Isaac and Rebekah, brother of Jacob, and ancestor of Edom.
To get free from danger, judgment, or confinement.
Escapeth; older third-person singular form of 'escape'. See 'escape'.
Escaping; present participial form of 'escape', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'escape'.
Eschew; to shun, avoid, or turn away from.
Eschewed; past tense or past participial form of 'eschew'. See 'eschew'.
Escheweth; older third-person singular form of 'eschew'. See 'eschew'.
Esek; the well dug by Isaac's servants and so named because of contention.
Esek.note: Location: In the Gerar-to-Beersheba region of the southern Philistine borderlands where Isaac's herdsmen contended for water.

definitions: a Benjamite, son of Saul, also known as Ish-bosheth.
Eshban; a descendant in the line of Seir the Horite.
Eshcol; an Amorite ally of Abram, and also the name associated with the valley of the cluster.
Eshean; a hill-country town of Judah named among its settlements.
Eshean.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet et Taiyiba.

definitions: Eshek; a descendant of Saul in the Benjamite genealogies, father of several sons.
Eshkalonites; the people or inhabitants of Ashkelon, one of the chief Philistine cities.
Eshkalonites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Ashkelon.

definitions: Eshtaol; a Danite town associated with Samson and with the migration of Dan.
Eshtaol.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Deir Shubeib.

definitions: Eshtaulites; the clan or inhabitants associated with Eshtaol in Judah and Dan.
Eshtaulites.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Deir Shubeib, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Eshtemoa; a Judahite name in the genealogies, distinct from the city of the same name.
Eshtemoh; a hill-country town of Judah to which David sent spoil and which later became a priestly city.
Eshtemoh.note: Location: Commonly identified with As Samu.

definitions: Eshton; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Esli; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
In a especial manner or degree.
Espied; past tense or past participial form of 'espy'. See 'espy'.
Espousals; plural The act of contracting or affiancing man and woman to each other.
Espoused; past tense or past participial form of 'espouse'. See 'espouse'.
Espy; to catch sight of, observe, or spy out.
Esrom; a man named in Scripture, son of Pharez, husband of Machir, father of Ram.
To make firm, settled, or stable.
Establishes; makes firm, settled, or secure.
Often means confirming, strengthening, or setting on a sure footing.
the act of founding, settling, or making firm.
Condition, standing, or state of life.
Sometimes rank, dignity, or outward circumstances.
To value, account, or regard with a certain judgment.
Esteemeth; older third-person singular form of 'esteem'. See 'esteem'.
Esteeming; present participial form of 'esteem', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'esteem'.
Esther; the Jewish queen of Persia who interceded to save her people in the days of Ahasuerus.
Estimate; valuation, reckoning, or judgment of worth.
estimate, valuation, or regard.
Plural form of 'estimation'. See 'estimation'.
Estranged; past tense or past participial form of 'estrange'. See 'estrange'.
Etam; the name of a Judahite site associated with Solomon's building works, and also of the rock-cleft refuge to which Samson withdrew after striking the Philistines.
Etam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Arak Ismain.

definitions: everlasting, without end, or belonging to eternity.
endless duration or timeless existence.
Etham; the wilderness-stage where Israel camped on the edge of the desert after leaving Egypt.
Etham.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tell er Retaba.

definitions: Ethan; a wise man famed for wisdom and also the name of one associated with sacred song and other biblical figures.
Ethanim; the seventh month of the Hebrew year, later called Tisri.
Ethbaal; king of the Zidonians and father of Jezebel.
Ether; a southern town of Simeon or Judah named in the Negev lists.
Ether.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Ater.

definitions: Ethiopia; the southern kingdom beyond Egypt, often corresponding to Cush in the Old Testament setting.
Ethiopia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Meroe.

definitions: Ethiopian; belonging to Ethiopia or one of its people, often corresponding to Cush in the Old Testament setting.
Ethiopian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Meroe.

definitions: Ethiopians; the people of Ethiopia or Cush in biblical usage.
Ethiopians.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Meroe.

definitions: Ethnan; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ethni; a Levite ancestor in the line leading to Asaph, elsewhere represented by a variant name in the genealogies.
Eubulus; a Christian who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul.
Eunice; the mother of Timothy, remembered for her unfeigned faith.
Eunuch; a castrated man, or more broadly a court officer.
Eunuchs; castrated men, or more broadly court officers.
Euodias; a Christian woman at Philippi whom Paul exhorted to be of one mind.
Euphrates; the great river of Mesopotamia, one of the boundary markers of the promised land and a major river in biblical history.
Euphrates.note: Location: Usually identified with Euphrates River.

definitions: Euroclydon; the violent northeast storm wind mentioned in Acts 27.
Eutychus; the young man at Troas who fell from the window and was restored alive through Paul.
A preacher or bearer of the good tidings of the gospel.
Eve; the first woman, wife of Adam and mother of all living.
Even; indeed, exactly, fully, likewise, or level and smooth.
A flexible word often used for emphasis or exactness.
Evening is the declining part of the day toward night.
Plural form of 'evening'. See 'evening'.
Eveningtide; the time of evening.
Event; an outcome, occurrence, or result.
Eventide; evening time.
Ever; at any time, always, continually, or through all time.
Everlasting; enduring without end, or lasting through all ages according to the thing spoken of.
Forever; at all times henceforth; ever.
Each one of all within the class spoken of.
Evi; one of the Midianite kings slain in the days of Moses.
proof, testimony, or in some passages a written deed or document.
Plural form of 'evidence'. See 'evidence'.
Clear, plain, and able to be readily seen or known.
In a evident manner or degree.
Bad, harmful, wicked, or morally corrupt.
Evil-Merodach; king of Babylon who lifted up the head of Jehoiachin and treated him kindly.
Evildoer; one who does evil.
Plural form of 'evildoer'. See 'evildoer'.
The state, quality, or condition of being evilfavourednes.
Plural form of 'evil'. See 'evil'.
Ewe; the female sheep.
Plural form of 'ewe'. See 'ewe'.
Exact; to require or demand rigorously; as an adjective, strict or precise.
Exacted; past tense or past participial form of 'exact'. See 'exact'.
Exacteth; older third-person singular form of 'exact'. See 'exact'.
a severe demand, levy, or extortion.
Plural form of 'exaction'. See 'exaction'.
Plural form of 'exactor'. See 'exactor'.
Lifted up, raised in rank, or highly honored.
Exaltest; older second-person singular form of 'exalt', used with 'thou'. See 'exalt'.
Exalts; lifts up; raises in dignity or station.
careful inquiry, inspection, or testing.
Examine; to search, try, or question closely.
Examined; past tense or past participial form of 'examine'. See 'examine'.

definitions: Ezem; a southern town counted with Simeon's inheritance in the Negev.
Ezem.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Asan.

definitions: Ezer; chiefly the name of a Horite chief and of warriors who joined David, and also found in genealogies.
Ezion-Gaber; the port on the Gulf of Aqaba from which Solomon's ships sailed.
Ezion-gaber.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Jezirat Faraun, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Ezion-Geber; the southern port used for sea trade on the Gulf of Aqaba, also written Ezion-Gaber.
Ezion-geber.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Jezirat Faraun, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Eznite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Ezn in Scripture.

Plural form of 'forefather'. See 'forefather'.
Forefront; the front rank or foremost part.
The forehead is the front part of the head above the eyes.
It may also signify openness, boldness, or marked identity.
Plural form of 'forehead'. See 'forehead'.
Foreigner; one belonging to another land or people.
Plural form of 'foreigner'. See 'foreigner'.
Foreknew; the past tense of foreknow.
Foreknow; to know beforehand.
Foreknowledge; knowledge beforehand, especially God’s prior knowledge and purpose.
Foremost; first, chief, or in the front.
Foreordained; past tense or past participial form of 'foreordain'. See 'foreordain'.
Forepart; the front part.
Forerunner; one who goes before to prepare the way.
Foresaw; the past tense of foresee.
Foreseeing; present participial form of 'foresee', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'foresee'.
Foreseeth; older third-person singular form of 'foresee'. See 'foresee'.
the forepart or bow of a ship.
Foreskin; the covering removed in circumcision.
Plural form of 'foreskin'. See 'foreskin'.
A wooded region; a tract thick with trees.
Foretell; to declare beforehand what is to come.
Foretold; the past tense and past participle of foretell.
Forewarn; to warn beforehand.
Forewarned; past tense or past participial form of 'forewarn'. See 'forewarn'.
Forfeited; past tense or past participial form of 'forfeit'. See 'forfeit'.
Forgat; an older past tense of forget.
Forgavest; older second-person singular form of 'forgive', used with 'thou'. See 'forgive'.
Forged; shaped by hammering, or falsely made.
Plural form of 'forger'. See 'forger'.
Forget; to fail to remember, or to neglect.
Forgetful; apt to forget, neglectful in remembrance.
The state, quality, or condition of being forgetfulnes.
Forgettest; means failest to remember or neglectest.
Forgetteth; means forgets, fails to remember, or neglects.
Forgetting; failing to remember, overlooking, or neglecting.
To remit, pardon, or release from debt or offense.
Plural form of 'forgiveness'. See 'forgiveness'.
Forgives; remits the offense; releases from the debt or guilt of a wrong.
Forgiving; present participial form of 'forgive', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'forgive'.
Lost remembrance of; failed to keep in mind; neglected.
Plural form of 'fork'. See 'fork'.
Form; shape, pattern, outward appearance, or prescribed order.
Formed; shaped, fashioned, or arranged.
Former; previous, first, or earlier.
Formeth; means shapes, fashions, or arranges.
Forms; shapes, patterns, appearances, or prescribed arrangements.
Sexual uncleanness; unlawful sexual commerce.
Fornicator; one guilty of unlawful sexual intercourse.
Plural form of 'fornicator'. See 'fornicator'.
Forsaken; left, abandoned, or deserted.
Forsakes; leaves, abandons, or gives up.
Forsaking; abandoning, leaving, or turning away from someone or something.
Forsomuch; forasmuch, inasmuch, or since.
Forsook; the past tense of forsake.
Forsookest; older second-person singular form of 'forsook', used with 'thou'. See 'forsook'.
Forswear; to renounce by oath, deny on oath, or swear falsely.
Fort; a fortified place or stronghold.
Forward or outward from a place; out into view or action.
Frequently used to mark going out, being brought out, or proceeding onward.
Forthwith; immediately, without delay.
Fortieth; older third-person singular form of 'forty'. See 'forty'.
Fortified; past tense or past participial form of 'fortify'. See 'fortify'.
Fortify; to strengthen with defenses or make strong.
a fortified stronghold or place of defense.
Plural form of 'fortress'. See 'fortress'.
Plural form of 'fort'. See 'fort'.
Fortunatus; a Christian associated with Stephanas and Achaicus in Paul's correspondence.
The number forty.
Forum; a public place of meeting or market in a Roman town.
Forward; eager, ready, willing, or advanced toward the front.
The state, quality, or condition of being forwardnes.
The past tense of fight; strove or warred.
Foul; filthy, defiling, or offensive.
Fouled; past tense or past participial form of 'foul'. See 'foul'.
Fouledst; means thou didst foul or make unclean.
Came upon, discovered, or was found to be in a certain state.
That which is laid as the base of a building.
Figuratively, the ground, beginning, or settled basis upon which something stands.
Founded; past tense or past participial form of 'found'. See 'found'.
Founder; one who casts metal, or one who lays a foundation.
Foundest; older second-person singular form of 'found', used with 'thou'. See 'found'.
Fountain; a spring, source of water, or figuratively a source of life and blessing.
Plural form of 'fountain'. See 'fountain'.
The number four.
Fourfold; four times as much, or in four parts.
Fourfooted; having four feet, used especially of beasts.
Fourscore; eighty.
Foursquare; square on every side, equally proportioned.
The number fourteen.
The one next after the thirteenth; occupying the fourteenth place.
The one next after the third; occupying the fourth place.
Fowl; birds collectively.
Fowler; one who catches birds.
Plural form of 'fowler'. See 'fowler'.
Plural form of 'fowl'. See 'fowl'.
Fox; the crafty wild animal so called.
Plural form of 'fox'. See 'fox'.
Plural form of 'fragment'. See 'fragment'.
Frail; weak, fragile, or morally or physically feeble.
To form, shape, or fashion.
Also the formed structure or inward constitution of a thing.
Framed; past tense or past participial form of 'frame'. See 'frame'.
Frameth; older third-person singular form of 'frame'. See 'frame'.
Frankincense; a precious aromatic resin used in worship and anointing.
In a frank manner or degree.
Fraud; deceit, false dealing, or wrongful trickery.
Fray; to frighten, drive away, or wear by rubbing.
Freckled; marked with spots or small speckles.
Free; not bound, enslaved, hindered, or under obligation.
It may also mean liberal or openhanded.
Freed; past tense or past participial form of 'free'. See 'free'.
liberty or the state of being free.
In a free manner or degree.
Freeman; one who is free, not in bondage.
Freewill; voluntary willingness, especially in offerings freely given.
Freewoman; a woman not in bondage.
Frequent; happening often, common, or repeated.
Fresh; new, recent, or not stale.
Fresher; more fresh or more recent.
Fret; to be vexed, consume away, or wear by rubbing.
Fretted; vexed, worn away, or adorned with carved work.
Fretteth; means is vexed, consumes away, or rubs and wears.
Fretting; vexing, wearing away, or rubbing with irritation.
Fried; past tense or past participial form of 'fry'. See 'fry'.
Friend; one loved, allied, or kindly disposed toward another.
Friendly; kind, favorable, or disposed as a friend.
Plural form of 'friend'. See 'friend'.
the relation, affection, or goodwill of friends.
Fringe; a border, tassel, or hanging edge of a garment.
Plural form of 'fringe'. See 'fringe'.
Fro; away in the opposite direction, chiefly in the phrase to and fro.
Plural form of 'frog'. See 'frog'.
From; out of, away from, or proceeding out of.
Front; the forepart, face, or foremost side.
Plural form of 'frontier'. See 'frontier'.
Frontlets; , in the Hebrew, bands, filets.
Frost; frozen dew or cold rime.
Perverse; willfully contrary; hard to govern or persuade.
In a froward manner or degree.
Perverseness; stubborn contrary temper.
Frozen; made solid by cold.
Fruit; produce, offspring, or result.
Bearing fruit; productive; yielding increase.
Also rich in good works or spiritual increase.
Fruits; produce, offspring, or results.
Frustrate; to make void, disappoint, or bring to nothing.
Frustrateth; older third-person singular form of 'frustrate'. See 'frustrate'.
Fryingpan; a pan used for frying or preparing food over fire.
Fuel; material used to feed a fire.
Fugitive; fleeing, wandering, or unstable.
As a noun, one who flees or lives as a wanderer.
Plural form of 'fugitive'. See 'fugitive'.
Fulfil; to complete, accomplish, or fill up fully.
Completed, accomplished, or brought to full effect.
Fulfilling; present participial form of 'fulfill', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'fulfill'.
Full; filled, complete, abundant, or entire.
Fuller; one who cleanses and thickens cloth.
Plural form of 'fuller'. See 'fuller'.
In a ful manner or degree.
Fullness; the state of being full or complete.
Often abundance brought to completion or the whole measure appointed.
Furbish; to polish, especially a weapon.
Furbished; past tense or past participial form of 'furbish'. See 'furbish'.
Furious; full of rage, violent, or stormy in force.
In a furious manner or degree.
Plural form of 'furlong'. See 'furlong'.
A furnace is a heated place or structure for smelting, burning, or intense fire.
It often signifies severe trial or consuming judgment by figure.
Plural form of 'furnace'. See 'furnace'.
Furnish; to supply, equip, or provide.
Furnished; past tense or past participial form of 'furnish'. See 'furnish'.
equipment, furnishings, or household goods.
A furrow is the trench or line made in the earth by the plough.
Plural form of 'furrow'. See 'furrow'.
Further; to promote, advance, or help onward; as an adverb, farther.
advancement, aid, or promotion.
Furthered; past tense or past participial form of 'further'. See 'further'.
Furthermore; moreover, besides, or in addition.
Fury; fierce anger or wrath.
Gaal; the son of Ebed who opposed Abimelech at Shechem.
Gaash; the hill-country area associated with the burial place of Joshua and with the torrent valleys beside it.
Gaash.note: Location: In the hill country of Ephraim near Timnath-serah, commonly placed northwest of Jerusalem, though the exact ridge is uncertain.

definitions: Gaba; a town identified with Gibeah or a closely related Benjamite site in some biblical forms.
Gaba.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gabbai; a Benjamite dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Gabbatha; the paved place where Pilate sat in judgment over Jesus.
Gabbatha.note: Location: In Jerusalem at the praetorium or governor's judgment place; whether at Herod's palace or the Antonia area remains debated.

definitions: Gabriel; the angelic messenger sent to Daniel, Zacharias, and Mary.
Gad; the son of Jacob, the tribe descended from him, or the prophet so named.
Gadarenes; the inhabitants of the district associated with Gadara in the Gospel account of the demoniac.
Gaddest; means wanderest or roamest restlessly.
Gaddi; the father of Susi and a name in tribal records of Manasseh.
Gaddiel; the son of Sodi and spy of the tribe of Zebulun.
Gadi; the father of Menahem king of Israel.
Gadite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gad in Scripture.
Gadites; the members of the tribe descended from Gad, especially settled east of Jordan.
Gaham; a son born to Nahor by Reumah.
Gahar; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Gain; profit, advantage, or increase obtained.
Gained; past tense or past participial form of 'gain'. See 'gain'.
Plural form of 'gain'. See 'gain'.
To speak against, contradict, or oppose by words.
Plural form of 'gainsayer'. See 'gainsayer'.
Gaius; chiefly the New Testament name of Paul's companions and hosts, including the host mentioned in Romans.
Galal; the name of Levites and singers in the records of temple service.
Galatia; the inland region and Roman province of Asia Minor whose churches received Paul's Epistle to the Galatians.
Galatians; the people of Galatia, and especially the churches addressed in Paul's epistle.
Galbanum; an aromatic gum used in the holy incense.
Galeed; the heap of witness raised by Jacob and Laban, and the place named from it.
Galeed.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Galilaean; a person of Galilee or something belonging to that northern region.
Galilaeans; the people of Galilee, sometimes viewed distinctively by speech or region.
Galilee; the northern region of the land of Israel, especially associated with the ministry of Jesus.
In some passages the name is used by relation to the sea of Galilee.
Galilee.note: Location: In the north of the land of Israel, west of the upper Jordan and the Sea of Galilee.

definitions: Gall; bitterness, poisonous bitterness, or bile.
Gallant; brave, noble, or splendid in appearance.
Plural form of 'gallery'. See 'gallery'.
Gallery; a covered walk, upper passage, or projecting structure.
Galley; a large ship with oars.
Gallim; a village named in Isaiah's Assyrian advance toward Jerusalem and linked with the family of Palti.
Gallim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Kakul.

definitions: Gallio; the deputy of Achaia before whom Paul was brought at Corinth.
Plural form of 'gallow'. See 'gallow'.
Gamaliel; the respected Pharisee and doctor of the law who counselled moderation regarding the apostles.
Gammadims; a warrior people or military group mentioned among the defenders of Tyre in Ezekiel's lament.
Gammadims.note: Location: Often identified with Kamid el Loz, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gamul; the head of one of the priestly courses in David's arrangement.
Gap; a breach, opening, or break in a wall or hedge.
Gaped; past tense or past participial form of 'gap'. See 'gap'.
Plural form of 'gap'. See 'gap'.
A garden is a cultivated place for herbs, fruits, flowers, or pleasant growth.
In Scripture it may also suggest delight, refreshment, or enclosed care.
Gardener; one who tends a garden.
Plural form of 'garden'. See 'garden'.
Gareb; one of David's mighty men, and elsewhere the name of a hill or place near Jerusalem.
Plural form of 'garland'. See 'garland'.
Garlick; the pungent bulb used for food.
A piece of clothing; an article of raiment worn upon the body.
Garmite; one belonging to a Judahite family or clan associated with Garm.
A granary or storehouse for grain.
Garnish; to adorn, furnish, or beautify.
Garnished; past tense or past participial form of 'garnish'. See 'garnish'.
Garrison; a body of soldiers stationed in a fortified place, or the post they occupy.
Plural form of 'garrison'. See 'garrison'.
Gashmu; the Arabian opponent of Nehemiah, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Geshem.
Gat; an older past tense of get.
Gatam; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
A gate; the entrance of a city, building, or enclosure.
Gates; entrances or openings of cities, courts, or enclosures.
Gath; one of the chief Philistine cities, home of Goliath and refuge of David for a time.
Gath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Gath-Hepher; the town in Zebulun traditionally associated with the prophet Jonah.
Gath-hepher.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gat Hefer.

definitions: Gath-Rimmon; a Levitical town named in both Danite and Manassite contexts in the allotment lists.
Gath-rimmon.note: Location: The exact site is debated; one proposed identification is Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Collected together; assembled; brought into one place.
In some contexts also taken in, harvested, or brought to one's people in death.
Gatherer; one who gathers, especially fruit, grapes, or tribute.
Gatherest; older second-person singular form of 'gather', used with 'thou'. See 'gather'.
Gathers; collects into one; brings together.
It may also mean harvests, takes in, or draws by degrees.
Gathering; present participial form of 'gather', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'gather'.
Plural form of 'gathering'. See 'gathering'.
The past tense of give.
Died; breathed out the last breath of life.
Gavest; thou gavest; you gave or bestowed.
Showy; fine in outward appearance; splendidly dressed.
Gaza; one of the five chief Philistine cities, prominent in the histories of Samson and later imperial conflicts.
Gaza.note: Location: On the southwestern coastal plain near the route between Canaan and Egypt.

definitions: Gazathites; the inhabitants of Gaza, the Philistine city on the southwestern coast.
Gaze; to look steadily or with wonder.
Gazer; the name Gezer in another biblical spelling, the strategically important Canaanite city on the western approaches.
Gazer.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gezer.

definitions: Gazez; the name of men in the genealogies connected with Caleb's line.
Gazing; present participial form of 'gaze', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'gaze'.
Gazingstock; one made a spectacle to others.
Gazites; the people of Gaza, one of the chief Philistine cities.
Gazzam; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Geba; a Benjamite town and military post on the northern approach to Jerusalem.
Geba.note: Location: Commonly identified with El Jib.

definitions: Gebal; the Phoenician city later known as Byblos, famed for shipwrights and craftsmen.
Geber; one of Solomon's officers set over a district of Israel.
Gebim; a place named in Isaiah's picture of the Assyrian advance toward Jerusalem.
Gebim.note: Location: Probably a small site north of Jerusalem on the invasion route from the Benjamin plateau, in the same general approach as Nob and Anathoth.

definitions: Gedaliah; the governor set over Judah after Jerusalem fell to Babylon, and also the name of other biblical men.
Gedeon; the Greek name for Gideon, the judge who overthrew Midian.
Geder; a royal Canaanite town listed among the defeated kings of Joshua's campaign.
Geder.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Gederah; a town in the Shephelah of Judah named among the lowland settlements.
Gederah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gederathite; a person from Gederah or Gederath in the lowland towns of Judah.
Gederathite.note: Location: From the Shephelah of Judah, in the lowland towns south or southwest of Jerusalem.

definitions: Gederite; a person from Geder, one of the towns of Judah.
Gederite.note: Location: From the southern or lowland district of Judah, though the exact site of Geder is uncertain.

definitions: Gederoth; a lowland town of Judah named in the territorial lists and in Sennacherib's campaign context.
Gederoth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gederothaim; a town of Judah in the Shephelah lists, perhaps closely linked with Gederoth.
Gederothaim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Judraya.

definitions: Gedor; a Judahite or Benjamite name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Gehazi; the servant of Elisha who was smitten with leprosy after acting deceitfully in the matter of Naaman.
Geliloth; a border landmark near Jericho and the Jordan crossing in Joshua's territorial descriptions.
Geliloth.note: Location: Often identified with Araq ed Deir.

definitions: Gemalli; the father of Ammiel, the spy from the tribe of Dan.
Gemariah; the name of men in Scripture, especially one who heard Jeremiah's roll and one sent to Babylon by Zedekiah.
Gender; to produce, bring forth, or generate.
Gendered; past tense or past participial form of 'gender'. See 'gender'.
Gendereth; older third-person singular form of 'gender'. See 'gender'.
Plural form of 'genealogy'. See 'genealogy'.
Genealogy; a record or tracing of descent and family line.
common, broad, or universal; also a chief commander.
In a general manner or degree.
The people of one age or descent; an age or family line springing from common stock.
Sometimes the race or class of men marked by a certain character.
Gennesaret; the fertile plain or district on the northwestern shore of the Sea of Galilee.
The name may also be connected by relation with the adjoining waters in context.
Gennesaret.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with El Ghuweir.

definitions: Gentile; one of the nations outside Israel, or something pertaining to those nations.
Gentiles; the nations outside Israel, especially as distinguished from the Jews.
Gentle; mild, kind, or gracious in manner.
The state, quality, or condition of being gentlenes.
In a gent manner or degree.
Genubath; the son of Hadad the Edomite, reared in Pharaoh's house.
Gera; a Benjamite ancestor from whom lines in Benjamin were reckoned.
Plural form of 'gerah'. See 'gerah'.
Gerar; the Philistine city or district associated with Abraham, Isaac, and Abimelech.
Gerar.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Haror.

definitions: Gergesenes; the inhabitants of the district named in one Gospel form for the country of the demoniacs.
Gerizim; the mount near Shechem from which blessing was pronounced.
Gerizim.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Gerizim.

definitions: Gershom; the son of Moses, and also the name of a Levitical family descended from him and of other biblical men.
Gershon; a man named in Scripture, son of Levi, father of Libni.
Gershonite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gershon in Scripture.
Gershonites; the Levitical family descended from Gershon, charged with parts of the tabernacle service.
Geshan; a descendant in the line of Judah named in the genealogical lists.
Geshem; an Arabian opponent of Nehemiah who joined in resisting the rebuilding of Jerusalem.
Geshur; a small Aramean border kingdom linked with Talmai and with Absalom through his mother.
Geshur.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with the Golan region near Fiq.

definitions: Geshuri; one belonging to Geshur, the small Syrian kingdom associated with Absalom's maternal house.
Geshurites; the people of Geshur, whether the northern Aramean border kingdom or a southern group named in older conquest notices.
Geshurites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Fiq.

definitions: Gether; a son of Aram in the early post-Flood genealogies.
Gethsemane; a garden or enclosed cultivated place mentioned in Scripture.
Getteth; older third-person singular form meaning obtains, gains, or begets.
Getting; present participial form of 'get', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'get'.
Geuel; the Gadite spy sent from the wilderness, identified with Nahbi in the parallel list.
Gezer; the strategic Canaanite city on the western approaches to the hill country, later given to Solomon as part of Pharaoh's dowry gift.
Gezer.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gezer.

definitions: Gezrites; a people of the south country mentioned in David's wilderness campaigns.
Spirit; breath of life.
In the phrase 'gave up the ghost,' to yield up life.
Giah; a locality mentioned in the pursuit of Abner after the battle by Gibeon.
Giah.note: Location: East of Gibeon in Benjamin, on the route of Abner's retreat, though the exact locality is uncertain.

definitions: Giant; a man of extraordinary size or strength.
Plural form of 'giant'. See 'giant'.
Gibbar; the name attached to a returning family in the post-exilic lists, likely tied to the Gibeonites or to Gibeon.
Gibbethon; a Philistine-border town of Dan that became the scene of long conflict in the northern kingdom.
Gibbethon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Melat.

definitions: Gibea; a town in the hill country of Judah, distinct from the better-known Gibeah of Benjamin.
Gibea.note: Location: In the hill country of Judah south of Hebron, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Gibeah; a Benjaminite town associated with Saul and with the outrage that led to civil war in Judges.
Gibeah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gibeath; a hill or height named near Kirjath-jearim in the account of Abinadab and the ark.
Gibeath.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Tel el Ful, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gibeathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gibeath in Scripture.
Gibeathite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel el Ful.

definitions: Gibeon; the Hivite city that made covenant with Joshua, later important as a priestly and political center in Benjamin.
Gibeonite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Gibeon in Scripture.
Gibeonites; the inhabitants of Gibeon, remembered especially for making league with Israel in Joshua's day.
Giblites; the inhabitants or craftsmen of Gebal, the Phoenician city later known as Byblos.
Giddalti; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Giddel; the head of a family of Nethinims or servants of Solomon.
Gideon; the judge of Israel who overthrew the Midianites; in other passages he is called Jerubbaal.
Gideoni; the father of Abidan, prince of Benjamin.
Gidom; a locality named in the aftermath of the war against Benjamin in Judges.
Gidom.note: Location: In Benjamin near Gibeah and the routes mentioned in the war against Benjamin, though the exact locality is unknown.

definitions: Gier; an older name for a bird of prey, often identified with a vulture or kite.
Gift; something given freely, whether an offering, bribe, or gracious bestowal.
Gifts; things given freely, whether offerings, bribes, or gracious bestowals.
Gihon; the name of a river in Eden and also of the spring or water source associated with Jerusalem.
Gihon.note: Location: In Genesis it is one of Eden's rivers, often linked broadly with Cush; in Jerusalem it refers to the spring on the city's eastern side.

definitions: Gilalai; a priest or musician present in the dedication and worship records of Jerusalem.
Gilboa; the mountain range or heights associated especially with the death of Saul and Jonathan.
The name carries a solemn historical association in the books of Samuel.
Gilboa.note: Location: A ridge south of the Jezreel Valley, commonly identified with Mount Gilboa in northern Israel.

definitions: Gilead; the hill country east of Jordan, known for balm, pasture, and many important historical events.
Gilead.note: Location: East of the Jordan River, in the hill country between Bashan to the north and Moab to the south.

definitions: Gileadite; an inhabitant of Gilead east of the Jordan.
Gileadite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell edh Dhahab esh Sherqiyeh.

definitions: Gileadites; the inhabitants of Gilead east of the Jordan.
Gileadites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with the hill country of Gilead east of Jordan.

definitions: Gilgal; the early encampment of Israel after crossing Jordan, associated with covenant remembrance and later royal and prophetic events.
Gilgal.note: Location: Often identified with Araq ed Deir, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Giloh; the Judean town from which Ahithophel came.
Giloh.note: Location: In the hill country of Judah south of Jerusalem, usually identified in the Hebron region.

definitions: Gilonite; a person from Gilon in the hill country of Judah, such as Ahithophel.
Gilonite.note: Location: From Gilon in the hill country of Judah south of Jerusalem, likely in the Hebron region.

definitions: Gimzo; a lowland town of Judah taken by the Philistines in the days of Ahaz.
Gin; a snare, trap, or engine for catching.
Ginath; the father of Tibni, claimant to the throne of Israel.
Ginnetho; the name of a priestly house in the post-exilic records, also written Ginnethon.
Ginnethon; the name of a priestly house, especially one represented among the covenant signers after the exile.
Plural form of 'gin'. See 'gin'.
To bind about, equip, or prepare by fastening on what is needed.
Girdedst; means thou didst gird.
Girding; present participial form of 'gird', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'gird'.
A girdle is a belt, sash, or band worn about the loins.
It may signify readiness, strength, office, or binding close for action.
Plural form of 'girdle'. See 'girdle'.
Girgashite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Girgash in Scripture.
Girgashites; one of the peoples of Canaan named among the older inhabitants of the land.
Girgasite; a member of the Girgashite people, one of the ancient Canaanite nations of the land.
Girl; a young female child or maiden.
Plural form of 'girl'. See 'girl'.
Gispa; an overseer of the Nethinims in the days of Nehemiah.
Gittah-Hepher; another biblical spelling of Gath-hepher, the Zebulunite town associated with Jonah.
Gittah-hepher.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Gat Hefer.

definitions: Gittaim; a Benjamite town to which Beerothites fled after Saul's time.
Gittaim.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Abu Hamid.

definitions: Gittite; an inhabitant of Gath.
Gittite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Gittites; the people or inhabitants of Gath, the Philistine city.
Gittites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Granted, bestowed, delivered, or appointed.
Giver; one who gives.
Givest; thou givest; you give, bestow, or grant.
Gives; bestows; grants.
Giving; present participial form of 'give', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'give'.
Gizonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Gizon in Scripture.
Glad; pleased, joyful, or cheerful.
In a glad manner or degree.
Joy, cheerfulness, and inward delight.
glass or a shining transparent substance.
Plural form of 'glass'. See 'glass'.
Glean; to gather what remains after the main reaping or harvest.
Gleaned; past tense or past participial form of 'glean'. See 'glean'.
Gleaning; present participial form of 'glean', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'glean'.
Plural form of 'gleaning'. See 'gleaning'.
Glede; a bird of prey, commonly identified with a kite or similar raptor.
Shining brightly; sparkling.
Glitter; to shine with a bright flashing light.
Glittering; present participial form of 'glitter', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'glitter'.
The state, quality, or condition of being gloomines.
Gloriest; older second-person singular form of 'glory', used with 'thou'. See 'glory'.
Glorieth; older third-person singular form of 'glory'. See 'glory'.
Glorifieth; older third-person singular form of 'glorify'. See 'glorify'.
To honor, magnify, and make glorious in esteem or display.
Glorifying; present participial form of 'glorify', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'glorify'.
Glorious; full of splendor, honor, or excellence.
In a glorious manner or degree.
Honor, splendor, praise, or manifested excellence, especially that which belongs to God.
Glorying; present participial form of 'glory', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'glory'.
Glutton; one given to excessive eating.
Gluttonous; given to excessive eating or indulgence.
Gnash; to grind or strike together, especially the teeth.
Gnashed; past tense or past participial form of 'gnash'. See 'gnash'.
Gnasheth; older third-person singular form of 'gnash'. See 'gnash'.
Gnashing; present participial form of 'gnash', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'gnash'.
Gnat; the small stinging or flying insect so called.
Gnaw; to bite or wear away by repeated biting.
Gnawed; past tense or past participial form of 'gnaw'. See 'gnaw'.
Goad; a pointed staff used to drive oxen or other beasts.
Plural form of 'goad'. See 'goad'.
Goat; the animal so called, used for food, sacrifice, and figurative teaching.
Goath; a hill or district named in Jeremiah's vision of Jerusalem's future expansion.
Goath.note: Location: Near Jerusalem in Jeremiah's boundary description of the restored city, though the exact hill is uncertain.

definitions: Goats; the animals so called.
Plural form of 'goatskin'. See 'goatskin'.
Gob; a battle site in the wars between Israel and the Philistines in David's reign.
Gob.note: Location: In the Philistine border region, sometimes linked with the area of Gezer in the western lowlands.

definitions: Goblet; a cup or bowl for drinking.
God; the one true and living God, the Maker and Lord of all things.
A god; a false deity or object of worship when Scripture speaks of the gods of the nations.
God may also be used in a subordinate or figurative sense for one bearing divine title or authority in a few passages.
May it never be; certainly not.
God-ward; toward God, in relation to God, or with reference to Him.
a female deity.
Godhead; the divine nature or deity.
Reverent piety; a life shaped by the fear of God and likeness to His holiness.
Godly; devout, pious, and conformed to the will of God.
Gods; false deities, supposed divine beings, or rulers so called.
Goest; thou goest; you go, depart, or proceed.
Goes; proceeds; departs.
Often used of a person's course, movement, or the advancing of a matter.
Gog; a personal name in genealogy and also the name of the hostile ruler seen in prophecy.
Going; the act of departing, proceeding, or moving onward.
Goings; movements.
Golan; the Bashan city appointed as a city of refuge for Manasseh east of the Jordan.
Golan.note: Location: Often identified with Saham al Jawlan.

definitions: Gold; the precious metal so called.
Golden; made of gold, colored like gold, or of special worth.
Goldsmith; one who works in gold.
Plural form of 'goldsmith'. See 'goldsmith'.
Golgotha; the place of the skull outside Jerusalem where the Lord Jesus was crucified.
Golgotha.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Church of the Holy Sepulchre, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Goliath; the Philistine giant of Gath slain by David.
Gomer; the name of a son of Japheth and also of Hosea's wife in the prophetic sign-act.
Gomorrah; one of the cities of the plain destroyed with Sodom as an example of divine judgment.
Gomorrah.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Gomorrha; the Greek spelling of Gomorrah, one of the cities of the plain destroyed in judgment.
Gomorrha.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Gone; departed, passed away, or proceeded onward.
Good in quality, usefulness, uprightness, or moral worth.
Goodlier; more goodly, more fair, or more stately.
Goodliest; older second-person singular form of 'goodly', used with 'thou'. See 'goodly'.
The state, quality, or condition of being goodlines.
Goodly; fair, handsome, stately, or of pleasing appearance.
Goodman; the master of a house, householder, or husband.
Moral excellence, kindness, and beneficent quality.
Plural form of 'good'. See 'good'.
Gopher; the wood named in connection with Noah’s ark; the exact tree is uncertain.
Gore; to pierce or wound with a horn.
Gored; past tense or past participial form of 'gore'. See 'gore'.
Gorgeous; splendid, showy, or richly adorned.
In a gorgeous manner or degree.
Goshen; the district in Egypt where Jacob's family settled and multiplied before the Exodus.
Goshen.note: Location: Generally placed in the eastern Nile Delta of Egypt, though the exact bounds are uncertain.

definitions: The good tidings, especially the glad message of Christ and salvation in Him.
Got; the past tense and often the past participle of get.
Gotten; the past participle of get.
Gourd; a vine or plant with broad leaves, especially the one prepared over Jonah.
Plural form of 'gourd'. See 'gourd'.
Govern; to rule, direct, or control.
rule, administration, or governing authority.
Plural form of 'government'. See 'government'.
A governor; a ruler, overseer, or one set over others in authority.
The term may denote a provincial ruler, household overseer, or one entrusted with civil administration.
Gozan; the Assyrian district or city by the Habor where Israelite captives were settled.
Gozan.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Halaf.

definitions: Favor freely shown, especially the unmerited favor of God.
The word may also describe gracious influence or beauty of manner.
Full of grace, favor, kindness, and readiness to show mercy.
In a gracious manner or degree.
Graff; an older spelling of graft, meaning to insert or join a branch.
Graffed; past tense or past participial form of 'graff'. See 'graff'.
Grain is corn or seed gathered for food and increase.
Grandmother; the mother of one’s father or mother.
Grant; to give, bestow, or allow.
As a noun, a gift or concession.
Granted; past tense or past participial form of 'grant'. See 'grant'.
A grape is the fruit of the vine, used for eating and for making wine.
Grapegatherer; one who gathers grapes at vintage.
Plural form of 'grapegatherer'. See 'grapegatherer'.
Plural form of 'grapegleaning'. See 'grapegleaning'.
Plural form of 'grape'. See 'grape'.
green herbage or tender field growth.
Grasshopper; the leaping insect so called.
Plural form of 'grasshopper'. See 'grasshopper'.
Grate; a lattice or framework, especially of brass, or to rasp.
Weighty, serious, or dignified.
Not only the burial place.
Plural form of 'graveclothe'. See 'graveclothe'.
Graved; past tense or past participial form of 'grave'. See 'grave'.
Gravel; small stones or pebbles.
Graven; carved, engraved, or made by carving.
Plural form of 'grave'. See 'grave'.
Graveth; older third-person singular form of 'grave'. See 'grave'.
Graving; present participial form of 'grave', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'grave'.
Plural form of 'graving'. See 'graving'.
Seriousness, dignity, and weightiness of behavior.
Gray; hoary or gray in color, especially of hair with age.
Grayheaded; past tense or past participial form of 'grayheaded'. See 'grayheaded'.
Grease; fat or oily substance.
Large, mighty, weighty, eminent, or important.
Greater; larger, more important, or higher in degree.
Greatest; chiefest in size, rank, weight, or importance among those compared.
Greatly; much, exceedingly, or in a great degree.
Great extent, dignity, power, or excellence.
Greaves; plates of brass, or other defensive covering, for the front of the legs, well known as parts of ancient armour.
Grecia; the Greek world or realm of the Greeks.
Grecia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Athens.

definitions: Grecians; Greek-speaking people, especially Hellenistic Jews as distinguished from Hebrews in Acts.
Greece; the Greek world or land of the Greeks, especially in the later biblical setting.
In a greedi manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being greedines.
Greedy; eager to get, ravenous, or covetous.
Greek; one belonging to the Greek people or language, and in the New Testament often the Gentile in contrast with the Jew.
Greeks; the Greek people, and in some New Testament settings more broadly Greek-speaking Gentiles.
Green; fresh in color or growth, not dry.
Greenish; somewhat green in hue.
The state, quality, or condition of being greennes.
Greet; to salute or welcome.
Greeteth; older third-person singular form of 'greet'. See 'greet'.
Greeting; present participial form of 'greet', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'greet'.
Plural form of 'greeting'. See 'greeting'.
Grew; the past tense of grow.
Greyhound; a creature named in Proverbs among things of stately going; the exact animal is debated.
Grief; sorrow, pain, or vexation.
Plural form of 'grief'. See 'grief'.
a cause of complaint, injury, or suffering.
Grieve; to cause sorrow, pain, or distress; also to be sorrowful.
Grieved; past tense or past participial form of 'grieve'. See 'grieve'.
Grieveth; older third-person singular form of 'grieve'. See 'grieve'.
Grieving; present participial form of 'grieve', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'grieve'.
Heavy, painful, burdensome, or severe.
In a grievous manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being grievousnes.
Grind; to crush into powder, or to oppress harshly.
Plural form of 'grinder'. See 'grinder'.
Grinding; crushing into powder, or oppressing harshly in ongoing action.
Grisled; past tense or past participial form of 'grisled'. See 'grisled'.
Groan; to utter a deep mournful sound from pain, burden, or longing.
Groaned; past tense or past participial form of 'groan'. See 'groan'.
Groaneth; older third-person singular form of 'groan'. See 'groan'.
Groaning; present participial form of 'groan', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'groan'.
Plural form of 'groaning'. See 'groaning'.
Grope; to feel about blindly or uncertainly.
Gropeth; older third-person singular form of 'grope'. See 'grope'.
thick, coarse, or full; also the whole amount before deductions.
Ground; earth, soil, or the basis of a thing.
Grounded; founded, based, or set firmly on a ground.
Grove; in many passages the idolatrous sacred tree, pole, or Asherah set up for false worship.
Plural form of 'grove'. See 'grove'.
Grows; increases; develops; waxes greater.
Grown; having grown, come to full size, or increased.
Growth; increase, growing, or product that has grown.
To murmur, begrudge, or feel ill will at another's good.
In a grudging manner or degree.
Guard; watch, protection, or a body of men appointed for keeping.
Gudgodah; a wilderness-stage in Israel's journey, corresponding to Hor-Hagidgad in the Numbers itinerary.
Gudgodah.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Khadakhid, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Guest; one who is received or entertained in another's house.
Guestchamber; a room prepared for guests or lodging.
Plural form of 'guest'. See 'guest'.
Guide; one who leads or directs another, or the act of leading.
Guided; led or directed.
Guides; persons who lead or direct others.
Guiding; leading or directing.
Subtle deceit, craft, or treacherous cunning.
Guilt; liability to blame or punishment, especially for sin or bloodshed.
The state, quality, or condition of being guiltines.
Guiltless; innocent, free from guilt, or not chargeable with wrong.
Guilty; chargeable with offense or liable to condemnation.
Gulf; a great chasm or deep separation.
Guni; the name of men in Scripture, including ancestors in the tribes of Naphtali and Gad.
Gunites; descendants of Guni reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Gur; the ascent or slope near Ibleam by which Jehu pursued Ahaziah.
Gur.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Khirbet en Najjar, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Gur-Baal; a place or district associated with Arabian peoples subdued in the days of Uzziah.
Gush; to burst forth and flow suddenly.
Gushed; past tense or past participial form of 'gush'. See 'gush'.
Gutter; a channel for water, drain, or watercourse.
Plural form of 'gutter'. See 'gutter'.
Ha; an exclamation of surprise, mockery, or sudden notice.
Haahashtari; a Judahite name in the genealogies of Ashur's house.
Habaiah; the head of a priestly family whose genealogy could not be found after the exile.
Habakkuk; the prophet who pleaded with God concerning judgment and lived by faith.
Habaziniah; the father of Jaazaniah of the house of the Rechabites.
A coat or breastpiece of armor, commonly of mail.
Habitable; fit to be inhabited or lived in.
A habitation; a dwelling place, settled abode, or place of residence.
It may refer to a single dwelling, a settled district, or the place where a people or creature lives.
Habor; a river of Mesopotamia to which captives from Israel were carried.
Habor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khabur River.

definitions: Hachaliah; the father of Nehemiah.
Hachilah; the hill on whose side David hid from Saul in the wilderness of Ziph.
Hachilah.note: Location: Often identified with Zahrat al Kula.

definitions: Hachmoni; the ancestor or family-name associated with one of David's chief mighty men.
Hachmonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Hachmon in Scripture.
The past tense of have.
Hadad; chiefly the name of Edomite rulers and of the Edomite adversary raised up against Solomon; the name is also used of a Syrian deity in related contexts.
Hadadezer; a man named in Scripture, son of Rehob.
Hadadrimmon; the place in the valley of Megiddo remembered for great mourning, especially after Josiah's death.
Hadar; a royal or chief name in Edomite lines; in some passages the form Hadad appears instead.
Hadarezer; a man named in Scripture, son of Rehob.
Hadashah; a town in the lowland of Judah listed among the Shephelah settlements.
Hadashah.note: Location: In the Shephelah between Lachish and Gath, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Hadassah; the Hebrew name of Esther, the Jewish queen of Persia.
Hadattah; a southern town of Judah on the Negev side of the tribal lists.
Hadattah.note: Location: In the southern territory of Judah near Har Hezron, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Hadid; a Benjamite town resettled after the exile and associated with the Lydda-Ono region.
Hadid.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Haditha.

definitions: Hadlai; an Ephraimite leader who opposed harsh treatment of Judah's captives.
Hadoram; chiefly the son of Joktan and also the messenger sent from Tou king of Hamath to David.
Hadrach; a northern Syrian land or district named in Zechariah's oracle of judgment.
Hadrach.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Afis.

definitions: Hadst; means thou hadst.
Haft; the handle of a weapon or tool.
Hagab; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Hagaba; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Hagabah; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Hagaba.
Hagar; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Abram, mother of Ishmael.
Plural form of 'hagarene'. See 'hagarene'.
Hagarites; a desert people east of Jordan, often named in conflict with Israelite tribes.
Hagerite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Hager in Scripture.
Haggai; the prophet who stirred the returned exiles to resume the rebuilding of the temple.
Haggeri; a family designation borne by one of David's mighty men.
Haggi; a son of Gad from whom the Haggites were reckoned.
Haggiah; a Levite named in the genealogies.
Haggites; descendants of Haggi reckoned among the families of Gad.
Haggith; one of David's wives and the mother of Adonijah.
Hai; another biblical spelling of Ai, the city defeated in Joshua's conquest after Israel's first setback there.
Hai.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tell.

definitions: Hail; frozen drops of rain, or a greeting.
Plural form of 'hailstone'. See 'hailstone'.
Hair is the natural growth upon the head or body.
In Scripture it can also signify beauty, weakness, consecration, or minuteness.
Plural form of 'hair'. See 'hair'.
Hairy; covered with hair or having much hair.
Hakkatan; the father of Johanan, one of the returning exiles named by Ezra.
Hakkoz; the head of a priestly family or course in Israel.
Hakupha; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Halah; one of the Assyrian districts to which the ten tribes were carried away in exile.
Halah.note: Location: In northern Mesopotamia under Assyrian rule, probably in the Habur region east of the upper Euphrates.

definitions: Halak; the mountain marking a southern extent in Joshua's conquests.
Halak.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jebel Halaq, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hale; to draw or drag with force.
Half; one of two equal parts.
Halhul; a hill-country town of Judah north of Hebron, later known as a fortified highland settlement.
Hali; a town in the inheritance of Asher named among the northern settlements.
Hali.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Alil.

definitions: Haling; present participial form of 'hale', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hale'.
Hall; a large room, court, or public chamber.
Hallohesh; ruler of half the district of Jerusalem in the rebuilding days of Nehemiah.
To make holy, set apart, or treat as sacred.
Halohesh; the father of Shallum in Nehemiah, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Hallohesh.
To limp; to walk lamely.
In some places, to hesitate or waver between two courses.
Halting; present participial form of 'halt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'halt'.
Ham; a man named in Scripture, son of Noah, father of Canaan.
Haman; the Agagite enemy of the Jews in the book of Esther, overthrown by the providence of God.
Hamath; the great Syrian city and kingdom marking Israel's northern approaches in many biblical texts.
Hamath-Zobah; the northern district where Solomon exercised power in relation to Hamath and Zobah.
Hamath-zobah.note: Location: Often located in or identified with the Hama region, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hamathite; one belonging to Hamath or to the Hamathite branch named among Canaanite peoples.
Hammath; a fenced city of Naphtali, likely remembered for its hot springs.
Hammath.note: Location: On the western side of the Sea of Galilee, commonly linked with the hot-springs area south of Tiberias.

definitions: Hammedatha; the father of Haman the Agagite.
Hammelech; a royal designation used in names such as Jerahmeel the king's son.
Hammer; a tool for striking, or figuratively an instrument of breaking power.
Plural form of 'hammer'. See 'hammer'.
Hammoleketh; a woman named in the genealogies of Manasseh.
Hammon; a town named in tribal allotment lists, used of places associated with Asher and Naphtali.
Hammon.note: Location: The exact site is uncertain; one traditional association is the Hammath/Tiberias area.

definitions: Hammoth-Dor; a Levitical town in Naphtali, probably the same place otherwise called Hammath.
Hammoth-dor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Hamat Tiberias.

definitions: Hamon-Gog; the valley or burial-place named in Ezekiel's prophecy for the multitude of Gog.
Hamonah; the city named in Ezekiel's vision after the burial of Gog's multitude.
Hamor; a man named in Scripture, father of Shechem.
Hamuel; a Simeonite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Hamul; a son of Pharez from whom the Hamulites were reckoned.
Hamulites; descendants of Hamul reckoned among the families of Judah.
Hamutal; wife of Josiah and mother of Jehoahaz and Zedekiah.
Hanameel; the son of Shallum whose field Jeremiah purchased at Anathoth.
Hanan; chiefly a post-exilic name borne by officials, Levites, and heads of families in Jerusalem's restoration.
Hananeel; the name of a man in Scripture, and also of the tower on the north side of Jerusalem mentioned in prophetic and rebuilding contexts.
Hanani; the seer who reproved Asa and also the brother of Nehemiah, with other biblical men of the same name.
Hananiah; chiefly the faithful companion of Daniel called Shadrach and the false prophet opposed by Jeremiah, though borne by many others as well.
Hand; the bodily member, and by extension power, possession, agency, or control.
Handbreadth; a small measure equal to the breadth of the hand.
Handed; given over, delivered, or provided by hand.
Handful; as much as the hand can hold, or a small quantity.
Plural form of 'handful'. See 'handful'.
Plural form of 'handkerchief'. See 'handkerchief'.
Handle; to take in the hand, touch, or deal with.
Handled; past tense or past participial form of 'handle'. See 'handle'.
Plural form of 'handle'. See 'handle'.
Handleth; older third-person singular form of 'handle'. See 'handle'.
Handling; present participial form of 'handle', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'handle'.
A handmaid; a female servant, especially one speaking of herself in humble dependence.
The term often carries a tone of lowliness and submission.
A handmaiden is a handmaid, a female servant speaking or regarded in humble dependence.
Plural form of 'handmaiden'. See 'handmaiden'.
Handmaids; female servants, especially in a relation of humble dependence.
Hands; the bodily members, and by extension power, possession, labor, or agency.
Handstaves; weapons of some kind.
Handwriting; present participial form of 'handwriting', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'handwriting'.
Handywork; work made by the hand, workmanship.
Hanes; an Egyptian city mentioned by Isaiah in connection with Judah's appeal to Egypt.
Hanes.note: Location: Often identified with Ihnasiyyah al Madinah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hang; to suspend, be suspended, or put to death by hanging.
Hanged; suspended or put to death by hanging.
Hangeth; means hangs, is suspended, or remains hanging.
Hanging; the act or state of suspending, remaining suspended, or execution by hanging.
Hangings; suspended coverings, draperies, or things hanging.
Haniel; the son of Ephod and prince of the tribe of Manasseh.
Hannah; the mother of Samuel, known for fervent prayer and praise before the LORD.
Hannathon; a border town of Zebulun on the line toward the northwestern valleys.
Hannathon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Hanaton.

definitions: Hanniel; a name in the tribal genealogies and territorial records, especially in Asher and Manasseh.
Hanoch; chiefly the name of a son of Reuben and also a son of Midian in the genealogies.
Hanochites; descendants of Hanoch reckoned as a family in Israel, especially in Reuben.
Hanun; the Ammonite king who shamefully treated David's servants, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hap; chance, event, or occurrence.
Hapharaim; a town in the inheritance of Issachar.
Hapharaim.note: Location: Often identified with Et Taiyibeh.

definitions: Perhaps; by chance; it may be.
Happen; to occur or come to pass.
Happened; past tense or past participial form of 'happen'. See 'happen'.
Happeneth; older third-person singular form of 'happen'. See 'happen'.
Happier; more happy or more blessed.
Happy; blessed, fortunate, or joyful.
Hara; a mountain range or chain of heights mentioned in Scripture.
Hara.note: Location: Likely in the Assyrian-Mesopotamian region connected with Israel's exile, though the exact range is uncertain.

definitions: Haradah; an encampment of Israel during the wilderness journey.
Haradah.note: Location: On the wilderness route south of Canaan, probably in the northern Sinai or southern Negev, though the exact camp site is uncertain.

definitions: Haran; the brother of Abram and father of Lot, and also a Levitical name elsewhere in Scripture.
Hararite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Harar in Scripture.
Harbona; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus who mentioned Haman's gallows.
Harbonah; the chamberlain of Ahasuerus, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Harbona.
Hard; firm, difficult, severe, or stubborn.
To make hard, stubborn, or insensitive.
Hardeneth; older third-person singular form of 'harden'. See 'harden'.
Harder; more hard, more severe, or more difficult.
Hardhearted; stubborn, unfeeling, or resistant to pity and correction.
In a hard manner or degree.
Harshness, severity, or stubborn insensibility.
Often the hardening of heart against truth.
Hare; the animal so called, named among unclean creatures.
Hareph; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Hareth; older third-person singular form of 'hare'. See 'hare'.
Hareth.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Kharas, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Harhaiah; the father of Uzziel the goldsmith in Nehemiah's rebuilding record.
Harhas; father of Shallum, the husband of Huldah the prophetess, also written Hasrah.
Harhur; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Harim; the name of priestly or family groups after the exile, and also of individual men in genealogical and reform contexts.
Hariph; the name of a returning family and also of those represented among the covenant signers after the exile.
Harlot; a prostitute, or figuratively one guilty of spiritual unfaithfulness.
Plural form of 'harlot'. See 'harlot'.
Harm is injury or hurt; to harm is to injure or do wrong to.
Harmless; innocent, inoffensive, or doing no hurt.
Harnepher; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
The state, quality, or condition of being harnes.
Harnessed; past tense or past participial form of 'harness'. See 'harness'.
Harod; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
Harod.note: Location: Commonly identified with Well of Harod.

definitions: Harodite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Harod in Scripture.
Harodite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Harazan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Haroeh; an ancestor in the Judahite genealogies, elsewhere represented by a related form such as Reaiah.
Harorite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Haror in Scripture.
Harorite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Harazan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Harosheth; usually Harosheth of the Gentiles, the stronghold of Sisera in the days of Deborah and Barak.
Harp; the stringed instrument so called.
Harped; past tense or past participial form of 'harp'. See 'harp'.
Plural form of 'harper'. See 'harper'.
Harping; present participial form of 'harp', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'harp'.
Plural form of 'harp'. See 'harp'.
Harrow; an implement for breaking and smoothing soil, or to tear harshly.
Harrows; implements for breaking soil, or acts of harrowing and tearing.
Harsha; the head of a family whose genealogy could not be shown after the exile.
Hart; a stag or male deer.
Plural form of 'hart'. See 'hart'.
Harum; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Harumaph; the father of Jedaiah in the rebuilding record of Nehemiah.
Haruphite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Haruph in Scripture.
Haruz; the father of Meshullemeth, wife of Manasseh king of Judah.
The reaping and gathering of ripe grain or other produce.
By figure, a season of ingathering, reward, or judgment.
Harvestman; one who reaps the harvest.
Hasadiah; a descendant of Zerubbabel in the Davidic line.
Hasenuah; a Benjamite family-name or ancestor in the post-exilic records.
Hashabiah; chiefly a Levitical and administrative name in temple service and national oversight.
Hashabnah; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Hashabniah; the father of Hattush, and also a Levite name in post-exilic records.
Hashbadana; one of those who stood with Ezra at the reading of the law.
Hashem; father or family designation connected with David's mighty men, especially in the phrase 'the sons of Hashem.'
Hashmonah; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
Hashmonah.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route toward Edom, probably in the northern Sinai or Arabah fringe, though the exact camp site is uncertain.

definitions: Hashub; chiefly a post-exilic builder and signer associated with Jerusalem's restoration.
Hashubah; a descendant of Zerubbabel in the Davidic line.
Hashum; the head of a family named among the returned exiles and covenant signers.
Hashupha; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Hasrah; father of Shallum the keeper of the wardrobe and father-in-law of Huldah, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Harhas.
Hassenaah; the ancestor or family-name of the builders of the fish gate in Nehemiah.
Hasshub; chiefly a post-exilic builder or family head; this form corresponds closely to Hashub.
An older second-person singular form meaning 'have.'
Hasted; past tense or past participial form of 'hast'. See 'hast'.
Hasten; to make haste, speed, or urge on quickly.
Hastened; past tense or past participial form of 'hasten'. See 'hasten'.
Hasteneth; older third-person singular form of 'hasten'. See 'hasten'.
Makes haste; moves or acts quickly.
In a hasti manner or degree.
Hasting; present participial form of 'hast', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hast'.
Hasty; quick, rash, or done with too much speed.
Hasupha; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Hashupha.
Hatach; the chamberlain appointed to attend Esther.
Hatch; to bring forth from the egg, or to devise.
Hatcheth; means brings forth from the egg or devises and plots.
Hated; past tense or past participial form of 'hate'. See 'hate'.
Hateful; odious, detestable, or full of hatred.
In a hateful manner or degree.
Plural form of 'hater'. See 'hater'.
Hatest; thou hatest; you hate or regard with settled aversion.
Hates; regards with aversion or settled opposition.
Hath; the older third-person singular present of have.
Hathath; a descendant in the line of Othniel.
Hating; present participial form of 'hate', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hate'.
Hatipha; the head of a family of servants of Solomon.
Hatita; the head of a family of porters among the returned exiles.
Hatred; past tense or past participial form of 'hatred'. See 'hatred'.
Plural form of 'hat'. See 'hat'.
Hattil; the head of a family whose genealogy was uncertain after the exile.
Hattush; chiefly a descendant of David and one who returned with Ezra, though borne by more than one man.
In a haughti manner or degree.
Proud loftiness; arrogance of spirit.
Haughty; proud, lofty, and disdainful.
Haunt; a place of frequent resort, or to frequent habitually.
Hauran; the fertile upland district east of the Jordan known for rich pasture and grain lands.
Have; to possess, hold, receive, or be under.
Haven; a harbor or place of safety for ships.
Plural form of 'haven'. See 'haven'.
Havilah; the name of a son in early genealogies, distinct from the land of Havilah.
Having; possessing, holding, or being in a certain condition.
Havock; devastation, ruin, or violent destruction.
Havoth-Jair; the tent-villages or settlements associated with Jair in Bashan east of the Jordan.
Havoth-jair.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with the Bashan region east of Jordan.

definitions: Hawk; the bird of prey so called.
Hay; dried grass used for fodder, and figuratively what soon withers.
Hazael; a man named in Scripture, father of Benhadad.
Hazaiah; a man of Judah dwelling at Jerusalem after the exile.
Hazar-Addar; a southern border place in the territorial description of Judah.
Hazar-addar.note: Location: In the far south near Kadesh-barnea and the wilderness of Zin on Judah's southern border.

definitions: Hazar-Enan; the northern boundary point named in Israel's ideal land borders.
Hazar-enan.note: Location: Often identified with Al Qaryatayn.

definitions: Hazar-Gaddah; a southern town named in the inheritance of Judah.
Hazar-gaddah.note: Location: In the southern Negev of Judah, likely in the Beersheba district.

definitions: Hazar-Hatticon; the middle Hazar, a boundary place in Ezekiel's northern border description.
Hazar-hatticon.note: Location: Often identified with Al Qaryatayn.

definitions: Hazar-Shual; a southern town allotted to Judah and later counted to Simeon.
Hazar-shual.note: Location: In the southern Negev near Beersheba.

definitions: Hazar-Susah; a southern Simeonite town in the Negev, probably related to Hazar-Susim in a parallel list.
Hazar-susah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet esh Shamsaniyat.

definitions: Hazar-Susim; a southern Simeonite town in the Negev, probably parallel to Hazar-Susah in another list.
Hazar-susim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet esh Shamsaniyat.
