definitions: Hazarded; past tense or past participial form of 'hazard'. See 'hazard'.
Hazarmaveth; a son of Joktan and ancestor of an Arabian people or district.
Hazazon-Tamar; another name associated with En-Gedi near the Dead Sea.
Hazazon-tamar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ein Gedi.

definitions: Hazel; the tree or wood so called.
Hazelelponi; a woman named in the genealogies of Judah.
Hazerim; villages or settled encampments, and in some contexts a named locality on the southern fringe of the land.
Hazeroth; a wilderness encampment where Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses.
Hazeroth.note: Location: Often identified with Ain el Khadra.

definitions: Hazezon-Tamar; a place near the western side of the Dead Sea, identified with En-gedi in Genesis and Chronicles.
Hazezon-tamar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Ein Gedi.

definitions: Haziel; a son of Shimei in the Levitical genealogies.
Hazo; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Hazor; the great Canaanite royal city in the north, later destroyed in Joshua's conquest and remembered in the days of Jabin and Deborah.
Hazor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Hazor.

definitions: The masculine singular personal pronoun.
Head; the bodily member, and by extension the chief place, ruler, or top part.
Plural form of 'headband'. See 'headband'.
Headlong; with the head foremost, violently, rashly, or precipitately.
Heads; the bodily members, and by extension chiefs, tops, or leading parts.
A headstone is the chief stone, especially the top or principal stone in a building.
Heady; rash, headstrong, or reckless.
to cure, restore, or make whole.
Made whole; cured or restored.
Healer; one who heals or restores.
Healeth; older third-person singular form of 'heal'. See 'heal'.
Healing; present participial form of 'heal', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'heal'.
Plural form of 'healing'. See 'healing'.
Health; soundness, healing, or welfare.
A heap is a pile or gathered mass of things; to heap is to pile up.
In Scripture it may describe ruins, gathered stones, or abundance laid together.
Heaped; past tense or past participial form of 'heap'. See 'heap'.
Heapeth; older third-person singular form of 'heaps'. See 'heaps'.
Plural form of 'heap'. See 'heap'.
Heard; listened to; received by hearing.
Often also took notice of or granted audience to.
Heardest; thou heardest; you heard or gave ear to.
A hearer is one who hears, especially one who listens to teaching or command.
Plural form of 'hearer'. See 'hearer'.
Hearest; thou hearest; you hear.
Frequently implies that you listen with attention or heed what is spoken.
Hears; receives by the ear; listens to.
Frequently implies heeding, attending, or obeying what is heard.
Hearing; present participial form of 'hear', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hear'.
To listen attentively; to give ear with obedience or regard.
Hearkenedst; means thou didst hearken.
Hearkening; present participial form of 'hearken', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hearken'.
The heart of the body.
Very often the inward man: the seat of thought, desire, purpose, and affection.
Hearted; past tense or past participial form of 'heart'. See 'heart'.
A hearth is the paved or prepared place of a fire within a house.
In a hearti manner or degree.
Hearts; inward beings, minds, affections, or wills.
Hearty; sincere, vigorous, or from the heart.
Heat; warmth, burning force, or fervor.
Heated; past tense or past participial form of 'heat'. See 'heat'.
Heath; a bare or desert shrub-place, and by extension a desolate place.
The nations, especially those outside the covenant people and ignorant of the true God.
Heave; to lift up, raise, or move with effort.
Heaved; past tense or past participial form of 'heave'. See 'heave'.
Heaven; the visible sky or the dwelling place of God and the blessed.
Heavenly; belonging to heaven, divine, or spiritually exalted.
Heavier; more heavy, weightier, or more grievous.
In a heavi manner or degree.
Sorrow, grief, or burden of spirit.
Sometimes literal weight, but often inward sadness.
Heavy; weighty, burdensome, sorrowful, or dull.
Heber; especially Heber the Kenite in the days of Deborah and Jael, and also the name of an ancestor in older genealogies and other biblical men.
Heberites; descendants of Heber reckoned among the families of Asher.
Hebrew; one of the covenant people descended from the patriarchs, especially viewed over against foreign nations.
a Hebrew woman.
Hebrews; the people of Israel considered as Hebrews, especially in distinction from other nations or from Hellenists.
Hebron; one of the oldest cities in the land, associated with Abraham, the patriarchal tombs, and David's early reign.
Hebron.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Hebronites; descendants of Hebron reckoned among the Levitical families.
Hedge; a fence or enclosure of thorns or plants, or a barrier set about.
Hedged; past tense or past participial form of 'hedge'. See 'hedge'.
Plural form of 'hedge'. See 'hedge'.
Careful attention; watchful regard.
As a verb, to give careful attention to.
Heel; the hinder part of the foot.
Plural form of 'heel'. See 'heel'.
Hegai; the keeper of the women in the court of Ahasuerus who had charge of Esther.
Hege; the chamberlain who had charge of the women in Esther, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Hegai.
Heifer; a young cow.
Height; elevation, high place, or exalted measure.
Plural form of 'height'. See 'height'.
Heinous; very wicked, grievous, or hateful.
One who receives an inheritance or stands to receive it by right.
Helah; one of the wives of Ashur in the genealogies of Judah.
Helam; the place where David's forces defeated the Arameans under Hadadezer's allies.
Helbah; a town in the territory of Asher named among the places from which the Canaanites were not expelled.
Helbah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet al Mahalib.

definitions: Helbon; a Syrian town famous for its wine, named among the trade goods of Tyre.
Held; the past tense and past participle of hold.
Heldai; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and a returned exile mentioned in Zechariah.
Heleb; one of David's mighty men, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Heled or Heldai.
Heled; one of David's mighty men; parallel lists also give the forms Heleb and Heldai.
Helek; a son of Gilead from whom the Helekites were reckoned.
Helekites; descendants of Helek reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Helem; the name of a man in the Asherite genealogies and also a returned exile in the visions of Zechariah.
Heleph; a point on the northern border of Naphtali in the tribal allotment lists.
Heleph.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Irbadeh.

definitions: Helez; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and a Judahite ancestor.
Heli; the father of Joseph in Luke's genealogy of Jesus.
Helkai; a priestly head in the days of Joiakim.
Helkath; a town of Asher given to the Levites.
Helkath.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Qashish.

definitions: Helkath-Hazzurim; a field or tract of ground mentioned in Scripture.
Helkath-hazzurim.note: Location: By the pool of Gibeon in Benjamin, where Joab's men fought Abner's men.

definitions: Hell; the unseen world of the dead in some passages, and in others the place of final punishment.
Helm; the steering gear or rudder of a ship.
A helmet is defensive armor worn upon the head.
In figurative use it may signify protection for the mind or hope of salvation.
Plural form of 'helmet'. See 'helmet'.
Helon; the father of Eliab, prince of the tribe of Zebulun.
Help; aid, assistance, or support.
Helped; past tense or past participial form of 'help'. See 'help'.
Helper; one who helps or aids.
Plural form of 'helper'. See 'helper'.
Helpeth; older third-person singular form of 'help'. See 'help'.
Helping; present participial form of 'help', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'help'.
Aids, supports, or forms of assistance.
Helve; the handle of an axe or similar tool.
Hem; the border or edge of a garment.
Hemam; a Horite chief in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same chief elsewhere written Homam.
Heman; the name of men in Scripture, especially the singer and wise man associated with temple music.
Hemath; a family-name or ancestor associated with the house of Rechab.
Hemdan; a Horite descendant in the line of Dishon, named in the Edomite genealogies.
Hemlock; a bitter and poisonous plant, or something compared to it for bitterness.
Plural form of 'hem'. See 'hem'.
Hen; the female bird, especially of domestic fowl.
Hena; a city named with other conquered places in Assyrian boasts against Judah.
Henadad; the name of post-exilic Levites and their family line in the rebuilding period.
From this place; away; from this cause or time.
From this time forward; hereafter.
Henceforward; from this time onward.
Henoch; the Greek spelling of Enoch or Hanoch in genealogical passages.
Hepher; chiefly a genealogical name in the tribes of Manasseh and Judah, also attached to a family or district.
Hepherites; descendants of Hepher reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Hephzi-Bah; the wife of Hezekiah, and also a symbolic name for Zion meaning My delight is in her.
The feminine objective or possessive form: her.
A herald; a public announcer or one who proclaims on behalf of authority.
Herb; a green plant, especially one used for food or healing.
Plural form of 'herb'. See 'herb'.
Herd; a company of cattle or beasts kept together.
Herdman; one who tends cattle or herds.
Herdmen; those who tend cattle or herds.
Plural form of 'herd'. See 'herd'.
In this place; at this point.
Hereafter; after this, from now on, or in time to come.
Hereby; by this means, by this thing, or in this matter.
Herein; in this, within this, or in this matter.
Hereof; of this.
Heres; the name of a mountain slope, boundary point, or district in several Old Testament passages.
Heres.note: Location: Used of more than one place; in context it may refer to a mount or border area in the hill country or toward the Jordan side.

definitions: Heresh; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Plural form of 'heresy'. See 'heresy'.
Heresy; a sect, party, or false doctrine that departs from sound truth.
Heretick; a factious or divisive person holding and spreading error.
Heretofore; before now, formerly, or until this time.
Hereunto; to this, unto this end, or for this cause.
Herewith; with this, or by means of this.
An inheritance, possession, or allotted portion received as one's own.
Plural form of 'heritage'. See 'heritage'.
Hermas; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Hermes; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Hermogenes; one of those in Asia who turned away from Paul.
Hermon; the lofty northern mountain marking the far north of the land.
Hermon.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Hermonites; people connected with the Hermon region or mountain district.
Hermonites.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Herod; the dynastic name of the rulers of the Herodian house in the New Testament.
Herodians; a party associated with the house, interests, or policy of Herod.
Herodias; the wife of Herod Philip and later of Herod Antipas, whose resentment led to John the Baptist's death.
Herodion; a kinsman of Paul greeted in the Epistle to the Romans.
Heron; the bird so called, named among unclean fowl.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
Hesed; a biblical personal name occurring in the genealogies.
Heshbon; the royal city of Sihon king of the Amorites, later held by Reuben and often named in prophecies concerning Moab.
Heshbon.note: Location: On the plateau east of the Jordan north of the Arnon, usually identified with Hesban in modern Jordan.

definitions: Heshmon; a town in the southern inheritance of Judah.
Heshmon.note: Location: In the southern Negev of Judah, probably in the Beersheba district.

definitions: Heth; older third-person singular form of 'he'. See 'he'.
Hethlon; a place marking the entrance route named in Ezekiel's northern boundary description.
Hew; to chop, cut, or shape by cutting.
Hewed; past tense or past participial form of 'hew'. See 'hew'.
Hewer; one who cuts wood or stone.
Plural form of 'hewer'. See 'hewer'.
Heweth; older third-person singular form of 'hew'. See 'hew'.
Hewn; cut out or shaped by cutting.
Hezeki; an ancestor in the post-exilic line of Benjamin.
Hezekiah; the king of Judah so called.
Hezion; an early Syrian ruler in the line of Rezon and Ben-hadad.
Hezir; the name of priestly heads and courses in Israel.
Hezrai; one of David's mighty men the Carmelite, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Hezro.
Hezro; one of David's mighty men, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Hezrai.
Hezron; especially a grandson of Judah and the ancestor of an important family line in Israel, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hezronites; descendants of Hezron reckoned as a family in Israel, especially in Judah.
Hiddai; one of David's mighty men from the brooks of Gaash, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Hurai.
Hiddekel; the ancient name of the Tigris River.
Hiddekel.note: Location: Usually identified with Tigris River.

definitions: To conceal, cover, or keep from sight.
Also to withdraw oneself from view or notice.
Hidest; thou hidest; you hide or conceal.
Hides; conceals; keeps from view or notice.
Hiding; present participial form of 'hide', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hide'.
Hiel; the Bethelite who rebuilt Jericho at the cost of his sons.
Hierapolis; the Phrygian city near Laodicea and Colossae, mentioned in connection with the churches of the Lycus valley.
Higgaion; a musical or meditative direction, often linked with solemn sound or meditation.
High; elevated in place, rank, dignity, or degree.
Higher; more high, more exalted, or more lofty.
Highest; most high, lofty, or exalted among those compared.
In a high manner or degree.
Highminded; past tense or past participial form of 'highminded'. See 'highminded'.
The state, quality, or condition of being highnes.
Highway; a raised road, main road, or public way.
Plural form of 'highway'. See 'highway'.
Hilen; a town in the hill country of Judah assigned to the priests, probably the same place elsewhere called Holon.
Hilen.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Alin.

definitions: Hilkiah; especially the high priest in Josiah's day who found the book of the law, and also the name of other biblical men.
A hill; a raised ground smaller than a mountain.
Hills in Scripture may mark habitations, worship sites, watch-points, or cultivated ground.
Hillel; the father of Abdon the judge of Israel.
Hills; raised grounds smaller than mountains.
The masculine singular objective personal pronoun.
Himself; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to a male person.
Hin; a Hebrew liquid measure.
Hind; a female deer.
To obstruct, delay, or keep back from progress.
Hindereth; older third-person singular form of 'hinder'. See 'hinder'.
Hindermost; farthest behind, rearmost, or last.
Hindmost; farthest behind, rearmost, or last.
Plural form of 'hind'. See 'hind'.
Plural form of 'hinge'. See 'hinge'.
Hinnom; the valley of Hinnom near Jerusalem, later associated with abomination and judgment.
Hip; the joint or side of the body at the upper thigh.
Hirah; the Adullamite friend and companion of Judah.
Hiram; the name of men in Scripture, especially the king of Tyre and also the craftsman who worked for Solomon.
Hire; wages, reward, or payment for service.
Hired; past tense or past participial form of 'hire'. See 'hire'.
A hireling; one who serves for wages.
The word often carries the sense of a hired servant whose care is measured by pay and not by faithful ownership.
Plural form of 'hire'. See 'hire'.
Hirest; older second-person singular form of 'hire', used with 'thou'. See 'hire'.
The masculine possessive form: belonging to him.
a sharp sibilant sound; also to make such a sound or deride.
Hissing; present participial form of 'hiss', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hiss'.
Hit; to strike or smite.
To this place; here.
Up to this time; until now; thus far.
Hittite; a member of the Hittite people.
Hittites; a people of Canaan, also represented in notable individuals such as Uriah the Hittite.
Hivite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hiv in Scripture.
Hivites; one of the peoples of Canaan named among the older inhabitants of the land.
Hizkiah; a man named among the returned exiles or covenant community.
Hizkijah; the name of men in post-exilic and Levitical records.
An exclamation expressing feeling or calling attention.
Hoar; gray or white with age or frost.
Hoarfrost; white frost formed from frozen vapor.
Hoary; gray or white with age, especially of hair, or ancient by extension.
Hobab; a Midianite relative of Moses associated with guiding Israel in the wilderness.
Hobah; the place north of Damascus to which Abram pursued the kings after rescuing Lot.
Hobah.note: Location: North of Damascus in Syria, marking the far point of Abram's pursuit toward the upper Anti-Lebanon routes.

definitions: Hod; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Hodaiah; chiefly a post-exilic Levitical and family-head name in the restored community.
Hodaviah; chiefly a post-exilic family and leadership name in the records of return and service.
Hodesh; a woman named in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Hodevah; a variant form linked with Hodaviah, especially in the post-exilic family and Levitical records.
Hodiah; the name of men in post-exilic records, including Levites and heads of families.
Hodijah; a Levite or covenant signer named after the exile.
Hoglah; one of the daughters of Zelophehad.
Hoham; king of Hebron who joined the confederacy against Gibeon.
Hoised; raised or lifted up; an older spelling equivalent to 'hoisted'.
Holden; an older form meaning held, kept, regarded, or the past participle of hold.
Holdest; thou holdest; you hold, keep fast, consider, or maintain.
The exact force must be determined from the passage.
Holds; keeps, grasps, contains, or maintains.
The exact force must be determined from the passage.
Holding; present participial form of 'hold', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hold'.
Plural form of 'hold'. See 'hold'.
Hole; an opening, hollow, or cavity.
Plural form of 'hole'. See 'hole'.
Holier; more holy.
Holiest; older second-person singular form of 'holy', used with 'thou'. See 'holy'.
In a holi manner or degree.
The state of being holy, set apart, and pure before God.
Hollow; empty within, sunken, or insincere.
Holon; a priestly town in the hill country of Judah.
Holpen; an older past participle of help.
Holy; set apart unto God, pure, sacred, and morally clean.
Holyday; a feast day or sacred observance.
Homam; a Horite chief in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same chief elsewhere written Hemam.
Home; one’s house, dwelling, or native place.
Homeborn; born in the house or land, not foreign.
Homer; a large Hebrew dry measure.
Plural form of 'homer'. See 'homer'.
Honorable, decent, or worthy of respect.
Often carrying the sense of morally seemly as well as truthful.
In a honest manner or degree.
Honesty; honorable conduct, decency, or uprightness.
Honey; the sweet substance made by bees, or sweetness of a similar kind.
Honeycomb; the comb containing honey.
Worthy of honor; held in esteem; dignified.
Honoured; past tense or past participial form of 'honour'. See 'honour'.
Honourest; older second-person singular form of 'honour', used with 'thou'. See 'honour'.
Honours; gives esteem, respect, or due value to.
Plural form of 'honour'. See 'honour'.
Plural form of 'hood'. See 'hood'.
Hoof; the hard foot of certain beasts.
Plural form of 'hoof'. See 'hoof'.
A hook is a curved instrument for catching, fastening, hanging, or drawing.
Plural form of 'hook'. See 'hook'.
Confident expectation of good; a looking forward with trust.
Hoped; past tense or past participial form of 'hope'. See 'hope'.
Hopes; expects with desire and confidence.
Hophni; the wicked son of Eli the priest, slain in the battle in which the ark was taken.
Hoping; present participial form of 'hope', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hope'.
Hor; the mountain on which Aaron died, though the name may refer to more than one height in Scripture.
Hor.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jabal Harun near Petra, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hor-Hagidgad; a wilderness encampment in Israel's journey, corresponding to Gudgodah in Deuteronomy.
Hor-hagidgad.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Khadakhid, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Horam; the name of kings and rulers in Scripture, including the king of Gezer and a king of the Zidonians.
Horeb; the mountain of God associated with Moses and Elijah.
It is closely related to Sinai and may denote the same mountain or the surrounding range.
Horeb.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Jebel Musa.

definitions: Horem; a town in the inheritance of Naphtali.
Horem.note: Location: In Upper Galilee within the territory of Naphtali.

definitions: Hori; the name of men in the genealogies, especially among the descendants of Seir.
Horims; the Horite people who dwelt in Mount Seir before Edom possessed it.
Horite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hor in Scripture.
Horites; the earlier inhabitants of Mount Seir before Edom became dominant there.
Hormah; the southern city associated with Israel's defeat in the wilderness and later with victory over the Canaanites.
Hormah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Masos.

definitions: Horn; the horn of an animal, and figuratively strength, power, or exaltation.
Hornet; the stinging insect so called.
Plural form of 'hornet'. See 'hornet'.
Plural form of 'horn'. See 'horn'.
Horonaim; a Moabite city often named in prophetic lament over Moab's downfall.
Horonaim.note: Location: Often identified with a site south of the Arnon, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Horonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Horon in Scripture.
Horrible; dreadful, shocking, or causing horror.
In a horrib manner or degree.
Horror; great fear, dread, or trembling astonishment.
Horse; the animal so called, often associated with war, strength, and swiftness.
Horseback; on the back of a horse.
Plural form of 'horsehoof'. See 'horsehoof'.
Horseleach; the bloodsucking leech used proverbially in Proverbs.
Horseman; one riding a horse, especially in war.
Horsemen; men riding horses, especially as mounted warriors or messengers.
Horses; the animals so called, often used for riding, chariots, or war.
Hosah; a Levite porter appointed to gatekeeping service.
Hosanna; a cry meaning Save now, later used as a shout of praise and acclamation.
Hosea; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also the earlier name of Joshua the son of Nun before Moses called him Jehoshua.
Hosen; stockings or leggings covering the legs.
Hoshaiah; chiefly a leader in the days of Jeremiah, though the name is borne by more than one man.
Hoshama; one of the sons of Jeconiah in the royal line.
Hoshea; the last king of Israel, and also the earlier name of Joshua and of other biblical men.
generous reception and care of guests or strangers.
Host; an army, a great company, the host of heaven, or one who receives guests.
Plural form of 'hostage'. See 'hostage'.
Hosts; armies, great companies, or multitudes.
Hot; full of heat, fervent, or vehement.
Hotham; an Asherite ancestor named in the tribal genealogies, elsewhere connected with the form Helem.
Hothan; the father of Shama and Jeiel in the tribe of Asher.
Hothir; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
In a hot manner or degree.
Hottest; older second-person singular form of 'hot', used with 'thou'. See 'hot'.
Hough; to hamstring, especially horses.
Houghed; past tense or past participial form of 'hough'. See 'hough'.
An hour; a measured part of the day, or a particular time or season.
Plural form of 'hour'. See 'hour'.
House; a dwelling, household, family line, dynasty, or temple.
All belonging to one house or family, including servants and dependents.
The master or head of a household.
Plural form of 'household'. See 'household'.
Houses; dwellings, households, family lines, or dynasties.
Housetop; the flat roof of a house.
Plural form of 'housetop'. See 'housetop'.
In what manner; to what degree; for what reason.
Nevertheless; yet; notwithstanding.
Howl; to cry aloud in pain, grief, or desolation.
Howled; past tense or past participial form of 'howl'. See 'howl'.
Howling; present participial form of 'howl', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'howl'.
Plural form of 'howling'. See 'howling'.
Howsoever; in whatever way, however.
Huge; very great in size.
Hukkok; a border town of Naphtali on the line toward Zebulun and Asher.
Hukkok.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Jemeijimeh.

definitions: Hukok; another biblical spelling of Hukkok, the Naphtalite border town.
Hukok.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Regev.

definitions: Hul; a son of Aram in the early post-Flood genealogies.
Huldah; the prophetess consulted in the days of Josiah concerning the book of the law.
Lowly in mind; not proud or self-exalting.
As a verb, to bring low or cause to be lowly.
Humbledst; means thou didst humble.
The state, quality, or condition of being humblenes.
In a humb manner or degree.
the act or state of humbling oneself; abasement.
Lowliness of mind; a modest and submissive temper.
Humtah; a town in the hill country of Judah near Hebron.
Humtah.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Tel Rumeida.

definitions: The number one hundred.
Hundredfold; a hundred times as much.
Plural form of 'hundred'. See 'hundred'.
Hundredth; the one-hundredth part, or the ordinal number one hundredth.
Lack of food; hunger.
Also to hunger, that is, to long or crave.
Hungerbitten; wasted or pinched by hunger.
Hungered; past tense or past participial form of 'hunger'. See 'hunger'.
Hungred; past tense or past participial form of 'hunger'. See 'hunger'.
Hungry; feeling hunger, or figuratively longing eagerly.
Hunt; to pursue animals or persons in order to catch them.
Hunted; past tense or past participial form of 'hunt'. See 'hunt'.
Hunter; one who hunts.
Plural form of 'hunter'. See 'hunter'.
Huntest; older second-person singular form of 'hunt', used with 'thou'. See 'hunt'.
Hunteth; older third-person singular form of 'hunt'. See 'hunt'.
Hunting; present participial form of 'hunt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hunt'.
Hupham; a Benjamite descendant whose name also stands behind the Huphamite clan.
Huphamites; descendants of Hupham reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Huppah; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Huppim; a Benjamite descendant whose name also stands behind the Huppimite clan.
Hur; the man who upheld Moses' hands with Aaron during the battle with Amalek, and also the name of other biblical men.
Hurai; one of David's mighty men from the brooks of Gaash, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Hiddai.
Huram; the name-form used especially of the king of Tyre and of the skilled workman employed by Solomon, corresponding to Hiram.
Huri; the father of Shaphat, prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Hurl; to cast or throw with force.
Hurleth; older third-person singular form of 'hurl'. See 'hurl'.
Hurling; present participial form of 'hurl', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hurl'.
To hurt is to injure, harm, or wound; hurt is injury or harm.
Hurtful; harmful, damaging, or injurious.
Hurting; present participial form of 'hurt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'hurt'.
A husband; a married man in relation to his wife.
A husbandman; a cultivator of the ground, especially a farmer or vine-dresser.
The term regularly belongs to agricultural life and is often used figuratively in parables.
Husbandmen; cultivators of the ground, especially farmers or vine-dressers.
Husbandry; cultivation of land, farming, or by figure God’s tillage.
Plural form of 'husband'. See 'husband'.
Hushah; a Judahite place-name remembered especially through Hushai the Archite's family background in later tradition.
Hushai; David's friend and counsellor, whose counsel helped frustrate Ahithophel during Absalom's rebellion.
Husham; the Edomite king who reigned after Bela.
Hushathite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Hushath in Scripture.
Hushathite.note: Location: Often identified with Husan, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Hushim; the name of persons in Scripture, including a son of Dan and a wife in the house of Shaharaim.
A husk is the outer covering of grain or fruit.
Plural form of 'husk'. See 'husk'.
Huz; a son of Nahor, and also a name associated with the land of Job.
Huzzab; a place or royal quarter named in Nahum's prophecy over the fall of Nineveh, though the exact reference remains uncertain.
Hymenaeus; a false teacher opposed by Paul in the Pastoral Epistles.
A hymn; a song of praise, especially directed unto God.
Hymns; songs of praise, especially directed unto God.
Plural form of 'hypocrisy'. See 'hypocrisy'.
Dissimulation in religion or morals; a false outward show concealing the inward reality.
Hypocrite; one who pretends to piety, goodness, or sincerity while concealing another heart.
Plural form of 'hypocrite'. See 'hypocrite'.
marked by pretense or hypocrisy.
Hyssop; the small plant used in sprinkling and ceremonial cleansing.
The first-person singular personal pronoun.
I-Chabod; the name given at the fall of Eli's house, meaning the glory is departed.
Ibhar; one of the sons of David born in Jerusalem.
Ibleam; a town in Manasseh's territory, later connected with royal flight and conflict in the days of Jehu.
Ibleam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Belameh.

definitions: Ibneiah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ibnijah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ibri; a Merarite name in the Levitical genealogies.
Ibzan; the judge of Israel from Bethlehem.
Ice; frozen water.
Iconium; the city of Lycaonia where Paul and Barnabas preached and faced strong opposition.
It is associated with preaching, division, and persecution in the book of Acts.
Iconium.note: Location: In the central plateau of Asia Minor, commonly identified with modern Konya in Turkey.

definitions: Idalah; a town in the inheritance of Zebulun named among its settlements.
Idalah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet el Huwarah.

definitions: Idbash; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Iddo; a prophet or seer, and also the name of heads of families and priestly lines.
Idle; inactive, vain, unprofitable, or empty.
The state, quality, or condition of being idlenes.
Idol; an image or false god worshipped in place of the true God.
Idolater; one who worships idols or gives religious honour to false gods.
Plural form of 'idolater'. See 'idolater'.
Plural form of 'idolatry'. See 'idolatry'.
Idolatrous; given to idolatry or marked by false worship.
Idolatry; the worship of idols or false gods, and by extension the giving of devotion to what belongs to God alone.
Idols; images or false gods worshipped in place of the true God.
Idumaea; the Greek name for Edom, the southern territory associated with Esau's descendants.
Idumaea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Idumea; the later Greek and Roman name for Edom, the southern land tied to Esau's line.
Idumea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Buseira.

definitions: If; on condition that; whether.
Igal; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men and one of the spies.
Igdaliah; the father of Hanan in the days of Jeremiah.
Igeal; a name borne by one of David's mighty men.
Ignominy; public shame, disgrace, or reproach.
Lack of knowledge; unawareness; uninstructed condition.
Lacking knowledge; uninstructed in the matter at hand.
Not necessarily stupid, but uninformed.
In a ignorant manner or degree.
Iim; another biblical spelling for Ije-abarim, the wilderness station on Israel's eastern route toward Moab.
Iim.note: Location: Often identified with Deir al Ghawi.

definitions: Ije-Abarim; a wilderness encampment east of the Jordan on the route toward Moab.
Ije-abarim.note: Location: Often identified with Medeineh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Ijon; a northern town taken in the Aramean and Assyrian invasions of Israel.
Ijon.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Dibbine.

definitions: Ikkesh; the father of Ira, one of David's mighty men.
Ilai; one of David's mighty men, the Ahohite, in a parallel place elsewhere called Zalmon.
Ill; evil, sick, harmful, or badly.
Illuminated; past tense or past participial form of 'illuminate'. See 'illuminate'.
Illyricum; the region northwest of Macedonia marking the far reach of Paul's preaching journey.
Illyricum.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Salona.

definitions: A likeness, representation, or formed resemblance.
At times an idol or graven representation.
Imagery; carved or pictured images, especially idolatrous representations, or figurative image-work by context.
a device, inward thought, or forming of ideas in the mind.
Plural form of 'imagination'. See 'imagination'.
Imagine; to devise in thought, purpose, or inward meditation, whether good or evil.
Imagined; past tense or past participial form of 'imagine'. See 'imagine'.
Imagineth; older third-person singular form of 'imagine'. See 'imagine'.
Imla; a name-form used for Imlah, the father of the prophet Micaiah.
Imlah; father of Micaiah the prophet who spoke before Ahab and Jehoshaphat.
Immanuel; a prophetic name meaning God with us, pointing to the promised presence of God with His people and applied to Christ.
Immediately; without delay; at once.
Immer; the name of a priestly family and its ancestral head.
not subject to death; also one who is deathless.
Freedom from death; endless life and incorruption.
unchangeableness.
Immutable; unchangeable, fixed, or not subject to alteration.
Imna; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the lines of Asher and Levite service.
Imnah; an Asherite ancestor and also the name of a Levite officer in the days of Hezekiah and other biblical men.
Impart; to give, communicate, or share with another.
Imparted; past tense or past participial form of 'impart'. See 'impart'.
a hindrance, obstacle, or obstruction.
Impenitent; not repentant, unremorseful, or hardened in sin.
Imperious; commanding, overbearing, or acting with lordly authority.
Implacable; not to be appeased, relentless, or unforgiving.
Implead; to bring into judgment, accuse, or sue before a court.
persistent urgent request or pressing solicitation.
Impose; to lay upon, place as a burden, or enjoin.
Imposed; past tense or past participial form of 'impose'. See 'impose'.
Impossible; not able to be done, attained, or brought about.
Impotent; weak, powerless, or unable to act effectively.
Impoverish; to make poor or reduce in substance.
Impoverished; past tense or past participial form of 'impoverish'. See 'impoverish'.
Imprisoned; past tense or past participial form of 'imprison'. See 'imprison'.
confinement in prison or captivity.
Plural form of 'imprisonment'. See 'imprisonment'.
Impudent; shamelessly bold, insolent, or disrespectful.
To reckon, attribute, or set to one's account.
Imputing; present participial form of 'impute', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'impute'.
Imrah; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Imri; the name of men in post-exilic and tribal records.
In; within, into, among, or in relation to.
In so far as; seeing that; because.
Incense; a fragrant substance burned in sacred service.
It may also figure prayer or acceptable worship rising before God.
Incensed; past tense or past participial form of 'incense'. See 'incense'.
Incline; to bend, turn, stoop, or dispose the heart toward something.
Inclined; past tense or past participial form of 'incline'. See 'incline'.
Inclineth; older third-person singular form of 'incline'. See 'incline'.
Inclose; to enclose, shut in, or surround.
Inclosed; past tense or past participial form of 'inclose'. See 'inclose'.
Plural form of 'inclosing'. See 'inclosing'.
Incontinency; lack of self-restraint, especially in sensual desire.
Incontinent; lacking self-restraint, especially in passions or appetite.
Incorruptible; not subject to decay, corruption, or moral bribery.
freedom from decay or corruption.
Growth, multiplication, or addition.
As a verb, to make or become greater.
Increasest; older second-person singular form of 'increase', used with 'thou'. See 'increase'.
Increasing; present participial form of 'increase', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'increase'.
Incredible; hard to believe, astonishing, or beyond common expectation.
Incurable; not able to be healed or remedied.
Indebted; past tense or past participial form of 'indebted'. See 'indebted'.
In truth; truly; really.
India; the far eastern land named in Esther as the extent of the Persian dominion.
India.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Indus River.

definitions: Strong displeasure or anger stirred by what is wrong.
Inditing; present participial form of 'indite', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'indite'.
Industrious; diligent, busy, and steady in labor.
Inexcusable; without excuse or just defense.
Infallible; not liable to fail, err, or deceive.
Infamous; of evil repute, disgraceful, or notorious for shame.
Infamy; disgrace, dishonour, or evil repute.
A very young child; a babe.
Of or relating to infancy or an early stage of growth.
Plural form of 'infant'. See 'infant'.
Inferior; lower in rank, place, or quality.
As a noun, one lower in station.
Infidel; an unbeliever, especially one without faith in the true God or in the Christian confession.
As an adjective, unbelieving.
Infinite; without limit or bound; immeasurable or unending.
Weakness, frailty, or feebleness, whether bodily or moral.
Inflame; to set on fire, kindle, or stir up strongly.
kindling, swelling, or intense heat in a body or matter.
Inflicted; past tense or past participial form of 'inflict'. See 'inflict'.
Plural form of 'influence'. See 'influence'.
Infolding; present participial form of 'infold', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'infold'.
Inform; to give knowledge, report, or make known.
Informed; past tense or past participial form of 'inform'. See 'inform'.
Ingathering; present participial form of 'ingather', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'ingather'.
To dwell in, occupy, or live within.
One who dwells in a place; a resident.
Plural form of 'inhabiter'. See 'inhabiter'.
Inhabitest; older second-person singular form of 'inhabit', used with 'thou'. See 'inhabit'.
Inhabiteth; older third-person singular form of 'inhabit'. See 'inhabit'.
Inhabiting; present participial form of 'inhabit', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'inhabit'.
That which is received as a possession, lot, or heritage.
Often an allotted possession from God or by family right.
Plural form of 'inheritance'. See 'inheritance'.
Inherited; past tense or past participial form of 'inherit'. See 'inherit'.
Inheriteth; older third-person singular form of 'inherit'. See 'inherit'.
Inheritor; one who inherits or receives an inheritance.
Moral crookedness, unrighteousness, or guilt.
Often wickedness considered in its perverse and blameworthy character.
Injured; past tense or past participial form of 'injure'. See 'injure'.
Harmful, unjust, or abusive.
Injustice; wrong, unfair dealing, or unrighteous judgment.
Ink; the liquid used for writing.
Inkhorn; a small vessel or case for holding ink.
Inn; a lodging place for travellers, a place of temporary shelter or entertainment on a journey.
Inner; inward, within, or more hidden from the outer part.
Innermost; farthest within or most inward.
Innocency; innocence, cleanness from guilt, or uprightness of conduct.
Free from guilt, sin, or blame.
Harmless or without evil intent.
Innocents; innocent persons.
Too many to be counted; countless.
Inordinate; excessive, immoderate, or disorderly beyond proper bounds.
search, inquiry, or careful investigation.
words written or engraved on something.
Inside; within the inner part or interior of something.
Insomuch; to such a degree, so that, or inasmuch as the matter extends so far.
divine influence communicating truth; also the breathing in of air.
Urgent, earnest, or pressing in action or request.
Sometimes ready at hand or immediately present, but often carrying the sense of persistence and earnestness.
Earnestly; urgently; with pressing persistence.
Sometimes also without delay, but not limited to that.
Instead; in place of, in exchange for, or as a substitute.
Instruct; to teach, direct, or furnish with understanding.
Instructed; past tense or past participial form of 'instruct'. See 'instruct'.
Instructer; one who teaches or gives direction; an older spelling of 'instructor'.
Plural form of 'instructer'. See 'instructer'.
Instructing; present participial form of 'instruct', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'instruct'.
Teaching, training, and discipline imparted for guidance.
Instructor; one who teaches, directs, or gives knowledge.
a tool, implement, document, or means by which something is done.
Plural form of 'instrument'. See 'instrument'.
a revolt or uprising against authority.
uprightness, honesty, or wholeness of character.
understanding, knowledge, or news received.
Intend; to purpose, mean, or have in design.
Intended; past tense or past participial form of 'intend'. See 'intend'.
Intendest; older second-person singular form of 'intend', used with 'thou'. See 'intend'.
Intending; present participial form of 'intend', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'intend'.
Closely attentive, fixed, or earnest in mind.
Purpose, design, or intention.
Plural form of 'intent'. See 'intent'.
Pleading on behalf of another; mediation by prayer or request.
Plural form of 'intercession'. See 'intercession'.
Intercessor; one who intercedes or pleads on behalf of another.
Intermeddle; to meddle in a matter, mix oneself in, or become involved.
Intermeddleth; older third-person singular form of 'intermeddle'. See 'intermeddle'.
a pause or temporary cessation.
Interpret; to explain, expound, or make known the meaning of words, dreams, or signs.
The declaring or unfolding of the meaning of a thing.
Often the explanation of a dream, vision, saying, or tongue.
Interpreted; past tense or past participial form of 'interpret'. See 'interpret'.
Interpreter; one who explains, translates, or makes another's speech understood.
Interpreting; present participial form of 'interpret', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'interpret'.
Into; from without to within; toward entrance or change.
Intreat; to entreat, ask earnestly, deal with, or treat.
Intreated; entreated, asked earnestly, dealt with, or treated.
Plural form of 'intreaty'. See 'intreaty'.
Intreaty; earnest request, entreaty, or supplication.
Intruding; present participial form of 'intrude', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'intrude'.
Invade; to enter by force, assail, or break in upon.
Invaded; past tense or past participial form of 'invade'. See 'invade'.
a hostile entrance into a land or place.
To devise, contrive, or frame in thought.
Invented; past tense or past participial form of 'invent'. See 'invent'.
Plural form of 'invention'. See 'invention'.
Plural form of 'inventor'. See 'inventor'.
Not seen or not visible to the eye.
Invited; past tense or past participial form of 'invite'. See 'invite'.
Within; on the inside; turned toward the inner part.
Also pertaining to the inner man, hidden thought, or secret affection.
In a inward manner or degree.
The inward parts, especially the entrails or inner organs.
Iphedeiah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ir; a son of Bela in the Benjamite genealogies.
Ir-Nahash; a Judahite place-name in the genealogies, probably a town near the Bethlehem region.
Ir-nahash.note: Location: Often identified with Dayr Nakhkhas.

definitions: Ir-Shemesh; another name for Beth-Shemesh, the border city of Judah and Dan.
Ir-shemesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Bet Shemesh.

definitions: Ira; one of David's priests and mighty men, and also the name of other biblical men.
Irad; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Iram; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Iri; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Irijah; the captain who arrested Jeremiah at the gate of Benjamin.
Iron; the metal so called.
Plural form of 'iron'. See 'iron'.
Irpeel; a town in Benjamin's inheritance named in the tribal border list.
Iru; a son of Caleb in the genealogies of Judah.
Is; the third-person singular present of be.
Isaac; the son of Abraham and father of Jacob.
Isaiah; son of Amoz, the major prophet of Judah through whom many great prophecies of judgment and Messiah were given.
Iscah; the daughter of Haran in the family of Abram.
Iscariot; a surname or identifying designation, used most notably of Judas Iscariot and commonly linked with Kerioth.
Ish-bosheth; son of Saul who reigned over Israel for a time after Saul's death.
Ish-Tob; the district or land of Tob from which Jephthah later drew fighting men.
Ish-tob.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tayibeh.

definitions: Ishbah; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Ishbak; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Ishbi-Benob; a giant of the Philistines who sought to slay David.
Ishi; chiefly a tribal genealogical name in the records of Israel's families.
Ishiah; chiefly a Levitical and Issacharite genealogical name.
Ishijah; the name of men in Scripture, including Levites and descendants in the line of Issachar.
Ishma; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Ishmael; the son of Abraham by Hagar, and also the name of other biblical men.
Ishmaelite; a descendant of Ishmael, or a member of the Ishmaelite people.
Ishmaelites; the descendants of Ishmael, often appearing as desert traders and nomadic peoples.
Ishmaiah; chiefly the Gibeonite leader who came to David, though borne by more than one man.
Ishmeelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Ishmeel in Scripture.
Ishmeelites; the descendants of Ishmael; an older spelling variant of 'Ishmaelites'.
Ishmerai; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Ishod; a descendant in the line of Manasseh.
Ishpan; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Ishuah; a son of Asher named among the tribal ancestors.
Ishuai; a name borne by a son of Asher and by the family descending from him.
Ishui; a name in the house of Saul and in the tribe of Asher, used of different persons in Scripture.
Island; a tract of land surrounded by water, and in some passages a distant coastland or maritime region.
Plural form of 'island'. See 'island'.
An isle; an island, or sometimes a coastland lying across the sea.
In prophetic or poetic passages it may point broadly to distant maritime lands.
Isles; islands or distant coastlands.
In Scripture the word often reaches beyond small islands to far maritime lands and peoples.
Ismachiah; a Levite appointed under Hezekiah in matters of offerings and dedicated things.
Ismaiah; chiefly a Zebulunite officer and one of David's mighty men, though borne by more than one man.
Ispah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Israel; the patriarch Jacob, the covenant people descended from him, or the kingdom bearing that name.
Israel.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Israelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Israel in Scripture.
Israelites; the covenant people descended from Jacob, the nation of Israel.
Israelitish; belonging to the Israelites or to Israel.
Issachar; chiefly the son of Jacob and the tribe descended from him, though the name also belongs to others in Scripture.
Isshiah; a Levite or family head named in the genealogies, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Jesiah.
Issue; a coming forth, offspring, result, or bodily discharge.
As a verb, to go out or proceed.
A discharge or continual flow of blood.
Issued; came forth, went out, proceeded, or was discharged.
Issues; comings forth, offspring, results, or discharges.
Isuah; the spelling used for Ishuah, a son of Asher named among the tribal ancestors.
Isui; an Asherite name in the tribal records, corresponding to the forms Ishuai or Jesui.
The neuter singular personal pronoun.
Italian; belonging to Italy or an Italian person.
Italy; the land of the Romans in the New Testament setting, especially associated with Rome and those from there.
Italy.note: Location: The Italian peninsula centered on Rome.

definitions: Itch; an irritating sensation that causes desire to scratch.
As a verb, to feel such irritation or figuratively to have an eager craving.
Itching; present participial form of 'itch', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'itch'.
Ithai; a name used of notable men, including David's loyal Gittite warrior; it corresponds to the form Ittai.
Ithamar; a man named in Scripture, son of Aaron.
Ithiel; a name in Proverbs associated with Agur's sayings, and also a post-exilic name.
Ithmah; one of David's mighty men, called the Moabite.
Ithnan; a southern town of Judah in the Negev lists.
Ithnan.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Mesudat Har Hemet.

definitions: Ithra; the father of Amasa, called an Ishmaelite or Israelite in parallel accounts.
Ithran; the name of men in the genealogies, especially among Seir and Asher.
Ithream; a son of David by Eglah.
Ithrite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Ithr in Scripture.
Ithrite.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Attir.

definitions: Ithrites; a clan or family group named among David's mighty men.
The neuter possessive form: belonging to it.
Itself; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to a thing.
Ittah-Kazin; a border place in Zebulun's inheritance near the Galilean uplands.
Ittah-kazin.note: Location: Often identified with Kefr Kenna.

definitions: Ittai; especially the Gittite who remained faithful to David in his flight, and also other men of the name.
Ituraea; a district northeast of Galilee, ruled with neighboring lands in the time mentioned by Luke.
Ituraea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Ivah; a city named with Hamath and other places conquered by Assyria.
Ivah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Kafr Aya.

definitions: The hard white material of an elephant's tusk or similar tooth.
Made of ivory or resembling it in whiteness.
Izehar; the spelling used for Izhar, a son of Kohath in the Levitical line.
Izeharites; Levite descendants of Izhar; a spelling variant of 'Izharites'.
Izhar; a son of Kohath and ancestor in an important Levitical branch.
Izharites; descendants of Izhar reckoned among the Levitical families.
Izrahiah; a descendant of Issachar in the tribal genealogies.
Izrahite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Izrah in Scripture.
Izri; one of the sons of Jeduthun, over the course of temple singers appointed for sacred music.
Jaakan; a wilderness station remembered in the phrase Beeroth of the children of Jaakan during Israel's journey.
Jaakobah; a Simeonite name in the genealogies.
Jaala; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Jaalah; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Jaala.
Jaalam; a son of Esau by Aholibamah.
Jaanai; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Jaare-Oregim; a personal name occurring in the Davidic battle records.
Jaasau; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jaasiel; the name of men in Scripture, including one associated with David's officers.
Jaazaniah; chiefly the name of leaders seen in Ezekiel's visions and of a Rechabite in Jeremiah's history.
Jaazer; another biblical spelling for Jazer, the important Transjordan city known for pastureland and vineyards.
Jaazer.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Sara.

definitions: Jaaziah; a Merarite name in the Levitical records.
Jaaziel; a name borne by men in Davidic and Levitical lists, corresponding in some records to Aziel.
Jabal; the early ancestor of those who dwell in tents and keep cattle.
Jabbok; the river where Jacob wrestled before meeting Esau.
Jabbok.note: Location: Usually identified with Zarqa River.

definitions: Jabesh; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place Jabesh-gilead.
Jabesh-Gilead; the Gileadite town whose people were rescued by Saul and later recovered his body from Beth-Shan.
Jabesh-gilead.note: Location: Often identified with Tell el Maqlub.

definitions: Jabez; the Judahite whose prayer for blessing and enlargement is specially remembered.
Jabin; the royal name of Canaanite kings, especially the king of Hazor in the days of Deborah and Barak.
Jabneel; the name of a border town in Judah and also a northern town in Naphtali in the allotment lists.
Jabneh; another biblical spelling for Jabneel, especially the coastal town later known as Jamnia.
Jachan; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Jachin; the right-hand pillar of Solomon's temple, and also the name of persons and a family in Israel.
Jachinites; descendants of Jachin reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Jacinth; a precious stone or deep bluish-purple colour named in apocalyptic description.
Jacob; the patriarch afterward called Israel, and by extension the people descended from him.
Jada; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jadau; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jaddua; the name of men in Scripture, especially a high priest in post-exilic times.
Jadon; one who helped in the rebuilding of Jerusalem in the days of Nehemiah.
Jael; the wife of Heber the Kenite who slew Sisera.
Jagur; a southern town of Judah named among the Negev settlements.
Jagur.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Ira.

definitions: Jah; a shortened divine name of the LORD, used especially in praise.
Jahath; chiefly a Levitical genealogical and temple-service name.
Jahaz; the city where Israel defeated Sihon king of the Amorites, later associated with Reuben and Moab.
Jahaz.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahazah; another biblical spelling for Jahaz, the city of Israel's victory over Sihon.
Jahazah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahaziah; the name of men in Scripture, including one who spoke in Ezra's assembly.
Jahaziel; the Levite upon whom the Spirit came in Jehoshaphat's day, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jahdai; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jahdiel; a name in tribal and pastoral records, including one over David's flocks.
Jahdo; the father of Obed, associated with the half tribe of Manasseh.
Jahleel; a son of Zebulun from whom the Jahleelites were reckoned.
Jahleelites; descendants of Jahleel reckoned among the families of Zebulun.
Jahmai; a descendant of Tola in the line of Issachar.
Jahzah; another biblical spelling for Jahaz, the city where Sihon was defeated.
Jahzah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Jahzeel; a son of Naphtali whose descendants formed a clan in Israel, also written Jahziel.
Jahzeelites; descendants of Jahzeel reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Jahzerah; a priestly name in the line of Aaron.
Jahziel; the spelling used for Jahzeel, a son of Naphtali and ancestor of a clan.
Jailor; keeper of a prison, a gaoler.
Jair; the judge of Israel and an ancestor linked with Jair's settlements in Gilead, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jairite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Jair in Scripture.
Jairus; the ruler of the synagogue whose daughter Jesus raised from the dead.
Jakan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Akan.
Jakeh; the father of Agur in Proverbs 30.
Jakim; a name in the genealogies, especially in the line of Simeon.
Jalon; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jambres; one of the names Paul gives for the men who withstood Moses.
James; chiefly the New Testament name of James the son of Zebedee, James the son of Alphaeus, and James the Lord's brother.
Jamin; the name of men in Scripture, especially sons of Simeon and descendants in priestly or tribal lists.
Jaminites; descendants of Jamin reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Jamlech; a Simeonite prince in the tribal records.
Jangling; idle, contentious, or empty talk; also the act of making a harsh clattering sound.
Janna; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jannes; one of the names Paul gives for the men who withstood Moses.
Janoah; a town on the border of Ephraim, also named among places taken by Assyria.
Janoah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Yanun.

definitions: Janohah; another biblical spelling for Janoah, the border town of Ephraim.
Janohah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Yanun.

definitions: Janum; a hill-country town of Judah named among its settlements.
Janum.note: Location: Often identified with Bani Nai’im.

definitions: Japheth; one of Noah's three sons, ancestor of several nations listed in Genesis.
Japhia; the name of men in Scripture, including a Canaanite king defeated in Joshua's day.
Japhlet; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Japhleti; one belonging to the house or clan descended from Japhlet, named among the family divisions in Israel.
Japho; the ancient port later called Joppa, from which cedar timber came and from which Jonah fled.
Jarah; a descendant in Saul's Benjamite line, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Jehoadah.
Jareb; a prophetic title for the king of Assyria, probably carrying the sense of an adversary or avenger.
Jared; past tense or past participial form of 'jar'. See 'jar'.
Jaresiah; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jarha; the Egyptian servant who became son-in-law to Sheshan.
Jarib; chiefly a priestly and post-exilic family-head name.
Jarmuth; the royal city of a Canaanite king defeated by Joshua, also the name of a Judahite town in later lists.
Jarmuth.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yarmuth.

definitions: Jaroah; a Gadite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Jashen; a family designation connected with David's mighty men, likely represented elsewhere by the form Hashem.
Jasher; a personal name distinct from the title of the book of Jasher.
Jashobeam; one of David's chief mighty men and captain over the first course.
Jashub; a son of Issachar and ancestor of a clan, elsewhere represented by the form Job.
Jashubi-Lehem; a Judahite place-name preserved in the genealogies, probably linked with the Bethlehem district.
Jashubites; descendants of Jashub reckoned among the families of Issachar.
Jasiel; one of David's mighty men.
Jason; the Thessalonian host who received Paul and suffered on his account.
Jasper; a precious stone used in descriptions of beauty and heavenly glory.
Jathniel; the son of Meshelemiah in the records of gatekeepers.
Jattir; a priestly town in the hill country of Judah to which David sent spoil after defeating Amalek.
Jattir.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Attir.

definitions: Javan; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with the Greek or Ionian peoples.
Javelin; a light spear or dart used in war.
Jaw; the bone or part of the mouth in which the teeth are set.
Jawbone; the bone of the jaw, especially used of the ass's jawbone in Samson's history.
Plural form of 'jaw'. See 'jaw'.
Jazer; an important city east of the Jordan in the territory later held by Gad, known for its surrounding pastureland and vineyards.
Jazer.note: Location: East of the Jordan north of Heshbon, commonly identified with Khirbet Jazzir in modern Jordan.

definitions: Jaziz; the Hagerite set over David's flocks.
Zealous to guard what belongs to oneself; intolerant of rivalry.
Suspicious or resentful because of another's favor or possession.
Jealousies; suspicions of rivalry, ardent zeal for exclusive right, or passionate resentments.
Jealousy; ardent zeal for exclusive right, suspicion of rivalry, or passionate resentment.
Jearim; a height or ridge associated with the border descriptions near Kirjath-jearim.
Jearim.note: Location: Near Kirjath-jearim on the Judah-Benjamin border, in the western hill country.

definitions: Jeaterai; a Levite ancestor in the line leading to Asaph, elsewhere represented by the variant Ethni.
Jeberechiah; the father of Zechariah's companion in Isaiah's sign.
the ancient name of Jerusalem before David took the stronghold.
Jebusi; another name for Jerusalem before David captured the stronghold of Zion.
Jebusite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Jebus in Scripture.
Jebusites; the older inhabitants of Jerusalem before David took the stronghold.
Jecamiah; the name of men in the genealogies, including descendants of Jeconiah and Caleb.
Jecholiah; the mother of king Uzziah, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Jecoliah.
Jechonias; the Greek name for Jeconiah or Jehoiachin.
Jecoliah; the mother of king Uzziah, also written Jecholiah.
Jeconiah; another name of Jehoiachin king of Judah.
Jedaiah; chiefly a priestly name in the post-exilic community and temple courses.
Jediael; the name of men in Scripture, including a Benjamite ancestor and one of David's mighty men.
Jedidah; the mother of Josiah king of Judah.
Jedidiah; a man named in Scripture, son of David, husband of Pharaoh, father of Taphath.
Jeduthun; especially the Levitical singer and musician appointed by David, and also the family or musical line associated with his name.
Jeezer; the spelling used for Abiezer, the Manassite clan especially associated with Gideon.
Jeezerites; descendants of Jeezer reckoned among the families of Manasseh; Jeezer is elsewhere called Abiezer.
Jegar-Sahadutha; a heap or marked pile of stones mentioned in Scripture.
Jegar-sahadutha.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Jehaleleel; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jehalelel; a Merarite or Levitical name in the temple records.
Jehdeiah; a Merarite name in the Levitical genealogies.
Jehezekel; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Jehiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites or porters connected with the ark and temple service.
Jehiel; chiefly a Levitical, princely, and administrative name borne by many men in Davidic and post-exilic records.
Jehieli; a name borne by men in Levitical and royal-administrative records; it corresponds closely to the form Jehiel.
Jehizkiah; an Ephraimite leader who opposed the cruel treatment of Judah's captives.
Jehoadah; a descendant in the line of Saul; the same figure appears under the form Jarah.
Jehoaddan; the mother of Amaziah king of Judah.
Jehoahaz; borne by kings of Israel and Judah who reigned in troubled times, and by other biblical men.
Jehoash; the name spelled elsewhere as Joash, especially for kings of Israel and Judah.
Jehohanan; the name of men in post-exilic and genealogical records.
Jehoiachin; king of Judah, son of Jehoiakim, carried away captive to Babylon.
Jehoiada; the priest who preserved Joash and restored proper worship in Judah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jehoiakim; king of Judah, son of Josiah, who reigned under Egyptian and then Babylonian pressure.
Jehoiarib; the head of the first priestly course, and also a family name among the priests.
Jehonadab; the son of Rechab who joined Jehu and became the forefather of the Rechabites, also written Jonadab.
Jehonathan; chiefly a Levitical and official name in temple and royal service.
Jehoram; the name of kings in Israel and Judah, sometimes shortened in the text to Joram.
Jehoshabeath; the daughter of king Jehoram who hid the young Joash from Athaliah, also written Jehosheba.
Jehoshaphat; especially the king of Judah, son of Asa, noted for seeking the LORD and for alliances that brought trouble.
Jehosheba; the daughter of king Jehoram who rescued and hid Joash, also written Jehoshabeath.
Jehoshua; a man named in Scripture, son of Nun.
Jehovah; an English form used for the covenant name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
Jehovah-jireh; the name Abraham gave to the place on Mount Moriah where the Lord provided a ram in place of Isaac.
Jehovah-jireh.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Mount Moriah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Jehovah-Nissi; a memorial or compound sacred name used in Scripture.
Jehovah-Shalom; a memorial or compound sacred name used in Scripture.
Jehozabad; chiefly the name of a servant involved in the death of Joash and also of Levites in temple service.
Jehozadak; the priest carried away in the captivity, father of Jeshua the high priest.
Jehu; especially the king of Israel who executed judgment on the house of Ahab, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jehubbah; a son of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Jehucal; son of Shelemiah, one of the princes sent to Jeremiah in the last days of Judah.
Jehud; a town in the territory of Dan on the western side of the central hill country.
Jehudi; the man who read Jeremiah's roll before king Jehoiakim.
Jehudijah; a woman named in the genealogies of Judah.
Jehush; chiefly a genealogical name borne by sons in the houses of David and Benjamin.
Jeiel; chiefly a widely used Levitical and genealogical name among gatekeepers, musicians, and heads of families.
Jekabzeel; another biblical spelling for Kabzeel, the southern town of Judah associated with Benaiah's family.
Jekabzeel.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Hora.

definitions: Jekameam; a Levitical name in the line of Hebron.
Jekamiah; the name of men in the royal and Judahite genealogies.
Jekuthiel; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jemima; one of the daughters of Job born after his restoration.
Jemuel; a son of Simeon, elsewhere represented by the form Nemuel.
Jeoparded; past tense or past participial form of 'jeopard'. See 'jeopard'.
Jeopardy; danger, hazard, or exposure to peril.
Jephthae; the Greek name for Jephthah the judge.
Jephthah; a judge of Israel who delivered the people from the Ammonites.
Jephunneh; best known as the father of Caleb.
Jerah; a son of Joktan in the early genealogies after the Flood.
Jerahmeel; the name of men in Scripture, especially a Judahite ancestor and a royal son in Jeremiah.
Jerahmeelites; descendants of Jerahmeel, a Judahite house in the south country.
Jered; a descendant of Judah named in the genealogies.
Jeremai; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jeremiah; the prophet of Judah who warned of coming judgment and ministered in the final days before the Babylonian fall.
Jeremias; the Greek name for Jeremiah.
Jeremoth; chiefly a Levitical, musical, and tribal-leadership name.
Jeremy; an English name-form of Jeremiah, used in the Apocryphal title of the prophet.
Jeriah; a chief of the Hebronites in the days of David.
Jeribai; one of David's mighty men.
Jericho; the city whose walls fell before Israel in Joshua, later also prominent in Gospel narratives.
Jericho.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell es Sultan.

definitions: Jeriel; a man of the tribe of Issachar in the genealogies.
Jerijah; a chief of the Hebronites in David's later administration.
Jerimoth; chiefly a princely and Levitical name, including one among David's sons.
Jerioth; a woman or family name associated with Caleb's house in the genealogies.
Jeroboam; the name of the kings of Israel so called, especially Jeroboam the son of Nebat.
Jeroham; chiefly a Levitical and genealogical name, including the father of one branch of Samuel's line.
Jerubbaal; another name of Gideon, given after the overthrow of Baal's altar.
Jerubbesheth; Gideon under a name-shape that avoids the Baal element; compare Jerubbaal.
Jeruel; the wilderness or desert district toward which Jehoshaphat's enemies advanced before their overthrow.
Jeruel.note: Location: In the wilderness east or southeast of Jerusalem toward Tekoa and the Dead Sea descent, though the exact line is uncertain.

definitions: Jerusalem; the chief city of Judah and the place of the temple, sometimes standing for its people or worship.
Jerusalem.note: Location: In the hill country of Judah, commonly identified with the historic city of Jerusalem.

definitions: Jerusha; the wife of king Uzziah and mother of Jotham, also written Jerushah.
Jerushah; the wife of king Uzziah and mother of Jotham, also written Jerusha.
Jesaiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites and descendants in Davidic lines.
Jeshaiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially among the descendants of Merari, Elam, and the royal line.
Jeshanah; a Benjaminite town captured by Abijah in his campaign against Jeroboam.
Jeshanah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Burj el Isaneh.

definitions: Jesharelah; a son of Asaph appointed in the courses of temple musicians, also written Asarelah.
Jeshebeab; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Jesher; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jeshimon; the desolate waste or wilderness tract near the Dead Sea.
Jeshimon.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Zahrat al Kula.

definitions: Jeshishai; one of the sons of Anak in the narrative of the spies.
Jeshohaiah; a Simeonite leader in the genealogies.
Jeshua; especially the high priest who stood with Zerubbabel after the return from exile, and also the name of other biblical men.
Jeshurun; a poetical title for Israel, used as an affectionate or honourable name for the covenant people.
Jesiah; the name of men in Levitical and genealogical records, corresponding in some places to Isshiah.
Jesimiel; one appointed over the sons of the king in David's administration.
Jesse; the Bethlehemite father of David, from whose line the Messiah was promised.
Jesting; present participial form of 'jest', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'jest'.
Jesui; an Asherite family name or clan designation, represented in related forms such as Isui and Ishuai.
Jesuites; descendants of Jesui reckoned among the families of Asher.
Jesurun; a spelling of Jeshurun, the poetical title for Israel.
Jesus; the personal name of the Lord Jesus Christ, and in some passages the Greek name for Joshua.
Jether; chiefly a family and military name, including kin of Gideon and members of David's wider family line.
Jetheth; an Edomite duke or chief named in the lists of Esau's descendants.
Jethlah; a town on the border of Dan.
Jethlah.note: Location: In the Shephelah or lower hill country west of Benjamin, near the Danite border.

definitions: Jethro; a man named in Scripture, father of Zipporah.
Jetur; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of the Ituraean line.
Jeuel; chiefly a Levitical and post-exilic family-head name in the return records.
Jeush; chiefly a genealogical name borne by sons in the houses of Esau, Benjamin, and Rehoboam.
Jeuz; a son in the line of Esau and another in the house of Caleb.
Jew; a member of the Jewish people, especially of the covenant nation descended from Judah or identified with the people of Israel after the exile.
Jewel; a precious ornament or gem of value and beauty.
Plural form of 'jewel'. See 'jewel'.
a Jewish woman.
Jewish; belonging to the Jews, their people, religion, or customs.
Jewish.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Jewry; the Jewish people or their region and community as described in older English.
Jewry.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: Jews; the people of Judah and later the covenant nation known by that name, especially in the post-exilic and New Testament period.
Jezaniah; the name of men in Scripture, including a captain in the troubled days after Jerusalem's fall, represented in related forms such as Jaazaniah.
Jezebel; a woman named in Scripture, daughter of Ethbaal, wife of Ahab, mother of Ahaziah.
Jezer; a son of Naphtali from whom the Jezerites were reckoned.
Jezerites; descendants of Jezer reckoned among the families of Gilead and Manasseh.
Jeziah; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Jeziel; the son of Azmaveth, associated with Saul's kindred and the Benjamites.
Jezliah; the name of a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Jezoar; one named among the sons of Asaph or temple singers in post-exilic records.
Jezrahiah; an overseer in the thanksgiving order at the dedication in Nehemiah's day.
Jezreel; a city and valley name tied to royal history, Naboth's vineyard, and prophetic judgment.
Jezreelite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Jezreel in Scripture.
Jezreelite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yizre’el.

definitions: Jezreelitess; Ahinoam, one of David's wives, so called because she was from Jezreel.
Jezreelitess.note: Location: From Jezreel; in context this usually refers to the Judean Jezreel in the southern hill country.

definitions: Jibsam; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jidlaph; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Jimna; the name of Asherite ancestors and related clan records, corresponding to the same line elsewhere written Jimnah or Imnah.
Jimnah; the name of Asherite ancestors and related family lines, corresponding to the same line elsewhere written Jimna or Imnah.
Jimnites; descendants of Jimna reckoned among the families of Asher.
Jiphtah; a town in the hill country of Judah named among its settlements.
Jiphthah-El; a valley named in the tribal boundary descriptions.
Jiphthah-el.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Wadi el Melek, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Joab; the captain of David's host.
Joah; chiefly a recorder or official name in royal service, and also a Levitical family name.
Joahaz; a son of Josiah listed in the genealogies of the kings of Judah.
Joanna; the wife of Chuza who ministered to Jesus and was a witness of the resurrection tidings.
Joash; especially the king of Judah preserved from Athaliah and later active in temple repair, and also the name of other biblical men.
Joatham; a man named in Scripture, son of Azariah, father of Ahaz.
Job; the righteous sufferer of the book of Job, famed for endurance under trial.
Jobab; chiefly the name of early kings and genealogical descendants in Genesis and allied records.
Jochebed; the mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, of the tribe of Levi.
Joed; a Benjamite named in the genealogies.
Joel; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also several other men of Israel bearing the same name.
Joelah; one of the warriors who came to David at Ziklag.
Joezer; one of the Benjamite archers who came to David.
Jogbehah; a Transjordan town built by Gad and later pursued by Gideon in the Midianite campaign.
Jogbehah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Jubeiha.

definitions: Jogli; the father of Bukki, prince of the tribe of Dan.
Joha; the name of men in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Johanan; chiefly a priestly, military, and post-exilic leadership name borne by many men in Scripture.
John; especially John the Baptist or John the apostle, though the name is borne by others.
Joiada; the name of men in Scripture, including a chief of the Aaronites in David's day.
Joiakim; the post-exilic high priest, son of Jeshua, in the days of Nehemiah.
Joiarib; the name of priestly heads and family lines in Israel.
Join; to unite, cleave to, attach oneself, or bring together.
Joined; past tense or past participial form of 'join'. See 'join'.
Joining; present participial form of 'join', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'join'.
Plural form of 'joining'. See 'joining'.
Joint; the place where parts meet, especially bones or members of the body.
Plural form of 'joint-heir'. See 'joint-heir'.
Plural form of 'joint'. See 'joint'.
Jokdeam; a town in the hill country of Judah named among its settlements.
Jokdeam.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet er Raqqa.

definitions: Jokim; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Jokmeam; a Levitical town, probably the same place elsewhere written Jokneam in the Carmel region.
Jokmeam.note: Location: Often identified with Tel al Mazar.

definitions: Jokneam; the Canaanite royal city by Carmel later assigned to the Levites.
Jokneam.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Yokneam.

definitions: Jokshan; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Joktan; a son of Eber and ancestor of a wide southern line in Genesis 10.
Joktheel; a Judahite town later renamed by Amaziah after his victory over Edom.
Joktheel.note: Location: In the Shephelah or southern hill-country region of Judah, often linked with the Lachish area.

definitions: Jona; the form used in the Gospels for the name Jonah or Jonas, especially in the patronymic of Simon Bar-jona.
Jonadab; the name of notable men in Scripture, especially the subtle son of Shimeah and Jehonadab son of Rechab.
Jonah; son of Amittai, the prophet sent to Nineveh.
Jonan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jonas; the Greek name for Jonah, and also the name used for Peter's father in the Gospels.
Jonathan; especially the son of Saul, though the name is borne by others.
Joppa; the coastal port associated with Jonah, the cedar shipments for the temple, and Peter's vision in Acts.
Joppa.note: Location: On the Mediterranean coast, commonly identified with Jaffa at modern Tel Aviv-Yafo.

definitions: Jorah; the name attached to a returning family in the exile lists; the same family appears under the form Hariph.
Jorai; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Joram; the shortened name of Jehoram, used especially of kings in Israel and Judah.
Jordan; the chief river of the land, flowing through the great valley from the north toward the Dead Sea.
The name often marks a boundary or crossing-place in Israelite history.
Jordan.note: Location: The river valley running from the north of the land down to the Dead Sea.

definitions: Jorim; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Jorkoam; a town in the hill country of Judah named among its settlements.
Jorkoam.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet er Raqqa.

definitions: Josabad; a Levite name in the records of temple service and restoration.
Josaphat; the Greek name for Jehoshaphat, especially the king of Judah.
Jose; a name-form of Joseph borne by men in the New Testament family records.
Josedech; the Greek name for Jehozadak.
Joseph; especially the son of Jacob, though the name is borne by others.
Joses; chiefly the New Testament name borne by one named among the Lord's brethren and also by Barnabas under a related form.
Joshah; a Simeonite prince in the genealogies.
Joshaphat; a name-form of Jehoshaphat used for men in genealogical or administrative records.
Joshaviah; a son of Elnaam and one of David's mighty men.
Joshbekashah; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Joshua; especially the son of Nun, successor of Moses, though the name is also borne by others.
Josiah; king of Judah who led a notable reform and renewed the covenant after the book of the law was found.
Josias; the Greek name for Josiah king of Judah.
Josibiah; a Simeonite ancestor in the tribal genealogies.
Josiphiah; the father of Shelomith in Ezra's list.
Jot; the smallest letter or least stroke, hence the least part.
Jotbah; the home of Meshullemeth, mother of king Amon of Judah.
Jotbah.note: Location: In Judah, likely in the southern hill country.
