definitions: Jotbath; another biblical spelling for Jotbathah, the wilderness station described as a land of brooks of water.
Jotbathah; a wilderness station in Israel's journey, remembered as a land of brooks of water.
Jotham; especially the king of Judah son of Uzziah, and also the name of other biblical men.
A journey is travel from one place to another; to journey is to travel.
Journeyed; past tense or past participial form of 'journey'. See 'journey'.
Traveling; going on a journey or course from place to place.
Plural form of 'journeying'. See 'journeying'.
Plural form of 'journey'. See 'journey'.
Joy; gladness, delight, or rejoicing.
Joyed; past tense or past participial form of 'joy'. See 'joy'.
Full of joy; glad and rejoicing.
In a joyful manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being joyfulnes.
Joying; present participial form of 'joy', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'joy'.
Full of joy; glad.
Jozabad; chiefly the name of Levites and captains in the days of Joash and Josiah, including a conspirator against Joash under a related form.
Jozachar; one of the servants who conspired against king Joash, elsewhere represented by the related name Zabad.
Jozadak; the name used for Jehozadak, father of Jeshua the high priest.
Jubal; the early ancestor of those who handle the harp and organ.
Jubile; the Jubilee year of release and restoration in Israel.
Jucal; the name used for Jehucal, son of Shelemiah and one of the princes in Jeremiah's time.
Juda; the Greek name for Judah, used of the patriarch, the tribe descended from him, and others bearing that name in Scripture.
Judaea; the southern region associated with Judah and Jerusalem.
In New Testament usage it may denote the wider Roman province or the Jewish land more generally.
Judaea.note: Location: The southern district of the land around Jerusalem and Bethlehem, corresponding broadly to ancient Judah.

definitions: Judah; the son of Jacob, the tribe descended from him, or the southern kingdom bearing that name.
In some passages the name may extend to the land, people, or royal line identified with that tribe.
Judas; the Greek name for Judah, especially used for Judas Iscariot, Judas Barsabas, Judas of Damascus, and Judas the brother of James.
Jude; the New Testament name-form of Judas, especially used of the servant of Jesus Christ and brother of James who wrote the Epistle of Jude.
Judea; the southern Jewish province or region centered on Jerusalem in the New Testament period.
Judea.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jerusalem.

definitions: To judge is to discern, decide, examine, or pronounce sentence.
As a noun, a judge is one appointed to decide causes, and in some contexts one who governs or delivers Israel.
Those who judge; rulers or officers appointed to decide causes.
The word may also refer to the governing deliverers of Israel or to the biblical book of Judges.
Judgest; thou judgest; you judge, decide, or pronounce upon.
Judging; present participial form of 'judge', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'judge'.
Sentence, decision, or act of judging.
The word may also mean justice, right order, wise discernment, or the punishment awarded by authority.
Judith; an Hittite wife of Esau, named among the women who grieved Isaac and Rebekah.
Juice; liquid pressed out from fruit or plant substance.
Julia; a Christian woman greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Julius; the centurion of Augustus' band who escorted Paul toward Rome.
Jumping; present participial form of 'jump', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'jump'.
Junia; a Christian greeted by Paul among his kinsfolk and fellow prisoners.
Juniper; a shrub or tree so named in Scripture, noted especially as a plant of the wilderness.
Jupiter; the Roman chief god, identified by the people of Lystra with Barnabas in Acts.
authority to judge, govern, or exercise power.
Jushab-hesed; one of the descendants of Zerubbabel named in the post-exilic genealogy.
Righteous, upright, and conformable to what is right.
Also fair or due in judgment and dealing.
Righteous judgment; that which is right and due in law or moral order.
The act of being accounted righteous; acquittal in judgment.
Justifier; one who justifies, declares righteous, or vindicates.
To account or declare righteous.
Sometimes to vindicate or show to be right.
Justifying; present participial form of 'justify', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'justify'.
Justle; to push, crowd, or bump against; an older spelling of 'jostle'.
In a just manner or degree.
Justus; chiefly the surname or Roman name of New Testament men such as Joseph Barsabas and Titus Justus.
Juttah; a priestly town in the hill country of Judah, traditionally associated with the family setting of John the Baptist.
Kabzeel; a southern town of Judah, remembered also as the home district of Benaiah son of Jehoiada.
Kabzeel.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Hora.

definitions: Kadesh; the important wilderness site associated with Israel's wandering, strife, and unbelief.
Kadesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Kadesh-Barnea; the major wilderness station from which Israel sent the spies and later wandered after refusing to enter the land.
Kadesh-barnea.note: Location: In the northern Sinai or southern Negev on the border of the wilderness of Zin, commonly linked with Ain el-Qudeirat.

definitions: Kadmiel; a Levite named among those connected with service after the exile.
Kadmonites; an eastern people named among the nations of the land promised to Abraham.
Kallai; a priestly head in the days of Joiakim.
Kanah; a brook or river marking part of a tribal boundary.
Kanah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Wadi Qana.

definitions: Kareah; the father of Johanan and Jonathan in the days after Jerusalem's fall; the name appears in related form as Careah.
Karkaa; a southern border place in the territorial description of Judah.
Karkaa.note: Location: In the far south near the wilderness border of Judah, often linked with the Ain Qoseimeh region.

definitions: Karkor; the place east of the Jordan where Gideon struck the remnant of the Midianite host.
Karkor.note: Location: In the desert east of Gilead toward the caravan routes.

definitions: Karnaim; a Bashan or Transjordan city associated with the Rephaim and later conflicts east of the Jordan.
Karnaim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Al Shaykh Saad.

definitions: Kartah; a town of Zebulun assigned to the Levites.
Kartah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Qureiyeh.

definitions: Kartan; a Levitical town in Naphtali, sometimes treated as related to Kirjathaim in parallel lists.
Kartan.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Qureiyeh.

definitions: Kattath; a town in the inheritance of Zebulun named among its settlements.
Kattath.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Far.

definitions: Kedar; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of an Arabian people bearing his name.
Kedemah; a son of Ishmael in the patriarchal genealogies.
Kedemoth; a Levitical town in the territory of Reuben from which Moses sent messengers to Sihon.
Kedesh; a city of refuge in the north, also significant in the history of Barak and Deborah.
Kedesh.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Qisyon.

definitions: Kedesh-Naphtali; the Kedesh in Galilee, a Levitical city and city of refuge in Naphtali.
Kedesh-naphtali.note: Location: In Upper Galilee in the territory of Naphtali, commonly linked with Tell Qedesh.

definitions: To guard, preserve, observe, or retain.
A keeper; one who guards, tends, watches, or has charge of something.
The word may refer to a guardian, jailer, herdsman, or other custodian.
Keepers; those who guard, tend, watch, or have charge of something.
Keepest; thou keepest; you keep, guard, or observe.
Often used of observing commandments or preserving something with care.
Keeps; guards; observes; preserves.
Often means to hold fast or carefully observe a command, charge, or way.
Keeping; present participial form of 'keep', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'keep'.
Kehelathah; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
Kehelathah.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route, often associated with the northern Sinai approach toward the Arabah.

definitions: Keilah; the Judean town David rescued from the Philistines before fleeing from Saul.
Keilah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Qila.

definitions: Kelaiah; a Levite in the days of Ezra, corresponding to the same man elsewhere written Kelita.
Kelita; a Levite who helped explain the law, and also one named in Ezra's reform.
Kemuel; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Nahor and a Levite in David's day.
Kenan; a patriarch in the line from Adam, corresponding to Cainan in another form.
Kenath; the Bashan town captured by Nobah and called by his name for a time.
Kenaz; the name of men in Scripture, especially connected with Edom and with Caleb's family.
Kenezite; one belonging to the line or house of Kenaz.
Kenite; a member of the Kenite people.
Kenite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Bani Dar, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Kenites; a people associated with Midian and the south, linked with Moses' in-laws and later dwelling among Israel.
Kenites.note: Location: Associated with the southern wilderness and Negev fringe, though not tied to one single site.

definitions: Kenizzites; an ancient people or clan connected with Kenaz, later associated in Israelite memory with Caleb's house.
Plural form of 'kerchief'. See 'kerchief'.
Keren-Happuch; one of the daughters of Job born to him after his affliction.
Kerioth; a Moabite city named in prophetic judgment, also used of a Judean place-name in another context.
Kerioth.note: Location: Often identified with Al Qurayyat.

definitions: Plural form of 'kernel'. See 'kernel'.
Keros; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Kettle; a vessel or pot for boiling or cooking.
Keturah; the wife or concubine of Abraham and mother of several sons.
Key; an instrument for opening and shutting, and figuratively authority to admit or exclude.
Plural form of 'key'. See 'key'.
Kezia; one of the daughters of Job after his restoration.
Keziz; a town in the inheritance of Benjamin.
Keziz.note: Location: In Benjamin east of the central ridge, likely in the Jericho-facing district.

definitions: Kibroth-Hattaavah; the wilderness station where the craving Israelites were judged and buried.
Kibroth-hattaavah.note: Location: Often identified with Erweis el Ebeirig.

definitions: Kibzaim; an Ephraimite town given to the Levites, perhaps the same as Jokmeam in a parallel list.
Kibzaim.note: Location: Often identified with Tel al Mazar.

definitions: Kick; to strike with the foot.
As a noun, a blow given with the foot.
Kicked; past tense or past participial form of 'kick'. See 'kick'.
Kid; a young goat.
Plural form of 'kidney'. See 'kidney'.
Kidron; the brook or valley east of Jerusalem, often associated with flight, mourning, and defilement cast out from the city.
Kidron.note: Location: Usually identified with Kidron River.

definitions: Plural form of 'kid'. See 'kid'.
Slain or put to death.
Killedst; means thou didst kill.
Killest; older second-person singular form of 'kill', used with 'thou'. See 'kill'.
Kills; puts to death.
In some figurative uses it means brings death, condemnation, or lifelessness.
Killing; present participial form of 'kill', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'kill'.
Kin; kindred, relatives, or those of the same family or race.
Kinah; a town in the southern inheritance of Judah.
Kinah.note: Location: Often identified with Horbat Uza.

definitions: Kind; sort, species, family, or natural class.
As an adjective it may mean gentle, fitting, or benevolent.
Kindle; to set on fire, inflame, stir up, or awaken strongly.
Set on fire, aroused, or stirred up.
Kindleth; older third-person singular form of 'kindle'. See 'kindle'.
In a kind manner or degree.
Goodness of disposition shown in gentle and helpful dealing.
Sometimes covenant favor or loving-kindness.
Relatives, family stock, or those of the same kin.
Kindreds; families.
Plural form of 'kind'. See 'kind'.
Kine; the older plural of cow, meaning cattle of the female kind.
A king; a sovereign ruler over a people or realm.
The word may also be used figuratively or supremely of divine rule.
The realm, rule, or reign of a king.
Often the dominion or royal rule of God.
Plural form of 'kingdom'. See 'kingdom'.
Kingly; royal, princely, or becoming a king.
Kings; sovereign rulers over peoples or realms.
The word may also be used of those holding royal dignity under a greater empire.
Kinsfolk; those of one's own kindred or family.
Plural form of 'kinsfolk'. See 'kinsfolk'.
A kinsman; one near in blood or family relation.
In some contexts the word has legal force, especially where a near relative may redeem or act on behalf of the family.
Kinsmen; men near in blood or family relation.
A kinswoman; a woman near in blood or family relation.
Kinswomen; female relatives or women of one's kin.
Kir; a distant region associated with Aram's origin and with exile in prophetic texts.
Kir.note: Location: Often placed in the Mesopotamian or Elamite east, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Kir-Haraseth; the chief fortified city of Moab, besieged in the days of Jehoram.
Kir-haraseth.note: Location: In Moab east of the Dead Sea, commonly identified with Kerak in modern Jordan.

definitions: Kir-hareseth; the chief fortified city of Moab; also referred to as Kir of Moab.
Kir-Haresh; another biblical spelling for the chief Moabite fortress otherwise called Kir-hareseth or Kir-heres.
Kir-heres; the Moabite city otherwise called Kir-hareseth.
Kiriathaim; a city east of the Jordan associated with Moab and the Reubenite territory.
Kiriathaim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Qureiyeh.

definitions: Kirjath; a shortened place-name form used in genealogical or territorial contexts, probably linked with Kirjath-Jearim.
Kirjath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Kirjath-Arba; the older name of Hebron, associated with Anak and the patriarchal burial site.
Kirjath-arba.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Kirjath-Arim; a shortened form related to Kirjath-Jearim in Judah's border traditions.
Kirjath-arim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Kirjath-Baal; another name for Kirjath-Jearim on the border of Judah and Benjamin.
Kirjath-baal.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Kirjath-Huzoth; the place to which Balak brought Balaam in the narrative of Moab.
Kirjath-huzoth.note: Location: In the Moabite region east of the Dead Sea, likely near the plateau centers associated with Balak.

definitions: Kirjath-Jearim; the town where the ark remained for many years before David brought it to Jerusalem.
Kirjath-jearim.note: Location: Commonly identified with Deir el Azar.

definitions: Kirjath-Sannah; another name for Debir in the hill country of Judah.
Kirjath-sannah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Rabud.

definitions: Kirjath-Sepher; another name for Debir, the Judahite city captured by Othniel.
Kirjath-sepher.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Rabud.

definitions: Kirjathaim; a city east of the Jordan associated with Moab and the Reubenite territory.
Kirjathaim.note: Location: In the tableland east of the Dead Sea, in the Moab-Reuben region.

definitions: Kish; especially the father of king Saul, and also the name of other men in Benjaminite or Levitical lines.
Kishi; a Levite ancestor in the line of Merari, corresponding to the same ancestor elsewhere written Kushaiah.
Kishion; an Issacharite town given to the Levites, perhaps the same place otherwise called Kedesh or Kishon in a parallel text.
Kishion.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Qisyon.

definitions: Kishon; the torrent or river associated with Deborah and Barak's victory over Sisera.
Kishon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Kishon River.

definitions: Kison; the brook associated with Deborah and Barak's victory, also written Kishon.
Kison.note: Location: Commonly identified with Kishon River.

definitions: a kiss; also to touch with the lips in affection or homage.
Kissed; past tense or past participial form of 'kiss'. See 'kiss'.
Plural form of 'kiss'. See 'kiss'.
Kite; a bird of prey mentioned in the list of unclean fowl.
Kithlish; a town in the Shephelah of Judah.
Kithlish.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Umm al Baqar.

definitions: Kitron; a town in Zebulun from which the Canaanites were not expelled.
Kitron.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Qutteina.

definitions: Kittim; a people or coastland line descended from Javan, later associated with western maritime powers.
Knead; to work dough or paste with the hands.
Kneaded; past tense or past participial form of 'knead'. See 'knead'.
Plural form of 'kneadingtrough'. See 'kneadingtrough'.
Knee; the joint between thigh and leg, and figuratively submission or posture in worship.
Kneel; to bend the knees in prayer, humility, or submission.
Kneeled; past tense or past participial form of 'kneel'. See 'kneel'.
Kneeling; present participial form of 'kneel', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'kneel'.
Plural form of 'knee'. See 'knee'.
The past tense of know.
Knewest; thou knewest; you knew or had knowledge of.
Knife; a cutting instrument with blade and handle.
Knit; to join closely, bind together, or become united.
As an adjective, closely joined.
Plural form of 'knife'. See 'knife'.
Knock; to strike with a blow, especially at a door.
As a noun, a stroke or sounding blow.
Knocked; past tense or past participial form of 'knock'. See 'knock'.
Knocketh; older third-person singular form of 'knock'. See 'knock'.
Knocking; present participial form of 'knock', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'knock'.
Knop; an ornamental knob or bud-shaped projection, especially in tabernacle and temple description.
Knobs or rounded ornamental projections.
Knowest; thou knowest; you know, perceive, or acknowledge.
Knows; has knowledge of; perceives or acknowledges.
Knowing; present participial form of 'know', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'know'.
Understanding, acquaintance, or true apprehension of a matter.
Known; perceived, understood, recognized, or made known.
Koa; a people or district named in Ezekiel among Babylon's allies or dependents.
Koa.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Kermanshah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Kohath; a man named in Scripture, son of Levi, father of Amram.
Kohathites; the Levitical family descended from Kohath, entrusted with the most holy furnishings of the sanctuary.
Kolaiah; the name of men in Scripture, including the father of Ahab the false prophet in Babylon.
Korah; especially the Levite who led a rebellion against Moses and Aaron, and also the name of other biblical men.
Korahite; a descendant of Korah, especially of the Levitical house so called.
Korahites; the family descended from Korah, especially known among the temple singers and gatekeepers.
Korathites; descendants of Korah, especially the Levitical line connected with temple service and song.
Kore; chiefly a Levitical and gatekeeping name in temple service.
Korhites; a name-form of Korahites, the descendants of Korah among the Levites.
Koz; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Kushaiah; the name used for Kishi, a Levite ancestor in the genealogies of temple song.
Laadah; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies of the linen workers at Beth-ashbea.
Laadan; a Gershonite Levite and the name of a family descending from him.
Laban; the Aramean brother of Rebekah, father of Leah and Rachel, with whom Jacob dwelt and served.
Labour; toil, work, travail, or the act of toiling.
Laboured; toiled, worked, travailed, or exerted oneself.
A labourer; one who works, especially by bodily toil.
The word may also be used figuratively of one who labours in the work of God.
Labourers; those who work, especially by bodily toil, and in some contexts those who labour in the work of God.
Laboureth; means toils, works, or travails.
Labouring; toiling, working, or travailing.
Labours; toils, works, travails, or exertions.
Lace; a cord, string, or band for binding.
As a verb, to fasten or draw together with a cord.
Lachish; the major fortified city of Judah in the Shephelah, prominent in Joshua, the monarchy, and Assyrian invasion accounts.
Lachish.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell ed Duweir.

definitions: Lack; want, need, or deficiency.
As a verb, to be without or come short of.
Lacked; past tense or past participial form of 'lack'. See 'lack'.
Lackest; older second-person singular form of 'lack', used with 'thou'. See 'lack'.
Lacketh; older third-person singular form of 'lack'. See 'lack'.
Lacking; present participial form of 'lack', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lack'.
Lad; a boy, youth, or young servant.
Ladder; a frame with steps or rungs for climbing.
Lade; to load, burden, or place a burden upon.
Laded; past tense or past participial form of 'lade'. See 'lade'.
Laden; loaded, burdened, or weighed down.
Ladeth; older third-person singular form of 'lade'. See 'lade'.
Plural form of 'lady'. See 'lady'.
Lading; present participial form of 'lade', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lade'.
Plural form of 'lad'. See 'lad'.
Lady; a woman of rank or a respectful title for a woman.
Lael; the father of Eliasaph, prince of the Gershonites.
Lahad; a Judahite ancestor associated with the families of the house of Ashbea.
Lahai-Roi; the well whose fuller name is Beer-lahai-roi, associated with Hagar and later Isaac.
Lahai-roi.note: Location: In the southern wilderness between Kadesh and Bered, in the northern Negev or Sinai fringe.

definitions: Lahmam; a town in the Shephelah of Judah named among the lowland settlements.
Lahmam.note: Location: Often identified with Horbat Lehem.

definitions: Lahmi; the brother of Goliath the Gittite, slain by Elhanan according to Chronicles.
The past tense of lay; placed, set down, or imposed.
Laidst; means thou didst lay.
Lain; the past participle of lie, meaning rested or remained lying.
Laish; the name of men in Scripture, distinct from the city later called Dan.
Lake; a body of water, and in Revelation the lake of fire.
Lakum; a border point in Naphtali's inheritance.
Lakum.note: Location: Often identified with Horbat Kush.

definitions: Lama; Aramaic for why, preserved in the cry of Jesus from the cross.
A lamb; a young sheep, often used sacrificially or figuratively.
Lambs; young sheep.
Lame; disabled in walking, halting, or figuratively weak and defective.
Lamech; the name of men in early Genesis, especially one in Cain's line and one the father of Noah.
To mourn aloud or express sorrow deeply.
Mournful, grievous, or fit to be lamented.
Mourning expressed in cries, words, or song.
A lamp is a vessel giving light by oil and flame.
In Scripture it often also signifies guidance, testimony, or continuing life and blessing.
Plural form of 'lamp'. See 'lamp'.
a spear or long thrusting weapon.
Plural form of 'lancet'. See 'lancet'.
Land, ground, territory, or country.
Landed; past tense or past participial form of 'land'. See 'land'.
Landing; present participial form of 'land', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'land'.
Landmark; a boundary mark or fixed sign dividing property or territory.
Plural form of 'landmark'. See 'landmark'.
Plural form of 'land'. See 'land'.
Plural form of 'lane'. See 'lane'.
speech, tongue, or manner of expression.
Plural form of 'language'. See 'language'.
Languished; past tense or past participial form of 'languish'. See 'languish'.
Languishes; grows faint, weak, or drooping.
Languishing; present participial form of 'languish', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'languish'.
Plural form of 'lantern'. See 'lantern'.
Laodicea; the city addressed among the seven churches in Revelation.
It is remembered especially for lukewarmness and self-satisfied spiritual poverty.
Laodicea.note: Location: In the Lycus valley of western Asia Minor, near modern Denizli in Turkey.

definitions: Laodiceans; the people or church of Laodicea addressed in New Testament correspondence.
Laodiceans.note: Location: Usually identified with Laodicea on the Lycus.

definitions: Lap; the part formed by the knees and thighs when sitting, or a fold or edge laid over another.
As a verb, to lick up, fold over, or take in with the tongue.
Lapidoth; the husband of Deborah the prophetess.
Lapped; past tense or past participial form of 'lap'. See 'lap'.
Lappeth; older third-person singular form of 'lap'. See 'lap'.
Lapwing; present participial form of 'lapwing', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lapwing'.
Large; great in size, broad, abundant, or free.
The state, quality, or condition of being largenes.
The state, quality, or condition of being lasciviousnes.
Lasea; the Cretan city near Fair Havens in the voyage narrative of Acts.
Lasha; a place marking an extent in the table of nations.
Lasha.note: Location: Probably somewhere southeast of the Dead Sea in the direction of the hot-springs region, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Lasharon; a locality likely associated with the Sharon region in Joshua's conquest list.
Lasharon.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Sharon Plain, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Last; final, hindmost, or latest.
Lasted; past tense or past participial form of 'last'. See 'last'.
Lasting; present participial form of 'last', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'last'.
Latchet; a lace, thong, or fastening, especially of a sandal.
Late; occurring after the expected time, or belonging to a recent past.
As an adverb, after the usual time.
In a late manner or degree.
Latin; the Latin language, or belonging to it.
Latter; the later or second of two, or what belongs to the later time.
A lattice is an openwork frame or window of crossed bars or network.
Laud; to praise or extol.
As a noun in older use, praise.
Laugh; to express mirth, scorn, or amazement by laughter.
As a noun, an act or sound of laughter.
Laughed; past tense or past participial form of 'laugh'. See 'laugh'.
Laugheth; older third-person singular form of 'laugh'. See 'laugh'.
Laughing; present participial form of 'laugh', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'laugh'.
Laughter; the expression or sound of laughing, whether in mirth, amazement, or scorn.
Launch; to set afloat, send forth, or begin with force.
As a noun in later use, a boat or act of launching.
Launched; past tense or past participial form of 'launch'. See 'launch'.
Laver; a basin or vessel for washing, especially in tabernacle or temple service.
Plural form of 'laver'. See 'laver'.
Lavish; to bestow freely or abundantly.
As an adjective, profuse or abundant.
Law; a command, rule, or body of divine or civil instruction binding upon those under it.
Allowed by right, law, or just rule.
In a lawful manner or degree.
A lawgiver is one who gives or ordains law.
In Scripture the title may belong to a human ruler or supremely to God.
Without law, restraint, or obedience; wickedly unruly.
Plural form of 'law'. See 'law'.
A lawyer; one learned in the law, especially the Mosaic law as expounded among the Jews.
The term in the New Testament points to an interpreter or doctor of the law rather than merely a civil advocate in the modern sense.
Lawyers; men learned in the law, especially interpreters or teachers of it among the Jews.
Layedst; means thou didst lay.
Layest; older second-person singular form of 'lay', used with 'thou'. See 'lay'.
Lays; places; sets down; imposes.
It may also mean to direct, apply, or prepare.
Laying; present participial form of 'lay', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lay'.
Lazarus; the brother of Mary and Martha whom Jesus raised from the dead, and also the beggar in the Lord's parable.
Leader; one who goes before, guides, or has chief place in directing others.
Plural form of 'leader'. See 'leader'.
Leadest; thou leadest; you lead or guide.
Leads; guides; conducts in a way.
Leaf; the foliage of a plant, and by extension a thin sheet or page-like part.
League; a covenant, confederacy, or formal alliance between parties.
Leah; the elder daughter of Laban and first wife of Jacob.
To rest or depend upon.
Also thin or lacking fat in the literal sense.
Leanfleshed; thin in flesh, gaunt, or poorly nourished.
Leaning; present participial form of 'lean', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lean'.
The state, quality, or condition of being leannes.
Leap; to spring, jump, or move suddenly upward or forward.
As a noun, a jump or spring.
Leaped; past tense or past participial form of 'leap'. See 'leap'.
Leaping; present participial form of 'leap', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'leap'.
Learn; to acquire knowledge, understand by instruction or experience, or be taught.
Learned; past tense or past participial form of 'learn'. See 'learn'.
Learning; present participial form of 'learn', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'learn'.
Falsehood; lies; vanity in speech.
Least; smallest in degree, rank, amount, or importance.
Leather; prepared animal hide used for clothing, belts, or other goods.
Leathern; made of leather.
Leaved; past tense or past participial form of 'leave'. See 'leave'.
Leaven; fermenting dough or yeast, and figuratively corrupting or permeating influence.
As a verb, to ferment or cause to rise.
Leavened; past tense or past participial form of 'leaven'. See 'leaven'.
Leaveneth; older third-person singular form of 'leaven'. See 'leaven'.
Plural form of 'leaf'. See 'leaf'.
Leaves; departs from; lets remain; abandons.
Leaving; present participial form of 'leave', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'leave'.
Lebana; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Lebanah; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Lebana.
Lebanon; the mountain region north of Israel, famed for its cedars and lofty heights.
The name may point either to the range itself, its forests, or the broader district.
Lebanon.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Jezzine.

definitions: Lebaoth; a southern town of Judah or Simeon, probably related to Beth-Lebaoth in parallel lists.
Lebaoth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Jebel el Biri.

definitions: Lebbaeus; a surname linked with the apostle Thaddaeus or Judas.
Lebonah; the village near Bethel and Shiloh named in the account of the daughters of Shiloh.
Lebonah.note: Location: Often identified with Khan el Lubban.

definitions: Lecah; a place in Judah mentioned in genealogical records.
The past tense of lead; guided, brought, or caused to go.
Leddest; older second-person singular form of 'led', used with 'thou'. See 'led'.
Plural form of 'ledge'. See 'ledge'.
Plural form of 'leek'. See 'leek'.
Plural form of 'lees'. See 'lees'.
The past tense or participle of leave; departed from or caused to remain.
Leftest; older second-person singular form of 'left', used with 'thou'. See 'left'.
Lefthanded; past tense or past participial form of 'lefthanded'. See 'lefthanded'.
Leg; the limb of a person or animal used in walking.
Legion; a large body of soldiers, and by extension a great multitude.
Plural form of 'legion'. See 'legion'.
Plural form of 'leg'. See 'leg'.
Lehabim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Lehi; the place where Samson slew the Philistines with a jawbone and where the spring En-hakkore was named.
Lehi.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet es Siyyagh.

definitions: freedom from pressing occupation; convenient time.
Lemuel; the king addressed in the sayings taught by his mother in Proverbs 31.
Lend; to give for a time expecting return, or in some contexts to devote or yield over.
Lender; one who lends, especially money or goods.
Lendeth; older third-person singular form of 'lend'. See 'lend'.
The measure from end to end; long extent.
Lengthen; to make longer or extend in duration or extent.
Lengthened; past tense or past participial form of 'lengthen'. See 'lengthen'.
Lengthening; present participial form of 'lengthen', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lengthen'.
Past tense and past participial form of 'lend'. See 'lend'.
Given for temporary use.
Plural form of 'lentil'. See 'lentil'.
Leopard; the spotted beast of prey so called, often used as an image of swiftness or fierceness.
Plural form of 'leopard'. See 'leopard'.
Leper; one afflicted with leprosy or pronounced leprous under the law.
Plural form of 'leper'. See 'leper'.
Leprosy; the defiling disease so called in Scripture, including related ceremonial cases of plague in persons, garments, or houses.
Affected with leprosy or a similar defiling skin disease.
Leshem; the northern city later captured by Dan and renamed Dan.
Leshem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Dan.

definitions: Smaller in amount, degree, or number; not so much.
Lesser; smaller, lower, or less in degree.
For fear that; so that not; to avoid the danger that.
To hinder; to restrain; to hold back.
In older use, the very opposite of the common modern sense 'to allow.'
Letter; a written character or epistle, and in some passages the mere outward writing as opposed to the spirit.
Plural form of 'letter'. See 'letter'.
Lettest; older second-person singular form of 'let', used with 'thou'. See 'let'.
Letting; present participial form of 'let', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'let'.
Letushim; a people or tribal branch descended from Dedan.
Leummim; a people or tribal branch descended from Dedan.
Levi; the son of Jacob and the tribe descended from him.
Leviathan; a great sea creature or monstrous beast, used in Scripture both literally and figuratively for overwhelming power.
Levite; a descendant of Levi, especially one belonging to the sacred tribe set apart for tabernacle or temple service.
Levites; members of the tribe of Levi, especially those appointed to sacred service around the sanctuary and temple.
pertaining to the Levites or to priestly service.
Levy; an imposed tax, tribute, or body of men raised for service.
As a verb, to impose, collect, or raise.
Lewd; wicked, base, or morally corrupt; in some contexts simply vile or lawless.
In a lewd manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being lewdnes.
Liar; one who speaks falsehood or denies truth deceitfully.
Plural form of 'liar'. See 'liar'.
Bountiful; generous; free in giving.
generosity, openhandedness, or largeness of mind.
Plural form of 'libertine'. See 'libertine'.
Freedom from restraint or bondage.
Often lawful freedom under right order, not lawless license.
Libnah; a royal city of Judah that revolted in the days of Jehoram and figured in Joshua's campaign.
Libnah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Bornat.

definitions: Libni; the name of Levites in Scripture, especially in the line of Gershon.
Libnites; descendants of Libni reckoned among the Levitical families.
Libya; the North African region west of Egypt, especially in connection with Cyrene.
Libya.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: Plural form of 'libyan'. See 'libyan'.
Libyans.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: Lice; the small parasitic insects of the plague in Egypt.
permission, liberty, or formal leave.
Lick; to pass the tongue over, or in some contexts to strike down or consume.
As a noun, a passing touch with the tongue.
Licked; past tense or past participial form of 'lick'. See 'lick'.
Licketh; older third-person singular form of 'lick'. See 'lick'.
Lid; a cover for a vessel, or an eyelid by context.
To lie stretched out at full length; to lie flat.
To continue pressing upon or hanging over, especially of a storm.
To project outward; to jut or extend beyond the rest.
Lied; past tense or past participial form of 'lie'. See 'lie'.
Lien; in older usage, the line, outline, or bodily form; in later legal use, a claim retained upon property.
Plural form of 'lier'. See 'lier'.
Plural form of 'lie'. See 'lie'.
Liest; older second-person singular form of 'lie', used with 'thou'. See 'lie'.
Lies; rests; is laid down.
It may also mean remains in a certain state, is situated, or lies concealed.
Plural form of 'lieutenant'. See 'lieutenant'.
Life; living existence; the course or condition of one who lives.
Lifetime; the duration of a person's life.
Raised up, taken up, or exalted.
Lifter; one who lifts or raises up.
Liftest; thou liftest; you lift, raise up, or exalt.
Lifts; raises up; exalts.
Lifting; present participial form of 'lift', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lift'.
Light; brightness, illumination, what makes things visible, or spiritual light.
Lighted; gave light, was illuminated, alighted, or came upon.
Lighten; to make light, relieve, illuminate, or flash.
Lightened; made light, relieved, illuminated, or flashed.
Lighteneth; means makes light, relieves, illuminates, or flashes.
Lighter; a small vessel used to convey goods, especially to or from a ship.
Also comparative of light, more light in weight or brightness.
Lightest; may mean givest light or alightest.
Lighteth; means gives light or alights.
Lighting; illuminating, alighting, or coming upon.
In a light manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being lightnes.
Lightning; present participial form of 'lightning', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lightning'.
Plural form of 'lightning'. See 'lightning'.
Lights; illuminations, luminous bodies, or means of giving light.
Lign; wood, especially in the phrase lign aloes, meaning fragrant aloes wood.
An aromatic wood, prized for fragrance.
a precious stone named in the high priest’s breastplate.
Like; similar, resembling, or such as.
Liked; past tense or past participial form of 'like'. See 'like'.
Of one mind; sharing the same inward disposition.
Liken; to compare, make like, or represent as similar.
Likened; past tense or past participial form of 'liken'. See 'liken'.
Resemblance, similarity, or form answering to another thing.
Sometimes an image or representation.
Liketh; older third-person singular form of 'like'. See 'like'.
In like manner; in the same way.
Likhi; a Manassite name in the genealogies.
Liking; present participial form of 'like', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'like'.
Plural form of 'lily'. See 'lily'.
Lily; the flower so called, often used in Scripture as an image of beauty or grace.
Lime; a calcareous substance used in mortar or plaster, and in some contexts a sticky substance for catching birds.
Limit; boundary, appointed measure, or utmost extent.
As a verb, to bound or restrict.
Limited; past tense or past participial form of 'limit'. See 'limit'.
Limiteth; older third-person singular form of 'limit'. See 'limit'.
A cord, measuring line, stretched thread, boundary, portion, or line of descent.
descent, ancestry, or family line.
Linen; cloth made from flax, often noted for fine or sacred garments.
Lingered; past tense or past participial form of 'linger'. See 'linger'.
Lingereth; older third-person singular form of 'linger'. See 'linger'.
Lintel; the upper beam or crosspiece of a doorway.
Plural form of 'lintel'. See 'lintel'.
Linus; a Christian who sent greetings to Timothy through Paul.
A lion; the beast so called, often used figuratively for strength, danger, or royalty.
The state, quality, or condition of being liones.
Plural form of 'lioness'. See 'lioness'.
Lionlike; like a lion in courage, fierceness, or appearance.
Plural form of 'lion'. See 'lion'.
The lip is the edge of the mouth.
In Scripture lips often stand for speech, confession, praise, or deceit.
Lips; the parts of the mouth, and by extension speech or profession.
Liquor; liquid substance, fluid, or drink.
Plural form of 'liquor'. See 'liquor'.
Listed; past tense or past participial form of 'list'. See 'list'.
Listen; to give ear, attend, or hearken.
Listeth; older third-person singular form of 'list'. See 'list'.
Plural form of 'litter'. See 'litter'.
Little; small in size, amount, age, or importance.
Lived; past tense or past participial form of 'live'. See 'live'.
Lively; living, full of life, or active in power.
In older usage often simply means living.
Liver; the inward organ of the body so called, and in sacrificial language a part taken with the offerings.
Plural form of 'life'. See 'life'.
Livest; older second-person singular form of 'live', used with 'thou'. See 'live'.
Lives; remains alive; continues in life.
In some places, lives in a certain manner or under a certain rule.
Alive; having life.
The word may also mean livelihood or means of support.
Lizard; a small creeping reptile named among the creatures of Scripture.
Look; behold; a word calling attention.
Lo-Ammi; a symbolic name or title used in Scripture.
Lo-Debar; the Transjordan town from which Mephibosheth was brought to David.
Lo-debar.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Dover.

definitions: Lo-Ruhamah; a symbolic name or title used in Scripture.
Loaden; loaded or burdened, an older participial spelling of loaded.
Loadeth; older third-person singular form of 'load'. See 'load'.
A loaf is a formed mass of bread baked for food.
Loan; something lent, or the act of lending.
To loathe is to feel disgust toward, abhor, or regard with strong aversion.
Loatheth; older third-person singular form of 'loathe'. See 'loathe'.
Loathsome; hateful, disgusting, or fit to be abhorred.
Plural form of 'loaf'. See 'loaf'.
Lock; a fastening for securing, or a tress or curl of hair.
Locked; fastened with a lock, shut in, or made secure.
Plural form of 'lock'. See 'lock'.
Locust; a devouring winged insect noted for swarming and consuming vegetation.
Plural form of 'locust'. See 'locust'.
Lod; the town in the plain later known as Lydda, associated with the return from exile and Peter's ministry.
Lodge; to stay for a night, dwell temporarily, or be laid up in a place.
As a noun, a temporary shelter or abode.
Lodged; past tense or past participial form of 'lodge'. See 'lodge'.
Lodgest; older second-person singular form of 'lodge', used with 'thou'. See 'lodge'.
Lodgeth; older third-person singular form of 'lodge'. See 'lodge'.
Lodging; present participial form of 'lodge', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lodge'.
Plural form of 'lodging'. See 'lodging'.
Loft; an upper room, raised chamber, or height.
In a lofti manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being loftines.
Lofty; high, exalted, proud, or elevated in place or spirit.
Log; a block or cut portion of wood, and in some contexts a small measure.
The lower part of the body about the hips and reins.
Often used figuratively for strength, readiness, or posterity.
Lois; the grandmother of Timothy, remembered for sincere faith.
Long; extended in measure or duration.
Longed; past tense or past participial form of 'long'. See 'long'.
Longedst; means thou didst long or yearn.
Longer; more long, or for a greater time.
Longeth; older third-person singular form of 'long'. See 'long'.
Longing; present participial form of 'long', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'long'.
Patient endurance under provocation; slowness to anger.
Longwinged; having long wings.
Looked; beheld, regarded, or turned the eyes toward.
Lookest; thou lookest; you look or behold.
At times it also means you regard or take notice of.
Looks; beholds; directs the eyes toward.
At times it also means to regard, attend to, or expect.
Looking; present participial form of 'look', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'look'.
Plural form of 'lookingglass'. See 'lookingglass'.
Plural form of 'look'. See 'look'.
Plural form of 'loop'. See 'loop'.
Loose; free, unbound, unfastened, or not fixed.
As a verb, to release or untie.
Loosed; past tense or past participial form of 'loose'. See 'loose'.
Looseth; older third-person singular form of 'loose'. See 'loose'.
Loosing; present participial form of 'loose', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'loose'.
Lop; to cut off branches or cut down projecting parts.
One having authority, rule, or ownership.
In Scripture often a title of dignity for men, and supremely the divine title of the Lord himself.
In a lord manner or degree.
dominion, authority, or the rank of a lord.
Lose; to forfeit, fail to keep, be deprived of, or destroy.
Loseth; older third-person singular form of 'lose'. See 'lose'.
privation, ruin, or what is lost.
Lost; gone from possession or sight, ruined, or the past tense and participle of lose.
Lot; the son of Haran and nephew of Abraham, and also a lot cast or portion assigned.
Lotan; a Horite chief in the genealogies of Seir.
Lothe; an older spelling of loathe, meaning to dislike greatly or abhor.
Lothed; past tense or past participial form of 'lothe'. See 'lothe'.
Lotheth; older third-person singular form of 'lothe'. See 'lothe'.
Lothing; present participial form of 'lothe', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lothe'.
Lots; things cast to determine a portion, or portions assigned by lot.
Loud; sounding strongly or with great voice.
Louder; more loud in sound or utterance.
Love; affection, goodwill, or devoted regard.
Loved; had affection for or showed goodwill toward.
Lovedst; means thou didst love.
Lovely; worthy of love, beautiful, or delightful.
Lover; one who loves, and in some contexts one joined in unlawful or passionate attachment.
Plural form of 'lover'. See 'lover'.
Loves; affections, beloved persons, or acts of loving.
Lovest; thou lovest; you love, delight in, or bear affection toward.
Loves; bears affection toward; delights in.
In Scripture often includes settled goodwill, covenant kindness, or holy delight rather than mere feeling.
Loving; present participial form of 'love', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'love'.
Steadfast and tender covenant love expressed in mercy and favor.
Plural form of 'lovingkindness'. See 'lovingkindness'.
Low; not high, humble, faint, or reduced.
Lower; less high, beneath, or of an inferior place.
As a verb, to bring down or let fall.
Lowest; least high, least exalted, or most humble among those compared.
Loweth; means lows or bellows like cattle.
Lowing; the bellowing of cattle.
Humility; a lowly mind free from proud self-exaltation.
Lowly; humble, meek, or low in condition.
Lowring; present participial form of 'lower', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lower'.
Lubim; a North African people associated with Libya.
Lubim.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Cyrene.

definitions: Lubims; the Libyan people named among the nations allied with Egypt.
Lucas; the Greek or Latin name for Luke, the beloved physician and companion of Paul.
Lucifer; the day-star figure used in Isaiah's taunt against the king of Babylon.
Lucius; the name of New Testament men, including one at Antioch and one greeted by Paul.
Lucre; gain or profit, often with the sense of greedy or dishonest gain.
Lud; a son in the early genealogies, associated with a people bearing his name.
Ludim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Luhith; the ascent or locality named in prophecies lamenting Moab's downfall.
Luke; the beloved physician and companion of Paul, traditionally linked with the third Gospel and Acts.
Lukewarm; moderately warm, and figuratively half-hearted or lacking zeal.
Lump; a compact mass, especially of dough or material not yet divided.
Lunatick; one afflicted with what was thought to vary with the moon, especially severe fits or mental disturbance.
As an adjective, so affected.
Lurk; to lie hidden, remain in secret, or wait concealed.
Lurking; present participial form of 'lurk', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lurk'.
Strong desire or craving.
Often a corrupt and unlawful desire, though the bare sense is broader.
Lusted; past tense or past participial form of 'lust'. See 'lust'.
Lusts; strongly desires, often in a corrupt or unlawful way.
Lusting; present participial form of 'lust', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lust'.
Lusty; strong, vigorous, or full of force.
Luz; the older name of Bethel, and also the name of another city founded in the land of the Hittites.
Lycaonia; an inland region of Asia Minor including places such as Lystra and Derbe.
Lycia; a coastal region in southwestern Asia Minor.
Lydda; the town later called Lod, associated in Acts with the healing of Aeneas.
Lydia; the seller of purple of Thyatira who first received Paul at Philippi.
Plural form of 'lydian'. See 'lydian'.
Lydians.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Sardis.

definitions: Lying; present participial form of 'lie', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'lie'.
Lysanias; the tetrarch of Abilene named by Luke.
Lysias; Claudius Lysias, the Roman chief captain at Jerusalem.
Lystra; a city of Lycaonia associated with Paul’s preaching, persecution, and the early life of Timothy.
It is remembered in Acts as a place both of miracle and opposition.
Lystra.note: Location: In the interior of Asia Minor in Lycaonia, commonly identified near Hatunsaray in Turkey.

definitions: Maacah; chiefly the name of women in royal and family lines, though also borne in related place and family traditions.
Maachah; chiefly the name of notable women in royal and family lines, and also of a small Aramean region.
Maachathi; belonging to Maachah or the people of Maachah.
Maachathite; one belonging to Maachah or the Maachathite territory.
Maachathite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Plural form of 'maachathite'. See 'maachathite'.
Maachathites.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Maadai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Maadiah; a priestly name in the post-exilic records, related to the forms Maaziah and Moadiah.
Maai; a priest or musician named in the thanksgiving order at Jerusalem.
Maaleh-Acrabbim; a named place or feature mentioned in Scripture.
Maaleh-acrabbim.note: Location: Commonly placed at or identified with Ma’ale Akrabbim.

definitions: Maarath; a town in the hill country of Judah named among its settlements.
Maarath.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Qufin.

definitions: Maaseiah; chiefly a widely used priestly, Levitical, and post-exilic name borne by many men in Scripture.
Maasiai; a priestly or Levitical name in the post-exilic records, elsewhere represented by the form Amashai.
Maath; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Maaz; the head of one of the priestly courses.
Maaziah; the name of a priestly course and of post-exilic priests associated with that house.
Macedonia; the northern Greek region into which the gospel first passed in Europe through Paul’s call.
The name may denote the district itself or the churches found there.
Macedonia.note: Location: The northern Greek region above Achaia, extending over much of what is now northern Greece and nearby areas.

definitions: Macedonian; belonging to Macedonia or one of its people.
Machbanai; a Gadite warrior who came to David in the wilderness.
Machbenah; a Judahite place-name preserved in the genealogies.
Machbenah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Hebra.

definitions: Machi; the father of Geuel, the spy from the tribe of Gad.
Machir; the son of Manasseh from whom a leading clan descended east of Jordan.
Machirites; the clan descended from Machir within the house of Manasseh.
Machnadebai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Machpelah; the cave and field near Hebron purchased by Abraham as the family burial place of the patriarchs.
Machpelah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Cave of the Patriarchs.

definitions: Mad; furious, beside oneself, wildly excited, or mentally disordered.
Madai; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with the Medes.
The past tense or participle of make; formed, done, or caused to be.
Acted as if; gave the appearance of doing so.
Madest; thou madest; you made, formed, or caused.
Madian; a Greek or alternate name-form of Midian, the desert land associated with Midianite tribes.
Madian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Al Bad.

definitions: Madmannah; a southern town in the territory of Judah and Simeon.
Madmannah.note: Location: In the Negev south of Judah, in the Beersheba region.

definitions: Madmen; a Moabite place named in Jeremiah's oracle of judgment.
Madmen.note: Location: In the region of Moab east of the Dead Sea.

definitions: Madmenah; a place named in Isaiah's description of the Assyrian advance toward Jerusalem.
The state, quality, or condition of being madnes.
Madon; a northern Canaanite royal city whose king joined the confederacy defeated by Joshua.
Madon.note: Location: In Upper Galilee or the far north of Canaan.

definitions: Magbish; a place whose people are listed among those who returned from exile with Zerubbabel.
Magbish.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Makhbiyeh.

definitions: Magdala; the Galilean town associated with Mary Magdalene on the shore of the lake.
Magdala.note: Location: On the western side of the Sea of Galilee, though the exact ancient site is debated.

definitions: Magdalene; a designation meaning a woman of Magdala, used especially of Mary Magdalene.
Magdiel; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Magician; one skilled in magical arts, enchantments, or secret arts.
Plural form of 'magician'. See 'magician'.
Magistrate; a civil ruler, judge, or public officer exercising authority.
Plural form of 'magistrate'. See 'magistrate'.
magnificent or splendid.
grandeur, splendor, or stately excellence.
To make great in esteem, praise, or apparent importance.
Magog; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with northern peoples in biblical prophecy.
Magor-Missabib; a symbolic title meaning terror on every side, given by Jeremiah to Pashur.
Magpiash; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Mahalah; a daughter of Ishmael in the patriarchal genealogies.
Mahalaleel; an early patriarch in the line from Seth to Noah.
Mahalath; the name of women in the family lines of Ishmael and David.
Mahali; a Greek or alternate name-form of Mahli in the Levitical genealogies.
Mahanaim; the Transjordan city where Jacob saw the angels and where David later found refuge during Absalom's revolt.
Mahanaim.note: Location: Often identified with Tell edh Dhahab el Gharbi.

definitions: Mahaneh-Dan; the camp of Dan associated with the movements of Samson and the Danites.
Mahaneh-dan.note: Location: Between Zorah and Eshtaol on the border of Dan and Judah.

definitions: Maharai; one of David's mighty men, a Netophathite.
Mahath; chiefly a Levitical name borne by men connected with temple service and reform.
Mahavite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mahav in Scripture.
Mahazioth; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Maher-Shalal-Hash-Baz; the symbolic name of Isaiah's son, meaning swift the spoil, speedy the prey.
Mahlah; one of the daughters of Zelophehad.
Mahli; a Merarite Levite and the name of the family descending from him.
Mahlites; descendants of Mahli reckoned among the Levitical families.
Mahlon; the son of Elimelech and Naomi, husband of Ruth.
Mahol; the father of Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Darda in the wisdom comparison with Solomon.
A maid; a young woman or girl, and in some contexts a female servant.
The exact force depends on whether the passage emphasizes age, unmarried condition, or service.
Maiden; a young unmarried woman or virgin.
Plural form of 'maiden'. See 'maiden'.
Maids; young women, girls, or female servants.
A maidservant; a female servant or bondwoman attached to a household.
The word emphasizes service and dependence rather than mere youth.
Maidservants; female servants attached to a household.
Mail; armour of linked metal or defensive covering.
Maimed; past tense or past participial form of 'maim'. See 'maim'.
Mainsail; the principal sail of a ship.
Maintain; to keep up, support, defend, or continue in force.
Maintained; past tense or past participial form of 'maintain'. See 'maintain'.
Maintainest; older second-person singular form of 'maintain', used with 'thou'. See 'maintain'.
support, provision, or the act of upholding.
Greatness, royal dignity, and exalted splendor.
Makaz; a district town in Solomon's administrative system.
Makaz.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Deir Muheisin.

definitions: Maker; one who makes, forms, or does, and supremely the Creator in many passages.
Plural form of 'maker'. See 'maker'.
Makest; thou makest; you make, form, or cause.
Makes; forms; causes; brings about.
Makheloth; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
Makheloth.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route, often associated with the northern Sinai approach toward the Arabah.

definitions: Making; present participial form of 'make', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'make'.
Makkedah; the royal Canaanite city where the five Amorite kings hid in the cave and were judged by Joshua.
Makkedah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Qom.

definitions: Maktesh; a district mentioned in Scripture.
Malachi; the prophet through whom the last book of the Old Testament is given.
Malcham; a form associated with the Ammonite deity Molech or Milcom in prophetic usage.
Malchi-Shua; a son of Saul slain in battle on Mount Gilboa.
Malchiah; chiefly the name of priests, builders, and officials in the days of Jeremiah and Nehemiah.
Malchiel; a son of Beriah in the tribe of Asher.
Malchielites; descendants of Malchiel reckoned among the families of Asher.
Malchijah; chiefly a priestly and official name in royal and post-exilic records.
Malchiram; a son in the royal line of Jeconiah.
Malchus; the servant of the high priest whose ear Peter cut off.
Male; of the sex that begets, whether of man or beast.
Malefactor; an evildoer or criminal.
Plural form of 'malefactor'. See 'malefactor'.
Maleleel; a Greek or alternate name-form of Mahalaleel, the patriarch in the line from Seth before the flood.
Plural form of 'male'. See 'male'.
Ill will, spite, or settled desire to injure.
Moved by malice; spiteful or evil in intent.
The state, quality, or condition of being maliciousnes.
deep ill will, harmful tendency, or extreme evil.
Mallothi; one of the sons of Heman appointed to musical service.
Plural form of 'mallow'. See 'mallow'.
Malluch; chiefly a priestly or post-exilic family name in Ezra and Nehemiah.
Mammon; riches or wealth regarded as an object of trust or devotion.
Mamre; the Amorite ally of Abram associated with the oaks near Hebron.
Man; a human being, and often an adult male person in particular.
A warrior; a fighting man; a soldier.
Manaen; a Christian teacher at Antioch, brought up with Herod the tetrarch.
Manahath; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Manahethites; a family or settlement group associated with Manahath in Judah.
Manasseh; the son of Joseph, the tribe descended from him, or the king of Judah so named.
Manasses; the Greek name for Manasseh, used especially of Joseph's son and of the king of Judah.
Manassites; members of the tribe of Manasseh.
Plural form of 'mandrake'. See 'mandrake'.
Maneh; a weight or monetary measure, related to the mina.
Manger; a feeding trough for cattle or beasts.
Plainly shown; openly revealed; made evident.
the act of making plain, visible, or evident.
Manifold; many in number, varied in kind, or diverse.
Mankind; the human race, humankind collectively.
Manna; the bread from heaven given to Israel in the wilderness, and figuratively divine nourishment.
Manner; way, kind, fashion, custom, or mode of acting or being.
Plural form of 'manner'. See 'manner'.
Manoah; a man named in Scripture, father of Samson.
A male servant or bondman.
Plural form of 'manservant'. See 'manservant'.
Mansions; like the mansiones of the Vulgate, which our translators followed, this word is used in its primary meaning of 'dwelling places,' 'resting places' (Gk.
Manslayer; one who kills another person, especially in the legal sense addressed by the cities of refuge.
Plural form of 'manslayer'. See 'manslayer'.
Mantle; a cloak, outer garment, or covering thrown over the shoulders.
Plural form of 'mantle'. See 'mantle'.
Many; numerous in number.
Maoch; father of Achish king of Gath.
Maon; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place-name.
Plural form of 'maonite'. See 'maonite'.
Maonites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tel Main.

definitions: Mar; to spoil, injure, deface, or make less perfect.
Mara; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Elimelech, mother of Chilion.
Marah; a sea or body of water mentioned in Scripture.
Marah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Hawarah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Maralah; a border town or point in Zebulun's inheritance.
Maralah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Thorah.

definitions: Maranatha; an Aramaic expression meaning the Lord comes or our Lord, come.
Marble; a hard polished stone used in building or ornament.
March; to go forward in order, especially as troops.
As a noun, an ordered movement or march.
Marched; past tense or past participial form of 'march'. See 'march'.
Marchedst; means thou didst march.
Marcus; a man named in Scripture.
Mareshah; a fortified town in the Shephelah of Judah, important in the histories of Asa and later Judean defense.
Mareshah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Maresha.

definitions: Plural form of 'mariner'. See 'mariner'.
Plural form of 'marish'. See 'marish'.
To observe closely, note, or take heed of.
Also a sign, token, or target.
Markest; older second-person singular form of 'mark', used with 'thou'. See 'mark'.
Market; the place of buying and selling, or the trade carried on there.
Marketh; older third-person singular form of 'mark'. See 'mark'.
Marketplace; a public place where goods are bought and sold.
Plural form of 'marketplace'. See 'marketplace'.
Plural form of 'market'. See 'market'.
Plural form of 'mark'. See 'mark'.
Maroth; a Judean place named in Micah's lament over coming disaster.
Maroth.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Qufin.

definitions: Marred; past tense or past participial form of 'mar'. See 'mar'.
the covenant state or union of husband and wife.
Plural form of 'marriage'. See 'marriage'.
Married; past tense or past participial form of 'marry'. See 'marry'.
Marrieth; older third-person singular form of 'marry'. See 'marry'.
Marrow; the soft substance within bones, and figuratively the richest or choicest part.
Marry; to take in marriage, give in marriage, or become joined as husband and wife.
Marrying; present participial form of 'marry', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'marry'.
Plural form of 'mar'. See 'mar'.
Marsena; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
Mart; a market or place of trade.
Martha; the sister of Mary and Lazarus in Bethany, known from the Gospel accounts of her service and faith.
Martyr; a witness, and especially one who suffers or dies for the truth.
Plural form of 'martyr'. See 'martyr'.
Marvel; a wonder or cause of astonishment.
As a verb, to wonder or be amazed.
Marvelled; past tense or past participial form of 'marvel'. See 'marvel'.
Wonderful, astonishing, or beyond ordinary expectation.
In a marvellous manner or degree.
Plural form of 'marvel'. See 'marvel'.
Mary; chiefly Mary the mother of Jesus and Mary Magdalene, with the name also borne by other New Testament women.
Mash; a son of Aram in the table of nations, elsewhere connected with the form Meshech in some lists.
Mashal; a town of Asher later given to the Levites, related to Mishal in parallel lists.
Mashal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Masons; workers in stone and builders in that craft.
Masrekah; an Edomite royal city named in the genealogy of the kings of Edom.
Masrekah.note: Location: In Edom south or southeast of the Dead Sea, in the hill country toward the Arabah.

definitions: Massa; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a people or territory bearing the name.
Massah; the place of testing where Israel tempted the Lord for water.
Massah.note: Location: In the wilderness on Israel's early route from Egypt, traditionally associated with the Sinai region.

definitions: Mast; the tall upright spar of a ship that carries sails or rigging.
A master; one who has authority over servants, disciples, or a household.
Masterbuilder; a chief builder, and figuratively one who lays a chief foundation.
Plural form of 'mastery'. See 'mastery'.
Masters; those holding authority over servants, disciples, households, or workmen.
Mastery; rule, superiority, skill, or self-control.
Plural form of 'mast'. See 'mast'.
Mate; a companion, fellow, or equal.
As a verb, to join, pair, or match.
Mathusala; the Greek name for Methuselah.
Matred; mother of Mehetabel in the Edomite genealogy.
Matri; the ancestor of the family from which Saul was taken by lot.
The womb.
Often used in the phrase 'openeth the matrix' for the firstborn issuing from the womb.
Mattan; the priest of Baal slain in the reform under Jehoiada, and also a name in the genealogy of Christ.
Mattanah; a wilderness station in Israel's journey east of the Jordan on the approach to Moab.
Mattanah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Medeiyineh.

definitions: Mattaniah; especially the king of Judah whose name was changed to Zedekiah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Mattatha; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mattathah; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's singers and one in Luke's genealogy.
Mattathias; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mattenai; chiefly a post-exilic name in Ezra's lists of returning and reforming families.
A matter; a thing, cause, business, affair, or case.
Plural form of 'matter'. See 'matter'.
Matthan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Matthew.
Matthat; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Matthew; the apostle and evangelist, formerly a tax collector called Levi.
Matthias; the disciple chosen to take the place of Judas among the twelve.
Mattithiah; chiefly a Levitical name borne by singers, gatekeepers, and other temple servants.
Mattock; a digging or grubbing tool like a hoe or pick.
Plural form of 'mattock'. See 'mattock'.
Maul; a heavy hammer or club used for striking.
Maw; the stomach or inward part used in eating, especially of animals.
To have permission or possibility; perhaps to happen.
May; art permitted or enabled to.
Often expresses permission, possibility, or fitness rather than certainty.
Mazzaroth; a term in Job for the ordered constellations or starry signs of the heavens.
Me; the objective first-person singular pronoun.
It seems to me; in my judgment.
Me-Jarkon; the waters or river region associated with the Yarkon near the coast.
Me-jarkon.note: Location: On the coastal plain near the mouth of the Yarkon River north of Joppa.

definitions: Meadow; grassland or low ground fit for pasture or growth.
Plural form of 'meadow'. See 'meadow'.
Meah; a tower-name in Jerusalem, likely linked with the idea of a hundred or a measured extent.
ground grain or flour; also food taken at one time.
Mealtime; the time appointed for eating.
Low in rank or outward state; humble or common.
As a verb, to intend or signify.
Meanest; older second-person singular form of 'mean', used with 'thou'. See 'mean'.
Means; intends; signifies.
Meaning; present participial form of 'mean', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'mean'.
resources or instrumentality by which something is done.
Past tense and past participial form of 'mean'. See 'mean'.
Intended, signified, or designed.
Mearah; a cave or cavernous district named on the northern side of the land in Joshua's boundary description.
Measure; a quantity, standard, portion, limit, or the act of measuring.
Measured; taken by measure, proportioned, or assessed.
Measures; standards, quantities, or acts of measuring.
Measuring; taking measure, proportioning, or assessing.
Food in general; nourishment.
Only sometimes flesh specifically.
Mebunnai; one of David's mighty men, commonly identified with Sibbecai.
Mecherathite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mecherath in Scripture.
Medad; one of the elders upon whom the Spirit rested in the wilderness.
Medan; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Meddle; to interfere, mix oneself in a matter, or busy oneself improperly with what does not belong to one.
Meddled; past tense or past participial form of 'meddle'. See 'meddle'.
Meddleth; older third-person singular form of 'meddle'. See 'meddle'.
Meddling; present participial form of 'meddle', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'meddle'.
Mede; one of the people of Media.
Medeba; a plateau city east of the Jordan frequently mentioned in the wars and prophecies concerning Moab.
Medeba.note: Location: In the Moabite plateau east of the Dead Sea, at or near modern Madaba.

definitions: Medes; the people of Media, later linked with the Medo-Persian power.
Media; the land of the Medes east of Mesopotamia, later linked with the Medo-Persian power.
Media.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell Hagmatana.

definitions: Median; a person of Media or something belonging to the Medes and their realm.
One who stands between parties to reconcile or transact on behalf of both.
Medicine; healing remedy, remedying art, or curative application.
Plural form of 'medicine'. See 'medicine'.
Meditate; to muse, ponder, devise, or speak to oneself thoughtfully.
deep thought, reflection, or devout pondering.
Gentle, lowly, and under restraint rather than self-asserting.
Strength governed, not weakness without courage.
The state, quality, or condition of being meeknes.
Fit, suitable, or proper.
Not chiefly an encounter, though that sense exists in other forms.
Meetest; older second-person singular form of 'meet', used with 'thou'. See 'meet'.
Meeteth; older third-person singular form of 'meet'. See 'meet'.
Meeting; present participial form of 'meet', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'meet'.
Megiddo; the strategic royal city commanding the Jezreel route, famous in Israel's wars and later prophetic memory.
Megiddo.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Megiddo.

definitions: Megiddon; another biblical spelling of Megiddo, the strategic city controlling the Jezreel gateway.
Megiddon.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Megiddo.

definitions: Mehetabeel; the wife of Hadar king of Edom.
Mehetabel; the name of women in Scripture, including one in the genealogy of Edom and one in Nehemiah.
Mehida; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Mehir; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Meholathite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Meholath in Scripture.
Mehujael; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Mehuman; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Mehunim; a named people or tribal group appearing in Judah's history and in post-exilic records, commonly linked with the Meunim.
Mehunims; a southern people or settlement group named in Old Testament warfare, reform, and genealogical records.
Mekonah; a southern town resettled by the returned exiles in the Negev of Judah.
Mekonah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tatrit.

definitions: Melatiah; the Gibeonite who helped repair the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Melchi; the name of men in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Melchi-Shua; a spelling of Malchi-Shua, the son of Saul.
Melchiah; a name-form of Malchiah borne by priests and postexilic figures connected with temple or rebuilding contexts.
Melchisedec; the Greek name for Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of the most high God.
Melchizedek; king of Salem and priest of the most high God, later used as a type of Christ's priesthood.
Melea; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Melech; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Benjamin.
Melicu; a post-exilic priestly name, also written Malluch.
Melita; the island on which Paul was shipwrecked and shown kindness by the inhabitants.
Melody; sweetness of song or ordered musical sound.
Plural form of 'melon'. See 'melon'.
Melted; past tense or past participial form of 'melt'. See 'melt'.
Melts; dissolves, softens, or loses firmness.
It may also speak of the heart failing with fear or sorrow.
Melting; present participial form of 'melt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'melt'.
Melzar; the steward or overseer set over Daniel and his companions in Babylon.
Member; a part or limb of the body, or one part belonging to a whole.
Plural form of 'member'. See 'member'.
something preserving remembrance; also serving to remind.
The faculty of remembering, or remembrance kept in mind.
an ancient capital city of Egypt.
Memucan; one of the princes who advised Ahasuerus concerning Vashti.
Men; persons of mankind, or males in particular.
Menahem; king of Israel noted for violence and for tribute paid to Assyria.
Menan; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Mend; to repair, make better, or restore.
Mending; present participial form of 'mend', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'mend'.
Mene; the Aramaic word in Daniel's writing on the wall, signifying that God had numbered the kingdom and finished it.
Plural form of 'menpleaser'. See 'menpleaser'.
Plural form of 'menservant'. See 'menservant'.
Plural form of 'menstealer'. See 'menstealer'.
Ceremonially unclean as in a woman's monthly impurity; defiled or rejected as unclean.
reference by word or name; also to refer to.
Mentioned; past tense or past participial form of 'mention'. See 'mention'.
Meonenim; the oak or terebinth landmark near Shechem mentioned in early Israelite narrative.
Meonenim.note: Location: Near Shechem in the central hill country, in the vicinity of modern Tell Balatah.

definitions: Meonothai; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Mephaath; a Levitical city on the eastern side of the Jordan in the territory of Reuben.
Mephaath.note: Location: In the tableland east of the Dead Sea in the Reubenite-Moabite region, near modern Umm ar-Rasas.

definitions: Mephibosheth; the crippled son of Jonathan, and also another lesser-known bearer of the name in Saul's house.
Merab; the elder daughter of Saul, first promised to David but given instead to Adriel.
Meraiah; the head of a priestly house in the days of Joiakim.
Meraioth; a priestly name in the ancestral line of the high priesthood.
Merari; a man named in Scripture, son of Levi, father of Mahali.
Merarites; the Levitical family descended from Merari, charged with structural parts of the tabernacle.
Merathaim; a prophetic name or district associated with Babylon in Jeremiah's oracle of judgment.
Goods bought and sold; traffic in wares.
A merchant; one who trades in goods for gain.
In Scripture the word may point to commerce broadly or to a trader of wealth and traffic among nations.
Merchantmen; trading men or merchants, especially those engaged in commerce by sea or caravan.
Merchants; those who trade in goods for gain, often in wider commerce.
Plural form of 'mercy'. See 'mercy'.
Disposed to show mercy; compassionate toward misery or guilt.
Mercurius; the Roman god Mercury, identified by the people of Lystra with Paul in Acts.
Compassion shown to the miserable or guilty; pity joined with help or pardon.
When spoken of God, his gracious readiness to spare, forgive, and do good to the undeserving.
Mercyseat; the covering of the ark of the covenant, where atonement blood was sprinkled and where God signified his presence.
Mered; a descendant of Judah named in the genealogies.
Meremoth; chiefly a priestly and rebuilding-era name borne by men in Ezra and Nehemiah.
Meres; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
a son of Jonathan, also known as Mephibosheth.
Meribah; the place of strife where Israel contended with Moses over water in the wilderness.
Meribah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Meribah-Kadesh; the Kadesh scene of Israel's strife and Moses' failure at the waters of Meribah.
Meribah-Kadesh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ain el Qudeirat.

definitions: Merodach; the Babylonian deity Marduk, named also in personal names and prophecy.
Merodach-Baladan; king of Babylon who sent to Hezekiah, also written Berodach-Baladan.
Merom; the waters or place of Merom in northern Canaan, remembered for Joshua's victory there.
Meronothite; a person from Meronoth, named especially in post-exilic building records.
Meronothite.note: Location: From a place near the Benjamin-Jerusalem district, often linked with the Mizpah region.

definitions: Meroz; the place cursed in Deborah's song for failing to come to the help of the Lord against the mighty.
Meroz.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Marus.

definitions: In a merri manner or degree.
Merry; cheerful, glad, lively, or in some contexts given to feasting or strong drink.
Merryhearted; cheerful, glad, or of a joyful heart.
Mesech; a Greek or alternate name-form of Meshech, a son of Japheth named in the table of nations.
Mesha; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Caleb and a king of Moab.
Meshach; a man named in Scripture.
Meshech; a son of Japheth and the people descending from him, and in prophecy a northern people named with Tubal.
Meshelemiah; a Korhite porter in the temple service, prominent among the gatekeepers in David's ordering.
Meshezabeel; a post-exilic ancestor associated with Pethahiah and the rebuilding records.
Meshillemith; a family ancestor named in the Benjamite or priestly genealogies, related to the form Meshillemoth.
Meshillemoth; the name of men in Scripture, especially among priests and leaders in temple or royal service.
Meshobab; a Simeonite prince in the tribal genealogies.
Meshullam; chiefly a common post-exilic priestly and Levitical name, especially among builders and heads of families.
Meshullemeth; wife of king Manasseh and mother of Amon.
Mesobaite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Mesoba in Scripture.
Mesobaite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Mesopotamia; the land between the rivers, the broad region lying between the Tigris and Euphrates.
Mesopotamia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Harran.

definitions: a portion of food set before someone; also a confused mixture.
a communication sent from one to another.
A messenger; one sent with a charge, report, command, or tidings.
The term may refer to a human envoy or one acting under a more solemn commission.
Messengers; persons sent with a charge, report, or command.
Plural form of 'mess'. See 'mess'.
Messiah; the Anointed One, the promised deliverer and king, corresponding to the Greek Christ.
Messias; the Hebrew title meaning the Anointed One; in the New Testament, Christ.
Met; the past tense of meet.
Mete; to measure, allot, or deal out.
As a noun in older usage, measure.
Meted; past tense or past participial form of 'met'. See 'met'.
Meteyard; a measuring rod or standard of measure.
Metheg-Ammah; a place-name used in the record of David's victory over the Philistines, likely tied to Gath and its control.
Metheg-ammah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Safi.

definitions: Methusael; an early descendant in the line of Cain.
Methuselah; the long-lived patriarch in the line from Adam before the flood.
Meunim; a named people or tribal group appearing in Judah's wars and in the return lists, related to the Mehunim.
