Mezahab; the father of Mehetabel in the Edomite royal genealogy.
Miamin; a priestly name in the temple courses and post-exilic lists, related to Mijamin and Miniamin.
Mibhar; the name associated with one of David's mighty men in Chronicles.
Mibsam; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Ishmael and a son of Simeon.
Mibzar; an Edomite chief in the line of Esau.
Micah; especially the prophet who spoke in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, and also the name of other biblical men.
Micaiah; a man named in Scripture, son of Imlah.
Mice; small rodents, mentioned both literally and in connection with the plague among the Philistines.
Micha; chiefly a genealogical name related to the more familiar form Micah.
Michael; chiefly the archangel who contends on God's behalf, and also a personal name in genealogies.
Michah; a name-form of Micah borne by men named in genealogical and historical records, especially in tribal and Levitical lists.
Michaiah; chiefly the prophet otherwise known as Micaiah and also a name borne by members of royal and priestly families.
Michal; especially Saul's daughter who became David's wife, and also the name of other women in Scripture by related form.
Michmas; a name-form of Michmash, the Benjamite town.
Michmas.note: Location: Often identified with Mukhmas, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Michmash; the Benjaminite town and pass famous for Jonathan's attack on the Philistines.
Michmethah; a ridge or height named in the tribal border descriptions.
Michmethah.note: Location: In the hill country on the border of Ephraim and Manasseh near Shechem.

definitions: Michri; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Midday; noon, the middle of the day.
Middin; a wilderness-side town in Judah near the Dead Sea region.
Middin.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Abu Tabaq.

definitions: Middle; the midst, central part, or what lies between the extremes.
Middlemost; in the very middle or occupying the central position.
Midian; a man named in Scripture, son of Abram, father of Abidah.
Midianite; a member of the people of Midian.
Midianite.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Al Bad.

definitions: Midianites; the people descended from Midian, associated with the desert regions south and east of Canaan and often appearing as traders, neighbours, or enemies of Israel.
Midianitish; belonging to the Midianites or to Midian.
Midnight; the middle of the night.
The middle, center, or inner part among surrounding things.
Midwife; a woman who assists in childbirth.
Plural form of 'midwife'. See 'midwife'.
Migdal-El; a Naphtalite town named among the northern settlements.
Migdal-el.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Majdel Selem.

definitions: Migdal-Gad; a town in the Shephelah of Judah.
Migdal-gad.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Mejdeleh.

definitions: Migdol; a frontier stronghold or tower-town, especially on Egypt's northeastern approach.
Migdol.note: Location: On Egypt's northeastern frontier toward the Sinai route; more than one fortified site may have borne the name.

definitions: Might; strength, power, or force.
Mightest; thou mightest; you might, could, or would be able to.
Mightier; more mighty, stronger, or of greater power.
Mighties; mighty or valiant men.
Mightiest; older second-person singular form of 'mighty', used with 'thou'. See 'mighty'.
In a mighti manner or degree.
Mighty; strong, powerful, or valiant.
Migron; a place near Gibeah mentioned in Saul's time and in Isaiah's description of an invading march.
Migron.note: Location: Near Gibeah and Michmash in Benjamin on the northern approach to Jerusalem.

definitions: Mijamin; a priestly name borne by a course and by men in the post-exilic temple records.
Mikloth; the name of men in Scripture, including a Benjamite ancestor and one set over a course in David's service.
Mikneiah; a Levite associated with the bearing of the ark and temple song.
Milalai; a priest present in the thanksgiving procession at Jerusalem.
Milcah; the wife of Nahor and also the daughter of Zelophehad.
Milch; giving milk, used of animals that are in milk.
Milcom; the god of the Ammonites, described in Scripture as the abomination of Ammon.
Mildew; a blighting or mould-like disease affecting grain, cloth, or surfaces.
Mile; a measure of distance, especially the Roman mile in New Testament context.
Miletum; the Greek city of Miletus where Paul met the Ephesian elders.
an Ionian city where Paul met the Ephesian elders.
Milk; the food drawn from the breast or udder, and figuratively simple nourishment or tender instruction.
A mill is the apparatus or place used for grinding grain into meal.
Millet; a small grain used for food.
Plural form of 'million'. See 'million'.
Millo; a fortification, terrace, or filled structure associated especially with the stronghold in Jerusalem.
Millo.note: Location: In the fortified area of ancient Jerusalem, associated with the City of David and the royal stronghold.

definitions: Plural form of 'mill'. See 'mill'.
A millstone is the stone used in a mill for grinding grain.
In Scripture it may also signify crushing weight or heavy judgment.
Plural form of 'millstone'. See 'millstone'.
Mincing; walking or speaking with affected small movements, or cutting into very small pieces.
Mind; understanding, intention, purpose, or inward disposition.
Minded; past tense or past participial form of 'mind'. See 'mind'.
Remembering; paying heed; taking thought for.
Minding; present participial form of 'mind', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'mind'.
Plural form of 'mind'. See 'mind'.
Belonging to me; the first-person singular possessive form, often used absolutely or for emphasis.
Mingle; to mix together, join, or blend.
Mingled; past tense or past participial form of 'mingle'. See 'mingle'.
Miniamin; the name of men in Scripture, especially priests or officers in the days of Hezekiah and Nehemiah.
Minish; to diminish, lessen, or make smaller.
Minished; past tense or past participial form of 'minish'. See 'minish'.
To serve, attend, or supply what is needed.
Whether in practical service or sacred office must be determined from the passage.
Ministering; present participial form of 'minister', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'minister'.
Ministers; those who serve, attend, or carry out appointed work, whether sacred or practical.
service, ministry, or attendance in office.
Minni; a kingdom or district named in Jeremiah among those summoned against Babylon.
Minni.note: Location: In the Armenian highlands or the region of ancient Mannai north or northeast of Mesopotamia.

definitions: Minnith; a place east of the Jordan noted in Jephthah's victory over Ammon and later for its wheat.
Minnith.note: Location: In Ammonite territory east of the Jordan, in the plateau region near Rabbah.

definitions: A minstrel; a player or singer skilled in music.
In Scripture the term often points more to the musician than to the wandering entertainer of later usage.
Minstrels; musicians or singers skilled in music.
Mint; an aromatic herb named among the small garden herbs.
Miphkad; a gate or gateway mentioned in Scripture.
Miracle; a mighty work or sign beyond ordinary power, bearing divine witness.
Plural form of 'miracle'. See 'miracle'.
Mire; soft wet earth, mud, or figuratively defilement and trouble.
Miriam; the sister of Moses and Aaron, a prophetess in Israel.
Mirma; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Gladness, merriment, or festive joy.
Miry; full of mire, muddy, or foul with clay-like filth.
Miscarrying; present participial form of 'miscarry', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'miscarry'.
Mischief; harm, trouble, injury, or wicked intent.
Plural form of 'mischief'. See 'mischief'.
Harmful, hurtful, or full of mischief.
Wretched, afflicted, or pitiable.
In a miserab manner or degree.
Plural form of 'misery'. See 'misery'.
Misery; distress, wretchedness, or grievous affliction.
Misgab; the stronghold or height named in Jeremiah's oracle against Moab.
Mishael; especially the companion of Daniel later called Meshach, and also the name of other biblical men.
Mishal; another biblical spelling of Mashal, the Asherite town given to the Levites.
Mishal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Misham; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Misheal; another biblical spelling of Mashal, the Levitical town in Asher.
Misheal.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Keisan.

definitions: Mishma; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a line bearing his name.
Mishmannah; one of the Gadite warriors who came to David in the wilderness.
Mishraites; a family or clan named in the Judahite genealogies.
Mispar; a post-exilic person named in the list of those who returned from captivity.
Mispereth; one of the returned exiles named in the lists of Ezra and Nehemiah.
Misrephoth-Maim; a place near the northern coast to which Joshua pursued the fleeing Canaanite kings.
Misrephoth-maim.note: Location: In the far north near the Phoenician coast, south of Sidon near the Litani watershed.

definitions: to fail to hit, reach, or attain; also a failure to do so.
Missed; past tense or past participial form of 'miss'. See 'miss'.
Missing; present participial form of 'miss', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'miss'.
fine vapor or a thin cloud near the ground.
a female master, owner, or woman having authority.
Misused; past tense or past participial form of 'misuse'. See 'misuse'.
Mite; a very small coin of little value.
Plural form of 'mite'. See 'mite'.
Mithcah; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary.
Mithcah.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route between Sinai and the Arabah.

definitions: Mithnite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Mithn in Scripture.
Mithredath; the name of Persian officials in the records of the return and rebuilding.
Mitre; the official head-dress of the high priest or priestly service.
Mitylene; the chief city of Lesbos where Paul's ship stopped in Acts.
Mitylene.note: Location: Commonly identified with ancient theater at Mitylene.

definitions: Mixed; past tense or past participial form of 'mix'. See 'mix'.
Mixt; an older spelling of mixed, meaning mingled or combined.
things mingled together, or a compound so formed.
Mizar; the 'little hill' named in the lament of Psalm 42 near the Jordan-Hermon region.
Mizar.note: Location: In the Jordan-Hermon region north of the Sea of Galilee, near the sources of the Jordan.

definitions: Mizpah; a heap or marked pile of stones mentioned in Scripture.
Mizpah.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jel‘ad, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Mizpeh; a lookout-town name borne by multiple places in Scripture, especially the Benjaminite assembly site associated with Samuel and the district center after Jerusalem's fall.
Mizpeh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell en Nasbeh.

definitions: Mizraim; a son of Ham and ancestor associated with Egypt and its peoples.
Mizzah; a son of Reuel in the line of Esau.
Mnason; an early disciple from Cyprus with whom Paul lodged on the way to Jerusalem.
Moab; the land and people descended from Lot's son of that name.
The name may refer to the country, the nation, or its inhabitants.
Moab.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Kerak.

definitions: Moabite; a member of the people of Moab, east of the Dead Sea.
Moabites; the people descended from Moab, dwelling east of the Dead Sea and often appearing as neighbours, tempters, or enemies of Israel.
Plural form of 'moabites'. See 'moabites'.
Moabitish; belonging to Moab or the Moabites.
Moadiah; a priestly name in the post-exilic lists, related to Maadiah and Maaziah.
Mock; to deride, scorn, or make sport of.
As a noun, an object of scorn.
Mocked; past tense or past participial form of 'mock'. See 'mock'.
Mocker; one who mocks, scorns, or derides.
Plural form of 'mocker'. See 'mocker'.
Mockest; older second-person singular form of 'mock', used with 'thou'. See 'mock'.
Mocketh; older third-person singular form of 'mock'. See 'mock'.
Mocking; present participial form of 'mock', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'mock'.
Plural form of 'mocking'. See 'mocking'.
In a moderate manner or degree.
Gentleness, restraint, and a yielding spirit that avoids excess.
Modest; marked by reserve, decency, or freedom from pride and self-display.
slightly wet or damp.
Moistened; past tense or past participial form of 'moisten'. See 'moisten'.
wetness, dampness, or dew-like fluid.
Moladah; a southern town of Judah and Simeon, later resettled after the exile.
Moladah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Waten.

definitions: Mole; the creature so named, or by some interpretations a burrowing or hidden creature mentioned with idols and uncleanness.
Molech; a false god associated with abominable worship, especially the offering of children in fire.
Plural form of 'mole'. See 'mole'.
Molid; a Simeonite place-name or line-name occurring in the tribal records; when treated as a person-name it refers to that ancestral association.
Mollified; past tense or past participial form of 'mollify'. See 'mollify'.
Moloch; the false god to whom children were offered in abominable rites.
Molten; melted by heat and made to flow.
a brief space of time, or weight and importance.
Money; coin, silver, or property reckoned for payment.
Plural form of 'moneychanger'. See 'moneychanger'.
Plural form of 'monster'. See 'monster'.
A stated division of time, commonly reckoned by the moon.
In a month manner or degree.
Plural form of 'monument'. See 'monument'.
Moon; the lesser light that rules the night, and by extension the monthly cycle marked by it.
Plural form of 'moon'. See 'moon'.
Morasthite; an inhabitant of Moresheth.
Morasthite.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Goded, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Mordecai; the Jew who reared Esther and was advanced in Persia after the downfall of Haman.
Greater in number, measure, or degree.
Moreh; the oak or terebinth landmark near Shechem, and by extension the hill region associated with that name.
Moreh.note: Location: Near Shechem in the central hill country, in the vicinity of modern Tell Balatah.

definitions: Besides; further; in addition to this.
Moresheth-Gath; the Judean town associated with the prophet Micah.
Moresheth-gath.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Goded.

definitions: Moriah; the mount or region associated with Abraham's offering of Isaac and later with the temple mount.
Moriah.note: Location: In Jerusalem at the temple mount, and by earlier association the mountain of Abraham's test.

definitions: Morning; the early part of the day from dawn onward.
The morrow; the next day.
Morsel; a small piece of food or portion.
Plural form of 'morsel'. See 'morsel'.
subject to death; also a human being.
the state of being subject to death.
In a mortal manner or degree.
Mortar; a vessel for pounding, or a cementing mixture used in building.
Morter; mortar, the binding mixture used in building, and figuratively what joins superficially.
Mortgaged; past tense or past participial form of 'mortgage'. See 'mortgage'.
To put to death; to subdue and crush the power of sinful practices.
Mosera; a wilderness station in Israel's journey, associated in Deuteronomy with the area where Aaron died.
Mosera.note: Location: On Israel's wilderness route near Mount Hor and the border of Edom, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Moseroth; an encampment of Israel in the wilderness itinerary, related to the route near Mosera.
Moseroth.note: Location: On the wilderness route near Mount Hor and the Edomite frontier.

definitions: Moses; the lawgiver and servant of God who led Israel out of Egypt.
Greatest in number, measure, or degree.
Mote; a very small particle, speck, or tiny thing.
Moth; the insect that consumes garments, often used as an image of decay.
Motheaten; consumed or spoiled by moths, and figuratively decaying.
A mother; a female parent, ancestress, or one standing in maternal relation.
Plural form of 'mother'. See 'mother'.
Plural form of 'motion'. See 'motion'.
Mouldy; spoiled with mould, stale, or decayed.
Mount; to go up, set upon, or place on, and also a hill or elevated place.
A mountain; a high elevation of land greater than an ordinary hill.
In Scripture mountains often mark strongholds, boundaries, remote places, or sites of notable events.
Mountains; high elevations or ranges of land, often contrasted with valleys and plains.
Mounted; went up, was placed upon, or was set on horseback or another beast.
Mounting; going up, ascending, or setting upon.
Mounts; hills or elevated places, or acts of ascending and setting upon.
To grieve, lament, or sorrow deeply.
Mourner; one who grieves, laments, or is engaged in mourning.
Plural form of 'mourner'. See 'mourner'.
Mourns; grieves; laments.
In a mournful manner or degree.
Mouse; a small rodent named among unclean creatures and plague imagery.
The bodily organ of speech and eating.
By extension it may mean speech itself, command, an opening, an edge, or an entrance.
Capable of being moved; not fixed or immovable.
Was stirred, shifted, affected, or caused to go.
Movedst; means thou didst move.
Mover; one who moves or causes movement.
Moves; changes place; stirs or influences.
Moving; present participial form of 'move', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'move'.
Mower; one who cuts grass or grain.
Plural form of 'mowing'. See 'mowing'.
Mown; cut down by mowing, the past participle of mow.
Moza; a Benjamite ancestor in the line connected with Saul.
Mozah; a town in the inheritance of Benjamin.
Mozah.note: Location: West of Jerusalem in the Benjaminite hill country, often associated with the Qalunya area.

definitions: Much; great in amount, measure, or degree.
Plural form of 'muffler'. See 'muffler'.
Mulberry; the tree so named, associated in Scripture with the balsam or mulberry trees near which David was directed.
Mule; the animal bred from an ass and a horse, used for riding or burden.
Plural form of 'mule'. See 'mule'.
Multiplied; past tense or past participial form of 'multiply'. See 'multiply'.
Multipliedst; means thou didst multiply.
Multiplieth; older third-person singular form of 'multiply'. See 'multiply'.
In a multip manner or degree.
Multiplying; present participial form of 'multiply', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'multiply'.
A multitude; a great number or crowd of persons or things.
Plural form of 'multitude'. See 'multitude'.
fortification, defense, or supplies for war.
Plural form of 'munition'. See 'munition'.
Muppim; a Benjamite ancestor whose descendants formed a clan; related forms of the name include Shupham and Shephuphan.
Murder; unlawful killing, blood-guiltiness, or the act of killing with malice.
As a verb, to kill murderously.
Murderer; one guilty of murder, whether by deed or in heart.
Murderers; persons guilty of murder, whether by deed or in heart.
Plural form of 'murder'. See 'murder'.
Murmur; to complain in a low voice, grumble, or whisper.
As a noun, such a complaint or low sound.
Murmured; past tense or past participial form of 'murmur'. See 'murmur'.
Plural form of 'murmurer'. See 'murmurer'.
Murmuring; present participial form of 'murmur', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'murmur'.
Plural form of 'murmuring'. See 'murmuring'.
Murrain; a deadly plague or disease among cattle.
Muse; to meditate deeply, ponder, or murmur in thought.
As a noun in later use, deep thought or inspiration.
Mused; past tense or past participial form of 'muse'. See 'muse'.
Mushi; a son of Merari from whom the Mushites were reckoned.
Mushites; descendants of Mushi reckoned among the Levitical families.
pertaining to music or harmony.
Musicians; those skilled in playing instruments, especially for ordered music and song.
Musick; music, melody, or the ordered use of instruments and song.
Musing; present participial form of 'muse', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'muse'.
A verb expressing necessity, duty, or what is bound to occur.
Mustard; the mustard plant or its seed, used in Scripture especially for its very small seed and notable growth.
Mustered; past tense or past participial form of 'muster'. See 'muster'.
Mustereth; older third-person singular form of 'muster'. See 'muster'.
Mutter; to speak in a low indistinct voice, murmur, or utter secretly.
Muttered; past tense or past participial form of 'mutter'. See 'mutter'.
reciprocal or shared by each toward the other.
Muzzle; a covering for the mouth or snout of an animal.
As a verb, to bind the mouth or restrain from speaking or feeding.
Belonging to me; the first-person singular possessive form.
Myra; a city of Lycia visited in the course of Paul’s voyage as a prisoner.
Its importance in Acts is chiefly as a port and stage in sea travel.
Myra.note: Location: On the southern coast of Asia Minor in Lycia, commonly identified near modern Demre in Turkey.

definitions: Myrrh; an aromatic gum or spice used for perfume, anointing, and burial.
Myrtle; the fragrant evergreen shrub so called, used in prophetic and festive imagery.
Myself; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to the speaker.
Mysia; the northwestern district of Asia Minor through which Paul and his company passed.
A truth once hidden, now made known by divine revelation.
Not merely something puzzling, but something disclosed by God.
N; the letter n.
Naam; a son of Caleb in the genealogies of Judah.
Naamah; the name of women in Scripture, including the sister of Tubal-cain and the wife of Solomon from Ammon.
Naaman; especially the Syrian captain healed of leprosy by Elisha, and also the name of other biblical men.
Naamathite; the title of Zophar, Job's friend, marking him as a man of Naamath.
Naamathite.note: Location: From a place probably in Arabia or the southeast, though the exact site is unknown.

definitions: Naamites; descendants of Naaman reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Naarah; one of the wives of Ashur in the Judahite genealogies.
Naarai; one of David's mighty men.
Naaran; a town on the eastern side of Ephraim near Jericho in the tribal border lists.
Naaran.note: Location: Often identified with Tell el Jisr.

definitions: Naarath; a border town of Ephraim near the Jericho plain.
Naarath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ein ed Duyuk.

definitions: Naashon; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Naasson; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Nabal; a man named in Scripture, husband of Abigail.
Naboth; a man named in Scripture.
Nachon; a threshingfloor or place-name associated with the transporting of the ark in David's time.
Nachor; a Greek or alternate name-form of Nahor, the name of Abraham's father and also of his brother.
Nadab; especially the son of Aaron and also a king of Israel, with other biblical men of the same name.
Nagge; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Nahalal; a town in Zebulun later assigned to the Levites.
Nahalal.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Nahaliel; an encampment of Israel on the approach to Moab east of the Jordan.
Nahaliel.note: Location: In the uplands east of the Dead Sea on Israel's route toward Moab, possibly near the Wadi Zerqa Ma'in system.

definitions: Nahallal; another biblical spelling of Nahalal, the Zebulunite town later given to the Levites.
Nahallal.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Nahalol; another biblical spelling of Nahalal, the town in Zebulun's inheritance.
Nahalol.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Horbat Sefan.

definitions: Naham; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Nahamani; one of the returned exiles named in Nehemiah.
Naharai; the Beerothite armourbearer of Joab and one of David's mighty men.
Nahash; the name of men in Scripture, especially the Ammonite king in the days of Saul and David.
Nahath; chiefly a genealogical and Levitical name in family and temple records.
Nahbi; the son of Vophsi and spy of the tribe of Naphtali.
Nahor; the name of Abraham's grandfather and also of his brother, both appearing in the ancestral line before the patriarchal migration.
Nahshon; a man named in Scripture, son of Amminadab, father of Salmon.
Nahum; the prophet of Elkosh who pronounced judgment against Nineveh.
Nail; the nail of the body or a metal spike driven in fastening.
As a verb, to fasten with nails.
Nailing; present participial form of 'nail', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'nail'.
Plural form of 'nail'. See 'nail'.
Nain; the Galilean village where Jesus raised the widow's son.
Naioth; the prophetic dwellings or settlement near Ramah where Samuel stayed with the company of prophets.
Naioth.note: Location: Near Ramah in Benjamin, north of Jerusalem.

definitions: Naked; uncovered, unclothed, bare, or exposed.
The state of being uncovered or unclothed.
Often also shameful exposure, especially where modesty, kinship, or ceremonial defilement is involved.
A name; also reputation, authority, character, or that by which a person is declared or known.
Named; past tense or past participial form of 'name'. See 'name'.
In a name manner or degree.
Names; designations, reputations, or persons so named.
Nameth; older third-person singular form of 'name'. See 'name'.
Naomi; a woman named in Scripture, wife of Elimelech, mother of Chilion.
Naphish; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a line bearing his name.
Naphtali; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Guni.
Naphtuhim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Napkin; a cloth for wiping, wrapping, or covering.
Narcissus; the man whose household is mentioned in Paul's greetings to the Romans.
Narrow; strait, confined, limited, or close.
As a verb, to make narrow or straitened.
Narrowed; past tense or past participial form of 'narrow'. See 'narrow'.
Narrower; more narrow, more confined, or more limited.
In a narrow manner or degree.
Nathan; especially the prophet who rebuked David and spoke the promise concerning his house, and also the name of other biblical men.
Nathan-Melech; an officer of the king mentioned in the reforms of Josiah.
Nathanael; the Israelite in whom was no guile, commonly identified with Bartholomew.
A nation; a people joined by common descent, land, or civil order.
It is often used of the Gentile peoples as distinguished from Israel, though not always.
Belonging by birth to a place or people; home-born.
A person born in a place; one of the original inhabitants.
birth, or the circumstances and place of birth.
according to nature or ordinary birth; also one native to a place.
In a natural manner or degree.
inherent character, native constitution, or the created order.
Worthless, evil, or of no account.
Sometimes simply nothing.
Nothing, or worthless and of no account.
The state, quality, or condition of being naughtines.
Naughty; wicked, worthless, or morally bad.
Naum; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Navel; the mark at the centre of the belly where the body was joined to the womb.
Plural form of 'nave'. See 'nave'.
Navy; a fleet of ships, especially for commerce or war.
No; and more than that; a strong denial or correction.
Nazarene; one from Nazareth, and especially the identifying title applied to Jesus as Jesus of Nazareth.
In some contexts, the name is also used for his followers or for the sect identified with him.
Nazarenes; those identified with Jesus of Nazareth, or in some contexts people of Nazareth.
Nazareth; the town of Galilee where Jesus was brought up.
The name often identifies Him as Jesus of Nazareth.
nazareth.note: Location: In lower Galilee, commonly identified with modern Nazareth in northern Israel.

definitions: Nazarite; one separated by a special vow unto God, marked by abstinence and consecration.
Nazarites; persons specially separated unto God by a vow of consecration.
Neah; a border point in Zebulun's inheritance.
Neapolis; the Macedonian port where Paul first landed on the voyage that brought him to Philippi.
Near; close to in place, time, or relation.
Nearer; more near in place, degree, or relation.
Neariah; a descendant of David in the royal genealogy.
Nebai; one of the signers of Nehemiah's covenant.
Nebaioth; the spelling used for Nebajoth, the firstborn son of Ishmael and ancestor of an Arabian people.
Nebajoth; the firstborn son of Ishmael, whose name also stands for the people descending from him.
Neballat; a Benjamite town resettled after the exile.
Neballat.note: Location: Commonly identified with Beit Nabala.

definitions: Nebat; a man named in Scripture, husband of Zeruah, father of Jeroboam.
Nebo; the name of a Babylonian deity and also of persons or places formed from that name.
Nebuchadnezzar; king of Babylon who took Jerusalem and figures prominently in Daniel.
Nebuchadrezzar; a biblical spelling of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon.
Nebushasban; the Rab-saris or Babylonian officer present at Jerusalem's fall.
Nebuzar-Adan; the captain of the guard of Babylon who oversaw the fall of Jerusalem.
Needful, required, or unavoidable.
Plural form of 'necessity'. See 'necessity'.
need, compulsion, or what cannot be avoided.
Necho; a man named in Scripture.
Neck; the part joining the head to the body, and by extension strength, stubbornness, or subjection.
Necks; bodily necks, or figurative symbols of strength, stubbornness, or subjection.
Necromancer; one who seeks knowledge by forbidden dealings with the dead or occult spirits.
Nedabiah; a son in the royal line of Jeconiah.
Needed; past tense or past participial form of 'need'. See 'need'.
Needest; older second-person singular form of 'need', used with 'thou'. See 'need'.
Needs; requires; stands in need of.
Needed, necessary, or useful for the occasion.
Needle; a slender pointed instrument for sewing.
Needlework; embroidered or sewn work done with the needle.
of necessity; necessarily.
Needy; poor, lacking necessary support, or in pressing want.
Sneezings.
Neglect; to disregard, fail to care for, or leave undone.
As a noun, careless omission or disregard.
Neglected; past tense or past participial form of 'neglect'. See 'neglect'.
Neglecting; present participial form of 'neglect', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'neglect'.
Careless, slack, or neglectful in duty.
Nehelamite; the title of Shemaiah the false prophet in Jeremiah, marking him as a man of Nehelam.
Nehelamite.note: Location: From an otherwise uncertain place-name, sometimes associated with the region north of Jerusalem.

definitions: Nehemiah; the cupbearer to Artaxerxes who rebuilt Jerusalem's wall and governed Judah.
Nehum; a returning leader named in the exile lists, commonly linked with the related forms Rehum or Harim.
Nehushta; the mother of Jehoiachin king of Judah.
Nehushtan; the name given to the brazen serpent when it became an object of idolatrous burning of incense.
Neiel; a town on Asher's border near the Carmel and Galilee region.
Neiel.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Yanin.

definitions: A neighbour; one dwelling near, or any other person regarded in relation and duty.
Neighbours; those dwelling near, or other persons regarded in relation and duty.
Neighed; past tense or past participial form of 'neigh'. See 'neigh'.
Neighing; present participial form of 'neigh', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'neigh'.
Plural form of 'neighing'. See 'neighing'.
Not either; not the one nor the other; and not.
Nekeb; a point in Naphtali's border description, probably near Adami and Jabneel.
Nekeb.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Adami.

definitions: Nekoda; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile, some with uncertain genealogy.
Nemuel; the name of men in Scripture, including a Simeonite ancestor; related forms include Jemuel in parallel tradition.
Nemuelites; descendants of Nemuel reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Nepheg; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of David and a descendant in Moses' line.
Nephew; in older usage a grandson, descendant, or other near kinsman; not always limited to the modern sense.
Plural form of 'nephew'. See 'nephew'.
Nephish; a son of Ishmael whose name also marks his descendants.
Nephishesim; the name attached to a family of Nethinims or temple servants in the return lists.
Nephthalim; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Guni.
Nephtoah; a spring or fountain mentioned in Scripture.
Nephusim; the name of a family of Nethinim or temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Nephishesim.
Nepthalim; an alternate name-form of Naphtali and of the people descended from him.
Ner; a Benjamite ancestor of Saul's house, known especially as the father of Abner.
Nereus; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
a Babylonian deity worshiped by the people of Cuth.
Nergal-Sharezer; a Babylonian officer or ruler named in connection with the capture of Jerusalem.
Neri; a name in the genealogy of Jesus recorded by Luke.
Neriah; the father of Baruch and Seraiah in the days of Jeremiah.
A bird's dwelling or resting place.
By figure, a settled and often lofty place of security.
A net is a mesh used to catch fish, birds, or prey.
By figure it may signify entanglement, snare, or hidden danger.
Nethaneel; chiefly a princely and Levitical name borne by many men in Scripture.
Nethaniah; chiefly a royal, priestly, and post-exilic family name, including the father of Ishmael in Jeremiah.
Nether; lower, beneath, or belonging to the lower part.
Nethermost; lowest, deepest, or farthest beneath.
Nethinims; temple servants given to assist the Levites in sanctuary duties.
Netophah; the village whose men are often named among David's warriors and the returned exiles.
Netophah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh.

definitions: Netophathi; a person or group belonging to Netophah.
Netophathite; an inhabitant of Netophah.
Netophathite.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Plural form of 'netophathite'. See 'netophathite'.
Netophathites.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Badd Faluh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Plural form of 'net'. See 'net'.
Plural form of 'nettle'. See 'nettle'.
Network; a net-like work or pattern of interwoven cords or metal.
Plural form of 'network'. See 'network'.
At no time; not ever.
Notwithstanding that; for all that; yet despite what has just been said.
New; fresh, recent, or not formerly existing in that condition.
Newborn; newly born.
As a noun, an infant newly born.
In a new manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being newnes.
Report, tidings, or fresh information.
Next; nearest in place, time, order, or relation.
Neziah; the head of a family whose genealogy was uncertain after the exile.
Nezib; a town in the Shephelah of Judah.
Nezib.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Beit Nesib esh Sharqiyyeh.

definitions: Nibhaz; a god of the Avites, named among the foreign deities set up in Samaria.
Nibshan; a wilderness-side town of Judah near the Dead Sea district.
Nibshan.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet el Maqari.

definitions: Nicanor; one of the seven chosen in Acts to serve the church at Jerusalem.
Nicodemus; the Pharisee and ruler of the Jews who came to Jesus by night and later aided in his burial.
Plural form of 'nicolaitan'. See 'nicolaitan'.
Nicolas; the proselyte of Antioch chosen among the seven in Acts.
a city where Paul intended to winter.
Niger; the surname of Simeon among the prophets and teachers at Antioch.
Near; close at hand; drawing near.
Night; the time of darkness between evening and morning.
Night; the time of darkness.
Plural form of 'night'. See 'night'.
Nimrah; a Transjordan town in the territory of Gad, related to Beth-Nimrah.
Nimrah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tall Bleibel.

definitions: Nimrim; a wadi or seasonal watercourse mentioned in Scripture.
Nimrim.note: Location: In Moab east of the Dead Sea, often linked with Wadi en-Numeirah.

definitions: Nimrod; the mighty hunter before the LORD and founder of an early kingdom after the Flood.
Nimshi; the ancestor of Jehu in the northern royal line.
The number nine.
The number nineteen.
Next after the eighteenth.
The number ninety.
Nineve; the Greek name for Nineveh, the great Assyrian capital visited by Jonah and later judged by God.
Nineveh; the great city of Assyria, especially known from the preaching of Jonah.
It also appears in prophecy as a symbol of imperial pride brought under judgment.
Nineveh.note: Location: On the Tigris in ancient Assyria, commonly linked with the area opposite modern Mosul in Iraq.

definitions: Plural form of 'ninevite'. See 'ninevite'.
The one next after the eighth; occupying the ninth place.
Nisan; the first month of the sacred Hebrew year after the exile, corresponding to Abib.
Nisroch; the Assyrian deity in whose temple Sennacherib was slain.
Nitre; a mineral substance used for cleansing or effervescing action, not exactly the same as modern nitre in every context.
Not any; none.
Noadiah; the name of a prophetess opposed to Nehemiah and also of a Levite in post-exilic records.
Noah; the righteous man preserved with his family through the flood in the ark.
Nob; the priestly city where David received the shewbread and where Saul later slaughtered the priests.
Nob.note: Location: Just north of Jerusalem on the Benjamin ridge, in view of the city.

definitions: Nobah; the man who captured Kenath and called it after his own name.
Noble; of high rank, generous spirit, or honorable quality.
As a noun, one of noble station.
Nobleman; a man of rank or station, one of noble standing.
Plural form of 'noble'. See 'noble'.
Nod; the land east of Eden to which Cain went after his judgment.
Nodab; an Arabian or tribal name associated with the descendants of Ishmael.
Noe; the Greek name for Noah.
Nogah; one of the sons of David born in Jerusalem.
Nohah; a Benjamite descendant named in the tribal genealogies.
Noise; sound, cry, tumult, or report.
Noised; past tense or past participial form of 'noise'. See 'noise'.
Noisome; hurtful, offensive, or unwholesome.
Non; a man named in Scripture, son of Elishama, father of Joshua.
Not one; no person or thing.
Noon; the middle of the day.
Noonday; the middle or brightest part of the day.
Noontide; noon or midday.
Noph; another biblical name for Memphis, the great Egyptian city named often in the prophets.
Nophah; a place named in the Amorite victory song preserved in Numbers.
Nor; and not; neither.
The northern quarter or region.
Northern; belonging to the north or situated toward the north.
Northward; toward the north, or situated on the north side.
Nose; the part of the face used for smelling and breathing, and in some passages the nostril or snout.
Plural form of 'nose'. See 'nose'.
Plural form of 'nostril'. See 'nostril'.
A word of denial or negation.
Marked, distinguished, or worthy of notice.
Note; a mark, observation, sign, or matter to be remembered.
As a verb, to mark, observe, or take notice.
Noted; past tense or past participial form of 'not'. See 'not'.
Not anything; no thing at all.
Notice; observation, attention, or knowledge taken of a thing.
As a verb, to observe or take note.
Notwithstanding; in spite of, nevertheless, or all the same.
Nought; nothing, no value, or what comes to nothing.
Nourish; to feed, sustain, cherish, or cause to grow.
Nourished; past tense or past participial form of 'nourish'. See 'nourish'.
Nourisher; one who nourishes, sustains, or feeds.
Nourisheth; older third-person singular form of 'nourish'. See 'nourish'.
Nourishing; present participial form of 'nourish', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'nourish'.
food, sustenance, or that which supports life.
Novice; one newly entered, inexperienced, or recent in the faith or office.
At this present time; as matters stand.
Number; a count, sum, or company counted.
Counted or reckoned by number.
Numberest; older second-person singular form of 'number', used with 'thou'. See 'number'.
Numbering; present participial form of 'number', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'number'.
Plural form of 'number'. See 'number'.
Nun; a man named in Scripture, son of Elishama, father of Joshua.
Nurse; one who suckles, tends, or brings up a child.
As a verb, to nourish or tend.
Nursed; past tense or past participial form of 'nurse'. See 'nurse'.
Nursing; present participial form of 'nurse', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'nurse'.
A foster father; one who nourishes, raises, or protects with paternal care.
upbringing, training, or nourishing care.
Plural form of 'nut'. See 'nut'.
Nymphas; a Christian in whose house a church met, mentioned in Colossians.
An exclamation or vocative particle used in address or strong feeling.
Oak; the strong tree so called, often used as a landmark or sacred site marker.
Plural form of 'oak'. See 'oak'.
Oar; a pole or blade used to row a boat.
Plural form of 'oar'. See 'oar'.
A solemn appeal to God in confirmation of a word, promise, or testimony.
Obadiah; chiefly the prophet whose book bears his name, though the name is borne by many men in Scripture.
Obal; a Joktanite descendant in the table of nations, corresponding in another record to Ebal.
Obed; the son of Boaz and Ruth and grandfather of David.
a Gittite associated with the keeping of the ark, and also the name of Levites in later lists.
Submission to rightful authority; heedful compliance with command.
Submissive to command; willing to obey.
a bow or act of reverence and submission.
To obey; to hear with submission and do what is commanded.
Obeyed; past tense or past participial form of 'obey'. See 'obey'.
Obeyedst; means thou didst obey.
Obeyeth; older third-person singular form of 'obey'. See 'obey'.
Obeying; present participial form of 'obey', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'obey'.
Obil; the Ishmaelite set over David's camels.
Object; a thing set before the mind or eyes, or something aimed at.
As a verb, to oppose or raise an objection.
An oblation; an offering solemnly presented unto God.
The word especially suits what is brought before Him in worship as a dedicated gift.
Oboth; a wilderness encampment on Israel's route toward Moab.
Oboth.note: Location: Often identified with Ein Weibeh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: not clear or little known; also to darken or make unclear.
darkness, uncertainty, or a low and hidden condition.
careful watching, notice, or observance.
Observe; to watch, heed, keep, or comply with.
Observed; watched, noted, kept, or complied with.
Observer; one who watches, notices, or in some contexts practices superstitious observing of times.
Plural form of 'observer'. See 'observer'.
Observest; means thou watchest, heedest, or keepest.
Observeth; means watches, heeds, keeps, or complies with.
Obstinate; stubborn, fixed in resistance, or hard to turn.
Obtain; to get, receive, gain, or secure.
Obtained; past tense or past participial form of 'obtain'. See 'obtain'.
Obtaineth; older third-person singular form of 'obtain'. See 'obtain'.
Obtaining; present participial form of 'obtain', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'obtain'.
an opportunity, cause, or event.
Occasioned; past tense or past participial form of 'occasion'. See 'occasion'.
Plural form of 'occasion'. See 'occasion'.
work, business, or employment.
Occupied; past tense or past participial form of 'occupy'. See 'occupy'.
Plural form of 'occupier'. See 'occupier'.
Occupieth; older third-person singular form of 'occupy'. See 'occupy'.
Occupy; to use, possess, engage in business, or keep busy.
Occurring or happening; incidental.
Ocran; the father of Pagiel, prince of Asher.
Odd; not paired, not even in number, or unusual.
Oded; the name of a prophet in the days of Ahaz and also of other Israelites in the genealogies.
Hateful, offensive, or deserving aversion.
Odour; smell, fragrance, or scent, whether pleasant or otherwise by context.
Plural form of 'odour'. See 'odour'.
Of; from, belonging to, concerning, or proceeding out of.
Away; from the place or thing in question.
A cause of stumbling, falling, or displeasure.
Sometimes an actual trespass or wrongdoing.
To cause to stumble, sin, or take hurt.
Also to commit an offense against another.
Offender; one who commits an offence or causes offence.
Plural form of 'offender'. See 'offender'.
Presented, especially as a sacrifice or gift; brought near for acceptance.
Offers; presents; brings forward.
Often used of presenting sacrifice or setting something before God.
An offering; that which is brought and presented before God or another.
In Scripture it often means a sacrificial gift or holy portion brought in worship.
Office; an appointed charge, service, duty, or official station.
An officer; one holding an appointed charge, duty, or authority under another.
The word may refer to a civil, military, judicial, or temple subordinate.
Officers; persons holding appointed charges, duties, or authority under higher rule.
The word may refer to civil, military, judicial, or temple subordinates.
Plural form of 'office'. See 'office'.
That which is scoured off as refuse.
Hence, something treated as vile, rejected, or despised.
Offspring; children, descendants, or that which is produced from a parent stock.
Often; frequently.
Often; many times, frequently, or repeatedly.
Oftener; more often, more frequently.
Often; on many occasions.
Ofttimes; often, frequently.
Og; a man named in Scripture.
Oh; an exclamation expressing grief, surprise, earnest desire, or strong feeling.
Ohad; a son of Simeon in the tribal genealogies.
Ohel; a son in the royal line of Jeconiah.
Oil; especially olive oil used for food, light, medicine, or anointing.
Olive oil.
Oiled; past tense or past participial form of 'oil'. See 'oil'.
A perfumed or medicinal oil or salve for anointing.
Old; advanced in age, long standing, or former.
The state, quality, or condition of being oldnes.
The olive tree or its fruit.
Made of olive wood or pertaining to the olive.
Olives; olive trees, olive fruit, or things pertaining to the olive.
Olivet; the mount or ridge of olives east of Jerusalem, commonly called the Mount of Olives.
Olivet.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount of Olives.

definitions: An oliveyard is a place planted with olive trees.
Plural form of 'oliveyard'. See 'oliveyard'.
Olympas; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Omar; a son of Eliphaz in the line of Esau.
Omega; the last letter of the Greek alphabet, used symbolically of the end, especially in the divine title Alpha and Omega.
Omer; a Hebrew dry measure, especially remembered as the daily portion of manna gathered in the wilderness.
Plural form of 'omer'. See 'omer'.
Omitted; past tense or past participial form of 'omit'. See 'omit'.
All-powerful; having unlimited might.
Omri; the king of Israel who established a powerful dynasty and built Samaria.
On; upon; resting above; concerning.
Onam; a Judahite name in the genealogies, distinct from Onan.
Onan; the son of Judah who was judged by God for wickedness in the matter of his brother's seed.
Once; at one time, formerly, or on one occasion.
One; a single person or thing.
Ones; persons or things spoken of as individual units.
Onesimus; the servant restored as a beloved brother in Paul's Epistle to Philemon.
Onesiphorus; the faithful friend who often refreshed Paul and sought him out at Rome.
Plural form of 'onion'. See 'onion'.
Alone; merely; no more than.
Ono; a town in the plain of Benjamin and Dan, known in the post-exilic period.
Ono.note: Location: Commonly identified with Kafr Ana.

definitions: Onward; forward in motion or progress.
As an adjective, directed forward.
Onycha; a fragrant ingredient of the sacred incense.
Onyx; a precious stone used in sacred ornaments and settings.
Opened; made open, began, or disclosed.
Openest; older second-person singular form of 'open', used with 'thou'. See 'open'.
Opens; makes open; reveals or begins access.
It may also mean to unfold understanding or to loose what was shut.
Opening; present participial form of 'open', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'open'.
Plural form of 'opening'. See 'opening'.
In a open manner or degree.
working, activity, or effect produced.
Plural form of 'operation'. See 'operation'.
Ophel; the fortified swelling or hill on the eastern side of Jerusalem associated with the temple area and later rebuilding.
Ophir; a son or ancestral name in the early genealogies, distinct from the famed land of gold.
Ophni; a town in Benjamin's inheritance.
Ophrah; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Opinion; judgment, view, or estimation held in the mind.
Plural form of 'opinion'. See 'opinion'.
a favorable time or occasion.
Oppose; to set against, resist, or stand contrary to.
Opposed; past tense or past participial form of 'oppose'. See 'oppose'.
Opposest; older second-person singular form of 'oppose', used with 'thou'. See 'oppose'.
Opposeth; older third-person singular form of 'oppose'. See 'oppose'.
Plural form of 'opposition'. See 'opposition'.
To crush, burden, or treat harshly by misuse of power.
Oppresseth; older third-person singular form of 'oppress'. See 'oppress'.
Oppressing; present participial form of 'oppress', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'oppress'.
Plural form of 'oppression'. See 'oppression'.
Oppressor; one who crushes, afflicts, or deals harshly with others.
Plural form of 'oppressor'. See 'oppressor'.
Or; introducing an alternative or equivalent.
Oracle; a divine utterance, the holy place where God speaks, or the inspired word so given.
Divine utterances or inspired sayings entrusted from God.
a formal speech or public address.
Orator; a public speaker or advocate skilled in speech.
Orchard; a place planted with fruit trees.
Plural form of 'orchard'. See 'orchard'.
Ordain; to appoint, establish, order, or set apart by authority.
Ordained; past tense or past participial form of 'ordain'. See 'ordain'.
Ordaineth; older third-person singular form of 'ordain'. See 'ordain'.
Order; arrangement, appointed manner, office, rank, or command.
Ordered; arranged, appointed, directed, or commanded.
Ordereth; means arranges, appoints, or directs.
Plural form of 'ordering'. See 'ordering'.
In a order manner or degree.
An appointed order, statute, or prescribed observance.
Ordinances; appointed orders, rites, or established rules.
Usual, customary, or regular.
Oreb; a Midianite prince slain in the days of Gideon.
Oren; a son of Jerahmeel in the genealogies of Judah.
Organ; an instrument of music, and more broadly an instrument or part fitted for a function.
Plural form of 'organ'. See 'organ'.
Orion; the constellation so named among the stars created by God.
an adornment or decorative beauty.
Plural form of 'ornament'. See 'ornament'.
Ornan; a man named in Scripture.
Orpah; the Moabitess who was sister-in-law to Ruth and returned to her own people.
Plural form of 'orphan'. See 'orphan'.
Osee; the Greek name for Hosea, especially used in the New Testament.
Oshea; a man named in Scripture, son of Nun.
Ospray; an unclean bird, commonly identified with an osprey or sea eagle-like bird.
a bird of prey named among the unclean birds.
Ostrich; the large swift bird of desert places, often identified with the owl-like desert bird in some KJV contexts by old zoological naming.
Plural form of 'ostrich'. See 'ostrich'.
Different, remaining, or additional.
Others; other persons or things besides those already mentioned.
Otherwise; in another way, under different conditions, or else.
Othni; a son in the line of Shemaiah among the gatekeepers.
Othniel; the first judge raised up to deliver Israel after the days of Joshua.
Ornamental settings or sockets, especially for precious stones.
Ought; anything at all, or what is due, fitting, or required.
Oughtest; older second-person singular form linked with owing or being bound, meaning hadst to or wast obliged.
Belonging to us; the first-person plural possessive form.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
Out; outside; forth from within.
Outcast; one driven out, rejected, or cast away, or something expelled.
Plural form of 'outcast'. See 'outcast'.
Outer; external, outward, or farther from the centre or inside.
Acts of going out, or the places where something goes forth.
Outlandish; foreign, strange, or belonging to another land.
Outlived; past tense or past participial form of 'outlive'. See 'outlive'.
Outmost; outermost or farthest out.
Violent, insolent, or exceedingly wicked.
Outrun; to run faster than, go beyond, or exceed.
Outside; the outer part or exterior.
Also outwardly or on the exterior side.
Outstretched; past tense or past participial form of 'outstretch'. See 'outstretch'.
Outward; on the outside, external, or directed outward.
In a outward manner or degree.
Past tense of 'outgo' or 'outgo beyond'; went beyond, surpassed, or went out before.
An oven is an enclosed heated place for baking.
Plural form of 'oven'. See 'oven'.
Over; above; across; beyond in measure or authority.
Overcharge; to burden too heavily, load excessively, or exact too much.
Overcharged; past tense or past participial form of 'overcharge'. See 'overcharge'.
To conquer, prevail over, or gain the victory against.
Overcomes; prevails against; gains victory over.
To drive too hard, oppress with labor, or urge beyond proper measure.
Overflow; to run over the bounds, spread beyond measure, or overwhelm.
As a noun, excess or inundation.
Overflowed; past tense or past participial form of 'overflow'. See 'overflow'.
Overfloweth; older third-person singular form of 'overflow'. See 'overflow'.
Overflowing; present participial form of 'overflow', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'overflow'.
Overflown; overflowed or spread beyond bounds.
Overlaid; covered over with a layer, especially of gold or another material.
Overlay; to cover over with another material, especially gold or metal.
As a noun, an outer covering laid over something.
Overlaying; present participial form of 'overlay', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'overlay'.
Overlived; past tense or past participial form of 'overlive'. See 'overlive'.
Overmuch; too much, excessively, or beyond due measure.
to pass over or beyond.
Overpast; gone by, past, or passed over.
surplus or remainder beyond what is needed.
Overran; past tense of overrun.
Overrunning; spreading over, overflowing, or running beyond bounds.
Oversee; to watch over, supervise, or inspect.
Overseer; one who supervises, watches over, or governs, including the office later called bishop.
Plural form of 'overseer'. See 'overseer'.
Overshadow; to cast a shadow over, cover, or overarch with protecting or overwhelming presence.
Overshadowed; past tense or past participial form of 'overshadow'. See 'overshadow'.
Oversight; watchful care, superintendence, or office of supervision.
Overspread; to spread over, cover broadly, or overrun.
Overspreading; present participial form of 'overspread', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'overspread'.
Overtake; to come up with, reach, seize, or come upon unexpectedly.
Overtaken; caught up with, surprised, or overcome; the past participle of overtake.
Overtaketh; older third-person singular form of 'overtake'. See 'overtake'.
Overthrew; the past tense of overthrow.
Overthrow; to cast down, subvert, destroy, or defeat utterly.
As a noun, ruin or defeat.
Overthroweth; older third-person singular form of 'overthrow'. See 'overthrow'.
Overthrown; thrown down, defeated, or ruined; the past participle of overthrow.
Overtook; the past tense of overtake.
Overturn; to throw down, upset, overthrow, or bring to ruin.
Overturned; past tense or past participial form of 'overturn'. See 'overturn'.
Overturneth; older third-person singular form of 'overturn'. See 'overturn'.
Overwhelm; to cover over, overpower, or bear down completely.
Overwhelmed; past tense or past participial form of 'overwhelm'. See 'overwhelm'.
Owe; to be indebted, bound, or under obligation.
Owed; past tense or past participial form of 'owe'. See 'owe'.
Owest; older second-person singular form of 'owe', used with 'thou'. See 'owe'.
Oweth; older third-person singular form of 'owe'. See 'owe'.
Owl; a nocturnal bird, used in lists of unclean creatures and desolate places.
Plural form of 'owl'. See 'owl'.
One's own; belonging particularly to the person or thing spoken of.
Owner; one who possesses lawful right in a thing.
Plural form of 'owner'. See 'owner'.
Owneth; older third-person singular form of 'own'. See 'own'.
An ox is the bovine beast used for labor, sacrifice, and provision.
The word may cover either the animal itself or its working use in husbandry.
Oxen; cattle of the ox kind used for labor or sacrifice.
Ozem; the name of men in Scripture, including a brother of David and a descendant of Jerahmeel.
Ozias; a man named in Scripture, son of Amaziah, husband of Jerusha, father of Jotham.
Ozni; a son of Gad whose descendants formed a clan in Israel, elsewhere represented by the form Ezbon.
Oznites; descendants of Ozni reckoned among the families of Gad.
Paarai; one of David's mighty men, called also Naarai in the parallel list.
Plural form of 'pace'. See 'pace'.
Pacified; past tense or past participial form of 'pacify'. See 'pacify'.
Pacifieth; older third-person singular form of 'pacify'. See 'pacify'.
Pacify; to quiet, appease, or bring to peace.
Padan; the plain or open country associated with Aram and the household of Laban.
Padan.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Harran, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Padan-Aram; the plain or district of Aram associated with the household of Laban and the journeys of Jacob.
Padan-aram.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Harran, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Paddle; a small implement or spade, especially the tool mentioned in the law for the camp.
Padon; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Pagiel; the son of Ocran and prince of the tribe of Asher in the wilderness.
Pahath-Moab; a family or post-exilic house named among those who returned from captivity.
Pai; the city of Hadar, one of the early kings of Edom.
Pai.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Fai.

definitions: Paid; gave what was due, or the past tense and participle of pay.
Bodily suffering or inward distress.
Also labor and travail in some contexts.
Pained; past tense or past participial form of 'pain'. See 'pain'.
Causing pain or grief.
In older use, painstaking, laborious, or diligent.
The state, quality, or condition of being painfulnes.
Painted; past tense or past participial form of 'paint'. See 'paint'.
Paintedst; means thou didst paint or adorn with color.
Painting; present participial form of 'paint', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'paint'.
Pair; two things or persons joined together.
As a verb, to join in twos or match together.
Palace; a royal house, great residence, or fortified hall of rule.
Plural form of 'palace'. See 'palace'.
Palal; one who repaired the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Pale; light in color, wan, faint, or a stake-enclosed boundary in older usage.
The state, quality, or condition of being palenes.
Palestina; a Greek or Latinized form for the Philistine region or land.
Palestina.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Ashkelon.

definitions: Palestine; an older geographical name used for the Philistine coastland and sometimes for the broader land.
Palestine.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Ashkelon.

definitions: Pallu; a son of Reuben whose descendants formed the Palluite clan.
Palluites; descendants of Pallu reckoned among the families of Reuben.
Palm; the inner part of the hand, and also the palm tree or its branch.
Palmerworm; a devouring worm or caterpillar named among the destructive insects of judgment.
Palms; hands, palm trees, or palm branches.
Plural form of 'palsy'. See 'palsy'.
Palsy; paralysis or a disabling palsied condition.
Palti; the son of Raphu and spy of the tribe of Benjamin.
Paltiel; the son of Azzan and prince of the tribe of Issachar, and also the man to whom Michal was given.
Paltite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Palt in Scripture.
Pamphylia; the coastal region of Asia Minor so called in Acts.
It appears especially in connection with apostolic travel and preaching routes.
Pamphylia.note: Location: A coastal region on the south of Asia Minor, along the Mediterranean shore of modern Turkey.

definitions: Pan; a shallow vessel or utensil used for cooking, carrying fire, or holding materials.
Plural form of 'pang'. See 'pang'.
Pannag; a choice article of trade in Ezekiel's list, likely a fine foodstuff or delicacy named among the wares of Tyre.
Pans; shallow vessels or utensils for cooking, carrying fire, or holding contents.
To breathe hard, gasp, or long eagerly after something.
Panted; past tense or past participial form of 'pant'. See 'pant'.
Panteth; older third-person singular form of 'pant'. See 'pant'.
Paper; writing material made into sheets or roll-like use.
Paphos; a city of Cyprus where Paul confronted Elymas and Sergius Paulus believed.
Paphos.note: Location: Usually identified with New Paphos.

definitions: Plural form of 'pap'. See 'pap'.
A comparison, likeness, or illustrative saying, especially one used to teach spiritual truth.
Plural form of 'parable'. See 'parable'.
Paradise; a garden or place of delight, and in New Testament use the blessed abode of the righteous with God.
Parah; a town in Benjamin's inheritance east of the central ridge.
Parah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Ein Fara.

definitions: Plural form of 'paramour'. See 'paramour'.
Paran; a wilderness region, and in some contexts a mountain-associated area in the south.
Paran.note: Location: Commonly placed at or identified with Jebel Musa.

definitions: Parbar; a precinct or suburb-like place connected with the temple area.
Parcel; a part, portion, or distinct piece of land or goods.
Parched; past tense or past participial form of 'parch'. See 'parch'.
Plural form of 'parchment'. See 'parchment'.
Pardon; forgiveness or remission of offense.
As a verb, to forgive or spare from penalty.
Pardoned; past tense or past participial form of 'pardon'. See 'pardon'.
Pardoneth; older third-person singular form of 'pardon'. See 'pardon'.
Pare; to cut away the outer part, trim, or shave off.
Plural form of 'parent'. See 'parent'.
Parlour; an inner room or chamber, often a more private room in a house.
Plural form of 'parlour'. See 'parlour'.
Parmashta; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
Parmenas; one of the seven chosen in Acts to serve the church at Jerusalem.
Parnach; the father of Elizaphan, prince of the tribe of Zebulun.
Parosh; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Parshandatha; one of the sons of Haman slain in the days of Esther.
A part; a portion, share, or side of a thing.
One who shares in a thing; a participant or partner.
Partakest; older second-person singular form of 'partake', used with 'thou'. See 'partake'.
Parted; past tense or past participial form of 'part'. See 'part'.
Parteth; older third-person singular form of 'part'. See 'part'.
Parthians; the people of Parthia, named among those present at Pentecost.
biased, favoring one side, or not complete.
unfair preference or favoritism.
Particular; distinct, individual, or special.
As a noun, an individual point or detail.
In a particular manner or degree.
Plural form of 'party'. See 'party'.
Parting; present participial form of 'part', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'part'.
a dividing structure or the act of dividing.
In part; to some extent; not wholly.
Partner; one who shares in a work, business, or companionship with another.
Plural form of 'partner'. See 'partner'.
Partridge; a game bird named in Scripture, sometimes used in imagery of restless or vain pursuit.
Parts; portions, regions, or divisions of a thing.
Paruah; the father of Jehoshaphat, one of Solomon's officers.
Parvaim; a region famous for fine gold used in Solomon's temple furnishings.
Pas-Dammim; the battlefield area between Socoh and Azekah where David fought Goliath, also called Ephes-Dammim.
Pas-dammim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Damun.

definitions: Pasach; a son in the line of Judah.
Paseah; the name of men in Scripture, including a family ancestor and one who repaired the wall.
Pashur; chiefly the priestly name associated with those who opposed Jeremiah, though borne by more than one priest.
the act of passing, or a route, pass, or ford.
Plural form of 'passage'. See 'passage'.
Passed; went by, crossed over, or came to an end.
Passedst; means thou didst pass.
Plural form of 'passenger'. See 'passenger'.
Passest; thou passest; you pass, go by, move onward, or surpass.
Passes; goes by; moves onward.
It may also mean surpasses, comes to an end, or is transferred from one state to another.
Passing; present participial form of 'pass', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'pass'.
strong feeling; suffering; especially the sufferings of Christ.
Passions; qames v.
The Passover; the feast appointed in memory of the LORD's passing over Israel in Egypt, and the lamb connected with that feast.
Plural form of 'passover'. See 'passover'.
Past; gone by, beyond, or former time.
Pastor; a shepherd, and figuratively one who feeds and guides God's people.
Plural form of 'pastor'. See 'pastor'.
grazing land or fodder; also to feed on such land.
Plural form of 'pasture'. See 'pasture'.
Patara; the Lycian port city where Paul found a ship sailing to Phoenicia.
Pate; the head or crown of the head.
Path; a way, track, course, or manner of life.
Pathros; Upper Egypt, the southern region of Egypt from which some Jews later returned.
Pathrusim; a people descended from Mizraim in the table of nations.
Paths; ways, tracks, courses, or manners of life.
Pathway; a path, track, or beaten way.
Steadfast endurance under trial; a calm continuing under burden without giving way.
Enduring calmly; able to bear delay, trial, or injury without murmuring.
In a patient manner or degree.
Patmos; the island where John received the visions recorded in Revelation.
The name is closely linked with exile, prophetic vision, and the Apocalypse.
Patmos.note: Location: A small island in the Aegean Sea off the coast of Asia Minor, commonly identified with modern Patmos.

definitions: Patriarch; one of the ancient fathers of the covenant people, and more broadly a chief father or ancestral head.
Plural form of 'patriarch'. See 'patriarch'.
Patrimony; inheritance from a father or ancestors, ancestral estate.
Patrobas; a Christian greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Pattern; model, example, form, or design set for imitation or construction.
Plural form of 'pattern'. See 'pattern'.
Pau; another biblical spelling of Pai, the city of Hadar king of Edom.
Pau.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi Fai.

definitions: Paul; the apostle of that name, formerly called Saul of Tarsus.
the Roman name borne by Sergius Paulus.
Paved; laid or covered with stones or a firm surface.
a paved surface or stone floor.
Pavilion; a tent, tabernacle, or covered shelter, sometimes used of royal or divine hiding place.
Plural form of 'pavilion'. See 'pavilion'.
Paw; the foot of a beast with claws or pads.
Paweth; older third-person singular form of 'paws'. See 'paws'.
Plural form of 'paw'. See 'paw'.
Pay; to give what is owed, render, or discharge a debt or vow.
Payed; past tense or past participial form of 'pay'. See 'pay'.
Payeth; older third-person singular form of 'pay'. See 'pay'.
the act of paying, or the thing paid.
Peace; quietness, safety, harmony, or well-being.
Disposed to peace; gentle and not quarrelsome.
In a peaceab manner or degree.
Plural form of 'peacemaker'. See 'peacemaker'.
Plural form of 'peacock'. See 'peacock'.
A pearl is a precious gem prized for beauty and worth.
In Scripture it often signifies what is of high price and value.
Plural form of 'pearl'. See 'pearl'.
Specially one's own; set apart as a treasured possession.
Not merely odd or strange.
Pedahel; the father of Kemuel, prince of the tribe of Naphtali.
Pedahzur; the father of Gamaliel, prince of Manasseh.
Pedaiah; chiefly a genealogical name, including the father of Zerubbabel in one biblical lineage.
Plural form of 'pedigree'. See 'pedigree'.
Peeled; past tense or past participial form of 'peel'. See 'peel'.
Peep; to chirp softly, speak in a muttering tone, or look out slightly.
As a noun, a faint chirp or quick look.
Peeped; past tense or past participial form of 'peep'. See 'peep'.
Pekah; king of Israel in the days of Ahaz, associated with the Syro-Ephraimite confederacy.
Pekahiah; king of Israel, son of Menahem, slain by Pekah.
Pekod; a Chaldean district named in prophetic judgment against Babylon and her allies.
Pekod.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with mouth of Nahr al Kassarah.

definitions: Pelaiah; the name of Levites in post-exilic records, especially those teaching or signing the covenant.
Pelaliah; a Levite in the lineage connected with temple service.
Pelatiah; chiefly the prince seen in Ezekiel's vision, though the name is also borne by men in royal lines.
Peleg; the son of Eber in the table of nations, remembered because in his days the earth was divided.
Pelet; the name of men in Scripture, including the father of one joined with Korah's rebellion.
Peleth; a Reubenite associated with Korah's rebellion, and also a descendant named in genealogies.
Pelethites; a body of royal guards or attendants associated with David's court.
Pelican; a large water bird, used in Scripture also as an image of loneliness in desolate places.
Pelonite; a designation attached to certain men in David's records, likely marking family or place origin.
Pen; a writing instrument, and in some passages an enclosure for animals.
the plural of penny, a small coin or sum of money.
Peniel; the place where Jacob wrestled with God and named the site for having seen God face to face.
Peniel.note: Location: Often identified with Tell edh Dhahab esh Sherqiyeh.

definitions: Peninnah; the other wife of Elkanah and rival of Hannah.
Penknife; a small knife for shaping pens or small cutting work.
Penny; the denarius or daily wage so rendered in the New Testament.
Pennyworth; as much as can be bought for a penny, a small amount purchasable for a given price.
Pentecost; the feast occurring fifty days after Passover, known in the Old Testament as the feast of weeks and in Acts as the day when the Holy Ghost was poured out.
Penuel; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah and Benjamin, distinct from the place-name Peniel.
Penury; poverty, want, or scarcity.
A people; a nation, multitude, or body of persons considered together.
Peoples; races, tribes.
Peor; the mountain height in Moab associated with Balaam and with Israel's sin at Baal-peor.
Peor.note: Location: In the Moabite highlands east of the Jordan opposite Jericho, near Beth-peor and the sanctuary of Baal-peor.

definitions: Perhaps; it may be; possibly.
Perazim; the height or ridge remembered from the place where the LORD broke forth against the Philistines in David's day.
Perazim.note: Location: Near the Valley of Rephaim west of Jerusalem, in the area of David's victory at Baal-perazim.

definitions: To see, understand, or become aware of.
Perceived; past tense or past participial form of 'perceive'. See 'perceive'.
Perceivest; older second-person singular form of 'perceive', used with 'thou'. See 'perceive'.
Perceiveth; older third-person singular form of 'perceive'. See 'perceive'.
Perceiving; present participial form of 'perceive', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'perceive'.
utter ruin, destruction, or final loss.
Peres; a Chaldee word from Daniel's writing on the wall, meaning divided.
Peresh; a son of Machir in the tribe of Manasseh.
Perez; a man named in Scripture, son of Judah, father of Hamul.
Perez-Uzza; the place where Uzzah was struck when the ark was brought up in David's day.
Perez-uzza.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Jerusalem.

definitions: Perez-Uzzah; another biblical spelling of Perez-Uzza, the place where Uzzah died beside the ark.
Perez-uzzah.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Jerusalem.

definitions: Complete, whole, or mature.
Often full-grown or fully furnished rather than absolutely sinless.
Perfected; past tense or past participial form of 'perfect'. See 'perfect'.
Perfecting; present participial form of 'perfect', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'perfect'.
Completeness, maturity, or full accomplishment.
In a perfect manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being perfectnes.
Perform; to do, fulfill, accomplish, or carry out.
the carrying out of what was promised, commanded, or intended.
Performed; past tense or past participial form of 'perform'. See 'perform'.
Performeth; older third-person singular form of 'perform'. See 'perform'.
Performing; present participial form of 'perform', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'perform'.
Perfume; fragrant substance or sweet scent.
As a verb, to scent or make fragrant.
Perfumed; past tense or past participial form of 'perfume'. See 'perfume'.
Plural form of 'perfume'. See 'perfume'.
Perga; the Pamphylian city visited by Paul and Barnabas in the first missionary journey.
Pergamos; the city addressed among the seven churches in Revelation.
It is described as a place of strong opposition and dangerous compromise.
Pergamos.note: Location: In western Asia Minor, commonly identified with ancient Pergamon near modern Bergama in Turkey.

definitions: Perhaps; possibly, it may be, or perchance.
Perida; the name attached to a family of Solomon's servants in the post-exilic return lists.
Peril; danger, risk, or exposure to harm.
Dangerous, hazardous, or full of peril.
Plural form of 'peril'. See 'peril'.
To be ruined, lost, or destroyed.
Perishing; present participial form of 'perish', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'perish'.
Perizzite; a member of the Perizzite people of Canaan.
Perizzites; one of the ancient Canaanite peoples of the land.
Perjured; guilty of perjury or having sworn falsely.
leave, liberty, or authorization.
Permit; to allow, suffer, or give leave.
Permitted; past tense or past participial form of 'permit'. See 'permit'.
Very harmful, destructive, or corrupting.
continual, enduring, or lasting without set end.
In a perpetual manner or degree.
Distressed with uncertainty; puzzled and troubled.
bewilderment, distress, or difficult uncertainty.
To pursue with hostility; to harass or oppress for hatred's sake.
Persecutest; older second-person singular form of 'persecute', used with 'thou'. See 'persecute'.
Persecuting; present participial form of 'persecute', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'persecute'.
Plural form of 'persecution'. See 'persecution'.
Persecutor; one who pursues others with hostility for their faith or cause.
Plural form of 'persecutor'. See 'persecutor'.
steadfast persistence in a course.
Persia; the eastern empire that succeeded Babylon and under which the Jews were allowed to return and rebuild.
Persia.note: Location: The land and empire centered east of Mesopotamia, corresponding broadly to modern Iran.

definitions: Persian; belonging to Persia or one of its people.
Persian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tell Hagmatana.

definitions: Persians; the people of Persia or those belonging to the Persian empire under which the Jews returned from exile.
Persis; a beloved woman greeted by Paul in the Epistle to the Romans.
Person; an individual human being, and in some passages outward status or respect of persons.
Plural form of 'person'. See 'person'.
Persuade; to convince, induce, or move by words or reasoning.
Convinced, assured, or won over in judgment.
Persuadest; older second-person singular form of 'persuade', used with 'thou'. See 'persuade'.
Persuadeth; older third-person singular form of 'persuade'. See 'persuade'.
Persuading; present participial form of 'persuade', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'persuade'.
A settled conviction or belief.
Also the act or power of winning over another's judgment.
Pertained; past tense or past participial form of 'pertain'. See 'pertain'.
Pertains; belongs properly to; relates to.
Pertaining; present participial form of 'pertain', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'pertain'.
Peruda; the name of a family of Solomon's servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Perida.
Perverse; turned from the right way, stubbornly corrupt, or twisted in speech or conduct.
In a perverse manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being perversenes.
Pervert; to turn aside from right, corrupt, distort, or misapply.
Perverted; past tense or past participial form of 'pervert'. See 'pervert'.
Perverteth; older third-person singular form of 'pervert'. See 'pervert'.
Perverting; present participial form of 'pervert', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'pervert'.
A grievous contagious disease; plague.
Often one sent as a severe public judgment.
Plague-bearing, infectious, or morally corrupting.
Pestle; a tool for pounding or crushing in a mortar.
Peter; the apostle Simon Peter.
Pethahiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially a ruler or priestly representative in post-exilic Judah.
Pethor; the Mesopotamian home of Balaam on the river.
Pethor.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Aushariye.

definitions: Pethuel; the father of Joel the prophet.
A formal request, earnest asking, or supplication.
Peulthai; a son of Obed-edom named among the keepers and singers connected with temple service.
Phalec; the Greek name for Peleg, in whose days the earth was divided.
Phallu; the name used for Pallu, son of Reuben and ancestor of the Palluites.
Phalti; the man to whom Saul gave Michal, daughter of David, elsewhere called Phaltiel.
Phaltiel; the man to whom Saul gave Michal, David's wife, elsewhere called Phalti.
Phanuel; the father of Anna the prophetess.
Pharaoh; the royal title of the kings of Egypt.
Pharaoh-Hophra; the Egyptian king called Apries in Greek history, mentioned in Jeremiah.
Pharaoh-Necho; the king of Egypt who slew Josiah and campaigned in Syria and the Euphrates region.
Pharaoh-Nechoh; another spelling of Pharaoh-Necho, the Egyptian king in the days of Josiah.
Phares; a man named in Scripture, son of Judah, father of Hamul.
Pharez; a man named in Scripture, son of Judah, father of Hamul.
Pharisee; one of the Pharisees, the strict Jewish sect prominent in the New Testament, noted for zeal toward the law, tradition, and ceremonial exactness.
Pharisees; the strict Jewish sect prominent in the New Testament, often in controversy with Jesus.
Pharosh; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Parosh.
Pharpar; one of the rivers of Damascus named by Naaman.
Pharpar.note: Location: Often identified with Awaj River, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Pharzites; descendants of Pharez reckoned among the families of Judah.
Phaseah; a Judahite and post-exilic name corresponding to the form Paseah.
Phebe; the servant of the church at Cenchrea commended by Paul.
Phenice; the Phoenician coastland, and in Acts also the name of a harbor in Crete.
Phenice.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tyre.

definitions: Phenicia; the Phoenician coastland north of Israel, known for Tyre and Sidon.
Phenicia.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tyre.
