Shadowing; present participial form of 'shadow', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shadow'.
Shadrach; a man named in Scripture.
Shady; full of shade, sheltered from the sun, or darkened by shade.
Shaft; a spear, arrow, or long slender part of a weapon or structure.
Shage; father of Jonathan one of David's mighty men, in a difficult genealogical notice.
Shaharaim; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shahazimah; a town in the inheritance of Naphtali.
Shahazimah.note: Location: Often identified with El Karm.

definitions: Shaked; past tense or past participial form of 'shake'. See 'shake'.
Shaken; the past participle of shake.
Shakes; causes to tremble, move, or be disturbed.
Shaking; present participial form of 'shake', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shake'.
Shalem; the locality near Shechem associated with Jacob's arrival, likely in that district rather than Jerusalem.
Shalim; the district through which Saul passed while searching for his father's lost asses.
Shalim.note: Location: In the hill country north or northeast of Benjamin, on the route Saul followed before meeting Samuel.

definitions: Shalisha; the district through which Saul passed while searching for the lost asses.
Shalisha.note: Location: In the central hill country north of Jerusalem, likely the region later remembered as Baal-shalisha.

definitions: Shall; will, is to, must, or is bound to.
Often expresses certainty, duty, or future determination.
Shallecheth; the gate of Jerusalem's temple or royal complex so called in the account of the porters.
Shallecheth.note: Location: In Jerusalem at the temple or royal complex, probably on the western side near the causeway leading up to it.

definitions: Shallum; especially a king of Israel who reigned briefly, and also the name of priests, gatekeepers, and other post-exilic figures.
Shallun; chiefly a ruler or gatekeeper in Nehemiah's day, and a recurring name in later records.
Shalmai; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Shalman; likely a shortened form for Shalmaneser, cited by Hosea in connection with the destruction of Beth-arbel.
Shalmaneser; the Assyrian king who came against Samaria in the last days of the northern kingdom.
Shalt; thou shalt; you shall or must.
Often expresses future certainty, command, or obligation.
Shama; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's mighty men in some parallel lists.
Shamariah; one of those named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
A meat market; a place where butchered flesh is sold.
Dishonor, reproach, or the painful sense of disgrace.
Shamed; past tense or past participial form of 'shame'. See 'shame'.
Modesty joined with a wholesome sense of shame and reserve.
Not fear of faces, but modest self-restraint.
Disgraceful, dishonorable, or causing shame.
In a shameful manner or degree.
In a shameless manner or degree.
Shamer; the name-form of Shomer used for the father of one of the servants who conspired against king Joash.
Shameth; older third-person singular form of 'shame'. See 'shame'.
Shamgar; the judge of Israel who slew six hundred Philistines with an ox goad.
Shamhuth; one of David's captains over the monthly courses.
Shamir; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place-name and the stone.
Shamma; a name in the genealogies and mighty-men lists, related to the better-known Shammah forms.
Shammah; especially one of David's mighty men, and also the name of a son of Jesse and other biblical men.
Shammai; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Judah.
Shammoth; the name of a warrior or officer in Davidic records, akin to the better-known Shammah forms.
Shammua; chiefly the name of one of David's sons and one of the spies sent from Israel.
Shamsherai; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Shape; form, figure, or appearance.
As a verb, to form or fashion.
Shapen; an old form meaning shaped or formed.
Plural form of 'shape'. See 'shape'.
Shapham; a Gadite name in the tribal genealogies.
Shaphan; the scribe in the days of Josiah through whom the recovered book of the law was brought before the king.
Shaphat; chiefly the father of Elisha and also the name of a tribal spy and other men.
Shapher; a mountain or station in the wilderness journey.
Shapher.note: Location: On the southern wilderness route, probably in the northern Sinai or Negev.

definitions: Sharai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Sharaim; another biblical spelling of Shaaraim, the lowland town in Judah.
Sharaim.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet es Sira.

definitions: Sharar; the father of Ahiam, one of David's mighty men; the name appears under the form Sacar.
Share; a portion, part, or allotment.
As a verb, to divide or partake with others.
Sharezer; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Sennacherib and one sent to inquire at the temple.
Sharon; the broad fertile plain along the Mediterranean coast, used in Scripture as an image of richness and beauty.
Sharon.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Sharon Plain.

definitions: Sharonite; one belonging to Sharon, the coastal plain so called.
Sharp; keen, cutting, severe, piercing, or intense.
Sharpen; to make sharp, keen, or more intense.
Sharpened; past tense or past participial form of 'sharpen'. See 'sharpen'.
Sharpeneth; older third-person singular form of 'sharpen'. See 'sharpen'.
Sharper; more sharp, more keen, or more piercing.
In a sharp manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being sharpnes.
Sharuhen; the southern stronghold besieged by the Egyptians after the Hyksos expulsion and later named in Judah's southern lists.
Sharuhen.note: Location: Often identified with Tall al Ajjul.

definitions: Shashai; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Shashak; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shaul; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of Simeon from whom the Shaulites were reckoned.
Shaulites; descendants of Shaul reckoned among the families of Simeon.
Shave; to cut off hair or beard close to the skin.
Shaved; past tense or past participial form of 'shave'. See 'shave'.
Shaveh; a valley or plain so named in Scripture, especially in the narrative of Abram and the king's dale.
Shaven; the past participle of shave.
Shavsha; the royal scribe or secretary in Davidic and Solomonic administrative records, represented by related forms such as Sheva and Shisha.
The feminine singular personal pronoun.
A sheaf is a bundle of stalks or grain bound together after reaping.
Sheal; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Shealtiel; the father of Zerubbabel in the royal line after the captivity.
Shear; to cut off with shears, especially wool or hair.
As a noun, a cutting or clipping.
Shear-Jashub; the son of Isaiah whose symbolic name means a remnant shall return.
Shearer; one who shears wool or hair from animals.
Plural form of 'shearer'. See 'shearer'.
Sheariah; a Benjamite descendant in the line of Saul.
Shearing; present participial form of 'shear', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shear'.
Sheath; a case or covering for a sword or other blade.
Plural form of 'sheaf'. See 'sheaf'.
Sheba; the name of men in Scripture, especially the Benjamite rebel who lifted up his hand against David.
Shebah; a southern settlement associated with Beersheba in the Negev lists.
Shebah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Beer Sheva.

definitions: Shebam; a Transjordan town in Reuben or Moab, also appearing in related forms such as Sibmah.
Shebam.note: Location: Often identified with Qarn al Qubish.

definitions: Shebaniah; chiefly a Levitical and covenant-signing name in the post-exilic community.
Shebarim; the descent or place near Ai where Israel was struck down before Achan's sin was exposed.
Shebarim.note: Location: Near Ai on its descent toward the valley, sometimes linked with the western slopes above Wadi Makkuk, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Sheber; a man in the genealogies of Judah.
Shebna; the scribe or steward in the days of Hezekiah, rebuked by Isaiah.
Shebuel; a son of Gershom in the line of Moses, set over the treasures in David's organization.
Shecaniah; especially a post-exilic leader who supported Ezra's reform, and also the name of other biblical men.
Shechaniah; chiefly a post-exilic and genealogical name corresponding to the form Shecaniah.
Shechem; the central city between Gerizim and Ebal, important in patriarchal, covenant, and royal history.
Plural form of 'shechemite'. See 'shechemite'.
Shechemites.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Balatah.

definitions: Shed; past tense or past participial form of 'she'. See 'she'.
Shedder; one who sheds, especially blood.
Sheddeth; older third-person singular form of 'shed'. See 'shed'.
Shedding; present participial form of 'shed', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shed'.
Shedeur; the father of Elizur, prince of the tribe of Reuben.
Sheep; the animals so called, often used figuratively of people under care.
Sheepcote; a fold or enclosure for sheep.
Plural form of 'sheepcote'. See 'sheepcote'.
Sheepfold; an enclosure or fold for sheep.
Plural form of 'sheepfold'. See 'sheepfold'.
Sheepmaster; one who owns or oversees flocks of sheep.
Plural form of 'sheepshearer'. See 'sheepshearer'.
Plural form of 'sheepskin'. See 'sheepskin'.
Sheet; a broad piece of cloth or fabric, and in some contexts a sail or large spread covering.
Plural form of 'sheet'. See 'sheet'.
Shehariah; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Shekel; a Hebrew weight and, by use, a measure of money weighed out.
Shekels; units of weight or money so called.
Shelah; the name of men in Scripture, especially the son of Judah by Tamar and also an ancestor in the postdiluvian line.
Shelanites; descendants of Shelah reckoned among the families of Judah.
Shelemiah; chiefly a priestly, gatekeeping, and official name in the later kingdom and post-exilic records.
Sheleph; a son of Joktan in the early genealogies.
Shelesh; a Benjamite name in the genealogies.
Shelomi; the father of Ahihud, prince of the tribe of Asher.
Shelomith; chiefly the daughter of Dibri and also a name borne by Levites and other figures.
Shelomoth; chiefly a Levitical family name in the genealogies and temple service lists.
Shelter; cover, protection, or refuge.
As a verb, to cover or protect.
Shelumiel; the son of Zurishaddai and prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Shem; a man named in Scripture, son of Noah, father of Asshur.
Shema; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the genealogies of Benjamin and Judah.
Shemaah; the father of a gatekeeper line in the chronicler's records.
Shemaiah; chiefly the prophet who warned Rehoboam, though the name is borne by many Levites and post-exilic figures.
Shemariah; chiefly the name of one of David's mighty men and other later figures.
Shemeber; king of Zeboiim in the days of Abram.
Shemer; the owner of the hill on which Omri built Samaria.
Shemida; a son of Gilead in the tribe of Manasseh, ancestor of a clan.
Shemidaites; descendants of Shemida reckoned among the families of Manasseh.
Sheminith; a musical direction in the psalm titles, probably referring to the eighth or to a lower register.
Shemiramoth; the name of Levites associated with music and the bearing of the ark.
Shemuel; a Hebrew name-form of Samuel, used of Samuel the prophet and of others bearing the same name.
Shen; the landmark near which Samuel set up the Ebenezer memorial after victory over the Philistines.
Shen.note: Location: Often identified with Burj el Isaneh.

definitions: Shenazar; a son in the royal line descended from Jeconiah.
Shenir; another name for Hermon used by neighboring peoples.
Shenir.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Shepham; a northern boundary point in the ideal land borders.
Shepham.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around Hermel.

definitions: Shephathiah; chiefly a son of David and a name also borne by post-exilic leaders.
Shephatiah; a name borne by men in royal and post-exilic records; it corresponds to the form Shephathiah.
A shepherd; one who keeps, feeds, and guides sheep.
The word is also used figuratively of rulers, teachers, and supremely of the Lord as shepherd of His people.
Shepherds; those who keep, feed, and guide sheep.
The word may also be used figuratively of rulers or spiritual guides.
Shephi; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Shepho.
Shepho; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Shephi.
Shephuphan; a Benjamite ancestor whose name appears in related forms such as Muppim and Shupham.
Sherah; a daughter in the line of Ephraim, remembered for building Beth-horon and Uzzen-sherah.
Sherd; a broken fragment of pottery or earthenware.
Plural form of 'sherd'. See 'sherd'.
Sherebiah; a Levite of understanding who served with Ezra and Nehemiah.
Sheresh; a woman of Manasseh named in the genealogies.
Sherezer; the name of men in Scripture, including one sent to inquire at the temple and a son of Sennacherib.
Plural form of 'sheriff'. See 'sheriff'.
Sheshach; a cryptic prophetic name for Babylon in Jeremiah's oracle.
Sheshai; one of the sons of Anak at Hebron.
Sheshan; a Judahite ancestor whose line continued through his daughter and the Egyptian Jarha.
Sheshbazzar; a prince of Judah associated with the first return from Babylon and the temple vessels.
Sheth; older third-person singular form of 'she'. See 'she'.
Shethar; one of the princes of Persia and Media in the court of Ahasuerus.
Shethar-Boznai; a Persian official who wrote concerning the rebuilding of the temple.
Sheva; the royal scribe in David's court; parallel records give related forms such as Seraiah and Shavsha.
To show; to make plain or visible.
Shewedst; means thou didst show.
Shewest; thou shewest; you show, make known, or set before the sight.
Sheweth; an older spelling of 'showeth', meaning shows, makes known, or sets before the sight or understanding.
Shewing; present participial form of 'shew', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shew'.
Shibboleth; the word used to distinguish the Ephraimites from the Gileadites; by extension a watchword or test word.
Shibmah; a town east of the Jordan known for its vineyards and associated with Reuben and Moab.
Shibmah.note: Location: In the tableland east of the Dead Sea near Heshbon.

definitions: Shicron; a border place of Judah in the Shephelah toward Ekron.
Shicron.note: Location: In the western border region of Judah toward Philistia and Ekron, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: A shield is a defensive weapon held to ward off blows or missiles.
In Scripture it is also a common figure for protection and defense from God.
Plural form of 'shield'. See 'shield'.
Shigionoth; a musical or poetic term in Habakkuk, likely denoting an impassioned or irregular style of song.
Shihor; a river name often used of the Nile or its eastern branch.
Shihor.note: Location: Usually identified with Nile River.

definitions: Shihor-Libnath; the stream or border district marking part of Asher's southern boundary.
Shihor-libnath.note: Location: Usually placed near the mouth of the Kishon River on the Carmel coast, though the exact identification is uncertain.

definitions: Shilhi; the father of Jehoshabeath, wife of Jehoiada the priest.
Shilhim; a southern town of Judah in the Negev, probably related to Sharuhen in parallel lists.
Shilhim.note: Location: Often identified with Tall al Ajjul.

definitions: Shillem; a son of Naphtali whose descendants formed a clan in Israel.
Shillemites; descendants of Shillem reckoned among the families of Naphtali.
Shiloah; the gently flowing waters of Jerusalem, especially the channel or pool later called Siloam.
Shiloah.note: Location: In Jerusalem on the southeastern side of the city, connected with the Gihon spring and the Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Shiloh; the place where the tabernacle stood in the days before the monarchy, later a warning example in the prophets.
Shiloh.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Seilun.

definitions: Shiloni; an ancestral name associated with the Shilonite line in Judah.
Shilonite; an inhabitant of Shiloh, or one belonging to that place.
Shilonite.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Seilun.

definitions: Shilonites; people of Shiloh, and in genealogical contexts a Judahite family associated with that name.
Shilshah; one of the sons of Zelophehad in a later genealogical form, or a family name in Judah.
Shimea; especially a brother of David, and also the name of descendants in Judah and Benjamin and other biblical men.
Shimeah; chiefly a variant family name in the house of Jesse and David, though borne by more than one man.
Shimeam; a Levite family name in the genealogical records, associated with the line of Gershom.
Shimeath; the Ammonitess mother of one of Joash's assassins.
Shimeathites; a scribal or clan group named among the families of Judah.
Shimei; chiefly the Benjamite who cursed David as he fled from Absalom, though the name is borne by many men.
Shimeon; a name-form of Simeon occurring in genealogical and New Testament records.
Shimhi; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shimi; a genealogical form related to the better-known name Shimei.
Shimites; descendants of Shimi reckoned among the Levitical families.
Shimma; a name in Davidic and Judahite family lines, represented in related forms such as Shammah and Shimeah.
Shimon; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shimrath; a Moabite woman, mother of one of Joash's assassins.
Shimri; the name of men in Scripture, especially Levites and tribal figures.
Shimrith; the Moabitess mother of Jozabad, one of the servants who conspired against king Joash.
Shimron; the name of a son of Issachar and also of a Canaanite royal city in Joshua's conquests.
Shimron-Meron; the royal Canaanite city whose king joined the northern coalition against Joshua.
Shimron-meron.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Shimron.

definitions: Shimronites; descendants of Shimron reckoned among the families of Issachar.
Shimshai; the Persian scribe who wrote against Jerusalem in the days of Artaxerxes.
Shinab; king of Admah in the days of Abram.
Shinar; the plain or land associated with Babel and the early Babylonian realm.
Shinar.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Babylon.

definitions: Shined; past tense or past participial form of 'shine'. See 'shine'.
Shines; gives light; appears bright or glorious.
Shining; present participial form of 'shine', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shine'.
Shion; a town in the inheritance of Issachar.
A ship; a vessel used for travel upon water.
Shiphi; a Levite name in the temple genealogies.
Shiphmite; the title of Zabdi, who was set over David's vineyards, marking him as a man of Shiphm.
Shiphmite.note: Location: From an uncertain place-name, perhaps in the northern hill country or Lebanon-facing region.

definitions: Shiphrah; one of the Hebrew midwives who feared God in Egypt.
Shiphtan; the father of Kemuel, prince of Ephraim.
Shipmaster; the master, captain, or chief officer of a ship.
Shipmen; sailors or men belonging to ships.
Shipping; present participial form of 'ship', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'ship'.
Plural form of 'ship'. See 'ship'.
Shipwreck; the wreck or loss of a ship.
As a verb, to suffer shipwreck or wreck a ship.
Shisha; the royal scribe or secretary in the early monarchy, represented in related forms such as Shavsha and Sheva.
Shishak; the king of Egypt who came up against Jerusalem in the days of Rehoboam.
Shitrai; the Sharonite set over David's herds in Sharon.
Shittah; the acacia tree or its wood, used especially in tabernacle materials.
Shittim; acacia wood or the acacia trees so called, used especially in tabernacle construction.
Plural form of 'shiver'. See 'shiver'.
Shiza; the father of Adina the Reubenite.
Shoa; a people named by Ezekiel among the proud eastern powers associated with Babylon.
Shobab; the name of men in Scripture, including a son of David and one in the line of Caleb.
Shobach; commander of Hadarezer's host, defeated by David, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Shophach.
Shobai; the head of a family of porters among the returned exiles.
Shobal; the name of men in Scripture, including a Horite chief and a Judahite ancestor linked with Kirjath-jearim.
Shobek; the ruler of half part of Keilah who repaired the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Shobi; the son of Nahash of Rabbah who helped sustain David in his distress.
Shocho; another biblical spelling of Socoh, the Judahite lowland town near the valley of Elah.
Shocho.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shochoh; another biblical spelling of Socoh, the lowland town of Judah near David's battle with Goliath.
Shochoh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shock; a heap or gathering of sheaves, especially in harvest imagery.
Plural form of 'shock'. See 'shock'.
Shoco; another biblical spelling of Socoh, the Judahite town in the Shephelah.
Shoco.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Shod; furnished with shoes, sandals, or iron, or the past participle of shoe.
Shoe; footwear for the foot, and in some passages a symbol in legal or covenant acts.
Shoelatchet; the thong or fastening of a sandal or shoe.
Plural form of 'shoe'. See 'shoe'.
Shoham; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Shomer; father of one of the servants who conspired against king Joash, elsewhere connected with the form Shimrith.
Shone; the past tense of shine.
Shook; the past tense of shake.
Shoot; to cast forth, send forth, spring up, or discharge a weapon.
As a noun, a sprout or branch.
Plural form of 'shooter'. See 'shooter'.
Shooteth; means casts forth, sends forth, springs up, or discharges a weapon.
Shooting; present participial form of 'shoot', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shoot'.
Shophach; the Syrian commander defeated by David, whose name also appears under the form Shobach.
Shophan; a Transjordan town built up by the tribe of Gad.
Shophan.note: Location: Often identified with Rujm Ataruz.

definitions: Shore; the edge or bank of the sea, river, or other water.
Shorn; cut off, especially of hair or wool, or the past participle of shear.
Short; not long, insufficient in length or measure, or abrupt.
Shortened; past tense or past participial form of 'shorten'. See 'shorten'.
Shorter; more short in length, measure, or duration.
Soon; in a little time; quickly.
Shot; a discharge from a weapon, or the past tense of shoot in some uses.
A verb of duty, fitness, or expected result.
The shoulder is the upper part of the side where the arm joins the body.
It may also signify bearing, carrying, or a place of strength and responsibility.
Plural form of 'shoulderpiece'. See 'shoulderpiece'.
Plural form of 'shoulder'. See 'shoulder'.
Shouldest; thou shouldest; you ought to, would be expected to, or would in fitting course.
Shout; to cry aloud, raise a war-cry or acclamation, or the cry itself.
Shouted; past tense or past participial form of 'shout'. See 'shout'.
Shouteth; older third-person singular form of 'shout'. See 'shout'.
Shouting; present participial form of 'shout', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'shout'.
Plural form of 'shouting'. See 'shouting'.
Shovel; an implement for taking up ashes, earth, or other loose matter.
Plural form of 'shovel'. See 'shovel'.
Shower; a fall of rain, or a plentiful descent of something.
As a verb, to pour down or bestow abundantly.
Plural form of 'shower'. See 'shower'.
Shows; makes known; displays; causes to be seen.
Shrank; past tense of shrink.
Shred; a small strip or fragment, and to cut or tear into strips.
Plural form of 'shrine'. See 'shrine'.
Shroud; a covering cloth, especially for burial.
As a verb, to cover or wrap over.
Plural form of 'shrub'. See 'shrub'.
Shua; the name of persons in Scripture, including a Canaanite connected with Judah's marriage and a son of Abraham by Keturah in related form.
Shuah; the name of persons in Genesis and Chronicles, including the father of one of Job's friends by related form.
Shual; an Asherite descendant named in the tribal genealogies.
Shubael; the descendant of Moses placed over the treasures, whose name also appears under the form Shebuel.
Shuham; the name-form used for Hushim, the son of Dan whose descendants formed a clan.
Shuhamites; descendants of Shuham reckoned among the families of Dan.
Shuhite; one belonging to Shuah or the people descended from that line.
Shulamite; the woman addressed in the Song of Solomon, probably linked with Shulem or Shunem in the poetic setting of the book.
Shumathites; a family or clan named among the Judahite houses.
Shun; to avoid, keep away from, or turn aside from.
Shunammite; a woman of Shunem, especially the notable woman in the history of Elisha and also Abishag in another context.
Shunem; the Issacharite town associated with the Shunammite woman and the Philistine encampment before Gilboa.
Shuni; a son of Gad from whom the Shunites were reckoned.
Shunites; descendants of Shuni reckoned among the families of Gad.
Shunned; past tense or past participial form of 'shun'. See 'shun'.
Shupham; a Benjamite ancestor whose name appears in related forms such as Muppim and Shephuphan.
Shuphamites; descendants of Shupham reckoned among the families of Benjamin.
Shuppim; a Benjamite ancestor or clan name in the genealogies.
Shur; the wilderness region on the northeastern border of Egypt through which Israel passed.
Shur.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Ar Ruwaysat, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Shushan; the Persian royal city better known as Susa, central in the books of Esther and Nehemiah.
To withhold pity and compassion.
Shuthalhites; descendants of Shuthelah reckoned among the families of Ephraim.
Shuthelah; a son of Ephraim from whom the Shuthelahites were reckoned.
Shutteth; means shuts, closes, or keeps from entrance or exit.
Shutting; the act of closing or fastening.
Shuttle; the instrument used in weaving to carry the thread through the warp.
Sia; the name attached to a family of temple servants in the return lists.
Siaha; the name of a family of temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Sia.
Sibbecai; one of David's mighty men, the Hushathite who slew a Philistine giant.
Sibbechai; one of David's mighty men, whose name also appears under the form Sibbecai.
Sibboleth; the Ephraimite pronunciation of 'Shibboleth' in Judges 12.
Sibmah; the vine-rich town of Moab and Reuben, also related to the form Shebam.
Sibmah.note: Location: Often identified with Qarn al Qubish.

definitions: Sibraim; a place on the northern border between Damascus and Hamath in Ezekiel's vision.
Sibraim.note: Location: Commonly identified with a site between Damascus and Hamath, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Sichem; the Greek name for Shechem, the city lying between Mount Gerizim and Mount Ebal.
Sichem.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Balatah.

definitions: Sick; diseased, weak, or unwell.
A sickle is the curved cutting instrument used in reaping.
In a sick manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being sicknes.
Plural form of 'sickness'. See 'sickness'.
Siddim; the valley associated with the cities later overwhelmed near the Salt Sea.
Siddim.note: Location: In the Salt Sea basin, traditionally placed at the southern end of the Dead Sea.

definitions: A side; one part, flank, or aspect of a thing.
Plural form of 'side'. See 'side'.
Sidon; the ancient Phoenician city on the coast, sometimes standing for its people or territory.
The name may also appear in the form Zidon.
Sidon.note: Location: On the Phoenician coast, commonly identified with modern Sidon in Lebanon.

definitions: Sidonians; the people of Sidon, a Phoenician coastal city north of Israel.
Sidonians.note: Location: Usually identified with Sandikli archaeological site in Sidon.

definitions: Siege; the surrounding and pressing of a city in war.
Sieve; a vessel with a perforated bottom used for sifting.
Sift; to separate by passing through a sieve, and figuratively to test or examine closely.
Sifted; past tense or past participial form of 'sift'. See 'sift'.
Sigh; to breathe deeply in grief, weariness, longing, or distress.
As a noun, a deep breath or expression of sorrow.
Sighed; past tense or past participial form of 'sigh'. See 'sigh'.
Sighest; older second-person singular form of 'sigh', used with 'thou'. See 'sigh'.
Sigheth; older third-person singular form of 'sigh'. See 'sigh'.
Sighing; present participial form of 'sigh', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sigh'.
Plural form of 'sigh'. See 'sigh'.
Sight; seeing; the act or power of vision; also appearance or open view.
Plural form of 'sight'. See 'sight'.
A sign; a token, mark, miracle, or indication pointing to something.
Signed; past tense or past participial form of 'sign'. See 'sign'.
Signet; a seal-ring or engraved seal used for authority and authentication.
Plural form of 'signet'. See 'signet'.
meaning or the sense conveyed by a word or sign.
Signified; past tense or past participial form of 'signify'. See 'signify'.
Signifieth; older third-person singular form of 'signify'. See 'signify'.
Signify; to make known, indicate, or mean.
Signifying; present participial form of 'signify', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'signify'.
Plural form of 'sign'. See 'sign'.
Sihon; a man named in Scripture.
Sihor; a river name commonly used of the Nile or its border waters.
Sihor.note: Location: Usually identified with Nile River.

definitions: Silas; a man named in Scripture.
Stillness or absence of speech.
Also the act of refraining from speech or the bringing of speech to an end.
Still, quiet, or not speaking.
Silk; a fine costly fabric made from silkworm thread.
Silla; a fortified place mentioned in Scripture.
Weak, helpless, or pitiable.
Sometimes simple or easily deceived, not merely playful foolishness.
Siloah; the gently flowing waters of Jerusalem, associated with the pool later called Siloam.
Siloah.note: Location: In Jerusalem on the southeastern side of the city, connected with the Gihon spring and the Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Siloam; a pool or reservoir mentioned in Scripture.
Siloam.note: Location: Usually identified with Pool of Siloam.

definitions: Silvanus; a man named in Scripture.
Silver; the precious metal, often used for money or vessels.
Plural form of 'silverling'. See 'silverling'.
Silversmith; one who works in silver.
Simeon; especially the son of Jacob and the devout man who received the child Jesus in the temple, and also the name of other biblical men.
Simeonites; the members of the tribe descended from Simeon.
likeness, resemblance, or comparison.
Plural form of 'similitude'. See 'similitude'.
Simon; the name of Peter before he was called Peter, and of several others in the New Testament.
Inexperienced; easily led; lacking sound judgment.
Not merely plain or uncomplicated.
Singleness, sincerity, or freedom from mixture and guile.
Not merely lack of complexity.
Simri; a Levite or tribal name in the genealogies and temple records.
Sina; the Greek name for Sinai, the mount where the law was given.
Sina.note: Location: Often placed at or identified with Jebel Musa, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Sinai; the mountain where the law was given through Moses.
The name may also extend to the surrounding wilderness region.
Sinai.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Jebel Musa.

definitions: Since; from the time that; because.
Sincere; pure, genuine, without deceit or mixture.
In a sincere manner or degree.
Purity, singleness, and freedom from hypocrisy or mixture.
Sinew; tendon, strength, or that which binds and strengthens.
Plural form of 'sinew'. See 'sinew'.
Marked by sin; guilty, corrupt, or contrary to God's law.
To sing; to utter in song.
Singed; past tense or past participial form of 'sing'. See 'sing'.
A singer; one who sings, especially in ordered praise or public song.
In the Old Testament the term often refers to persons appointed for sacred music.
Singers; those who sing, especially persons appointed for praise and sacred music.
Singeth; older third-person singular form of 'sing'. See 'sing'.
Singing; present participial form of 'sing', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sing'.
Single; one, simple, unmixed, or undivided.
The state, quality, or condition of being singlenes.
Singular; single, particular, or remarkable beyond the ordinary.
Sinim; a distant land or region named in prophecy.
Its exact identification is uncertain, but the point is of a far country from which people come.
Sinite; one belonging to the Sinite branch named among the Canaanite peoples.
Sink; to go down, settle lower, or descend beneath the surface.
Committed sin; offended against God or right.
Sinner; one guilty of sin, whether generally or as marked by open transgression.
Plural form of 'sinner'. See 'sinner'.
Sinnest; older second-person singular form of 'sin', used with 'thou'. See 'sin'.
Sins; commits sin; acts contrary to God's law.
Sinning; present participial form of 'sin', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sin'.
Sins; offences, transgressions, or acts contrary to the will of God.
Sion; in Deuteronomy this name is used of Hermon, distinct from Zion in Jerusalem.
Sion.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Siphmoth; a southern place to which David sent spoil after defeating Amalek.
Siphmoth.note: Location: Commonly identified with the area around As Samu.

definitions: Sippai; a Philistine giant slain in David's wars; the same giant is given under the related form Saph.
Sir; a respectful form of address used to a man, master, or superior.
Sirah; the well from which Joab called Abner back before Abner was slain at Hebron.
Sirah.note: Location: Often identified with Ain Sarah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Sirion; the Sidonian name for Hermon.
Sirion.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Mount Hermon.

definitions: Plural form of 'sir'. See 'sir'.
Sisamai; a Judahite ancestor in the genealogies.
Sisera; the Canaanite captain defeated in the days of Deborah and Barak.
A sister; a female child of the same parents, a near kinswoman, or one so called in spiritual relation.
Plural form of 'sister'. See 'sister'.
Sit down at table for a meal.
Since; seeing that.
Sitnah; the well over which Isaac's herdsmen contended after leaving Gerar.
Sitnah.note: Location: In the Gerar to Beersheba region where Isaac's servants dug wells, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Sittest; thou sittest; you sit or are seated.
It may also mean you dwell or remain in a settled place.
Sits; is seated; remains in a sitting place.
Often also means dwells, abides in rule, or occupies a settled position.
Sitting; the act or state of being seated.
Situate; placed, located, or set in a certain position.
In older usage, situated or placed.
place, position, or condition.
Sivan; the third month of the post-exilic Hebrew year.
The number six.
Sixscore; a hundred and twenty.
The number sixteen.
Next after the fifteenth.
The one next after the fifth; occupying the sixth place.
The number sixty.
Sixtyfold; sixty times as much, or in a sixtyfold degree.
Size; measure, bulk, or extent.
Plural form of 'sky'. See 'sky'.
Having skill; artful, able, or expert.
In a skilful manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being skilfulnes.
Skill; knowledge, understanding, or practiced ability in a matter.
Skin; the outer covering of the body, or a hide or skin used as material.
Plural form of 'skin'. See 'skin'.
Skip; to leap lightly or pass over quickly.
Skipped; past tense or past participial form of 'skip'. See 'skip'.
Skippedst; means thou didst skip or leap.
Skipping; present participial form of 'skip', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'skip'.
Skirt; the border, edge, lower part of a garment, or the outer edge of a region.
Plural form of 'skirt'. See 'skirt'.
Skull; the bony frame of the head.
Sky; the visible expanse above the earth, the heavens as seen overhead.
Slack; loose, slow, remiss, or weak in zeal or effort.
As a verb, to loosen or abate.
Slacked; past tense or past participial form of 'slack'. See 'slack'.
The state, quality, or condition of being slacknes.
Slander; false and injurious speech against another.
As a verb, to defame by such speech.
Slandered; past tense or past participial form of 'slander'. See 'slander'.
Plural form of 'slanderer'. See 'slanderer'.
Slanderest; older second-person singular form of 'slander', used with 'thou'. See 'slander'.
Slandereth; older third-person singular form of 'slander'. See 'slander'.
In a slanderous manner or degree.
Plural form of 'slander'. See 'slander'.
Slang; a stone or missile cast, or a line / device by context in older usage; not chiefly the modern speech sense.
Slaughter; great killing, especially in battle or judgment.
Slave; one held in bondage under the power of another.
Plural form of 'slave'. See 'slave'.
To kill, especially by violence.
Slayer; one who kills, whether lawfully or unlawfully.
Slayeth; older third-person singular form of 'slay'. See 'slay'.
Slaying; present participial form of 'slay', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'slay'.
Sleeper; one who sleeps.
Sleepest; older second-person singular form of 'sleep', used with 'thou'. See 'sleep'.
Sleeps; rests in sleep.
At times used more gently of death.
Sleeping; present participial form of 'sleep', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sleep'.
Sleight; craft, cunning, or skill used deceitfully.
Slept; the past tense of sleep, sometimes used figuratively for death.
Slewest; means thou slewest, that is, thou didst slay or strike down.
Slidden; the past participle of slide, often used of backsliding or slipping away.
Slide; to slip, glide, or move smoothly along.
As a noun, a slipping motion or decline.
Slideth; older third-person singular form of 'slide'. See 'slide'.
In a slight manner or degree.
Slime; sticky mud, bituminous material, or foul ooze.
Plural form of 'slimepit'. See 'slimepit'.
Sling; a strap or weapon for hurling stones, and to cast or throw with such a sling.
Plural form of 'slinger'. See 'slinger'.
Plural form of 'sling'. See 'sling'.
Plural form of 'slingstone'. See 'slingstone'.
Slip; to slide, fall away, escape, or move quietly.
As a noun, a falling away, a small cutting, or something slender and easily moved.
Slipped; past tense or past participial form of 'slip'. See 'slip'.
Slippery; smooth and apt to cause slipping, and figuratively unsafe or unstable.
Slippeth; older third-person singular form of 'slip'. See 'slip'.
Plural form of 'slip'. See 'slip'.
Lazy, sluggish, or slow in duty.
The state, quality, or condition of being slothfulnes.
Slow; not quick, tardy, or dull in response or motion.
In a slow manner or degree.
Sluggard; a habitually lazy or idle person.
Plural form of 'sluice'. See 'sluice'.
Slumber; sleep, light sleep, or spiritual drowsiness.
As a verb, to sleep lightly or be inactive.
Slumbered; past tense or past participial form of 'slumber'. See 'slumber'.
Slumbereth; older third-person singular form of 'slumber'. See 'slumber'.
Plural form of 'slumbering'. See 'slumbering'.
Small; little in size, amount, rank, or number.
Smallest; older second-person singular form of 'small', used with 'thou'. See 'small'.
Smart; to sting or feel sharp pain.
As an adjective, brisk, active, or showy in later use.
As a noun, sharp pain.
Smell; the sense of scent, an odor, or the act of perceiving by scent.
As a verb, to perceive or emit an odor.
Smelled; past tense or past participial form of 'smell'. See 'smell'.
Smelleth; older third-person singular form of 'smell'. See 'smell'.
Smelling; present participial form of 'smell', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'smell'.
Plural form of 'smiter'. See 'smiter'.
Smitest; older second-person singular form of 'smite', used with 'thou'. See 'smite'.
Smites; strikes, wounds, or afflicts.
A smith; a worker in metal, such as iron or brass.
The word may refer generally to a metal craftsman rather than to only one specialized trade.
Smiths; workers in metal, such as iron or brass.
Smiting; present participial form of 'smite', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'smite'.
Struck, wounded, or plagued.
Smoke; the vapor arising from fire, and figuratively what vanishes, darkens, or signals wrath.
Smoking; present participial form of 'smoke', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'smoke'.
Smooth; even, not rough, or gentle to the touch.
As a verb, to make smooth or flatter by fair speech.
Smoother; more smooth, more even, or more flattering in speech.
Smootheth; older third-person singular form of 'smooth'. See 'smooth'.
Smote; the older past tense of smite, meaning struck or slew.
Smotest; older second-person singular form of 'smote', used with 'thou'. See 'smote'.
Smyrna; the city addressed among the seven churches in Revelation.
It is especially associated there with suffering, poverty, and faithfulness.
Smyrna.note: Location: On the Aegean coast of Asia Minor, commonly identified with modern Izmir in Turkey.

definitions: Snail; the creature so named, used in Scripture as an image of wasting away.
A trap or entangling device.
Figuratively, anything that draws one into danger or sin.
Snared; past tense or past participial form of 'snare'. See 'snare'.
Snatch; to seize suddenly or violently.
As a noun, a sudden grasp or fragment.
Sneezed; past tense or past participial form of 'sneeze'. See 'sneeze'.
Snorting; present participial form of 'snort', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'snort'.
Snout; the projecting nose or muzzle of an animal.
Frozen moisture falling in white flakes from the sky.
Snowy; white like snow or full of snow.
Plural form of 'snuffdish'. See 'snuffdish'.
Snuffed; past tense or past participial form of 'snuff'. See 'snuff'.
Snuffers; plural An instrument for cropping the snuff of a candle.
Snuffeth; older third-person singular form meaning draws in with the nose, despises with disdain, or trims a wick.
In this manner; to this degree; therefore.
Soaked; past tense or past participial form of 'soak'. See 'soak'.
Soap; cleansing substance, especially in the sense of fuller's soap or purifying agent.
Self-restrained; sound in mind; temperate and serious.
Sobriety; soundness of mind, temperance, seriousness, or self-restraint.
Socho; a town of Judah near the region of David and Goliath's battle, better known under the form Socoh.
Sochoh; another biblical spelling of Socoh, the lowland town of Judah near the valley of Elah.
Sochoh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: Socket; a hollow base, holder, or receptacle into which something is fitted.
Plural form of 'socket'. See 'socket'.
Socoh; the Judahite lowland town near the valley of Elah where the Philistines encamped against Israel.
Socoh.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Socho.

definitions: To boil or seethe.
Sodering; the act of joining metals together; an older spelling of 'soldering'.
Sodi; the father of Gaddiel, the spy from Zebulun.
Sodom; the city of the plain destroyed by divine judgment, often paired with Gomorrah.
Sodom.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Sodoma; the Greek name for Sodom, the city destroyed as an example of divine judgment.
Sodoma.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Sodomite; in Scripture, usually a man given to unnatural uncleanness, often linked with idolatrous corruption.
Plural form of 'sodomite'. See 'sodomite'.
Soever; a word joined for emphasis to pronouns or adverbs, meaning at all, in any way, or without exception.
Soft; not hard, gentle, tender, or quiet.
Softer; more soft, less hard, or more gentle.
In a soft manner or degree.
Soil is earth or ground considered as the surface in which things grow or are planted.
Sojourned; past tense or past participial form of 'sojourn'. See 'sojourn'.
Sojourner; one who dwells temporarily in a place, a stranger residing among others.
Plural form of 'sojourner'. See 'sojourner'.
Sojourns; dwells as a stranger for a time; resides temporarily.
Sojourning; present participial form of 'sojourn', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sojourn'.
Solace; comfort, relief, or consolation.
As a verb, to comfort or cheer.
Given over for a price; delivered by sale or betrayal.
A soldier; a man serving in war under command.
The word may also be used figuratively of disciplined service in the cause of Christ.
Soldiers; men serving in war under command.
Sole; the bottom of the foot, and as an adjective the only one.
Serious, grave, and set apart for weighty use or worship.

a solemn observance or ceremony.
In a solemn manner or degree.
Plural form of 'sole'. See 'sole'.
In a solitari manner or degree.
Alone, lonely, or set apart from company.
Solomon; the son of David and king of Israel famed for wisdom and wealth.
A certain portion or number not fully specified.
Sometimes certain persons or things distinguished from others.
Somebody; some person, someone of a certain but unspecified identity.
Something; some thing, some part, or some amount not precisely specified.
Sometime; at some time, formerly, or on a certain occasion.
At times; on some occasions.
Somewhat; in some degree, something, or a certain amount.
A son; a male child, descendant, member of a class, or one bearing a certain character.
A song; words ordered for singing.

Sons; male children, descendants, or members of a class so described.

Soothsaying; present participial form of 'soothsay', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'soothsay'.

Sophereth; ancestor of a family of temple servants who returned from exile.

Plural form of 'sorcery'. See 'sorcery'.

Greatly; grievously; painfully severe.

Sorer; more sore, more grievous, or more painful.

Grief, sadness, or inward pain.
Sorrowed; past tense or past participial form of 'sorrow'. See 'sorrow'.
Sorroweth; older third-person singular form of 'sorrow'. See 'sorrow'.

Sorrowing; present participial form of 'sorrow', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sorrow'.

Plural form of 'sort'. See 'sort'.

Sotai; the head of a family of servants of Solomon.
Sottish; dull, foolish, or brutishly ignorant.
The older past tense and participle of seek.
The living soul or self; the life, person, or inward being of a man.

Soundeth; means is heard, makes a sound, or is sound and whole.
Sounding; making a sound, being heard, or testing depth by line.
The state, quality, or condition of being soundnes.
Sounds; things heard, tones, reports, or healthy and whole states when used verbally.
Sour; sharp or acid in taste, and figuratively harsh or embittered.
As a verb, to make or become sour.
The southern quarter or region.
Southward; toward the south, or situated on the south side.

In Scripture the word often also figures one who spreads the word or begins a work that will afterward bear fruit.

Sows; scatters seed; plants for increase.
Figuratively, it may mean to spread or produce something that will later bear fruit.
Sowing; present participial form of 'sow', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sow'.
Sown; the past participle of sow.
Space; room, interval, extent, or length of time.
Spain; the far western land named by Paul as a hoped-for field of mission.
Spain.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Hispania.

definitions: An older past-tense form meaning 'spoke.'
Spakest; thou spakest; you spoke or uttered words.
Span; a short measure of length, commonly the breadth of the hand from thumb to little finger spread out.
As a verb, to stretch across or extend over.
Spanned; past tense or past participial form of 'span'. See 'span'.
Spare; to refrain from striking, to keep back, or to have pity by withholding judgment.

Spark; a small fiery particle or flash.
Sparkled; past tense or past participial form of 'sparkle'. See 'sparkle'.
Plural form of 'spark'. See 'spark'.
Sparrow; a small common bird, used in Scripture to illustrate littleness and God's care.
Plural form of 'sparrow'. See 'sparrow'.
Spat; past tense of spit.
To utter words, say, or make known by speech.

Speaking; the act of uttering words or addressing others.
Plural form of 'speaking'. See 'speaking'.

Plural form of 'spear'. See 'spear'.
particular, distinct, or set apart; also something specially appointed.
In a special manner or degree.
Speckled; marked with spots or speckles.
Spectacle; a public sight, show, or object of observation.
Sped; went, prospered, advanced, or was caused to hasten.
Speech; spoken words, manner of speaking, or discourse.

Unable or unwilling to speak; silent from astonishment, guilt, or restraint.
Speed; swiftness, success, prosperous progress, or the act of hastening.
In a speedi manner or degree.

Spendeth; older third-person singular form of 'spend'. See 'spend'.
Past tense and past participial form of 'spend'. See 'spend'.
Exhausted, used up, or worn out.
Spewing; present participial form of 'spew', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'spew'.

Spiced; past tense or past participial form of 'spice'. See 'spice'.
Spicery; spices collectively, or a place where spices are kept.
Plural form of 'spice'. See 'spice'.
Spider; the creature so named, used in Scripture also in imagery of frailty and weaving.
Spied; past tense or past participial form of 'spy'. See 'spy'.
Plural form of 'spy'. See 'spy'.
Spikenard; a costly fragrant ointment or perfume.
Spilled; past tense or past participial form of 'spill'. See 'spill'.

Spite; malice, ill will, or injury done out of hostility.

Spitted; past tense or past participial form of 'spit'. See 'spit'.
Spitting; present participial form of 'spit', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'spit'.
