Sufferest; older second-person singular form of 'suffer', used with 'thou'. See 'suffer'.
Suffering; present participial form of 'suffer', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'suffer'.
Plural form of 'suffering'. See 'suffering'.
Suffice; to be enough, satisfy, or meet the need.
Sufficed; past tense or past participial form of 'suffice'. See 'suffice'.
Sufficeth; older third-person singular form of 'suffice'. See 'suffice'.
Sufficiency; enough, adequacy, or competent supply.
Enough, adequate, or fully able.
In a sufficient manner or degree.
Suit; agreement, fitness, or petition, and in some contexts a set of clothing or legal pursuit.
As a verb, to fit, agree with, or make suitable.
Plural form of 'suit'. See 'suit'.
Sukkiims; a people associated with Egypt and its allies in the days of Rehoboam.
Sum; total amount, whole number, or full substance of a matter.
As a verb, to amount or bring together in total.
Summer; the hot season of the year, and by extension summer produce.
In a sumptuous manner or degree.
The sun; the great light of the day.
Sunder; to divide, separate, or break apart.
Sundered; past tense or past participial form of 'sunder'. See 'sunder'.
Several; diverse; of various sorts.
Sung; the past participle of sing.
Sunk; the past participle of sink.
Sunrising; the rising of the sun; the east or sunrise direction.
Sup; to take food or drink in the evening, or to sip.
An overflowing excess; abundance beyond what is fitting.
In moral use, rank overgrowth of wickedness.
More than enough; unnecessary or excessive.
writing placed above, upon, or around something.
misguided religious fear, scruple, or observance.
Excessively devoted to religious fear, scruple, or observance.
Supped; past tense or past participial form of 'sup'. See 'sup'.
Supper; an evening meal, and in the New Testament especially the supper of the Lord or a notable feast meal.
To overthrow by craft, take another's place, or trip up figuratively.
Supplanted; past tense or past participial form of 'supplant'. See 'supplant'.
Supple; flexible, bending easily, or compliant.
Plural form of 'suppliant'. See 'suppliant'.
Supplication; earnest pleading or humble entreaty in prayer.
The word emphasizes need, dependence, and importunity before God.
Supplied; past tense or past participial form of 'supply'. See 'supply'.
Supplieth; older third-person singular form of 'supply'. See 'supply'.
In a supp manner or degree.
Support; that which bears up, sustains, or maintains.
As a verb, to uphold or sustain.
To think, assume, or reckon as likely.
Supposing; present participial form of 'suppose', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'suppose'.
Supreme; highest in rank, authority, or degree.
Sur; a gate or gateway mentioned in Scripture.
Certain, firm, secure, or reliable.
Certainly; assuredly; without doubt.
Plural form of 'surety'. See 'surety'.
the state or obligation of being surety for another.
Security or pledge given for another.
A person who becomes bound on behalf of another.
Surfeiting; present participial form of 'surfeit', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'surfeit'.
Plural form of 'surmising'. See 'surmising'.
Surname; an added name, title, or second designation.
As a verb, to give such a name.
Surnamed; past tense or past participial form of 'surname'. See 'surname'.
Surprised; overtaken unexpectedly, seized suddenly, or filled with wonder.
Susanchites; inhabitants of Susa or the surrounding district, named among peoples settled in Samaria.
Susanna; one of the women who ministered to Jesus of their substance.
Susi; the father of Gaddi, the spy from Manasseh.
Sustain; to uphold, support, bear, or keep from failing.
Sustained; past tense or past participial form of 'sustain'. See 'sustain'.
food, support, or means of living.
Swaddled; past tense or past participial form of 'swaddle'. See 'swaddle'.
Wrapping closely with bands of cloth, especially an infant.
Swaddlingband; the band or cloth in which an infant is wrapped.
Swallow; to take down into the mouth or engulf utterly.
As a noun, the bird so called.
Swallowed; past tense or past participial form of 'swallow'. See 'swallow'.
Swalloweth; older third-person singular form of 'swallow'. See 'swallow'.
Swan; the bird so named among the creatures of Scripture.
Swarest; means thou swarest, especially by oath.
Swarm; a multitude of small creatures moving together.
As a verb, to abound or move in crowds.
Plural form of 'swarm'. See 'swarm'.
To bind oneself with an oath or solemn declaration.
Plural form of 'swearer'. See 'swearer'.
Swears; takes an oath or speaks with solemn appeal.
Swearing; present participial form of 'swear', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'swear'.
Sweat; perspiration.
As a verb, to perspire or labour heavily.
Sweep; to move over with force, clean by brushing, or carry away broadly.
As a noun, a broad range or stroke.
Sweeping; present participial form of 'sweep', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'sweep'.
Sweet; pleasant to the taste, smell, or mind.
Sweeter; more sweet, more pleasant to taste or feeling.
In a sweet manner or degree.
The state, quality, or condition of being sweetnes.
Sweetsmelling; having a pleasant fragrance or odor.
Swell; to grow larger, rise, or be lifted up.
As a noun, a rising or increase.
Swelled; past tense or past participial form of 'swell'. See 'swell'.
Swelling; present participial form of 'swell', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'swell'.
Plural form of 'swelling'. See 'swelling'.
Swept; the past tense and past participle of sweep.
Swerved; past tense or past participial form of 'swerve'. See 'swerve'.
Swift; quick, speedy, or rapid in motion or action.
Swifter; more swift, faster in motion or action.
In a swift manner or degree.
Swim; to move through water by the action of the body or by floating.
As a noun in older usage, a swimming movement or act of crossing water.
Swimmest; means thou swimmest, moving through water by the body or by floating.
Swimmeth; means swims, moving through water by the body or by floating.
Swine; hogs or pigs, considered unclean under the law.
Swollen; enlarged, puffed up, or inflated, the past participle of swell.
Swoon; to faint or fall into a temporary loss of strength.
As a noun, a fainting fit.
Swooned; past tense or past participial form of 'swoon'. See 'swoon'.
A sword; a weapon of cutting, and by extension force, judgment, or warfare.
Plural form of 'sword'. See 'sword'.
Sycamine; the tree named by Christ, commonly identified with a mulberry rather than the sycamore fig.
Sychar; the Samaritan town near Jacob's well where Jesus spoke with the woman of Samaria.
Sychem; a man named in Scripture, son of Hamor.
Sycomore; the sycamore or mulberry-fig tree so called in Scripture.
Plural form of 'sycomore'. See 'sycomore'.
Syene; the Egyptian frontier city at the far south, often paired with Cush in prophetic texts.
A synagogue; a Jewish place of assembly for worship and instruction.
Plural form of 'synagogue'. See 'synagogue'.
Syntyche; a Christian woman at Philippi whom Paul urged to be of one mind with Euodias.
Syracuse; the Sicilian port city where Paul's ship stayed for three days on the voyage to Rome.
Syria; the land and kingdom so called, often including Damascus and the Aramean power.
In some contexts the name reaches to the people or military strength of that realm.
Syria.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Damascus.

definitions: the Aramean kingdom centered at Damascus.
Syria-damascus.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Damascus.

definitions: Syria-Maachah; the Aramean territory associated with Maachah near Israel's northern border.
Syria-maachah.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Tel Abel Beth Maacah.

definitions: Syriack; Syriac or Aramaic language, or belonging to it.
Syrian; one belonging to Syria or to the Aramean peoples of that region.
Syrian.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Damascus.

definitions: Syrians; the Aramean people of Syria, often appearing in warfare or alliance with Israel and Judah.
Syrophenician; a Phoenician of the Syrian coast region, as in the woman who came to Jesus in the Gospel narrative.
T; the letter t.
Taanach; a Canaanite and later Israelite city in the Jezreel Valley, associated with the battles of Deborah and Barak.
Taanach.note: Location: In the Jezreel Valley near Megiddo, commonly identified with Tell Ta'anakh.

definitions: Taanath-Shiloh; a border place of Ephraim eastward toward the Jordan descent.
Taanath-shiloh.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Tana el Fauqa.

definitions: Tabbaoth; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Tabbath; the place toward which the Midianites fled after Gideon's victory.
Tabbath.note: Location: Often identified with Ras Abu Tabat.

definitions: Tabeal; the father of the man whom the kings of Syria and Israel planned to set over Judah.
Tabeel; the name used in Isaiah's prophecy for the father of the man whom Syria and Israel planned to set over Judah; it corresponds to the form Tabeal.
Taberah; the wilderness station called the place of burning because the LORD's fire broke out among Israel there.
Taberah.note: Location: On Israel's wilderness route after leaving Sinai, before the march deeper into the desert, though the exact site is uncertain.

definitions: Tabering; present participial form of 'taber', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'taber'.
The tabernacle; a tent or sacred dwelling, especially the movable sanctuary of Israel in the wilderness.
More broadly the word can mean a dwelling or temporary habitation.
Plural form of 'tabernacle'. See 'tabernacle'.
Tabitha; the disciple at Joppa known for good works and raised by Peter; in Greek her name is Dorcas.
A table; a board or flat surface for food, use, or inscription.
Plural form of 'table'. See 'table'.
Plural form of 'tablet'. See 'tablet'.
Tabor; the isolated mountain rising from the plain of Jezreel.
Tabor.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Jebel et Tur.

definitions: Tabret; a small drum or tambourine used in music and dancing.
Plural form of 'tabret'. See 'tabret'.
Tabrimon; the father of Ben-hadad king of Syria.
Plural form of 'tache'. See 'tache'.
Tachmonite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Tachmon in Scripture.
Tackling; tackle, equipment, rigging, or gear, especially of a ship.
Plural form of 'tackling'. See 'tackling'.
Tadmor; the desert city built or fortified by Solomon, later known as Palmyra.
Tahan; an Ephraimite ancestor from whom the Tahanites were reckoned.
Tahanites; descendants of Tahan reckoned among the families of Ephraim.
Tahapanes; an Egyptian city, usually identified with Daphnae, where Jews fled after Jerusalem's fall.
Tahapanes.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Defenneh.

definitions: Tahath; the name of men in Scripture, especially in the line of Ephraim and among the Levites.
Tahpanhes; an Egyptian city, usually identified with Daphnae, where Jews fled after Jerusalem's fall.
Tahpanhes.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Defenneh.

definitions: Tahpenes; the Egyptian queen who cared for Hadad the Edomite.
Tahrea; a Benjamite descendant in the line of Saul, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Tarea.
Tahtim-Hodshi; a district named in the route of David's census in the far north.
Tahtim-hodshi.note: Location: Often identified with the region around Tell Nebi Mend, though the exact wording remains difficult.

definitions: Tail; the hinder part of an animal, and figuratively the low or dependent end.
Plural form of 'tail'. See 'tail'.
Do not be anxious or over-careful.
To be anxious, concerned, or full of care.
Taken; seized, received, led, or taken away.
Taker; one who takes, receives, or seizes.
Takest; means thou takest, receiving or laying hold on.
Takes; receives, lays hold on, removes, carries away, or accepts.
Taking; present participial form of 'take', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'take'.
Tale; a number, account, report, or narrated matter.
Talebearer; one who carries reports, gossip, or slander from one person to another.
A large weight or monetary measure; in the parable, a sum entrusted to a servant.
Plural form of 'talent'. See 'talent'.
Plural form of 'tale'. See 'tale'.
Talitha; Aramaic for little girl or damsel, preserved in the phrase Talitha cumi.
Speech, discourse, or conversation.
Also to speak or converse.
Talked; past tense or past participial form of 'talk'. See 'talk'.
Plural form of 'talker'. See 'talker'.
Talkest; older second-person singular form of 'talk', used with 'thou'. See 'talk'.
Talketh; older third-person singular form of 'talk'. See 'talk'.
Talking; present participial form of 'talk', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'talk'.
Tall; high in stature or notably lofty.
Taller; more tall, higher in stature.
Talmai; the name of men in Scripture, including the king of Geshur and one of the sons of Anak.
Talmon; a chief porter or gatekeeper in temple records.
Tamah; the name attached to a family of temple servants in the return lists.
Tamar; the name of women in Scripture, especially Judah's daughter in law and the daughter of David who was wronged by Amnon.
Tame; subdued, not wild, or gentle.
As a verb, to subdue or make gentle.
Tamed; past tense or past participial form of 'tame'. See 'tame'.
Tammuz; the deity mourned in idolatrous rites mentioned by Ezekiel.
Tanach; another biblical spelling of Taanach, the important Canaanite and later Israelite city in the Jezreel region.
Tanach.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Ta’anakh.

definitions: Tanhumeth; father of Seraiah the Netophathite in the history after Jerusalem's fall.
Tanner; one whose trade is to prepare hides or leather.
Tapestry; woven ornamental cloth or hangings.
Taphath; a daughter of Solomon and wife of one of his officers.
Tappuah; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place-name.
Tarah; a wilderness encampment in Israel's itinerary.
Tarah.note: Location: Generally placed in the area around Wadi Umm Hashim; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Taralah; a town in Benjamin's inheritance.
Taralah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Erha.

definitions: Tare; a noxious weed among grain, especially in the parable of the tares.
Tare may also mean to determine the empty weight of a container.
Tarea; a Benjamite descendant in the line of Saul, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Tahrea.
Plural form of 'tare'. See 'tare'.
Target; a shield, protective piece of armor, or an object aimed at.
Plural form of 'target'. See 'target'.
Tarpelites; one of the foreign peoples named among those settled in Samaria.
Tarriest; means thou tarriest, remaining, staying, or waiting.
Tarrieth; older third-person singular form of 'tarry'. See 'tarry'.
To remain, stay, or wait.
Tarshish; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the place or maritime region.
Tarsus; the chief city of Cilicia and the native city of Saul, later Paul.
The name is chiefly important in connection with Paul’s background and travels.
Tarsus.note: Location: In Cilicia near the northeastern Mediterranean, commonly identified with Tarsus in modern Turkey.

definitions: Tartak; a god of the Avites, named with Nibhaz among the imported deities of Samaria.
Tartan; an Assyrian military title borne by a commander sent by the king of Assyria.
Task; appointed labour, burden, or assigned work.
Plural form of 'taskmaster'. See 'taskmaster'.
Plural form of 'task'. See 'task'.
Taste; to try by the mouth, experience, perceive, or partake.
As a noun, flavor or perception by tasting.
Tasted; past tense or past participial form of 'taste'. See 'taste'.
Tasteth; older third-person singular form of 'taste'. See 'taste'.
Tatnai; the governor beyond the river in the days of Darius.
Plural form of 'tattler'. See 'tattler'.
Instructed, trained, or caused to know.
Taunt; reproachful or mocking speech.
As a verb, to mock or revile.
Taunting; present participial form of 'taunt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'taunt'.
Plural form of 'tavern'. See 'tavern'.
assessment or levying of taxes or tribute.
Taxed; past tense or past participial form of 'tax'. See 'tax'.
Plural form of 'tax'. See 'tax'.
Taxing; present participial form of 'tax', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tax'.
Teacher; one who instructs, guides, or trains others in knowledge or doctrine.
Plural form of 'teacher'. See 'teacher'.
Teachest; thou teachest; you teach or instruct.
Teacheth; means teaches, instructs, or imparts knowledge or doctrine.
Teaching; present participial form of 'teach', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'teach'.
Tears; rends or pulls apart violently.
Plural form of 'tear'. See 'tear'.
Plural form of 'teat'. See 'teat'.
Tebah; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Tebaliah; one of the sons of Hosah among the gatekeepers.
Tebeth; the tenth month of the Hebrew sacred year in the post-exilic calendar.
Wearisome, burdensome, or causing delay.
The hard parts in the mouth used for biting and chewing.
Often used figuratively of devouring power, rage, or fierce injury.
Tehaphnehes; the Egyptian city better known as Tahpanhes.
Tehaphnehes.note: Location: Usually identified with Tell Defenneh.

definitions: Tehinnah; a Judahite ancestor associated with the city of Nahash.
Teil; a tree-name in Isaiah, commonly identified with the terebinth or linden in older interpretation.
Tekel; the Aramaic word in Daniel's writing on the wall, signifying that one has been weighed in the balances and found wanting.
Tekoa; another biblical spelling of Tekoah, the Judean town of the prophet Amos and the wise woman.
Tekoa.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Teku’a.

definitions: Tekoah; the Judean town south of Bethlehem associated with Amos and several royal-era events.
Tekoah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Teku’a.

definitions: Tekoite; an inhabitant of Tekoa.
Tekoite.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Teku’a.

definitions: Plural form of 'tekoite'. See 'tekoite'.
Tekoites.note: Location: Usually identified with Khirbet Teku’a.

definitions: Tel-Abib; the settlement by the river Chebar where Ezekiel lived among the exiles.
Tel-abib.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Nippur.

definitions: Tel-Haresha; one of the Babylonian settlements from which returnees came but could not prove their genealogy.
Tel-haresha.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Nippur.

definitions: Tel-Harsa; one of the Babylonian settlements named among returnees of uncertain genealogy.
Tel-harsa.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Nippur.

definitions: Tel-Melah; one of the Babylonian settlements named among returnees whose genealogy could not be shown.
Tel-melah.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Nippur.

definitions: Telah; a descendant of Ephraim in the tribal genealogies.
Telaim; the place where Saul numbered the people before the campaign against Amalek.
Telaim.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Abu Tulul.

definitions: Telassar; the Assyrian or Mesopotamian district named among lands conquered by the kings of Assyria.
Telassar.note: Location: Often identified with Til Barsip.

definitions: Telem; the name of men in Scripture, including one of David's gatekeepers and one named in Ezra.
Tellest; older second-person singular form of 'tell', used with 'thou'. See 'tell'.
Tells; relates, declares, or counts.
Telling; present participial form of 'tell', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tell'.
Tema; a son of Ishmael and ancestor of a desert people or region bearing his name.
Teman; the name of an Edomite ancestor, distinct from the region of Teman.
Temani; belonging to Teman or the people of Teman.
Temanite; an inhabitant of Teman in Edom.
Temanites; people of Teman in Edom, known especially from wisdom and prophetic contexts.
Temeni; a Judahite designation in the genealogies.
To mix, soften, or bring into a fitting condition.
Also inward disposition or frame of spirit in some uses.
Self-control; mastery over appetites and passions.
Temperate; self-controlled, moderate, or restrained.
Tempered; past tense or past participial form of 'temper'. See 'temper'.
A violent storm, especially with wind and disturbance.
Stormy, violent, or full of tumult like a tempest.
A temple; a sacred house or sanctuary, especially the house of the LORD at Jerusalem.
The word may also be used figuratively of the body or of the people among whom God dwells.
Plural form of 'temple'. See 'temple'.
pertaining to time or to this present life.
To try, test, or prove.
The word often means to entice toward evil.
Plural form of 'temptation'. See 'temptation'.
Tempter; one who entices to sin, especially a title of Satan.
Tempteth; older third-person singular form of 'tempt'. See 'tempt'.
Tempting; present participial form of 'tempt', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tempt'.
The number ten.
Tend; to attend, care for, or be directed toward.
Soft, delicate, or gentle.
Often compassionate and easily touched in feeling.
Tenderhearted; compassionate, gentle, or easily moved with pity.
The state, quality, or condition of being tendernes.
Tendeth; older third-person singular form of 'tend'. See 'tend'.
Plural form of 'tenon'. See 'tenon'.
Tenor; the general sense, course, or purport of a matter.
Plural form of 'ten'. See 'ten'.
A tent; a movable dwelling of cloth or skins.
The one next after the ninth; occupying the tenth place.
Plural form of 'tentmaker'. See 'tentmaker'.
Tents; movable dwellings of cloth or skins.
Terah; the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran, from whose household Abram departed at God's call.
Teraphim; household images or idols kept for superstitious or idolatrous use.
Teresh; one of the chamberlains who conspired against Ahasuerus.
Termed; past tense or past participial form of 'term'. See 'term'.
Plural form of 'terrace'. See 'terrace'.
earthly; belonging to the earth.
Dreadful, awe-inspiring, or causing fear.
The state, quality, or condition of being terriblenes.
In a terrib manner or degree.
Terrified; past tense or past participial form of 'terrify'. See 'terrify'.
Terrifiest; older second-person singular form of 'terrify', used with 'thou'. See 'terrify'.
Terrify; to fill with fear, alarm, or dread.
Great dread, fear, or cause of fearful trembling.
Tertius; the scribe who wrote down Paul's Epistle to the Romans.
Tertullus; the orator who accused Paul before Felix.
a covenant, solemn disposition, or will.
Testator; one who makes a testament or covenant disposition, especially by will.
Testified; past tense or past participial form of 'testify'. See 'testify'.
Testifiedst; means thou didst testify.
Testifieth; older third-person singular form of 'testify'. See 'testify'.
Testify; to bear witness, give solemn declaration, or warn earnestly.
Testifying; present participial form of 'testify', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'testify'.
Witness borne; solemn declaration; evidence given.
Also God's revealed witness or covenantal charge.
Tetrarch; a ruler over a fourth part of a territory, or a lesser prince under greater imperial authority.
Tha; a dialectal form of thou.
Thaddaeus; the apostle also associated with the names Judas and Lebbaeus.
Thahash; a son of Nahor in the patriarchal genealogies.
Thamah; the name of a family of temple servants, corresponding to the same family elsewhere written Tamah.
Thamar; the Greek name for Tamar, used in the genealogy of Christ.
Than; introducing the second member of a comparison.
Thank; to give gratitude or acknowledgment for a benefit received.
Thanked; past tense or past participial form of 'thank'. See 'thank'.
Grateful, ready to give thanks.
The state, quality, or condition of being thankfulnes.
Thanking; present participial form of 'thank', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'thank'.
Thanks; gratitude expressed in acknowledgment of kindness or mercy.
Thanksgiving; the giving of thanks or grateful acknowledgment offered to God.
It may refer either to the inward grace of gratitude or to its spoken and public expression.
Thankworthy; deserving thanks or approval.
Thara; the Greek name for Terah, father of Abraham.
Tharshish; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from Tarshish the place.
That; the thing or person spoken of, or a conjunction introducing a clause.
The definite article.
Theatre; a public place for spectacle or assembly.
Thebez; the town where Abimelech was mortally wounded by the millstone cast from the tower.
Thee; the singular second-person objective form, meaning you.
Thee-ward; toward thee, in thy direction, or with respect to thee.
Theft; stealing or that which is stolen.
Plural form of 'theft'. See 'theft'.
Belonging to them; the third-person plural possessive form.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
Thelasar; the Greek form corresponding to Telassar, the district named among Assyrian conquests.
Thelasar.note: Location: Often identified with Til Barsip.

definitions: The third-person plural objective pronoun.
Themselves; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to they.
At that time; afterward; in that case.
From that place; from that source; from there.
Thenceforth; from that time onward.
Theophilus; the person addressed at the opening of Luke and Acts, likely a patron or man of rank.
There; in that place, at that point, or in that matter.
Thereabout; about that place, number, or matter.
Thereat; at that, because of that, or on account of that.
By that means; through that.
For that reason; on that account; consequently.
Therefrom; from that or from there.
In that; within that matter or place.
Thereinto; into that or into it.
Of that; belonging to that; concerning that.
On that; upon that thing or place.
Thereout; out of that or out from there.
To that; unto that; in addition to that.
Thereunto; to that, unto that matter, or for that purpose.
Thereupon; upon that, immediately after that, or in consequence of that.
With that; together with it; by means of that.
These; the plural demonstrative of this.
Plural form of 'thessalonian'. See 'thessalonian'.
Thessalonica; the Macedonian city where Paul founded the church later addressed in his epistles.
Theudas; the insurgent named by Gamaliel in Acts as an earlier pretender.
The third-person plural personal pronoun.
Thick; dense, close, heavy, or clouded.
Thicker; more thick, denser, or less fine.
Thicket; a dense growth of bushes or small trees.
Plural form of 'thicket'. See 'thicket'.
The state, quality, or condition of being thicknes.
Thief; one who steals secretly or unjustly takes another's goods.
Plural form of 'thief'. See 'thief'.
The thigh is the upper part of the leg from the hip to the knee.
In Scripture it may also be named in connection with strength, posterity, or solemn gesture.
Plural form of 'thigh'. See 'thigh'.
Thimnathah; a name-form of Timnath or Timnah used of a town in hill-country and border traditions.
Thimnathah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Batash.

definitions: Thin; not thick, slight, lean, or sparse.
The older second-person singular possessive form, often used before a vowel or for emphasis: belonging to thee.
A thing; a matter, object, affair, or event.
Things; matters, objects, affairs, or events.
To think is to consider, suppose, judge, or hold in the mind.
Thinkest; thou thinkest; you think, suppose, or consider.
Thinketh; older third-person singular form of 'think'. See 'think'.
Thinking; the act of considering, supposing, judging, or holding something in the mind.
Next after the second.
In a third manner or degree.
Thirst; the desire for drink, or to long intensely for drink or figuratively for something desired.
Thirsted; past tense or past participial form of 'thirst'. See 'thirst'.
Thirsteth; older third-person singular form of 'thirst'. See 'thirst'.
Thirsty; suffering thirst, eager for drink, or figuratively longing strongly.
The number thirteen.
Next after the twelfth.
The one next after the twenty-ninth; occupying the thirtieth place.
The number thirty.
Thirtyfold; thirty times as much, or in a thirtyfold degree.
This; the person or thing near at hand or in present view.
A thistle is a prickly weed of the field.
It belongs to the imagery of barrenness, curse, and useless growth.
Plural form of 'thistle'. See 'thistle'.
To that place; there.
Toward that place; in that direction.
Thomas; one of the twelve apostles, also called Didymus, remembered for his confession of the risen Christ.
Plural form of 'thong'. See 'thong'.
A thorn is a sharp prickle or thorny plant.
In Scripture it may signify curse, hindrance, pain, or vexing trouble.
Plural form of 'thorn'. See 'thorn'.
In a thorough manner or degree.
The plural of that; those persons or things.
The older second-person singular personal pronoun.
Though; although; even if.
Thought; inward reasoning, care, design, or purpose.
Thoughtest; older second-person singular form meaning didst think, consider, or purpose.
Thoughts; inward reasonings, purposes, or imaginations of the mind.
A thousand; the number of ten hundreds.
Plural form of 'thousand'. See 'thousand'.
Thread; a fine twisted strand used in weaving or sewing.
Threaten; to utter menace against, or give signs of approaching harm.
Threatened; past tense or past participial form of 'threaten'. See 'threaten'.
Threatening; present participial form of 'threaten', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'threaten'.
Plural form of 'threatening'. See 'threatening'.
The number three.
Threefold; three times as much, or in a threefold manner.
Sixty.
To thresh is to beat or tread out grain from the husk.
Threshed; past tense or past participial form of 'thresh'. See 'thresh'.
Thresheth; older third-person singular form of 'thresh'. See 'thresh'.
Threshing; present participial form of 'thresh', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'thresh'.
A threshingfloor is the prepared place where grain is beaten or trodden out from the husk.
In Scripture it may also take on sacrificial, judicial, or providential significance by association with notable events.
Plural form of 'threshingfloor'. See 'threshingfloor'.
Threshingplace; a place where grain is threshed.
Threshold; the sill or entry-part of a doorway.
Plural form of 'threshold'. See 'threshold'.
Threw; past tense of throw, meaning cast, hurled, or flung.
Threwest; older second-person singular form of 'throw', used with 'thou'. See 'throw'.
Thrice; three times.
Throat; the passage of the neck and mouth, and figuratively appetite or speech.
A throne; the seat of royal authority, and by extension rule or dominion.
Plural form of 'throne'. See 'throne'.
Throng; a crowded multitude.
As a verb, to crowd, press upon, or fill closely.
Thronged; past tense or past participial form of 'throng'. See 'throng'.
Thronging; present participial form of 'throng', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'throng'.
Through; from end to end, by means of, or by reason of.
In a through manner or degree.
Thoroughly equipped and supplied for use.
Throughout; in every part of; from one end to the other.
Throw; to cast, hurl, put down, or cause to fall.
Throwing; present participial form of 'throw', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'throw'.
Thrown; the past participle of throw.
Thrust; to push or drive with force, or a forceful push.
Thrusteth; means pushes, drives, or thrusts forward.
Thumb; the short thick first digit of the hand, set apart from the fingers.
Plural form of 'thumb'. See 'thumb'.
Thummim; the sacred counterpart to the Urim, joined with it in the high priest's breastplate for inquiry before the LORD.
Thunder; the loud sound attending storm, and figuratively a voice or manifestation of divine power.
As a verb, to sound loudly or speak with force.
Plural form of 'thunderbolt'. See 'thunderbolt'.
Thundered; past tense or past participial form of 'thunder'. See 'thunder'.
Thundereth; older third-person singular form of 'thunder'. See 'thunder'.
Plural form of 'thundering'. See 'thundering'.
Plural form of 'thunder'. See 'thunder'.
Thus; in this manner; so.
The older second-person singular possessive form: belonging to thee.
Thyatira; the city addressed among the seven churches in Revelation, and also the home of Lydia.
The name is associated with trade and with a church needing purity and steadfastness.
Thyatira.note: Location: In western Asia Minor, commonly identified with Akhisar in modern Turkey.

definitions: Thyine; belonging to fragrant thyine wood, named among costly wares in Revelation.
Thyself; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to thou.
Tiberias; the city on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee, and by extension the sea itself in some New Testament passages.
Tiberias.note: Location: Usually identified with Sea of Galilee.

definitions: Tiberius; the Roman emperor whose reign dates the beginning of John the Baptist's ministry.
Tibhath; a Syrian city from which David took much bronze after defeating Hadadezer.
Tibhath.note: Location: Often identified with a site between Baalbek and Kadesh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Tibni; the rival claimant to the throne of Israel against Omri.
Tidal; king of nations in the confederacy of Chedorlaomer.
Plural form of 'tiding'. See 'tiding'.
Tie; to bind, fasten, or join by a knot or band.
As a noun, a bond or fastening.
Tied; past tense or past participial form of 'tie'. See 'tie'.
Tiglath-Pileser; the Assyrian king who invaded the land and carried many away captive.
Tikvah; the name of men in Scripture, including the father of Shallum the husband of Huldah.
Tikvath; the name-form of Tikvah used especially in the record concerning Huldah's husband.
Tile; a flat baked piece used in roofing, paving, or for writing as in Ezekiel.
Tilgath-Pilneser; another spelling of Tiglath-Pileser, king of Assyria.
Tiling; present participial form of 'tile', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tile'.
Until; up to the time of.
the cultivation of the ground.
Tilled; past tense or past participial form of 'till'. See 'till'.
Tiller; one who tills the ground, or the steering bar of a vessel.
Tillest; older second-person singular form of 'till', used with 'thou'. See 'till'.
Tilleth; older third-person singular form of 'till'. See 'till'.
Tilon; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Timaeus; the father of blind Bartimaeus.
Timber; wood prepared for building, beams, or standing trees fit for such use.
Timbrel; a hand drum or tambourine used in music, rejoicing, and procession.
Plural form of 'timbrel'. See 'timbrel'.
Time; a season, occasion, or measured period.
Times; seasons, occasions, or measured periods.
Timna; a personal name in Edomite genealogies, distinct from the place-name.
Timnah; a personal name in the genealogies of Esau and his line, distinct from the place-name.
Timnath; a town-name used of sites such as Timnah and Timnath-Serah in biblical geography.
Timnath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Batash.

definitions: Timnath-heres; the burial place and inheritance city of Joshua in mount Ephraim.
Timnath-heres.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Tibnah.

definitions: Timnath-Serah; the town in Ephraim given to Joshua, where he was later buried.
Timnath-serah.note: Location: In the hill country of Ephraim, commonly linked with Khirbet Tibnah.

definitions: Timnite; an inhabitant, descendant, or member associated with Timn in Scripture.
Timnite.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Batash.

definitions: Timon; one of the seven chosen in Acts to serve the church at Jerusalem.
Timotheus; a man named in Scripture.
Timothy; a man named in Scripture.
Tin; a metal named among the metals and trade goods of Scripture.
Tingle; to feel a sharp thrilling sensation, especially from shock or alarm.
Tinkling; present participial form of 'tinkle', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tinkle'.
Tip; the end, point, or extremity of a thing.
Tiphsah; the Euphrates crossing city known from Solomon's dominion and later northern kingdom violence.
Tiphsah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell Abu Zarad, though Thapsacus on the Euphrates is the traditional association.

definitions: Tiras; a son of Japheth in the table of nations.
Tirathites; a scribal or clan group named among the families of Judah.
Tire; attire or ornamental head-dress in older usage.
As a verb in later usage, to weary.
Tired; past tense or past participial form of 'tire'. See 'tire'.
Plural form of 'tire'. See 'tire'.
Tirhakah; king of Ethiopia who came out against Sennacherib.
Tirhanah; one of the descendants in the line of Caleb.
Tiria; a son in the line of Jehallelel.
Tirshatha; a Persian governor or official title used of the civil ruler in the post-exilic community.
Tirzah; the name of women in Scripture, including one of the daughters of Zelophehad.
Tishbite; one belonging to Tishbe, especially used of Elijah.
Tishbite.note: Location: Generally between Kedesh-naphtali and Hazor; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Tithe; the tenth part set apart, especially for sacred or legal use.
As a verb, to take or give tithes.
Plural form of 'tithe'. See 'tithe'.
Tithing; present participial form of 'tithe', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tithe'.
Title; a name of honour or designation, and also a written superscription or claim.
Plural form of 'title'. See 'title'.
Tittle; a very small stroke or point in writing, hence the least detail.
Titus; a man named in Scripture.
Tizite; one belonging to, descended from, or associated with Tiz in Scripture.
To; expressing motion toward, relation to, purpose, result, or extent.
Namely; that is to say; to know.
Toah; a Levite ancestor in Samuel's line, represented in related forms such as Tohu and Nahath.
Tob; the Transjordan district where Jephthah lived in exile and gathered followers.
Tob.note: Location: Often identified with Et Tayibeh.

definitions: Tob-Adonijah; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Tobiah; especially the Ammonite adversary who opposed Nehemiah and the rebuilding of Jerusalem.
Tobijah; the name of men in Scripture, including those in post-exilic and temple-related records.
Tochen; a southern town in the Simeonite lists, likely related to another Negev settlement tradition.
Tochen.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet el Ater.

definitions: Toe; one of the digits of the foot.
Plural form of 'toe'. See 'toe'.
Togarmah; a son in the line of Gomer and ancestor associated with northern peoples.
Together; in company, in one place, or in union with one another.
Tohu; a Levite ancestor in Samuel's line, represented in related forms such as Toah and Nahath.
Toi; king of Hamath who sent greeting and gifts to David after Hadarezer was smitten, also written Tou.
Toil; labour, wearisome work, or trouble.
As a verb, to labour strenuously.
Toiled; past tense or past participial form of 'toil'. See 'toil'.
Toiling; present participial form of 'toil', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'toil'.
Token; a sign, mark, pledge, or proof by which something is known or confirmed.
Plural form of 'token'. See 'token'.
Tola; the name of men in Scripture, including the judge of Israel and an Issacharite ancestor.
Tolad; a southern town of Simeon in the Negev lists.
Tolad.note: Location: Sometimes placed in the area around Tel Beer Sheva.

definitions: Tolaites; descendants of Tola reckoned among the families of Issachar.
Spoke, recounted, informed, or declared.
Bearable or able to be endured.
Toll; tax, tribute, or custom taken on goods or passage.
As a verb, to exact such payment, or in later use to sound a bell.
Tomb; a sepulchre or place of burial.
Plural form of 'tomb'. See 'tomb'.
Plural form of 'tong'. See 'tong'.
The tongue; the bodily organ of speech, and by extension language or speech itself.
Plural form of 'tongue'. See 'tongue'.
Too; also, besides, or excessively.
The past tense of take.
Tookest; means thou tookest, that is, thou didst take.
Tool; an instrument used in work or craft.
Tooth; one of the hard parts set in the jaws for biting and chewing, or anything shaped like one.
The top; highest part, summit, or upper end.
Topaz; a precious stone named among the jewels of Scripture.
Tophel; a place named in the introductory wilderness setting of Deuteronomy.
Tophet; an altar or high place mentioned in Scripture.
Tophet.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Hinnom Valley.

definitions: Topheth; a site in the valley of Hinnom near Jerusalem, notorious for idolatrous burning rites and later used as a figure of judgment.
Topheth.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Hinnom Valley.

definitions: Plural form of 'top'. See 'top'.
Torch; a lighted brand or flambeau.
Plural form of 'torch'. See 'torch'.
severe pain or anguish; also to afflict with such pain.
Tormented; past tense or past participial form of 'torment'. See 'torment'.
Plural form of 'tormentor'. See 'tormentor'.
Plural form of 'torment'. See 'torment'.
Torn; the past participle of tear.
Tortoise; an unclean creature named in the law, though the old term may refer more broadly than the modern tortoise alone.
Tortured; past tense or past participial form of 'torture'. See 'torture'.
Toss; to throw lightly, fling, or be driven to and fro; also a tossing motion.
Tossed; past tense or past participial form of 'toss'. See 'toss'.
Plural form of 'tossing'. See 'tossing'.
Tottering; present participial form of 'totter', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'totter'.
Tou; the name-form of Toi used for the king of Hamath who honoured David after his victory.
Touched; past tense or past participial form of 'touch'. See 'touch'.
Toucheth; means touches, comes into contact with, reaches, affects, or concerns.
Touching; present participial form of 'touch', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'touch'.
Touching; concerning, regarding, or with respect to.
Tow; coarse broken flax or hemp fibres used for spinning or as combustible material.
In the direction of; with respect to; near unto.
Towel; a cloth used for wiping or drying.
A tower is a raised structure for defense, watch, strength, or height.
It may also signify security or exalted strength by figure.
Plural form of 'tower'. See 'tower'.
Town; an inhabited place, usually smaller than a city.
Townclerk; the chief civic recorder or secretary of a city, as at Ephesus.
Plural form of 'town'. See 'town'.
a rugged district northeast of the Sea of Galilee.
Trachonitis.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Lajat.

definitions: Trade; commerce, exchange, or a course of buying and selling.
As a verb, to traffic or deal in goods.
Traded; past tense or past participial form of 'trade'. See 'trade'.
Trading; present participial form of 'trade', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'trade'.
teaching or practice handed down from the past.
Plural form of 'tradition'. See 'tradition'.
Traffick; trade or commerce.
As a verb, to deal, trade, or carry on commerce.
Plural form of 'trafficker'. See 'trafficker'.
Train; a retinue, trailing part, or ordered company.
As a verb, to teach, discipline, or draw along.
Trained; past tense or past participial form of 'train'. See 'train'.
Traitor; one who betrays trust, loyalty, or cause.
Plural form of 'traitor'. See 'traitor'.
Trample; to tread down under foot or crush by treading.
a state of ecstasy or suspended outward awareness.
calmness, quietness, or peace.
Transferred; past tense or past participial form of 'transfer'. See 'transfer'.
Transfigured; past tense or past participial form of 'transfigure'. See 'transfigure'.
Transformed; past tense or past participial form of 'transform'. See 'transform'.
Transforming; present participial form of 'transform', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'transform'.
Went beyond the commanded boundary; broke the law or charge given.
Transgressest; thou transgressest; you go beyond the command and so offend.
Transgressing; present participial form of 'transgress', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'transgress'.
A going beyond the boundary of law; offense against command.
Transgressor; one who breaks the law, commandment, or covenant.
Plural form of 'transgressor'. See 'transgressor'.
Translate; to transfer, carry over, or render into another form or language.
Translated; past tense or past participial form of 'translate'. See 'translate'.
the act of carrying over in language, or removal from one place or state to another.
Clear so as to be seen through.
Trap; a snare or device for catching.
As a verb, to ensnare or catch.
Plural form of 'trap'. See 'trap'.
Painful labor, especially labor in childbirth.
By extension, severe toil or inward anguish.
Travailed; past tense or past participial form of 'travail'. See 'travail'.
Travailest; thou travailest; you labor painfully, especially as in sore toil or birth-pain.
Travailing; present participial form of 'travail', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'travail'.
Travel; to journey, labour, or in older usage travail in pain.
As a noun, journeying or labour.
Travelled; past tense or past participial form of 'travel'. See 'travel'.
Traveller; one who journeys from place to place.
Plural form of 'traveller'. See 'traveller'.
Travelleth; older third-person singular form of 'travel'. See 'travel'.
Travelling; present participial form of 'travel', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'travel'.
Traversing; passing across, going through, or moving to and fro.
False, deceitful, or ready to betray.
In a treacherous manner or degree.
Treachery; betrayal, faithlessness, or deceitful disloyalty.
Treader; one who treads, especially of winepresses or threshing.
Plural form of 'treader'. See 'treader'.
Treads; steps upon; walks over; tramples.
Treading; present participial form of 'tread', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'tread'.
Treason; betrayal of trust, especially rebellion or faithlessness against a ruler.
stored wealth or something highly precious; also to keep as precious.
Treasured; past tense or past participial form of 'treasure'. See 'treasure'.
A treasurer; one entrusted with money, revenue, or stored wealth.
The office may belong to a kingdom, temple, or private estate.
Treasurers; those entrusted with money, revenue, or stored wealth.
Plural form of 'treasure'. See 'treasure'.
Treasurest; older second-person singular form of 'treasure', used with 'thou'. See 'treasure'.
Plural form of 'treasury'. See 'treasury'.
Treasury; a storehouse or chamber for treasure, offerings, or public wealth.
Treatise; a written account, orderly discourse, or composition.
A tree; the woody plant so called.
Trees; woody plants of that kind.
To shake with fear, weakness, or strong feeling.
Trembleth; older third-person singular form of 'tremble'. See 'tremble'.
Trench; a ditch, entrenchment, or cut in the ground.
A trespass; an offence, transgression, or wrongful act.
Trespassed; past tense or past participial form of 'trespass'. See 'trespass'.
Plural form of 'trespass'. See 'trespass'.
Trespassing; present participial form of 'trespass', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'trespass'.
testing, proving, or legal examination.
A tribe; one of the family divisions of a people, especially of Israel.
Tribes; the divisions of a people, especially of Israel.
Pressure, affliction, and distress under trial.
Plural form of 'tributary'. See 'tributary'.
Paying tribute or subject to another power.
One who pays tribute, or a stream flowing into a larger one in broader usage.
Tribute; tax, levy, imposed payment, or in some contexts a gift rendered in acknowledgment.
Trickleth; older third-person singular form of 'trickle'. See 'trickle'.
Tried; past tense or past participial form of 'try'. See 'try'.
Triest; older second-person singular form of 'try', used with 'thou'. See 'try'.
Trieth; older third-person singular form of 'try'. See 'try'.
Trimmed; past tense or past participial form of 'trim'. See 'trim'.
Trimmest; older second-person singular form of 'trim', used with 'thou'. See 'trim'.
Triumph; exulting victory or procession of conquest.
As a verb, to exult or lead in triumph.
Triumphed; past tense or past participial form of 'triumph'. See 'triumph'.
Triumphing; present participial form of 'triumph', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'triumph'.
Troas; the coastal city and region from which Paul passed toward Macedonia.
It is associated with apostolic visions, departures, and gatherings of the church.
Troas.note: Location: On the northwestern coast of Asia Minor near ancient Troy.

definitions: Trodden; the past participle of tread.
Trode; the past tense of tread.
Trogyllium; a headland or projecting height mentioned in Scripture.
Trogyllium.note: Location: Usually placed at or identified with Cape Mycale.

definitions: Troop; a band, company, or body of men or animals.
As a verb, to gather or move in company.
Plural form of 'troop'. See 'troop'.
Trophimus; the Ephesian companion of Paul whose presence in Jerusalem stirred accusation.
Troubled; disturbed, agitated, or afflicted in mind or condition.
Troubledst; means thou didst trouble, disturb, afflict, or make anxious.
Troubler; one who causes distress, disorder, or calamity.
Troubles; afflictions, disturbances, anxieties, or acts of troubling.
Troublest; older second-person singular form of 'trouble', used with 'thou'. See 'trouble'.
Troubles; disturbs, afflicts, or throws into agitation.
Troubling; present participial form of 'trouble', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'trouble'.
Full of trouble, disorder, or distress.
A trough is a hollow vessel or channel for holding water, food, or washing.
Plural form of 'trough'. See 'trough'.
To think, suppose, or believe.
Plural form of 'trucebreaker'. See 'trucebreaker'.
True; faithful, genuine, certain, or in accordance with fact.
In a tru manner or degree.
Trump; a trumpet or its sounding, especially in apocalyptic and martial use.
Trumpet; a wind instrument used for signal, proclamation, worship, alarm, or war.
Trumpeters; those who sound trumpets, especially in worship, procession, or alarm.
Plural form of 'trumpet'. See 'trumpet'.
To rely upon with confidence; to place dependence in.
Trustedst; means thou didst trust.
Trustest; thou trustest; you trust, rely, or place confidence in.
Trusting; present participial form of 'trust', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'trust'.
Trusty; trustworthy, faithful, or reliable.
That which is true, sure, and according to reality.
Also faithfulness, steadfastness, and sincerity, especially when spoken of God or of covenant dealing.
Try; to test, prove, examine, or attempt.
Trying; present participial form of 'try', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'try'.
Tryphena; a Christian woman greeted by Paul as labouring in the Lord.
Tryphosa; a Christian woman greeted by Paul as labouring in the Lord.
Tubal; a son of Japheth and ancestor associated with northern trading peoples.
Tubal-Cain; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Tumbled; past tense or past participial form of 'tumble'. See 'tumble'.
Tumult; noise, uproar, confusion, or violent commotion.
Plural form of 'tumult'. See 'tumult'.
Noisy, disorderly, or full of uproar.
Turned; changed direction, returned, or was changed in state or affection.
Turnest; thou turnest; you turn, return, or change course.
Turns; changes direction; returns.
Often used of turning the heart, changing course, or converting from one state or way to another.
Turning; the act of turning, changing direction, or converting.
Turtle; the turtledove, especially in sacrificial or poetic context.
Turtledove; a small dove used in sacrifice and often associated with innocence or mourning.
Plural form of 'turtledove'. See 'turtledove'.
Plural form of 'turtle'. See 'turtle'.
Plural form of 'tutor'. See 'tutor'.
Two; a pair considered as two persons or things.
Next after the eleventh.
The number twelve.
The one next after the nineteenth; occupying the twentieth place.
The number twenty.
Twice; two times, doubly, or in a twofold manner.
Plural form of 'twig'. See 'twig'.
Twilight; the dim light before sunrise or after sunset.
Twined; past tense or past participial form of 'twin'. See 'twin'.
Twinkling; a quick flashing or gleaming, or a very brief moment.
Plural form of 'twin'. See 'twin'.
The number two.
Twoedged; having two edges; double-edged.
Twofold; double, twice as much, or in a twofold manner.
Tychicus; the beloved brother and faithful minister who carried Paul's letters and messages.
Tyrannus; the man whose school Paul used at Ephesus.
Tyre; the great Phoenician city on the coast, famed for trade, wealth, and maritime power.
In prophecy it often stands for proud commercial strength and worldly glory.
Tyre.note: Location: On the Phoenician coast south of Sidon, commonly identified with Tyre in modern Lebanon.

definitions: Tyre, the great Phoenician city.
Ucal; the companion named with Ithiel in the words of Agur.
Uel; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Ulai; the river or canal beside which Daniel saw one of his visions.
Ulai.note: Location: Commonly identified with Ulai canal.

definitions: Ulam; the name of men in Scripture, especially a son in the line of Eshek and another in the line of Jerahmeel.
Ulla; an Asherite descendant whose sons are named among mighty men and archers.
Ummah; a town in the inheritance of Asher near the coast.
Ummah.note: Location: Often identified with Tel Akko.

definitions: Unaccustomed; not used to, unfamiliar with, or not habitual.
In a unadvised manner or degree.
Without being aware; unexpectedly; before one perceives it.
Unbelief; want of faith, refusal to believe, or faithlessness.
Plural form of 'unbeliever'. See 'unbeliever'.
Unbelieving; not believing, distrustful, or lacking faith; also used substantively for an unbelieving person.
Not blameworthy; without just ground of accusation.
In a unblameab manner or degree.
Uncertain; not fixed, doubtful, or unsure.
In a uncertain manner or degree.
Not subject to change; constant and immutable.
Not circumcised; outside the covenant sign.
Often used figuratively for what is unrenewed or unpurified.
the state of not being circumcised; also the Gentile condition in contrast with the circumcision.
Uncle; the brother of one's father or mother, and in older usage a near kinsman.
Defiled, impure, or ceremonially unfit.
At times morally impure as well.
Impurity, whether ceremonial or moral.
It may refer to defilement under the law, or to filthy and sinful corruption in conduct and desire.
Plural form of 'uncleanness'. See 'uncleanness'.
Unclothed; not clothed, stripped, or left without covering.
In a uncome manner or degree.
Uncondemned; not condemned or not yet sentenced.
Not liable to corruption, decay, or moral defilement.
The state, quality, or condition of being uncorruptnes.
Uncover; to remove a covering, reveal, expose, or in certain passages uncover nakedness.
Uncovered; past tense or past participial form of 'uncover'. See 'uncover'.
Uncovereth; older third-person singular form of 'uncover'. See 'uncover'.
anointing; especially the spiritual anointing of the Holy Spirit.
Undefiled; not defiled, unpolluted, pure, or unstained.
Under; beneath; subject to; less than.
Undergirding; supporting from beneath, especially by binding or bracing.
Underneath; beneath, below, or under the support or position of something.
Undersetters; supports, bases, or sockets set underneath for bearing weight.
Understandest; means thou understandest, perceiving, comprehending, or discerning.
Understands; perceives the meaning of; discerns rightly.
Discernment, intelligence, and right apprehension of a matter.
Understood; the past tense and past participle of understand.
Undertake; to take in hand, attempt, promise, or answer for.
Undertook; past tense of undertake.
Undo; to loose, reverse what was done, or bring to ruin.
Undone; ruined, left unfinished, brought to nothing, or not done.
Undressed; not clothed, not prepared, or not adorned.
not equal; uneven or unmatched.
In a unequal manner or degree.
Faithless, disloyal, or not true in trust or covenant.
In a unfaithful manner or degree.
Without hypocrisy; sincere; genuine.
Barren, not productive, or yielding no good result.
Ungirded; loosed from a girdle, unbelted, or relaxed from restraint.
The state, quality, or condition of being ungodlines.
Without godliness; irreverent and wicked toward God.
In a unho manner or degree.
Unicorn; the animal so named in the KJV, generally understood as a strong wild horned beast rather than the later mythical horse-like creature.
