In which; in what thing or matter.
Whereinsoever; in whatever thing or in whatsoever respect.
Whereinto; into which or into that place.
Of which; concerning which.
On which; upon which thing or place.
In whatever place; wherever.
Whereto; to which place, end, or purpose.
To which; unto which thing or purpose.
Whereupon; upon which, after which, or as a consequence of which.
Wherewith; with which, or by means of which.
the means or resources by which something is done.
Whet; to sharpen, or figuratively to stimulate appetite or desire.
Whether; introducing alternatives or uncertainty between one thing and another.
Which; what one of the set spoken of.
During the time that; as long as; for a short time.
During the time that; meanwhile.
Whilst; an older spelling of while, meaning during the time that or while.
Whip; a lash or scourge.
As a verb, to strike with a whip or lash.
Plural form of 'whip'. See 'whip'.
Whirleth; older third-person singular form of 'whirl'. See 'whirl'.
A violent wind moving in a circular course.
Often a sign of overwhelming force or divine appearance.
Whisper; to speak softly in a low voice.
As a noun, a low or secret utterance.
Whispered; past tense or past participial form of 'whisper'. See 'whisper'.
Whisperer; one who secretly slanders or spreads tales.
Plural form of 'whisperer'. See 'whisperer'.
Plural form of 'whispering'. See 'whispering'.
Whit; the least particle, bit, or degree.
White; bright or pale in color, and sometimes clean or pure figuratively.
Whited; past tense or past participial form of 'white'. See 'white'.
Whiter; more white, more bright, or more pure in colour.
To what place; to which place.
To whatever place; wherever one may go.
Who; what person.
Whole; entire, complete, or sound.
Wholesome; healthful, sound, or beneficial.
Entirely; fully; altogether.
Whom; the objective form of who.
Whomsoever; any person whom, whoever in the objective relation.
Whore; a harlot or prostitute, and figuratively one guilty of spiritual unfaithfulness.
As a verb, to commit whoredom.
fornication; in Scripture often spiritual unfaithfulness or idolatry.
Plural form of 'whoredom'. See 'whoredom'.
Whoremonger; one given to fornication or unchaste vice.
Plural form of 'whoremonger'. See 'whoremonger'.
Plural form of 'whore'. See 'whore'.
Whoring; present participial form of 'whore', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'whore'.
Whorish; belonging to whoredom, unchaste, or spiritually adulterous in the prophetic sense.
Whose; the possessive form of who.
Whoso; whoever, any person who, or the one who.
Whoever; any person whatsoever without naming which one in particular.
For what reason; wherefore.
Evil in character and conduct; guilty of moral wrong.
In a wicked manner; evilly; with moral wrong or impiety.
Evil conduct, moral wrong, or active ungodliness.
Wide; broad, spacious, far apart, or extensive.
The state, quality, or condition of being widenes.
A widow; a woman whose husband is dead and who remains in that condition.
In Scripture the word often carries the added sense of social weakness and special claim to compassion and justice.
the state of being a widow.
Widows; women whose husbands are dead, often viewed in Scripture as specially needing care and justice.
A wife; a married woman in relation to her husband.
Wild; untamed, uncultivated, or growing without human ordering.
A wilderness; a desert, waste, or uncultivated region with little settled habitation.
It often denotes a solitary tract apart from cities and fruitful fields.
Crafty methods, subtle schemes, or deceitful devices.
In a wilful manner or degree.
In a wili manner or degree.
Will; desire, purpose, choice, or determination.
Willeth; older third-person singular form of 'will'. See 'will'.
Willing; present participial form of 'will', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'will'.
In a willing manner or degree.
Willow; the tree so named, especially in riverside imagery and mourning.
Plural form of 'willow'. See 'willow'.
Wilt; the older second-person singular of will.
Plural form of 'wimple'. See 'wimple'.
Win; to gain, obtain, overcome, or bring over.
Wind; moving air, breath, or spirit.
Winding; turning and twisting, or the act of winding.
A window is an opening in a wall or roof to admit light and air, often with lattice or covering.
In Scripture it may also be used figuratively of an opening from heaven or toward heaven.
Plural form of 'window'. See 'window'.
Winds; moving airs, breaths, or spirits.
Windy; full of wind, empty in speech, or unstable by context.
Wine; the fermented juice of the grape.
Winebibber; one given to drinking wine excessively.
Plural form of 'winebibber'. See 'winebibber'.
A winefat is the vat or receptacle into which the juice of the grape runs or is gathered in pressing.
a press for treading grapes and collecting the juice.
Plural form of 'winepress'. See 'winepress'.
Plural form of 'wine'. See 'wine'.
Wing; present participial form of 'we', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'we'.
Winged; past tense or past participial form of 'wing'. See 'wing'.
Wings; organs of flight, and by extension edges, extremes, or sheltering cover.
Wink; to close the eye quickly, or figuratively to overlook.
As a noun, a quick closing of the eye.
Winked; past tense or past participial form of 'wink'. See 'wink'.
Winketh; older third-person singular form of 'wink'. See 'wink'.
Winneth; older third-person singular form of 'win'. See 'win'.
Winnowed; past tense or past participial form of 'winnow'. See 'winnow'.
Winnoweth; older third-person singular form of 'winnow'. See 'winnow'.
Winter; the cold season of the year.
Wintered; past tense or past participial form of 'winter'. See 'winter'.
Winterhouse; a house or apartment for the cold season.
Wipe; to rub or cleanse by drawing something over a surface, or to blot out.
Wiped; past tense or past participial form of 'wipe'. See 'wipe'.
Wipeth; older third-person singular form of 'wipe'. See 'wipe'.
Wiping; present participial form of 'wipe', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'wipe'.
Plural form of 'wire'. See 'wire'.
Right judgment joined with understanding and skill in godly living.
Endued with sound judgment, understanding, and skill in right conduct.
Sometimes prudent in earthly affairs, but often morally and spiritually discerning.
In a wise manner or degree.
Wiser; more wise, more prudent, or more skillful than another.
Wish; to desire, long for, or express a desire.
As a noun, a desire or longing.
Wished; past tense or past participial form of 'wish'. See 'wish'.
Wishing; present participial form of 'wish', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'wish'.
Wist; knew, an older past-tense form related to wit in the sense of knowing.
Knew not; did not know.
Wit; understanding, knowledge, or mental perception, especially in the phrase to wit.
As a verb in old use, to know.
Witch; one who practises forbidden magical arts or sorcery.
Witchcraft; sorcery, magical arts, or forbidden dealings with occult power.
Plural form of 'witchcraft'. See 'witchcraft'.
With; in company with, by means of, or in relation to.
With the rest; besides; together with; at the same time.
Withdraw; to draw back, depart, take away, or keep apart.
Withdrawest; older second-person singular form of 'withdraw', used with 'thou'. See 'withdraw'.
Withdraweth; older third-person singular form of 'withdraw'. See 'withdraw'.
Withdrawn; drawn back, removed, or retired from presence or action.
Withdrew; past tense of withdraw, meaning drew back, retired, or removed.
Dried up, shrunk, or wasted away for lack of life or moisture.
Withers; dries up; wastes away for lack of life or moisture.
Withheld; held back or refused, the past form of withhold.
Withheldest; older second-person singular form of 'withheld', used with 'thou'. See 'withheld'.
Withhold; to hold back, keep in, restrain, or refuse to give.
Withholden; an older participial spelling meaning withheld or held back.
Withholdeth; older third-person singular form of 'withhold'. See 'withhold'.
Within; inside, inwardly, or before the passing of a given time.
Without; outside of; beyond; lacking or destitute of.
Plural form of 'with'. See 'with'.
Withstand; to stand against, resist, oppose, or endure against.
Withstood; past tense of withstand, meaning resisted, opposed, or stood firm against.
One who bears testimony, or the testimony itself that is borne.
Also a token, evidence, or public sign that proves or confirms a matter.
Witnessed; past tense or past participial form of 'witness'. See 'witness'.
Witnesseth; older third-person singular form of 'witness'. See 'witness'.
Witnessing; present participial form of 'witness', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'witness'.
Plural form of 'wit'. See 'wit'.
In a witting manner or degree.
Witty; skilful, ingenious, or clever; in the KJV often meaning artful or inventive rather than merely humorous.
Wives; married women in relation to their husbands.
Wizard; one thought to possess occult knowledge or magical power.
Plural form of 'wizard'. See 'wizard'.
Woe; sorrow, misery, calamity, or an exclamation announcing grief or judgment.
Full of woe; sorrowful, grievous, or miserable.
Plural form of 'woe'. See 'woe'.
Wolf; a fierce ravening beast, often used figuratively of cruel or destructive men.
Plural form of 'wolf'. See 'wolf'.
Woman; an adult female, and often a wife where the context requires it.
Womankind; the female sex collectively, or women in general.
The womb; the place in which an unborn child is conceived and carried.
Plural form of 'womb'. See 'womb'.
Women; adult females or wives.
Plural form of 'womenservant'. See 'womenservant'.
Won; gained, obtained, or conquered, the past form of win.
Wonder; amazement, marvel, or something that causes astonishment.
As a verb, to marvel or be astonished.
Wondered; past tense or past participial form of 'wonder'. See 'wonder'.
Full of wonder; marvelous, astonishing, or extraordinary.
In a wonderful manner or degree.
Wondering; present participial form of 'wonder', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'wonder'.
Plural form of 'wonder'. See 'wonder'.
Marvelous, astonishing, or worthy of wonder.
In a wondrous manner or degree.
Wont; accustomed, used, or habitual.
As a noun, habit or custom.
Wood; timber, trees, or wooden material.
Plural form of 'wood'. See 'wood'.
Woof; the cross threads woven through the warp.
Wool; the fleece or hair of sheep used for clothing and weaving.
Woollen; made of wool, or resembling wool in material.
A word; a saying, message, report, matter spoken, or declared command.
Words; sayings, utterances, messages, or matters spoken.
Work; labor, deed, undertaking, or what is made or done.
Worker; one who labours, works, or performs a task.
Plural form of 'worker'. See 'worker'.
Works; acts; performs or produces an effect.
Often means operates inwardly or brings something to pass.
Workfellow; a fellow-worker or companion in labour.
Working; present participial form of 'work', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'work'.
Workman; one who labours at a craft, trade, or appointed work.
the quality or product of skilled work.
Workmen; those who labour at a craft, trade, or appointed work.
Works; deeds, labors, or things wrought.
World; the earth, the inhabited order, or mankind considered together.
In a world manner or degree.
Worlds; ages, ordered worlds, or mankind considered in successive states.
A worm is a creeping creature of low and frail kind.
In Scripture it often signifies corruption, consuming decay, or lowliness.
Plural form of 'worm'. See 'worm'.
Wormwood; a bitter plant, and figuratively bitterness, sorrow, or poisonous judgment.
Worse; more evil, more grievous, or in a lower condition than another.
Honor, reverence, and adoration offered to one deemed worthy.
In Scripture most often the reverent service and homage due unto God.
A worshipper; one who worships or pays reverent service to God or to that which is treated as divine.
Worshippers; those who worship or pay reverent service.
Worshipping; present participial form of 'worship', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'worship'.
Worst; most evil, most severe, or that which is worst.
As a verb, to defeat or overcome.
Worth; value, price, merit, or desert.
Plural form of 'worthy'. See 'worthy'.
In a worthi manner or degree.
Fit, suitable, deserving, or of corresponding value.
Know; knoweth; knows.
Would; an auxiliary verb expressing willingness, habitual action, intention, or result in a given circumstance.
I wish that; if only it were so.
Wouldest; thou wouldest; you would, wish, or were inclined to.
Often expresses willingness, desire, or what one would do in a supposed case.
Wound; an injury or hurt inflicted by a blow, cut, or weapon.
As a verb, to inflict such hurt.
Wounded; past tense or past participial form of 'wound'. See 'wound'.
Woundedst; means thou didst wound.
Woundeth; older third-person singular form of 'wounds'. See 'wounds'.
Wounding; present participial form of 'wound', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'wound'.
Plural form of 'wound'. See 'wound'.
Wove; past tense of weave.
Woven; interlaced into fabric, the past participle of weave.
Wrap; to fold or cover by winding around.
As a noun, a covering or wrapper.
Wrapped; past tense or past participial form of 'wrap'. See 'wrap'.
Strong anger or indignation.
Often righteous judgment when spoken of God.
Full of wrath; angry and fierce.
Plural form of 'wrath'. See 'wrath'.
Wreath; a twisted or woven band, garland, or ring-like ornament.
Wreathed; past tense or past participial form of 'wreath'. See 'wreath'.
Wreathen; twisted, interwoven, or made in winding form.
Plural form of 'wreath'. See 'wreath'.
Wrest; to twist, distort, force a meaning, or take by violence.
Wrestle; to strive bodily with another, or contend earnestly.
As a noun, such a struggle.
Wrestled; past tense or past participial form of 'wrestle'. See 'wrestle'.
Plural form of 'wrestling'. See 'wrestling'.
Wretched; miserable, afflicted, or pitiable.
The state, quality, or condition of being wretchednes.
Wring; to twist tightly, squeeze, or extort.
Wringed; past tense or past participial form of 'wring'. See 'wring'.
Wringing; the act of twisting or squeezing.
Wrinkle; a small fold or crease.
As a verb, to crease or make wrinkled.
Plural form of 'wrinkle'. See 'wrinkle'.
To write; to inscribe, record, or set down in writing.
Writer; one who writes, records, or serves as a scribe.
Writest; older second-person singular form of 'write', used with 'thou'. See 'write'.
Writeth; older third-person singular form of 'write'. See 'write'.
Writing; present participial form of 'write', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'write'.
Plural form of 'writing'. See 'writing'.
Written; put into writing or recorded in written form, the past participle of write.
Injustice, injury, or that which is contrary to right.
Also wrongful or unjust.
Wronged; past tense or past participial form of 'wrong'. See 'wrong'.
Wrongeth; older third-person singular form of 'wrong'. See 'wrong'.
In a wrongful manner or degree.
Wrote; the past tense of write.
Angry; filled with wrath.
Wrought; worked, performed, or brought about, the older past form or participle of work.
Wroughtest; older second-person singular form of 'wrought', used with 'thou'. See 'wrought'.
Wrung; twisted, squeezed, or extorted, the past form of wring.
Yarn; spun thread prepared for weaving.
The older second-person plural personal pronoun.
Yes; indeed; moreover for stronger affirmation.
A year; the common annual measure of time.
Yearly; every year, or belonging to each year.
Yearn; to long deeply, and in older usage to be moved with tender compassion.
Yearned; past tense or past participial form of 'yearn'. See 'yearn'.
Years; annual measures of time.
Yell; to cry out loudly and harshly.
As a noun, such a cry.
Yelled; past tense or past participial form of 'yell'. See 'yell'.
Yellow; of yellow colour.
As a noun, yellow colour or yellowish appearance.
Yes; a word of assent, affirmation, or agreement.
Yesterday; the day before today, or figuratively former time.
Yesternight; last night, or in the night just past.
Yet; still, as yet, nevertheless, or besides.
To give, render, or produce.
Also to give way, submit, or surrender.
Yieldeth; older third-person singular form of 'yield'. See 'yield'.
Yielding; present participial form of 'yield', denoting ongoing action or process. See 'yield'.
A yoke is the frame laid on beasts for drawing, or by figure any burden, service, or subjection borne.
The word may signify bondage, discipline, union, or obligation.
Yoked; past tense or past participial form of 'yoke'. See 'yoke'.
Yokefellow; a companion bound in the same work, relation, or burden.
Plural form of 'yoke'. See 'yoke'.
Yonder; over there, at some distance, or that which is farther off.
You; the second-person pronoun, singular or plural.
You-Ward; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Young; not advanced in age; youthful.
Younger; more young, or the junior of two in age or standing.
Least in age; the most young among those compared.
Belonging to you; the second-person possessive form.
A pronoun of reference, possession, or person according to its use.
Yourselves; the reflexive or emphatic pronoun referring to you.
The early part of life; young age; the state of being young.
Belonging to youth; young or characteristic of youth.
Plural form of 'youth'. See 'youth'.
Zaanaim; the place of the oak or plain near Kedesh where Heber the Kenite pitched his tent.
Zaanaim.note: Location: Often identified with Hanot Taggarim.

definitions: Zaanan; a Judean town named in Micah's lament over the coming invasion.
Zaanan.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tell ed Duweir.

definitions: Zaanannim; another biblical spelling of Zaanaim, the place near Kedesh associated with Heber the Kenite.
Zaanannim.note: Location: Often identified with Hanot Taggarim.

definitions: Zaavan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Zavan.
Zabad; chiefly the name of a conspirator against Joash and also of men in genealogical records.
Zabbai; the name of post-exilic men, including one who repaired the wall and one who had taken a strange wife.
Zabbud; the son of Nathan, serving as a principal officer and king's friend under Solomon.
Zabdi; chiefly the ancestor of Achan and also the name of other Israelites in the records.
Zabdiel; the name of men in Scripture, including a priestly chief and the father of Jashobeam.
Zabud; the son of Nathan, priest and king's friend in Solomon's court.
Zabulon; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Elon.
Zaccai; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Zacchaeus; the chief publican of Jericho whom Jesus called and saved.
Zacchur; chiefly a Levitical, musical, and rebuilding-era name in the post-exilic community.
Zaccur; chiefly a Levitical and covenant-signing name after the exile.
Zachariah; a name-form of Zechariah used of many men in Scripture, including prophet, priest, counsellor, and father of John the Baptist.
Zacharias; the Greek name-form of Zechariah, especially the priest and father of John the Baptist.
Zacher; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies, related in form to Zechariah.
Zadok; the priest who remained faithful to David and whose house held the high-priestly line.
Zaham; a son of Rehoboam in the royal line of Judah.
Zair; the place to which Joram crossed in his campaign against Edom.
Zair.note: Location: Often identified with Al Safi, though the exact site is debated.

definitions: Zalaph; the father of Hanun, who helped repair the wall in Nehemiah's day.
Zalmon; one of David's mighty men, the Ahohite; the same warrior appears under the form Ilai.
Zalmonah; a wilderness encampment in Israel's itinerary after Mount Hor.
Zalmonah.note: Location: Often identified with Wadi es Salmaneh, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Zalmunna; a Midianite king slain by Gideon after the overthrow of Midian.
Zamzummims; an ancient giant-like people of Ammonite tradition, akin to the Rephaim.
Zanoah; the Judahite town rebuilt after the exile, also known from the lowland and hill-country lists.
Zanoah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Khirbet Zanu.

definitions: Zaphnath-Paaneah; a compound proper name or titled expression found in Scripture.
Zaphon; a town in the territory of Gad in the Jordan valley.
Zaphon.note: Location: Commonly identified with a site near the Jordan valley; Jebel Aqra is a debated proposal.

definitions: Zara; the Greek name-form of Zerah, especially the son of Judah by Tamar.
Zarah; a name-form of Zerah used especially for the son of Judah by Tamar.
Zareah; another biblical spelling of Zorah, the Danite town associated with Samson.
Zareah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zareathites; people from Zorah or descended from those associated with Zorah.
Zareathites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zared; the brook or valley crossed by Israel on the border of Moab.
Zarephath; the Phoenician town where Elijah stayed with the widow, also known in Greek as Sarepta.
Zaretan; another biblical spelling of Zarthan or Zeredathah in the Jordan valley near Solomon's bronze works.
Zaretan.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zareth-Shahar; a town in the uplands east of the Dead Sea within Reuben's territory.
Zarhites; descendants of Zerah reckoned among the families of Judah.
Zartanah; a spelling of Zarthan, the Jordan-valley locality associated with Solomon's foundry work.
Zartanah.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zarthan; the Jordan-valley locality where Solomon cast bronze vessels.
Zarthan.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zatthu; the head of a family of returned exiles, a form related to Zattu.
Zattu; the head of a family whose descendants returned from exile.
Zavan; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Zaavan.
Zaza; a son of Jonathan in the genealogies of Judah.
Fervent eagerness and ardor of spirit for a cause or person.
Fervent, eager, or ardent in devotion and effort.
In a zealous manner or degree.
Zebadiah; chiefly a widely used Levitical, official, and tribal name borne by many men in Scripture.
Zebah; a Midianite king captured and slain by Gideon.
Zebaim; the name attached especially to the valley of Zeboim and related Benjaminite references.
Zebedee; the father of James and John, the sons of Zebedee.
Zebina; one named in Ezra among the men who had taken strange wives.
Zeboiim; one of the cities of the plain destroyed with Sodom and Gomorrah.
Zeboiim.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Zeboim; another biblical spelling of Zeboiim, one of the cities of the plain destroyed in judgment.
Zeboim.note: Location: Often identified with south of the Lisan.

definitions: Zebudah; the mother of Jehoiakim king of Judah.
Zebul; the ruler of the city under Abimelech in the narrative of Shechem.
Zebulonite; a member of the tribe of Zebulun.
Zebulun; a man named in Scripture, son of Jacob, father of Elon.
Zebulunites; the members of the tribe descended from Zebulun.
Zechariah; chiefly the prophet, the father of John the Baptist, and a name borne by many priests and rulers.
Zedad; a northern boundary point in the ideal land border descriptions.
Zedekiah; especially the last king of Judah before the fall of Jerusalem, and also the name of other biblical men.
Zeeb; a Midianite prince slain in the days of Gideon.
Zelah; a town in Benjamin where Saul and Jonathan were buried.
Zelah.note: Location: Often identified with Khirbet Salah.

definitions: Zelek; one of David's mighty men, an Ammonite.
Zelophehad; a man of Manasseh whose daughters appealed for an inheritance because he had no sons.
Zelotes; the surname of Simon the apostle, meaning the Zealot.
Zelzah; the place near Rachel's sepulchre named in Samuel's sign to Saul.
Zelzah.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Khirbet Salah.

definitions: Zemaraim; a mount or ridge in Benjamin, also associated with a nearby settlement name.
Zemaraim.note: Location: Generally placed in the area around Ras et Tahuneh; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Zemarite; one belonging to the Zemarite branch named among the Canaanite peoples.
Zemira; a Benjamite ancestor in the genealogies.
Zenan; a lowland town of Judah, likely the same place lamented by Micah as Zaanan.
Zenan.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tell ed Duweir.

definitions: Zenas; the lawyer whom Paul urged Titus to help on his journey.
Zephaniah; the prophet whose book bears his name, and also the priest active in Jeremiah's day.
Zephath; the southern Canaanite city later devoted to destruction and called Hormah.
Zephath.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Masos.

definitions: Zephathah; a valley mentioned in the days of Asa near Mareshah.
Zephi; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Zepho.
Zepho; a Horite descendant in the line of Seir, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Zephi.
Zephon; a son of Gad whose descendants formed a clan, also written Ziphion.
Zephonites; descendants of Zephon reckoned among the families of Gad.
Zer; a town in the inheritance of Naphtali.
Zer.note: Location: Sometimes identified with Tell el Khirbeh.

definitions: Zerah; especially the son of Judah by Tamar and also an Edomite chief, with other biblical men of the same name.
Zerahiah; the name of men in Scripture, especially priests and leaders in Ezra's return.
Zered; the brook or valley crossed by Israel near the boundary of Moab during the wilderness journey.
Zered.note: Location: Usually identified with Wadi al Hasa.

definitions: Zereda; the Ephraimite home of Jeroboam son of Nebat.
Zereda.note: Location: Often identified with Deir Ghassaneh.

definitions: Zeredathah; the Jordan-valley locality named with Succoth in the account of Solomon's bronze casting.
Zeredathah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zererath; a place toward which the Midianites fled after Gideon's victory.
Zererath.note: Location: Often identified with Tell es Sa’idiyeh.

definitions: Zeresh; the wife of Haman in the book of Esther.
Zereth; older third-person singular form of 'zer'. See 'zer'.
Zeri; the name-form used for Izri, one of the sons of Jeduthun set over sacred music.
Zeror; an ancestor in Saul's Benjamite line.
Zeruah; the mother of Jeroboam son of Nebat.
Zerubbabel; leader of the returned exiles and governor in Judah after the captivity, associated with the rebuilding of the temple.
Zeruiah; a woman named in Scripture, daughter of Nahash, mother of Abishai.
Zetham; a son of Laadan in the Levitical genealogies.
Zethan; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Zethar; one of the chamberlains of Ahasuerus in Esther.
Zia; the head of a father's house in the tribe of Gad.
Ziba; a man named in Scripture.
Zibeon; a Horite chief and father of Anah in the line of Seir.
Zibia; the mother of Joash king of Judah.
Zibiah; the name of women in Scripture, including the mother of king Joash.
Zichri; chiefly a common priestly, Levitical, and military name borne by many men in Scripture.
Ziddim; a town in the territory of Naphtali.
Zidkijah; the father of Chenaanah, bearing a name akin to the better-known form Zedekiah.
Zidon; the son of Canaan in the table of nations, and also the ancient Phoenician city called Sidon.
Zidonians; the people of Zidon or Sidon, the Phoenician city north of Israel.
Zidonians.note: Location: Usually identified with Sandikli archaeological site in Sidon.

definitions: Zif; the second month of the old Hebrew year, associated with the time when Solomon began building the temple.
Ziha; the head of a family of Nethinims or temple servants.
Ziklag; the southern town given to David by Achish, from which he later recovered his people after the Amalekite raid.
Ziklag.note: Location: Often identified with Tell esh Sharia.

definitions: Zillah; one of the wives of Lamech in the line of Cain.
Zilpah; Leah's handmaid and the mother of Gad and Asher.
Zilthai; the name of men in Scripture, especially Benjamite or Manassite warriors who joined David.
Zimmah; the name of Levites and family heads in the genealogies.
Zimran; a son of Abraham by Keturah.
Zimri; especially the king of Israel who reigned seven days and the Simeonite slain at Baal-peor, and also the name of other biblical men.
Zin; the wilderness region connected with Kadesh and Israel's wandering.
Zin.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Zin Desert.

definitions: Zina; a descendant in the line of Shimei, corresponding to the same person elsewhere written Zizah.
Zion; the hill and city of Jerusalem in its sacred and royal aspect, often standing for God's dwelling and kingdom.
Zion.note: Location: Originally the stronghold hill in Jerusalem, then by extension the city and sanctuary centered there.

definitions: Zior; a hill-country town of Judah.
Zior.note: Location: Often placed in the area around Tel Rumeida.

definitions: Ziph; a personal name in the genealogies of Judah, distinct from the place-name.
Ziphah; a woman in the genealogies of Judah.
Ziphion; the name-form used for Zephon, a son of Gad and ancestor of a clan.
Ziphites; inhabitants of Ziph in the hill country of Judah.
Ziphites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tell Zif.

definitions: Ziphron; a northern boundary point in the ideal land border descriptions.
Zippor; the father of Balak king of Moab.
Zipporah; the wife of Moses and daughter of Jethro.
Zithri; a son in the line of Uzziel among the Levites.
Ziz; the ascent or pass by which enemy forces approached in the days of Jehoshaphat.
Ziz.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Wadi Hasasah, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Ziza; chiefly a genealogical name borne by a son of Rehoboam and an Asherite line.
Zizah; the name of men in genealogical records, including a descendant in the line of Shimei.
Zoan; the great Egyptian city also known as Tanis, often named in the prophets.
Zoar; a personal name in the genealogies, distinct from the city of Zoar.
Zoba; the name-form of Zobah used for the northern Aramean kingdom opposed by David.
Zoba.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Zobah; an Aramean kingdom in the north, often opposed by David in his campaigns.
Zobah.note: Location: Usually located in or identified with Brital.

definitions: Zobebah; a Judahite name in the genealogies.
Zohar; the name of men in Scripture, including a Hittite father-in-law line associated with Ephron and a Simeonite ancestor.
Zoheleth; a stone near En-rogel by which Adonijah held his feast when he sought the kingdom.
Zoheleth.note: Location: Generally placed in the area around Bir Ayub; the exact site is not certain.

definitions: Zoheth; a descendant in the genealogies of Judah.
Zophah; a descendant of Asher in the tribal genealogies.
Zophai; a Levite ancestor in Samuel's line, corresponding to the same name elsewhere written Zuph.
Zophar; the Naamathite friend of Job who spoke in the debates.
Zophim; a lookout or watch-place name associated with the heights of Pisgah.
Zophim.note: Location: Often located in or identified with Tila as Safa, though the exact identification is debated.

definitions: Zorah; the Danite town associated with Samson and the border between Dan and Judah.
Zorah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zorathites; inhabitants of Zorah or descendants associated with that town in Judah and Dan.
Zorathites.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zoreah; another biblical spelling of Zorah, the town associated with Samson's family.
Zoreah.note: Location: Commonly identified with Tel Tzora.

definitions: Zorites; a family or clan connected with the land of Edom or Seir in genealogical lists.
Zorobabel; the Greek name-form of Zerubbabel, leader of the returned exiles.
Zuar; the father of Nethaneel, prince of the tribe of Issachar.
Zuph; a Levite ancestor from whom the land of Zuph took its name, elsewhere represented by the form Zophai.
Zur; one of the kings or princes of Midian slain in the days of Moses.
Zuriel; the son of Abihail and prince of the Merarites.
Zurishaddai; the father of Shelumiel, prince of the tribe of Simeon.
Zuzims; an ancient people mentioned with Rephaim-like groups east of Jordan.
An account; a reckoning, report, explanation, or consideration of something.

To believe; to accept as true, place confidence in, or rely upon.

To break, shatter, divide, interrupt, break off, or violate.
To carry, lead, cause to come, or bring to pass.

To build, construct, establish, or strengthen.
Plural form of 'burden'. See 'burden'.

To call, name, summon, appoint, or invite.
Plural form of 'captain'. See 'captain'.
To care, give heed, or be concerned for; sometimes to be anxious.
To cause, make happen, or bring about by power or ordering.
To cease, stop, or come to an end.
Plural form of 'charger'. See 'charger'.

Chasteneth; older third-person singular form of 'chasten'. See 'chasten'.
To chew, grind with the teeth, or chew the cud.
Plural form of 'city'. See 'city'.
Plural form of 'closet'. See 'closet'.
Plural form of 'coast'. See 'coast'.
Comforted; past tense or past participial form of 'comfort'. See 'comfort'.
Comforteth; older third-person singular form of 'comfort'. See 'comfort'.
Plural form of 'comforter'. See 'comforter'.
To command, order with authority, or give charge.
Commended; past tense or past participial form of 'commend'. See 'commend'.
Commendeth; older third-person singular form of 'commend'. See 'commend'.
To commit, do, perform, entrust, or deliver into another's charge.
Communicated; past tense or past participial form of 'communicate'. See 'communicate'.
Plural form of 'communication'. See 'communication'.
Compasseth; older third-person singular form of 'compass'. See 'compass'.
Plural form of 'compassion'. See 'compassion'.
Compelled; past tense or past participial form of 'compel'. See 'compel'.
Condemned; past tense or past participial form of 'condemn'. See 'condemn'.
Confessed; past tense or past participial form of 'confess'. See 'confess'.
Present participial form of 'confess'. See 'confess'.
Confirmed; past tense or past participial form of 'confirm'. See 'confirm'.
Confounded; past tense or past participial form of 'confound'. See 'confound'.
Plural form of 'congregation'. See 'congregation'.
To observe thoughtfully, ponder, or take due notice of.
Constrained; past tense or past participial form of 'constrain'. See 'constrain'.
Consumed; past tense or past participial form of 'consume'. See 'consume'.
Consumeth; older third-person singular form of 'consume'. See 'consume'.
A turning, especially a turning back or turning to God.
Converted; past tense or past participial form of 'convert'. See 'convert'.
Convinced; past tense or past participial form of 'convince'. See 'convince'.
Plural form of 'creature'. See 'creature'.
Plural form of 'crown'. See 'crown'.
Crucified; past tense or past participial form of 'crucify'. See 'crucify'.
Cursed; past tense or past participial form of 'curse'. See 'curse'.
Curseth; older third-person singular form of 'curse'. See 'curse'.
Plural form of 'deacon'. See 'deacon'.
To act toward, handle, distribute, or transact.
Plural form of 'death'. See 'death'.
Deceived; past tense or past participial form of 'deceive'. See 'deceive'.
Deceiveth; older third-person singular form of 'deceive'. See 'deceive'.
Declared; past tense or past participial form of 'declare'. See 'declare'.
To make unclean, polluted, or profane.
Delighted; past tense or past participial form of 'delight'. See 'delight'.
Delivered; past tense or past participial form of 'deliver'. See 'deliver'.
Delivereth; older third-person singular form of 'deliver'. See 'deliver'.
Denied; past tense or past participial form of 'deny'. See 'deny'.
Denieth; older third-person singular form of 'deny'. See 'deny'.
Departed; past tense or past participial form of 'depart'. See 'depart'.
Departeth; older third-person singular form of 'depart'. See 'depart'.
Desired; past tense or past participial form of 'desire'. See 'desire'.
Plural form of 'desire'. See 'desire'.
Older third-person singular form of 'desire'. See 'desire'.
To ruin, consume, slay, or bring to nothing.
Devised; past tense or past participial form of 'devise'. See 'devise'.
To consume greedily or utterly.
To die; to cease living, perish, or come under death.
Discerned; past tense or past participial form of 'discern'. See 'discern'.
Discerneth; older third-person singular form of 'discern'. See 'discern'.
To divide, part, separate, or distribute.
To do, act, perform, or make.
Plural form of 'doctrine'. See 'doctrine'.
To draw, pull, bring near, lead forth, or draw out.
Dwelt; past tense form of 'dwell'. See 'dwell'.
Dwelleth; older third-person singular form of 'dwell'. See 'dwell'.
To eat, consume food, or partake.
Endeavoured; past tense or past participial form of 'endeavour'. See 'endeavour'.
To continue, remain under trial, or bear steadfastly.
Enlarged; past tense or past participial form of 'enlarge'. See 'enlarge'.
Plural form of 'ensample'. See 'ensample'.
Entangled; past tense or past participial form of 'entangle'. See 'entangle'.
To go in, come into, or enter upon.
Enticed; past tense or past participial form of 'entice'. See 'entice'.
Enticeth; older third-person singular form of 'entice'. See 'entice'.
Entreated; past tense or past participial form of 'entreat'. See 'entreat'.
Present participial form of 'envy'. See 'envy'.
Erred; past tense or past participial form of 'err'. See 'err'.
